column1: You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.
column2: 你帶著太多東西，幾乎無法移動。你最好趕快丟掉一些東西。

column1: Ah, that's better.  You're no longer so overloaded.
column2: 啊，好多了。你不再負重過度了。

column1: That really hurt.  But it only really hurt for a few moments.  The pain is quite gone now.  So is every other sensation.  Taking quick stock of your condition, you find the reason -- you're dead.  Better run away next time.
column2: 那真的很痛。但痛楚只持續了幾秒鐘。現在疼痛已經完全消失，其他知覺也一樣。你快速盤點自己的狀況，找到了原因——你死了。下次最好還是快逃吧。

column1: Obviously a graduate of the Famous Adventurers' Correspondence School.
column2: 一看就是「著名冒險家函授學校」的高材生。

column1: It is %s.
column2: 這是%s。

column1: You can't open %s.
column2: 你無法打開%s。

column1: You can't close %s.
column2: 你無法關上%s。

column1: The smell is exactly as you would expect.
column2: 這氣味正如你所預期的一樣。

column1: You can't move %s.
column2: 你無法移動%s。

column1: You wouldn't want to eat %s.
column2: 你不會想吃%s的。

column1: You can't get %s.
column2: 你無法取得%s。

column1: You can't climb %s.
column2: 你無法攀爬%s。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to %s.
column2: 別費心跟%s說話了。

column1: Quest for Glory II:  Trial By Fire  [%d of %d]
column2: 英雄傳奇 II：烈火試煉  [%d之%d]

column1: You collapse in the hot desert air and never awaken.  Truly it is said that "Water is life itself in a desert land."
column2: 你在灼熱的沙漠空氣中倒下，再也沒有醒來。正如人們所說：「在沙漠之地，水就是生命本身。」

column1: You must get water soon, or you will surely die of thirst.
column2: 你必須盡快取得水，否則必將渴死。

column1: The dry desert air seems to suck the moisture right out of your body.
column2: 乾燥的沙漠空氣似乎正把你體內的水分一點一滴抽走。

column1: Your Reversal spell has worn off.
column2: 你的「反轉」術效力已過。

column1: Your Reversal spell is wearing thin.
column2: 你的反轉法術效力正逐漸減弱。

column1: Memory fragmented.
column2: 記憶體零碎化。

column1: You thought for a moment that you saw a deadly Fragmentation Bug, but then realize that it is actually the harmless Southern variety.  You squash it under your boot, and it vanishes.
column2: 你一度以為看到了致命的「分裂蟲」，但隨即發現那其實是無害的南方變種。你一腳將牠踩扁，牠便消失無蹤。

column1: Great idea.  If you could, you would.
column2: 好主意。如果辦得到，你早就做了。

column1: The lamp seems to enjoy that (but it's just your imagination).
column2: 那盞燈似乎很享受（不過那只是你的想像）。

column1: You faintly hear "To the left... up... ahh, right there!"  The lamp glows faintly with pleasure, then decides it isn't dark enough to really put on a show, and goes out again.
column2: 你隱約聽到「往左…再上面一點…啊，就是那裡！」燈微微發出愉悅的光芒，接著覺得天色還不夠暗、不值得真正表演一番，於是又熄滅了。

column1: It's not as effective as Raseirian Express.
column2: 效果不如「拉希爾特快」。

column1: Silly you!  They're right there on the end of your nose!
column2: 你這傻瓜!它們不就在你鼻尖上嗎!

column1: You put on the Ali Fakir Genuine X-Ray Glasses.  Suddenly, everything appears as though you were looking through a veil.
column2: 你戴上了阿里·法基爾正宗透視眼鏡。突然間，一切看起來就像隔著一層薄紗。

column1: Yeh!  Glasses!  Maybe that's what you need to stop those migraines.  Too bad you don't have any.
column2: 對啦!眼鏡!說不定那正是治你偏頭痛的東西。可惜你一副也沒有。

column1: You take off the glasses and put them away.
column2: 你摘下眼鏡收了起來。

column1: You're not wearing them.
column2: 你沒穿著它們。

column1: Ok, you get the empty pot.
column2: 好，你拿到了空罐子。

column1: Ok, you get the pot of dirt.
column2: 好，你拿到了裝土的罐子。

column1: You have it already.
column2: 你已經有這個了。

column1: There aren't any here.
column2: 這裡沒有。

column1: Ok, you pick up the bag.
column2: 好，你撿起了袋子。

column1: You can't get that.
column2: 那個你拿不到。

column1: You retrieve your shield.
column2: 你取回你的盾牌。

column1: There isn't a shield here.
column2: 這裡沒有盾牌。

column1: You will have to finish off your opponent first.
column2: 你得先解決掉對手才行。

column1: You pick up the loose daggers.
column2: 你撿起那些散落的匕首。

column1: You retrieve your knives and carefully wipe them clean for reuse.
column2: 你收回了飛刀，仔細擦拭乾淨以便重複使用。

column1: Ok, you pick %s up.
column2: 好，你撿起了%s。

column1: Which one?
column2: 哪一個？

column1: I don't know what you're trying to drop.
column2: 我不知道你想丟什麼。

column1: You drop your shield.
column2: 你放下了盾牌。

column1: You'd better not.  That's your last dagger, and you may need it for combat.
column2: 還是別了。那是你最後一把匕首,戰鬥時或許用得上。

column1: O.k.  Your Saurus is now standing on the ground.
column2: 好，你的索魯斯現在站在地面上了。

column1: You drop the %s.
column2: 你放下了%s。

column1: You can't drop something you don't have.
column2: 你不能丟你沒有的東西。

column1: The silver dagger isn't balanced properly for throwing.
column2: 這把銀匕首的重心不適合用來投擲。

column1: You don't have one to throw.
column2: 你沒有可以投擲的東西。

column1: That isn't useful here.
column2: 這在這裡沒有用。

column1: I'm a frayed knot.
column2: 我是個磨破的繩結哪(I'm afraid not 的諧音)。

column1: To do that, you must cast the Levitate spell.
column2: 要這麼做，你必須施展「飄浮術」。

column1: You don't need to retreat if you're not in combat.
column2: 你不在戰鬥中，不需要撤退。

column1: You're already sneaking.
column2: 你已經在潛行了。

column1: You're about as stealthy as the average Goon.
column2: 你潛行起來,跟一般的打手一樣藏不住身。

column1: Pardon, Effendi.  I don't know how you would use that here.
column2: 抱歉，閣下。我不知道那東西在這裡該怎麼用。

column1: Aggressive, aren't you?
column2: 還挺好鬥的嘛?

column1: You don't need to climb it.
column2: 你不需要攀爬它。

column1: No one responds.
column2: 沒人回應。

column1: You certainly do!
column2: 你當然需要！

column1: There's no point in trying that here.
column2: 在這兒這麼做沒什麼意義。

column1: You delicately insert the lockpick into your left nostril.
column2: 你輕輕地把開鎖工具插進你的左鼻孔。

column1: Unfortunately, you push it in too far, causing yourself a cerebral hemorrhage.  Guess you should have practiced some more on less difficult locks.
column2: 可惜,你伸得太深,害自己腦溢血了。看來你該先拿簡單些的鎖多練練。

column1: Success!  You now have an open nose.
column2: 成功!你的鼻子現在「暢通無阻」了。

column1: You get no response.
column2: 你沒得到任何回應。

column1: It's not for sale.
column2: 那不是賣的。

column1: You're welcome!
column2: 不客氣！

column1: That's OK.
column2: 沒關係。

column1: Hi!
column2: 嗨！

column1: Bye!
column2: 再見！

column1: You're not close enough to a door.
column2: 你離門太遠。

column1: No one hears you knocking.
column2: 沒有人聽見你敲門。

column1: You don't need to.
column2: 你不需要。

column1: There is no one here who would appreciate them.
column2: 這裡沒有人會欣賞這些。

column1: You have no reason to do that.
column2: 你沒理由那麼做。

column1: You don't find anything interesting.
column2: 你沒找到有趣的東西。

column1: This is a serious game, %s.
column2: 這是場嚴肅的遊戲，%s。

column1: You see no silly clowns here, at least not now.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到任何滑稽的小丑，至少現在沒有。

column1: Don't worry about it.  It's all being taken care of.
column2: 別擔心，一切都已經處理好了。

column1: Sorry, but there is nothing I can tell you about that.
column2: 抱歉，關於那件事我無可奉告。

column1: You don't see any here.
column2: 你看不到這裡有任何東西。

column1: You really can't rest here.
column2: 你真的不能在這裡休息。

column1: This isn't a safe place to sleep.
column2: 這裡不是安全的睡覺地方。

column1: Boy, did you look silly!
column2: 老天,你那模樣可真蠢!

column1: Wait a minute!  You can't do that here!!!
column2: 等一下！你不能在這裡這麼做！！！

column1: You will not need to use the word "%s" in this game.
column2: 你在遊戲中不需要用到「%s」這個詞。

column1: Please try a different way of saying that.
column2: 試試用另一種說法。

column1: I'm not sure what you're trying to do.
column2: 我不確定你想做什麼。

column1: You're not close enough.
column2: 你還不夠近。

column1: Please, Effendi.  A thousand pardons, but I do not understand
column2: 拜託，閣下。萬分抱歉，但我不明白

column1: You've already done that.
column2: 你已經做過了。

column1: You can't do that now.
column2: 你現在沒法那麼做。

column1: You don't have it.
column2: 你沒有。

column1: That wouldn't be a good idea right now.
column2: 現在這麼做不妙。

column1: Go ahead.  Just do it.
column2: 去吧，就這樣做。

column1: do you mind if I join you?
column2: 你介意我加入你嗎？

column1: Could you run that by me once more, please?
column2: 能否請你再說一次？

column1: Every hero needs a break now and then.  Have a nice nap.
column2: 每位英雄都需要時不時休息一下。\n好好睡一覺吧。

column1: DURING THE GAME: Click at the top of the screen or press ESC to use the menus. Additional shortcuts are shown there. Click Right or Shift-Click Left Mouse Button on an object to look at it.       IN TYPING WINDOWS: Arrows, Home and End move the cursor, or click anywhere with the mouse. Ctrl-C clears the line.       IN DIALOG WINDOWS: Select the outlined item with <Enter> and use Tab and Shift-Tab to move between  choices.  Or click with the mouse to select an item.  ESC always cancels.
column2: 遊戲進行中：點擊螢幕上方或按 ESC 開啟選單，選單上會列出更多快速鍵。點擊滑鼠右鍵，或按住 Shift 點擊滑鼠左鍵可查看物件。       輸入視窗中：方向鍵、Home 與 End 可移動游標，也可用滑鼠點擊任意位置。Ctrl-C 可清除整行。       對話視窗中：按 <Enter> 選取外框標示的項目，用 Tab 與 Shift-Tab 在選項間  切換。  或用滑鼠點擊選取項目。  ESC 一律可取消。

column1: You place your thumb upon your nose with the hand held perpendicular to the face and the fingers outspread.  You then wiggle your fingers while focusing your eyes on the thumb and patting your belly with the other hand.
column2: 你把拇指抵在鼻尖,手與臉垂直、五指張開,接著一邊扭動手指、目光盯著拇指,一邊用另一隻手拍著肚子。

column1: (We don't show any of that here, in order not to embarrass those people with tender sensibilities.)
column2: (這裡就不一一展示了,免得讓那些心思細膩的人難為情。)

column1: You touch the palm of your right hand to your left shoulder, extend the third digit of your left hand until the pad of the finger touches the underside of your right eye, touch your left toe to your left wrist, and twiddle your thumbs furiously.
column2: 你以右手掌心貼住左肩,伸出左手中指,直到指腹觸及右眼下方,再讓左腳趾碰著左手腕,然後拚命轉動兩根拇指。

column1: Placing the index finger of each hand in opposing corners of your mouth, you simultaneously pull your fingers outward, extending and wiggling your tongue vertically.
column2: 你把雙手食指分別放在嘴角兩側，同時向外拉扯，並上下擺動伸出的舌頭。

column1: Sticking your shoomflufluf out sideways, you touch your bozombie (wet side up, of course) to your flanigoogle, being careful not to cut off the circulation to your capitalary artery.
column2: 你把你的「咻夫拉夫」往旁邊一伸,讓你的「波喪比」(當然啦,濕的那面朝上)碰上你的「法尼咕咕」,還得小心別切斷通往「資本動脈」的血流。

column1: CAUTION:  Do not try this at home!
column2: 注意：請勿在家嘗試！

column1: Using both hands, you touch your left knee, your right instep, both temples, and that small spot in between your shoulder blades that always itches.
column2: 你用雙手依序碰觸左膝、右腳背、兩側太陽穴，以及肩胛骨之間那個總是發癢的小地方。

column1: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
column2: 沒這必要,你又不餓。

column1: You eat some of the magical fruit.  It tastes strange.
column2: 你吃了一些魔法水果。味道很奇怪。

column1: You don't have any fruit.
column2: 你沒有水果。

column1: The rations are tasteless but filling.
column2: 這乾糧雖然無味,倒挺管飽。

column1: You aren't carrying any rations.
column2: 你沒有帶乾糧。

column1: Ugh.  You don't want to eat THAT!
column2: 噁。你才不會想吃那個！

column1: You'd better not.
column2: 你最好別這麼做。

column1: You can't drink that.
column2: 你不能喝那個。

column1: I don't know what kind of pill that is.  Remember, just say no to unknown pills.  Your local apothecary can give you advice on safe and sane pill use.
column2: 我不知道那是什麼藥丸。記住，來路不明的藥丸一律拒絕。當地的藥師可以給你安全用藥的建議。

column1: You don't have any of those pills.
column2: 你沒有那種藥丸。

column1: That feels good; just what you needed to relieve some of the aches and pains of everyday adventuring.
column2: 這感覺真好,正好替你舒緩了日常冒險帶來的痠痛。

column1: You take the pill, but it doesn't seem to have any effect.  Next time, follow the prescription.
column2: 你服下藥丸,卻似乎毫無作用。下次,照處方來。

column1: You feel a surge of power as the magical energy of the pill releases itself into your system.
column2: 藥丸的魔法能量釋入你體內,你感到一股力量湧起。

column1: You feel refreshed, invigorated, ready to take on any challenge -- at least, for a while.
column2: 你覺得神清氣爽、精力充沛,準備好迎接任何挑戰——至少能撐一陣子。

column1: Unlike heads, two poison cure pills are no better than one.  But the pill does no harm either.
column2: 跟頭不一樣,解毒藥丸吃兩顆並不比一顆好,不過吃了也無妨。

column1: The only effect is a tingling sensation.  But, if the apothecary is to be trusted, you should be safe from the effects of poison for a while.
column2: 唯一的效果是一陣刺痛感。不過，如果藥師的話可信，你應該能暫時免受毒素影響。

column1: The pill makes you thirsty, so you pull out your waterskin.
column2: 這藥丸讓你感到口渴，於是你拿出了水袋。

column1: Alas, you are out of water.
column2: 唉,你的水喝光了。

column1: You drink the last drops from your waterskin.  You might want to start looking around for a place where you can refill it.
column2: 你喝下了水袋裡最後幾滴水。你該開始找個地方把它裝滿了。

column1: Your water skin is getting much easier to carry.  Unfortunately, that probably means you don't have much water left.
column2: 你的水袋變得輕巧許多，好拿多了。可惜這大概表示你剩下的水已經不多了。

column1: You take a careful sip from your waterskin.  It tastes great.
column2: 你小心地就著水袋啜了一口,味道好極了。

column1: After an hour's rest, you feel refreshed.
column2: 休息了一小時後，你感到神清氣爽。

column1: You will have to practice some more to improve your lockpicking skill.
column2: 你得多加練習，才能提升開鎖技巧。

column1: You are so exhausted that everything you do hurts.  Better get some rest.
column2: 你筋疲力盡到做任何事都會傷害你。最好休息一下。

column1: Talk about over-exerting yourself... that last little maneuver wore you out so much it hurt.  In fact, it hurt so much, it killed you.  Take it easier next time.
column2: 這就叫用力過猛了……剛才那一小招把你累得發疼。事實上,疼到把你給疼死了。下回悠著點。

column1: %s on day %d.
column2: %s，第 %d 天。

column1: You are getting tired.
column2: 你開始覺得累了。

column1: You are exhausted from lack of sleep.
column2: 你因睡眠不足而精疲力竭。

column1: You just ate your last ration; you'd better get some more food soon.
column2: 你剛吃掉最後一份糧食；你最好趕快找點東西吃。

column1: You're starving.  Better find some food *soon*!
column2: 你快餓死了。趕快找食物！

column1: While it's true that some foods may be bad for your health, *no* food is even worse.  You have, quite literally, starved to death.
column2: 雖然有些食物確實對健康不好，但完全*不吃*東西更糟。你就這樣，貨真價實地餓死了。

column1: You're really getting hungry.
column2: 你真的開始餓了。

column1: You don't have any flowers.
column2: 你沒有任何花。

column1: You already have the %s.
column2: 你已經有%s了。

column1: How many %s do you want?
column2: 你想要幾個%s？

column1: You put the %s away.
column2: 你把%s收起來了。

column1: You pay for the %s and put %s away.
column2: 你付了%s的錢，並把%s收起來了。

column1: You see nothing out of the ordinary.
column2: 你沒看到任何異常之處。

column1: What you see doesn't hold your interest.
column2: 你看到的東西引不起你的興趣。

column1: You look around and things look normal.
column2: 你環顧四周，一切看起來很正常。

column1: It doesn't seem important right now.
column2: 現在看起來不太重要。

column1: Day is dawning
column2: 天色漸明

column1: It is mid-morning
column2: 已是上午中段

column1: It is midday
column2: 正是中午

column1: It is mid-afternoon
column2: 已是下午中段

column1: Sunset approaches
column2: 太陽西下

column1: The night is still young
column2: 夜幕尚淺

column1: It is the middle of the night
column2: 夜深了

column1: It is not yet dawn
column2: 天色未明

column1: blah
column2: 廢話

column1: Brass Centime%s
column2: 銅仙%s

column1: One hundred tiny brass centimes are worth a single Golden Dinar.
column2: 一百枚細小的銅仙相當於一枚金第納爾。

column1: Golden Dinar%s
column2: 金第納爾%s

column1: The heavy golden Dinar is the coin preferred by nobility.  The front bears the image of Suleiman ben Daoud, Master of the Djinn.
column2: 沉甸甸的金第納爾是貴族偏愛的錢幣。正面刻著精靈之主蘇萊曼·本·達伍德的肖像。

column1: Food Ration%s
column2: 口糧%s

column1: Standard adventuring rations.
column2: 標準冒險糧食。

column1: Broadsword%s
column2: 闊劍%s

column1: Your heavy, one-handed broadsword is an admirable weapon for a strong warrior.
column2: 你那把沉重的單手闊劍,對強壯的戰士而言是件令人稱羨的兵器。

column1: Dagger%s
column2: 匕首%s

column1: Small and sleek local daggers are most useful for throwing or for close fighting.
column2: 這種小巧俐落的當地匕首，最適合用於投擲或近身格鬥。

column1: Leather Armor%s
column2: 皮甲%s

column1: Your leather armor is made of leather boiled in wax, a process which preserves and strengthens the material.  As a side effect, there is a certain amount of olfactory discomfort in warm climates.
column2: 你的皮甲是用蠟煮過的皮革製成,這道工序能保養並強化材質。副作用是:在溫暖的氣候下,會有那麼點氣味上的不適。

column1: Shield%s
column2: 盾牌%s

column1: Your shield is made of hardwood and iron.  It is both strong and heavy.
column2: 你的盾牌以硬木與鐵製成,既堅固又沉重。

column1: Piece%s of Paper
column2: 紙張%s

column1: Blank pieces of paper.
column2: 空白紙片。

column1: Small Rock%s
column2: 小石頭%s

column1: Small rocks, suitable for throwing.  Generously available in rocky, desert areas.
column2: 適合投擲的小石頭，在多岩的沙漠地帶隨處可見。

column1: Lock Pick%s
column2: 開鎖器%s

column1: An Acme Lock Pick, suitable for most standard locks and a few squirrely ones.
column2: 一把「頂尖牌」開鎖器,應付大多數標準鎖綽綽有餘,連幾種刁鑽的也難不倒它。

column1: Thief's Tool Kit%s
column2: 盜賊工具組%s

column1: Your Thief's Tool Kit contains a variety of slim, sharp instruments.  In the right hands, these implements will open any locked (but unbarred) door.
column2: 你的盜賊工具組裡有各式細薄鋒利的工具。在行家手中，這些工具能打開任何上鎖（但未加閂）的門。

column1: Thieves' Guild License%s
column2: 盜賊公會執照%s

column1: Your Thieves' Guild License authorizes you to do business with Thieves without getting killed by them.
column2: 你的盜賊公會執照讓你能與盜賊做生意，而不會被他們殺掉。

column1: Pouch%s of Incense
column2: 香料袋%s

column1: You have in your possession a quantity of Fire Elemental-attracting incense.
column2: 你身上帶著一些能吸引火元素精靈的薰香。

column1: Whirl%s of Beard
column2: 鬍鬚捲%s

column1: In your pack is a whirl of the Desert Dervish's beard.
column2: 你的背包裡裝著一捲沙漠苦行僧的鬍鬚。

column1: Chainmail Armor%s
column2: 鎖子甲%s

column1: Your chainmail armor is quite effective for protection, but it is VERY heavy.
column2: 你的鎖子甲防護效果相當好,但「非常」沉重。

column1: Magic Rope%s
column2: 魔法繩索%s

column1: The Magic Rope you purchased from the Magic Shop looks like an ordinary rope, although it coils tighter and weighs less.
column2: 你從魔法商店買來的魔法繩索，外觀就像一條普通繩子，只是盤起來更緊、也更輕。

column1: Gold Coin%s
column2: 金幣%s

column1: You still retain golden coins from the land of Spielburg.
column2: 你身上還留著來自史畢柏格之地的金幣。

column1: Healing Pill%s
column2: 治療藥丸%s

column1: You have healing pills to magically restore your health.
column2: 你有可以用魔法恢復生命值的治療藥丸。

column1: Mana Pill%s
column2: 法力藥丸%s

column1: You have mana pills to magically restore your magical ability.
column2: 你有可以用魔法恢復法力值的法力藥丸。

column1: Vigor Pill%s
column2: 體力藥丸%s

column1: You have vigor pills to magically restore your stamina.
column2: 你有可以用魔法恢復體力值的體力藥丸。

column1: Dispel Potion%s
column2: 解除藥水%s

column1: You have a potion which will dispel enchantments.
column2: 你有一瓶能解除魔咒的藥水。

column1: Map%s
column2: 地圖%s

column1: You have a map of the streets and plazas of Shapeir.
column2: 你有一張夏皮爾街道與廣場的地圖。

column1: Compass%s
column2: 羅盤%s

column1: Your compass never tells you which way to go, but it always tells you which way you're facing.
column2: 你的羅盤從不告訴你該往哪走,卻總能告訴你正面朝著哪。

column1: Fine Sword%s
column2: 精良長劍%s

column1: You are carrying a fine sword from the weapon shop of Issur.
column2: 你帶著一把來自伊蘇爾武器店的精良長劍。

column1: Bellows
column2: 風箱

column1: Saul's bellows (well, actually, they're Issur's).
column2: 掃羅的風箱(嗯,其實是伊蘇爾的)。

column1: Flower Bouquet%s
column2: 花束%s

column1: Flowers seem to wilt quickly in the desert heat.
column2: 在沙漠的酷熱下,花兒似乎謝得特別快。

column1: Sapphire Pin%s
column2: 藍寶石胸針%s

column1: The Kattas gave you this sapphire pin in thanks for your service to their kind.
column2: 卡塔族送給你這枚藍寶石胸針，感謝你為他們族群所做的貢獻。

column1: Griffin Feather%s
column2: 獅鷲羽毛%s

column1: The Griffin's feather is grey-striped with silver at the tip.  There is brown fur attached to the quill.
column2: 這根獅鷲羽毛帶著灰色條紋,尖端泛銀,羽根上還黏著棕色的絨毛。

column1: Scorpion Tail%s
column2: 蠍尾%s

column1: The scorpion's tail looks as venomous as it is.
column2: 這條蠍尾看起來和實際一樣毒。

column1: Ghoul claw%s
column2: 食屍鬼爪%s

column1: Ghoul claws are horrible things to look at.
column2: 食屍鬼的爪子看著就讓人發毛。

column1: Soulforge%s
column2: 魂鑄劍%s

column1: You are carrying Rakeesh's flaming sword, "Soulforge."
column2: 你帶著拉基什的烈焰之劍,「魂鑄劍」。

column1: Pot%s of Dirt
column2: 土罐%s

column1: It is simply a small, clay pot filled with dirt.
column2: 只是個裝滿泥土的小陶罐。

column1: Brass Lamp%s
column2: 黃銅油燈%s

column1: The shiny brass lamp is engraved on the bottom: "Aladdin Lamp Co."
column2: 這盞閃亮的黃銅油燈底部刻著:「阿拉丁燈具公司」。

column1: Basket%s
column2: 籃子%s

column1: The local reed baskets are simple but elegant.
column2: 這種本地蘆葦籃簡樸而優雅。

column1: Empty Pot%s
column2: 空罐%s

column1: You have an empty clay pot.
column2: 你有一個空陶罐。

column1: Compassion Fruit%s
column2: 慈悲果%s

column1: From the branches of Julanar, you have obtained The Fruit of Compassion.
column2: 你從茱拉娜的枝椏上摘得了慈悲之果。

column1: Waterskin%s
column2: 水囊%s

column1: Your leather waterskin is crucial adventuring equipment in the desert.
column2: 你的皮製水囊是沙漠冒險不可或缺的裝備。

column1: Cloth Bag%s
column2: 布袋%s

column1: You are carrying a large cloth bag.
column2: 你帶著一個大布袋。

column1: Powder%s of Burning
column2: 燃燒粉%s

column1: From Harik you have obtained a quantity of the Powder of Burning.
column2: 你從哈里克那裡取得了一些燃燒粉。

column1: EOF Secret Membership Badge%s
column2: EOF秘密會員徽章%s

column1: This genuine imitation brass Secret Membership Badge, with its secret code and secret runic engraving (so secret that NOBODY knows what it means), marks you as an EOF member in good standing.
column2: 這枚如假包換的仿黃銅秘密會員徽章,上頭刻有秘密代碼與秘密符文(秘密到連誰也不知道是什麼意思),證明你是EOF的正式會員。

column1: Raseirian Visa%s
column2: 拉希爾簽證%s

column1: It's your Raseirian Visa.  Don't leave town without it.
column2: 這是你的拉希爾簽證,出城千萬別忘了帶。

column1: Container%s of Oil
column2: 油罐%s

column1: You possess a small container of saurus oil.
column2: 你擁有一小罐索魯斯油。

column1: Waterskin%s with Water Elemental
column2: 裝有水元素的水囊%s

column1: You are carrying a leather waterskin containing the essence of the Water Elemental.
column2: 你帶著一個皮水袋,裡頭封著水元素精靈的精華。

column1: Djinni Ring%s
column2: 精靈戒指%s

column1: The still, small voice within this ring must be that of a Djinni -- a very weak one.
column2: 這戒指裡那細微的低語,想必是個鎮尼——而且是非常孱弱的一個。

column1: Silk Scarf%s
column2: 絲巾%s

column1: This is the simple silken scarf given you as a token by the harem girl.
column2: 這是後宮女子贈予你作為信物的樸素絲巾。

column1: Hand Mirror%s
column2: 手鏡%s

column1: This is the harem girl's hand mirror.
column2: 這是那後宮女子的手鏡。

column1: Black Bird%s
column2: 黑鳥%s

column1: You have a statuette of a mysterious black bird -- a falcon perhaps?
column2: 你有一尊神祕黑鳥的小雕像——也許是隻獵鷹?

column1: Poison Cure Pill%s
column2: 解毒丸%s

column1: You have antidote pills to magically cure venomous stings and scratches.
column2: 你擁有能以魔法治癒毒螫毒傷的解毒丸。

column1: Change%s of Clothing
column2: 換洗衣物%s

column1: Shema has given you a spare suit of clothes for overnight trips, sales meetings, etc.
column2: 雪瑪給了你一套備用衣裳,供你過夜出行、洽談生意等場合穿。

column1: Saurus%s
column2: 索魯斯%s

column1: You own a trusty riding Saurus.
column2: 你擁有一匹可靠的坐騎索魯斯。

column1: Bag%s of Sand
column2: 沙袋%s

column1: You are carrying a cloth bag with sand from the desert.
column2: 你帶著一個裝有沙漠黃沙的布袋。

column1: Bag%s with Earth Elemental
column2: 裝有土元素的布袋%s

column1: Inside a cloth bag you have placed the essence of the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你把土元素的精華裝進了一個布袋裡。

column1: Silver Tea Service%s
column2: 銀製茶具%s

column1: The silver tea service is very finely wrought and was once the property of Kareem bin Ma'amar.  It's a good thing you didn't take Kareem with your tea.
column2: 這套銀茶具做工極其精細,曾是卡里姆·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。幸好你沒把卡里姆連茶一起「取」了去。

column1: Pair%s of X-Ray Glasses
column2: X光眼鏡%s

column1: Keapon Laffin sold you one pair of Ali Fakir's Genuine X-Ray Glasses.
column2: 姬朋·拉芬賣給你一副阿里·法基爾的正牌透視眼鏡。

column1: Leather Purse%s
column2: 皮製錢包%s

column1: Your leather purse uses a drawstring for closure.
column2: 你的皮製錢包用抽繩束口。

column1: Silver Dagger%s
column2: 銀製匕首%s

column1: You have in your possession a beautifully crafted dagger made of pure silver which was once the property of Jabbar bin Ma'amar.
column2: 你擁有一把以純銀打造、做工精美的匕首,它曾是賈巴爾·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。

column1: Emerald Bowl%s
column2: 翡翠碗%s

column1: This beautiful emerald-studded silver bowl was once the property of the silversmith Abu bin Ma'amar.
column2: 這只鑲著翡翠的精美銀碗,曾是銀匠阿布·賓·馬阿瑪爾的財產。

column1: Rusty Nail%s
column2: 生鏽釘子%s

column1: It is an ordinary, slightly rusty nail.
column2: 一根普通的釘子,還有點生鏽。

column1: It's just your average, everyday magic lamp containing a Fire Elemental.
column2: 就是一盞再平常不過、封著火元素精靈的魔法神燈罷了。

column1: Issur's bellows really blow up a storm with the Air Elemental inside.
column2: 把風元素精靈關進去,伊蘇的風箱當真能颳起一場風暴。

column1: Sorry about that.  The monster is dead broke.
column2: 抱歉,這隻怪物身無分文。

column1: Quit sightseeing and watch what you're doing!
column2: 別光顧著看風景,專心一點!

column1: You're too close.
column2: 你靠得太近了。

column1: The large amount of metal in your shield makes spell-casting impossible.
column2: 你盾牌上大量的金屬讓你無法施法。

column1: You don't have time for that.
column2: 你沒空做那個。

column1: Your weapon is now magically charged.
column2: 你的兵器現在帶著魔法電荷了。

column1: Your Dazzle spell doesn't seem to have had any effect on your opponent.
column2: 你的眩光術似乎對敵人毫無效果。

column1: That doesn't seem to have done much of anything.  There must not have been a spell to Trigger.
column2: 這似乎沒起什麼作用,想必沒有可觸發的法術。

column1: You are now protected by the power of Reversal.
column2: 你現在受「反轉」之力護佑。

column1: As you know, that spell is not usable in combat.  Casting it would leave you open to attack.
column2: 你知道那個法術不能在戰鬥中使用,施展它會讓你門戶大開。

column1: It was a tough battle, and you lost. Never fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and... What do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
column2: 這場戰鬥很激烈,你輸了。別害怕!你只需要讀檔,然後……等等,你說什麼「讀什麼檔?」

column1: You pick up a few small rocks.
column2: 你撿起了幾塊小石頭。

column1: Which way?
column2: 往哪個方向?

column1: There's no need to get that.
column2: 沒必要拿那個。

column1: No one responds
column2: 沒有人回應

column1: One thing at a time, Hero.
column2: 一次一件事,英雄。

column1: You're at the end of your rope.  It's magic is gone, and so is it.
column2: 你的繩子已經用到極限,魔力耗盡,繩子也隨之消失。

column1: You are suddenly, forcefully reminded that "It isn't the fall that hurts, it's when you hit the ground."
column2: 你猛然想起一句話:「跌落本身不痛,痛的是著地那一刻。」

column1: As your mind thinks the thought of falling...
column2: 就在你腦中閃過墜落的念頭時……

column1: Ouch!  Why do you treat yourself so inappropriately?
column2: 哎喲!你幹嘛這樣折騰自己?

column1: Good thing the Powder of Burning is only activated by Magical beings.  Too bad you threw it away.
column2: 幸好燃燒粉只對魔法生物起作用,可惜你已經把它丟掉了。

column1: Good thing you can't hurt yourself like that.
column2: 幸好你不會這樣傷到自己。

column1: You detect no magic here.
column2: 你在這裡沒偵測到魔法。

column1: That isn't a spell.
column2: 那不是法術。

column1: You don't know how to cast spells.
column2: 你不知道怎樣施法。

column1: You don't know that spell.
column2: 你不知道那個咒語。

column1: You don't have enough Magic Points to cast that spell.
column2: 你沒有足夠的法力來施展那個咒語。

column1: Give yourself a little room, Hero.
column2: 給自己留點空間,英雄。

column1: That's hard to do without %s.
column2: 沒有%s很難辦到這件事。

column1: Uh oh!  You don't have any dirt.
column2: 糟了!你沒有任何泥土。

column1: You don't have any sand.
column2: 你沒有任何沙子。

column1: You have no more powder.
column2: 你的粉末用完了。

column1: Not now!
column2: 現在不行！

column1: Your rope is beginning to feel limp.  Perhaps its magical power is limited.
column2: 你的繩子開始發軟。也許它的魔力是有限的。

column1: Your rope is so limp that you don't know if it will rise for you at all.  It's magic has been drained.
column2: 你的繩子軟垂無力,魔力已被耗盡,你不確定它還能不能升起。

column1: The well-kept Plazas of Shapeir are octagonal in shape.  Windows overlook the daily goings-on, and alleys lead outward toward other parts of the city.
column2: 夏皮爾整潔的廣場呈八角形,窗戶俯瞰著每日的市井百態,巷弄則通向城市其他角落。

column1: The crystal clear water of the fountain is a priceless treasure in a land such as this.
column2: 在這樣的土地上,噴泉裡清澈見底的水是無價之寶。

column1: The cracked and dry fountain is a pitiful sight.
column2: 這座乾裂枯竭的噴泉景象淒涼。

column1: The fountain water is cool and refreshing.
column2: 噴泉的水又涼又爽口。

column1: You don't have a waterskin.

column1: But you do take advantage of the opportunity to quench your thirst.
column2: 但你還是趁機解了渴。

column1: Your waterskin is full of water.
column2: 你的水袋裝滿了水。

column1: Your waterskins are full of water.
column2: 你的幾個水袋都裝滿了水。

column1: You fill your waterskin%c with cool, clear water from the fountain.
column2: 你用噴泉裡沁涼清澈的水,把水囊%c裝滿了。

column1: The Water Elemental cooly responds with a wave.
column2: 水元素冷冷地揮手回應。

column1: Any adventurer who would try to get water from an Elemental-inhabited fountain must be wet behind the ears.  That's no way to make a splash.
column2: 敢去元素盤據的噴泉打水的冒險者,八成是個嫩到滴水的菜鳥。這可不是闖出名堂的法子。

column1: For your information, "Go with the flow" is just an expression!  Wave bye-bye, everyone...
column2: 順帶一提,「順其自然」只是個比喻而已!大家揮手說掰掰吧……

column1: You pick up the waterskin that now contains the essence of the Water Elemental.
column2: 你撿起那個如今裝著水元素精靈精華的水袋。

column1: You put the waterskin away.
column2: 你收起了那個水袋。

column1: Your efforts managed to blow the Water Elemental out of the fountain temporarily, but it quickly returned when you stopped.
column2: 你一番努力,總算暫時把水元素精靈吹出了噴泉,可你一停,它又迅速回來了。

column1: I am Abu al-Nasir.
column2: 我是阿布·納希爾。

column1: I am called Gharib Ajib.
column2: 我叫加里布·阿吉布。

column1: I have been named Badr Basim.
column2: 我名叫巴德爾·巴西姆。

column1: My name is Sayf al-Maluk.
column2: 我叫賽夫·馬魯克。

column1: I am known as Jabir bin Ma'amar.
column2: 人稱我為賈比爾·賓·馬阿馬爾。

column1: Hakim bin Yusuf al-Thakiff.
column2: 哈金·賓·優素夫·薩基夫。

column1: You may call me Fakhr Taj.
column2: 你可以叫我法赫爾·塔吉。

column1: Ma fhimt.
column2: Ma fhimt.

column1: The townsperson doesn't seem to understand you.
column2: 那市民似乎聽不懂你的話。

column1: The merchant's prices are all too high.
column2: 商人開的價錢都太高了。

column1: It is hard to steal when someone is watching you as carefully as the merchant is.
column2: 商人這麼緊盯著你,很難偷到東西。

column1: Merchants only sell; they don't buy things.
column2: 商人只賣不買。

column1: A most interesting tale.
column2: 真是個有意思的故事。

column1: My pleasure it is to serve you, Effendi.
column2: 能為您效勞是我的榮幸,閣下。

column1: Greetings, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 您好,史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: %s sells %s in the Plaza of the %s.
column2: %s所賣的%s在「%s」廣場。

column1: Please be more specific, Effendi.
column2: 請說清楚一點,閣下。

column1: buy: unexpected CheckFunds result: %d
column2: buy: unexpected CheckFunds result: %d

column1: You pay for the %s and put it away.
column2: 你付了%s的錢,把它收了起來。

column1: I have none of those today, Effendi.
column2: 今天我沒有那個了,閣下。

column1: You find you do not have enough money to buy it.
column2: 你發現身上的錢不夠買它。

column1: Alas, you will need to visit the Money Changer before you can buy something here.
column2: 可惜,你得先去找兌幣商,才能在這裡買東西。

column1: Please approach me if you wish to purchase my wares, Effendi.
column2: 若您想購買我的商品,請靠近一些,閣下。

column1: %s: unknown cue
column2: %s: unknown cue

column1: The price of the %s is %d centimes.
column2: %s的價格是%d生丁。

column1: Throwing knives when guards are around is not the wisest course of action.
column2: 衛兵在旁時擲刀,可不是什麼明智之舉。

column1: By picking a lock you were choosing your fate.
column2: 你撬開那把鎖的那一刻,就等於選好了自己的下場。

column1: Next time you break into a shop, make sure there are no guards around.
column2: 下次闖進店裡之前,先確認附近沒有守衛。

column1: The guard says, "I warned you once!", as he hauls you off to the local pokey.  Now play it again, Sam.
column2: 守衛說:「我警告過你一次了!」便把你拖進了當地的牢房。好戲,再來一次吧。

column1: Is that what you are doing here?  It is certainly not right to steal, but if you are just borrowing those bellows to save the city, I certainly will not stop you.  I will talk to Issur in the morning and explain matters.
column2: 原來你是為了這個?偷東西當然不對,但你若只是借這風箱來救城,我自然不會攔你。我明早會去跟伊蘇爾說清楚原委。

column1: What are you doing there?
column2: 你在那兒幹什麼?

column1: The door is barred and your magic has no effect.
column2: 這扇門被閂住了,你的魔法對它不起作用。

column1: The door's lock is too strong for your magic.
column2: 這道門鎖對你的魔法來說太堅固了。

column1: Don't worry about closing the door.
column2: 不用擔心把門關上。

column1: The door is already open.
column2: 門已經開著了。

column1: The door is locked.
column2: 門鎖著。

column1: Just walk over to the door.
column2: 走到門邊就行了。

column1: The door is not locked.
column2: 這扇門沒有上鎖。

column1: You daren't try to pick the lock in broad daylight!
column2: 你可不敢在光天化日之下撬鎖!

column1: You aren't close enough.
column2: 你離得不夠近。

column1: This door appears to be barred from the inside.  Picking the lock will not help you enter.
column2: 這扇門似乎是從裡面閂上的,撬鎖對進門毫無幫助。

column1: You're up to your compass in elementals!  You can't use your map now.
column2: 你被元素精怪圍得團團轉!現在沒辦法看地圖。

column1: There's too much going on to take time out for the map right now.
column2: 現在事情太多,沒空看地圖。

column1: Why, you feel better already!
column2: 呀，你已經感覺好多了！

column1: signal $%04x
column2: signal $%04x

column1: You're already seated.
column2: 你已經坐好了。

column1: You're already standing.
column2: 你已經站著了。

column1: Forgive us, friend, but there are no meals prepared at present, nor will there be until morning.
column2: 抱歉了,朋友,目前沒有準備好的餐點,得等到早上才有。

column1: Forgive us, friend, but Shema is asleep.
column2: 抱歉了,朋友,舍瑪已經睡了。

column1: Forgive us, friend, but Shema must prepare for the evening meal.  Perhaps you could wait until later for Shema to serve you.
column2: 抱歉了,朋友,舍瑪得準備晚餐了。或許你可以晚點再讓舍瑪為你服務。

column1: There is also my nephew Scoree in the bazaar at the fountain plaza, who will be very happy to sell you something to eat.
column2: 我姪子史柯里也在噴泉廣場的市集裡,他會很樂意賣你點吃的。

column1: If you will be seated, Shema will serve you.
column2: 只要您入座,舍瑪就會為您服務。

column1: Certainly you do not wish for more to eat!
column2: 你肯定不想再吃了吧!

column1: You have some tea, Hero.
column2: 你有些茶,英雄。

column1: Here is some of our wonderful tea.
column2: 這是我們上好的茶,請用。

column1: I am here to serve you.
column2: 我在此為您效勞。

column1: Shema will be with you shortly%s
column2: 舍瑪很快就會來為您服務%s

column1: You are pleased at the delicious smells and delightful ambiance of the newly remodeled Katta's Tail Inn.  You give it three and a half stars.
column2: 這間剛翻修過的「卡塔尾旅店」香氣誘人、氣氛宜人,讓你相當滿意。你給它三顆半星的評價。

column1: You are, of course, most welcome, my Hero.
column2: 當然,別客氣,我的英雄。

column1: Your tea is on the table.
column2: 你的茶就在桌上。

column1: Perhaps you should sit and ordered some.
column2: 或許你該坐下來點一些。

column1: The tea, as Abdulla has told you many times, is wonderful.
column2: 這茶,正如阿布杜拉跟你說過好多次的,棒極了。

column1: Aside from your water, you have nothing to drink.  You would no doubt receive tea with a meal.
column2: 除了你自己的水,你沒有別的飲料。點一份餐,想必會附上茶水。

column1: You would undoubtedly receive tea with a meal, were you to order one.
column2: 如果你點一份餐,想必一定會附上茶水。

column1: Your breakfast looks delicious.
column2: 你的早餐看起來很美味。

column1: It makes your mouth water.
column2: 這讓你直流口水。

column1: You think twice about eating more than one meal at a sitting.  Bicarbonate of soda hasn't been invented yet.
column2: 一頓吃兩餐?你還是再想想吧。小蘇打可還沒發明呢。

column1: It is most polite to eat restaurant meals while seated at a table.
column2: 在餐館用餐,坐到桌邊吃才最合禮數。

column1: Your breakfast is delicious.
column2: 你的早餐很美味。

column1: You have a wonderful meal.  There are numerous salads, the two main courses, fruit, hot mint tea, dessert, and all the bread you could want.  You feel stuffed and content.
column2: 你享用了一頓豐盛的美食。有多種沙拉、兩道主菜、水果、熱薄荷茶、甜點,還有吃不完的麵包。你感到心滿意足。

column1: Unfortunately, you never really cared for sauerkraut and knockwurst, but you are too polite to tell Shema that.  You must admit the honey and lemons on the knockwurst aren't too bad, but the sauerkraut curry is a bit much.
column2: 可惜,你向來不怎麼愛吃酸菜配大香腸,只是你太有禮貌,沒好意思跟舍瑪說。你得承認,大香腸淋上蜂蜜和檸檬還算不賴,但那酸菜咖哩就有點過頭了。

column1: Fortunately, Heroes never get bellyaches.
column2: 幸好,英雄從不會鬧肚子痛。

column1: Fortunately, your strenuous regimen as a Hero prevents you from looking as round as Abdulla.
column2: 幸好,英雄嚴格的鍛鍊讓你不會像阿布杜拉那樣圓滾滾的。

column1: After a meal like that, you feel it will be a struggle even to make it to bed.  But, what the hey, you're a Hero, you can do it!
column2: 吃了那樣一頓飯後，你覺得連走到床邊都很吃力。不過，管他的，你可是個英雄，你辦得到！

column1: You should order a meal first.  It would be rude to eat your travel rations in your friends' Inn.
column2: 你應該先點一份餐點。在朋友的旅店裡吃自己的旅途乾糧，未免太失禮了。

column1: You have no food.
column2: 你身上沒有食物。

column1: Perhaps it would be best, Effendi, if you returned it to Omar himself.
column2: 閣下,或許最好還是由您親自把它還給奧瑪爾。

column1: It is hoped you enjoyed your first sights of our beautiful city of Shapeir.
column2: 但願你會喜歡初次見到我們美麗的夏皮爾城的這番景象。

column1: If you will be seated, then you will see my Shema dance.
column2: 只要您入座,便能看我家舍瑪起舞。

column1: Hero-friend, a most strange fire has been seen outside the Inn.  Take care as you leave.
column2: 英雄朋友，旅店外出現了一團極為古怪的火焰。你出門時務必小心。

column1: I fear, Hero-friend of the Katta, that Shapeir again needs your aid.  There is talk that an Elemental of the Earth is destroying the city.
column2: 卡塔的英雄朋友,只怕夏皮爾又得仰仗你的相助了。外頭傳言,有個土之元素正在摧毀這座城。

column1: The poet has personally asked that you be present tonight, for he wishes to speak with you after his reading.
column2: 詩人親自邀請你今晚出席，他朗誦完後想找你談談。

column1: The poet will soon speak, if you will but be seated.
column2: 只要你入座，詩人很快就會開始朗誦。

column1: Welcome, Hero.
column2: 歡迎你,英雄。

column1: It is said that Uhura at the Guild Hall has a message for you as well.
column2: 聽說公會大廳的烏胡拉也有話要傳給你。

column1: Soon Shapeir will be awash in flames.  Hero, you must save us all again!
column2: 夏皮爾就快被火海吞沒了。英雄,你得再一次拯救我們大家!

column1: Alas, again our great city is in peril!  The most feared of all Elementals has been unleashed upon us -- the Water Elemental!
column2: 唉,我們的偉大城市又陷入危難了!所有元素中最令人聞風喪膽的那一個,如今降臨在我們頭上——水之元素!

column1: Did you have a chance to meet and speak with those in our fair bazaar?  It is said there are many rumors in this city (but don't quote me on that).
column2: 你有沒有機會跟我們這座美麗市集裡的人聊聊呢？聽說這城裡流言可多了（不過別說是我講的）。

column1: I must be sure to consult the Astrologer about the Fire Elemental.  It must certainly be a bad omen.
column2: 我一定得去請教占星師關於火之元素的事。這肯定是個不祥之兆。

column1: It is a good thing that you are around to save us, %s.
column2: 幸好有你在身邊救我們,%s。

column1: I am here to serve you, Hero.
column2: 我在此為您效勞，英雄。

column1: Here is your tea.
column2: 這是您的茶。

column1: I will return with a splendid meal for you.
column2: 我這就去替您備一頓豐盛的餐點。

column1: Do not forget that Shema dances this night!
column2: 別忘了今晚舍瑪要跳舞!

column1: The Poet Omar will thrill all who hear with his words of song after the setting of the sun, in our own Inn!
column2: 日落之後，詩人奧馬爾將在我們的旅店裡吟詩高歌，讓所有聽眾為之陶醉！

column1: We are most honored by your presence at our Inn, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 史畢柏格的英雄，您大駕光臨本店，我們深感榮幸。

column1: You are most welcome.
column2: 不客氣。

column1: Thank you, they are most beautiful.
column2: 謝謝你，這些真是太美了。

column1: It is always a pleasure to see you, Great Hero.
column2: 能再見到您，偉大的英雄，總是令人愉快。

column1: Shema looks shyly down and shakes her head.
column2: 舍瑪害羞地垂下頭,搖了搖。

column1: You awaken much refreshed.
column2: 你醒來時精神煥發。

column1: I am so sorry that I have no answer to your question.
column2: 很抱歉，你的問題我答不上來。

column1: Forgive me, Hero-friend, but I am ignorant of such things.
column2: 請原諒我,英雄朋友,這種事我實在一無所知。

column1: It is good to be here again.  You will always be welcome in our Inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.
column2: 能再次回到這裡真好。因為有你的幫助，我們才能重返此地，你在我們的旅店裡永遠受歡迎。

column1: All Kattas in that city were driven out of their homes there.  Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave.  I fear for him.
column2: 那座城裡所有的卡塔族都被逐出了家園。只有我的表親沙拉夫沒有離開，我很擔心他。

column1: I am not as skilled in the ways of magic as some.  I know only the quiet magic of the dance, and of how to make an inn seem a home.
column2: 論魔法之道,我不如有些人那般精通。我只懂得舞蹈那份靜默的魔力,以及如何讓一間客棧有家的感覺。

column1: The Sultan here has always protected and welcomed our people.
column2: 這裡的蘇丹,向來庇護並接納我們族人。

column1: The old Emir of Raseir was a kind and gentle man.  I cannot understand how the new Emir can be so cruel to our people.
column2: 拉希爾從前那位埃米爾,是個仁慈溫和的人。我實在不明白,新任的埃米爾怎能對我們族人如此殘酷。

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our Inn.  You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿在我們旅店的東邊。你可以從宮殿廣場的大門上方看見它。

column1: The journey to Raseir is very far and very dangerous.  It can only be made by caravan.  If you do journey there, please look for my cousin, Sharaf.
column2: 前往拉希爾的路途十分遙遠,也十分凶險,只能隨商隊同行。你若真要去那兒,請替我尋一尋我的堂親沙拉夫。

column1: There are many dangers in the desert.  You must be very careful there.
column2: 沙漠裡危機四伏。你在那兒可得萬分小心。

column1: You have no need of money in our home.  You will always be our guest.
column2: 在我們家裡,你用不著錢。你永遠是我們的座上賓。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild is northwest, off the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在西北方,鬥士廣場旁邊。

column1: This is your home as well as ours.
column2: 這裡既是我們的家,也是你的家。

column1: I prefer our warm, dry air to the cold and wet of Spielburg.
column2: 比起史畢柏格那又濕又冷的天氣,我更喜歡咱們這兒溫暖乾爽的空氣。

column1: The fountain is directly north of here.  It is the source for all our water.
column2: 噴泉就在這兒的正北方。我們所有的水都靠它供應。

column1: I have heard that even now my cousin Sharaf works with the Underground to free Raseir.
column2: 我聽說,我表親沙拉夫至今仍與地下組織合作,要解放拉希爾。

column1: Be wary of her.  It is said she uses men.
column2: 對她可得當心。聽說她慣於玩弄男人。

column1: He is a wonderful poet and he knows many things.  We are most lucky that he comes here.
column2: 他是位了不起的詩人,而且博學多聞。他願意上這兒來,真是我們天大的福氣。

column1: What better job for a Katta then one where you sit all day in the warm sun and chatter with friends?
column2: 對卡塔族來說，還有什麼工作比整天坐在暖陽下跟朋友聊天更好呢？

column1: Our people were here long before your kind moved here.  This land shall always be our home.
column2: 早在你們這族人遷來之前,我們族人就在這兒了。這片土地永遠是我們的家。

column1: My name, in the language of our people, means "She of the Dance."
column2: 用我們族人的語言來說，我的名字意思是「舞者」。

column1: His name means "Honor".  He is very brave.  It is said that he refused to leave Raseir so that he could work to bring freedom to his homeland again.
column2: 他的名字意思是「榮譽」。他非常勇敢。據說他拒絕離開拉希爾，就是為了設法讓家鄉重獲自由。

column1: It is hoped that you will enjoy what I prepare.  You have only to order it.
column2: 但願我做的菜能合你心意。你只管點便是。

column1: The tea is made from fresh herbs and honey and is brewed by the sun.
column2: 這茶是用新鮮香草與蜂蜜製成,靠日光慢慢沏出來的。

column1: We traveled to Spielburg in hopes of finding a hero to restore Raseir to our people.
column2: 我們遠赴史畢柏格,盼著能找到一位英雄,把拉希爾重新交回我們族人手中。

column1: If you had not returned our treasures to us, we would still be in that cold land, away from friends and family.
column2: 若不是你把財寶歸還給我們,我們至今仍會困在那片寒冷的土地上,遠離親朋故舊。

column1: All Kattas are related in some manner to one another.
column2: 所有卡塔族彼此多少都有些親戚關係。

column1: It is a movement to overthrow the current government of Raseir and restore the old ways.
column2: 那是一場運動,旨在推翻拉希爾現今的政權,恢復昔日的舊制。

column1: He too, is our forever friend.
column2: 他也是我們永遠的朋友。

column1: The dance that I perform was, at one time, to ready our people for battle, and...ah...for fertility.  Now it is only for fun.
column2: 我表演的這支舞，曾經是用來讓族人為戰鬥做準備，還有……呃……祈求生育的。現在純粹是為了好玩。

column1: He is both my husband and my soul-mate.
column2: 他既是我的丈夫，也是我的靈魂伴侶。

column1: If the new Emir hates Kattas so, Sharaf could be in deadly trouble.
column2: 如果新任埃米爾如此痛恨卡塔族，沙拉夫恐怕會有致命的危險。

column1: We typically eat such things for meals around here.
column2: 在我們這兒,這類東西平常就是拿來當飯吃的。

column1: The wild desert Saurus is quite tasty when properly prepared.
column2: 野生的沙漠索魯斯只要料理得當,味道相當不錯。

column1: I had them specially imported from Spielburg for you.
column2: 這些是我特地為你從史畢柏格進口來的。

column1: Katta are very wary of flames.  Please be careful around it.
column2: 卡塔族對火焰非常戒慎,請你在它附近多加小心。

column1: People would come from far away to shop and browse in the bazaars of the Katta in Shapeir and Raseir.  Now there is no bazaar at all in Raseir.
column2: 從前人們會大老遠跑來夏皮爾和拉希爾,逛卡塔族的市集、採買貨物。如今拉希爾連一座市集都沒有了。

column1: I worry for you in these dangerous times, Hero.
column2: 在這樣危險的時局裡,我很為你擔心,英雄。

column1: I only know that Elementals are dangerous. Perhaps others could tell you more.
column2: 我只知道元素精靈很危險。也許別人能告訴你更多。

column1: Tonight's meal is honeyed lamb with almonds and curried chicken.  It is hoped you will enjoy yourself.
column2: 今晚的菜色是蜜汁杏仁羊肉與咖哩雞。願您吃得盡興。

column1: I have prepared a meal of rabbit with apricots and roast chicken with sherbet for this night's meal.  May it bring you pleasure.
column2: 今晚的菜我備了杏桃燉兔肉,還有烤雞佐果露。願它讓您吃得開心。

column1: Here is lemon chicken with garlic and lamb stewed with herbs.  Eat it in good health.
column2: 這是蒜香檸檬雞,還有香草燉羊肉。祝您吃得健健康康。

column1: Lamb Felafels and soured cream, along with a rabbit curry, are part of the meal for tonight.  May it do you good.
column2: 今晚的餐點有羊肉費拉費配酸奶油，還有兔肉咖哩。祝你吃得開心。

column1: The supper tonight is Saurus stuffed with grapes and saffron rice, and honeyed rabbit.  This is a specialty of Shapeir and I hope you will like it.
column2: 今晚的晚餐是釀了葡萄與番紅花飯的索魯斯,還有蜜汁兔肉。這是夏皮爾的招牌菜,希望你會喜歡。

column1: Tonight I have prepared something especially for you so that you do not become too homesick.  It is knockwurst and sauerkraut.
column2: 今晚我特地替你準備了點東西,免得你太想家。是德式香腸佐酸菜。

column1: Y'get hash tonight, Mudball!  The designers didn't count on you eatin' on Sundays!
column2: 今晚就給你吃雜燴飯，泥巴蛋！設計者可沒想到你星期天也要吃飯！

column1: Here is your morning meal of warm bread and honeyed butter, fresh fruit, and tea, just as you prefer.
column2: 這是您的早餐:溫熱的麵包配蜜奶油、新鮮水果,還有茶,正合您的口味。

column1: Good day, Hero of Spielburg.  I hope you slept well.
column2: 你好,史畢柏格的英雄。希望你睡得安穩。

column1: May you have a good day today.
column2: 願你今天順心如意。

column1: The sun is bright and the day is hot already this morning.
column2: 今早陽光刺眼,天氣已經熱了起來。

column1: The Katta are most thankful that you have come to Shapeir.
column2: 卡塔族對你來到夏皮爾感激不盡。

column1: ack!
column2: 呃啊！

column1: It would please me if you would come to my dance this evening.
column2: 你今晚若肯來看我跳舞,我會很高興。

column1: The Katta merchants could not set up in the plaza outside because of the fire this morning.
column2: 因為今早那場火,卡塔族商人沒法在外頭的廣場擺攤。

column1: Hero, please take care of yourself.  I worry for you.
column2: 英雄,請保重自己。我為你擔心。

column1: I beg you to be careful when you leave the Inn today.  The thing of fire grows ever more dangerous.
column2: 求你今天離開旅店時務必小心。那團火焰之物變得越來越危險了。

column1: My second cousin Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace, and my third cousin once removed, Tiram the Rug Merchant, were unable to set up their stands today.  An Elemental of Air is filling the plaza!
column2: 我的表姪——在宮殿廣場開店的珠寶商薩沙南，還有我的遠房表親、地毯商提拉姆，今天都沒辦法擺攤。一隻風元素正盤據在廣場上！

column1: The wind grows stronger, and the merchants had great difficulty setting up their stands today.
column2: 風越颳越大,今天商人們擺攤都吃足了苦頭。

column1: It is rumored that you wish to journey to Raseir.  If you do so, please try to find my cousin Sharaf.
column2: 聽說你想前往拉希爾。你若真去了,請設法找到我表親沙拉夫。

column1: The Katta are again afraid to set up their shops.  The Creature of Earth grows ever more powerful.
column2: 卡塔族又不敢開張做生意了。那土之造物越來越強大。

column1: The Creature of Water prevents any from approaching the fountain.  I fear we will soon have no water.
column2: 那水之造物不准任何人靠近噴泉。我怕我們很快就要沒水了。

column1: The Katta wish to thank you for your bravery and kindness.  Please go visit Sashanan the Jeweler in the Plaza of the Palace today.
column2: 卡塔族想感謝你的勇氣與善心。請你今天去宮殿廣場拜訪珠寶商薩沙南。

column1: You will have to ask Abdulla about that.
column2: 那件事你得去問阿布杜拉。

column1: There is much of which I have no knowledge.  Forgive me.
column2: 有太多事是我不知道的,請你見諒。

column1: Ah, Hero of the North, I am but a Katta.  I cannot speak upon what I do not know.
column2: 啊,來自北方的英雄,我不過是個卡塔族罷了。我不知道的事,實在無從說起。

column1: This city is but an island of beauty amidst the shifting sands and timeless mountains.
column2: 這座城市不過是流沙與亙古群山之間,一座美麗的孤島罷了。

column1: That city used to be but a mirror to Shapeir.  Now it is said that it is a foreshadow of what is to come.
column2: 那座城市從前不過是夏皮爾的一面鏡子。如今有人說,它預示著即將到來的命運。

column1: There are many creatures in the desert who would seek to harm you.
column2: 沙漠裡有許多生物,個個都想加害於你。

column1: Katta magic is subtle and hidden from most people.  We let you see our magic only because you are our friend.
column2: 卡塔族的法術細膩而隱秘,對大多數人從不外露。我們讓你見識我們的法術,只因你是我們的朋友。

column1: The Sultan Harun Al-Rashid welcomed and sheltered the Kattas driven from their homes in Raseir.  His name will be honored forever by our people.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德收留並庇護了被逐出拉希爾家園的卡塔族。他的名字將永遠受我族人敬重。

column1: Those driven from Raseir speak highly of the former Emir Arus Al-din. Of his brother, Ali Hasan, they merely spit.
column2: 那些被逐出拉希爾的人對前任埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁讚譽有加。至於他的兄弟阿里·哈山，他們只會啐一口。

column1: The Sultan's Palace lies directly east of the Plaza of the Fountain.  It is said that although no one sees the Sultan, the Sultan sees all.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿就在噴泉廣場的正東方。據說,雖然沒人見得著蘇丹,蘇丹卻洞悉一切。

column1: Abdulla is the one to ask about that.
column2: 那件事該去問阿布杜拉。

column1: Sands that change colors with the time of day, the desert is dear to our people.  However, it is best viewed at night.  It is hard to survive the heat of the sun.
column2: 沙色隨著一天時辰流轉變幻,沙漠對我們族人而言彌足珍貴。不過,它最美還是在夜裡;白晝的烈日之下實在難以撐住。

column1: You have no need for your money in our Inn.  You will need to see the Money Changer, however, before you can buy something in Shapeir.
column2: 在我們的旅店裡你用不上錢。不過，要在夏皮爾買東西之前，你得先去找兌幣商人。

column1: The Guild is west of the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 公會在噴泉廣場的西邊。

column1: This is truly what an Inn should be, the center of all.  With Shema to dance, and the Poet Omar to entertain, there will always be something of interest here.
column2: 這才是旅店該有的樣子，是一切的中心。有舍瑪跳舞、詩人奧馬爾表演助興，這裡永遠有新鮮事。

column1: It is gloriously warm as always.
column2: 還是一如往常地暖洋洋,真教人舒坦。

column1: The pathway to the Fountain is North of our Plaza.
column2: 通往噴泉的路在我們廣場的北邊。

column1: I have heard that the armies the Sultan sent to Raseir were destroyed by a great wind.
column2: 我聽說蘇丹派往拉希爾的軍隊,被一陣狂風給摧毀了。

column1: The Money Changer will change the money of Spielburg for the coins of Shapeir.
column2: 兌幣商人會把史畢柏格的錢幣換成夏皮爾的貨幣。

column1: The Kattas believe one's fate lies within oneself, not in the stars above.
column2: 卡塔族相信，命運掌握在自己手中，而不在天上的星辰。

column1: I have heard only that she is very knowing in the ways of magics.
column2: 我只聽說她對各種法術之道極為通曉。

column1: Poetry is revered in this land.  The words of the poet Omar speak both to the heart and to the mind.
column2: 這片土地上，詩歌備受尊崇。詩人奧馬爾的字句，既觸動人心，也啟迪心智。

column1: Kattas are fine sellers of many things, for they know the true value of wealth.
column2: 卡塔族擅長販售各種物品，因為他們深知財富的真正價值。

column1: We are a little people and few, but like grains of sand, we will always belong to the desert.
column2: 我們是個渺小而稀少的民族,卻像一粒粒沙子,永遠屬於這片沙漠。

column1: Shameen, in the tongue of my kindred, means, "He of the Lion."
column2: 「沙明」在我族人的語言裡，意思是「獅之子」。

column1: There is none in all Shapeir to compare to my beautiful and wondrous mate.
column2: 放眼整個夏皮爾,沒有人比得上我那美麗又奇妙的伴侶。

column1: Abdulla may seem foolish at times, but there is much in what he says.
column2: 阿布杜拉有時看似憨傻,但他話裡可大有玄機。

column1: Kattas feel you should go where your heart leads you, and there you will find yourself.
column2: 卡塔族認為，你應該追隨自己內心的指引，如此才能找到真正的自己。

column1: It is not good to speak of evil, but there is much of it in Raseir.
column2: 談論邪惡並非好事,但拉希爾如今正充斥著這樣的東西。

column1: Shema believes he still lives, but from all I have heard of Raseir, I am doubtful.
column2: 舍瑪相信他還活著,但聽過種種關於拉希爾的傳聞後,我心裡是存疑的。

column1: We journeyed to Spielburg to bring back a Hero.
column2: 我們曾遠赴史畢柏格，就是為了帶回一位英雄。

column1: When Shema dances, fires awaken.  At least, for Katta.
column2: 舍瑪起舞時,火焰也隨之甦醒。至少對卡塔族來說是如此。

column1: To be truly wealthy, you must learn what it is to be content.
column2: 要真正富有,你必須先學會什麼叫知足。

column1: We are kin, the Liontaur race and the Kattas.
column2: 獅人一族與卡塔族，本是同宗。

column1: He is my sister's eldest boy, and a fine cook in his own right.
column2: 他是我姊姊的長子,本身也是個出色的廚子。

column1: In the Plaza of the Fountain and other plazas, the Katta merchants set up their shops.
column2: 在噴泉廣場和其他幾座廣場上,卡塔族的商人都擺起了攤子。

column1: Your room awaits you, just off the passage behind me.  You will have a place to stay here as long as you wish to be our guest.
column2: 你的房間就在我身後那條走道的旁邊,正等著你。只要你願意當我們的客人,這裡就一直有你落腳的地方。

column1: In the morning and evening, you have but to sit at your table and Shema will be honored to serve you.  You are our guest.
column2: 早晚時分，你只需坐到你的桌邊，舍瑪必定樂意為你效勞。你是我們的貴賓。

column1: Because you defeated the Brigands in Spielburg and returned our stolen treasure, we shall be forever in your debt.  Joining us on our journey to Shapeir and helping us set up this Inn has made you our friend, as well.
column2: 因為你在史畢柏格擊敗了那群強盜，找回我們被劫走的財寶，我們永遠虧欠你這份恩情。你隨我們一同前往夏皮爾，還幫忙開起這間旅店，這也讓你成了我們的朋友。

column1: The Enchantress Aziza sends you this message of gratitude and requests that you visit her home tomorrow.
column2: 女術士阿姿莎託我捎來這封致謝的口信,並請你明日到她家中一趟。

column1: I regret that I cannot tell you what you wish to know.
column2: 很抱歉,你想知道的事,我沒法告訴你。

column1: It grieves me very much, but I do not know what to say.
column2: 這令我十分難過,可我實在不知該說什麼好。

column1: The land of Shapeir may be plagued with Elementals and Djinns, but this city will ever stand, as long as there are heroes such as yourself.
column2: 夏皮爾這片土地或許飽受元素與精靈之患，但只要還有像你這樣的英雄，這座城市就永遠屹立不搖。

column1: Raseir and Shapeir were sister cities, separated by the sands of the desert.  Now it is said that the fountain of Raseir runs no more, and that the city's soul has shriveled like a grape in the sun.
column2: 拉希爾與夏皮爾本是姊妹城，僅隔著沙漠的黃沙相望。如今據說拉希爾的噴泉已經枯竭，城市的靈魂也如陽光下的葡萄般乾癟枯萎。

column1: Djinn are magical beings, huge monstrous creatures of power capable of tearing a man limb from limb, although I have never personally experienced this.
column2: 精靈是有魔力的存在,身軀龐大如怪物,力量足以把人撕成四分五裂——儘管我本人從沒親身領教過就是了。

column1: The Elementals are some sort of magical things which could destroy us all!
column2: 那些元素精怪是某種魔法造物,足以把我們全給毀了!

column1: Living flames which seek to consume everything!
column2: 那是有生命的烈焰,一心想把一切吞噬殆盡!

column1: We shall be burned to cinders like a saurus steak left too long on the grill!
column2: 我們會被燒成灰燼,活像一塊在烤架上擱太久的索魯斯排!

column1: A Water Elemental in the Fountain of town would be the greatest of misfortunes.
column2: 若有水元素盤據城裡的噴泉，那將是最大的不幸。

column1: We should all die of thirst!
column2: 我們都會渴死的!

column1: It is said to be a living Whirlwind.
column2: 據說那是個活生生的旋風。

column1: We will all be blown to bits!
column2: 我們全都會被吹得粉身碎骨!

column1: It is rock and stone molded into a living creature with all the destructive power of the Earthquake.
column2: 那是由岩石塑成的活體生物，蘊含著堪比地震的破壞之力。

column1: The city will fall and I will perish unless you prevent this!
column2: 除非你出手阻止,否則這座城會淪陷,我也將喪命!

column1: I have fought many monsters in my travels through the desert.
column2: 我在橫越沙漠的旅途中,跟許多怪物交過手。

column1: This is a land of magic and mystery.
column2: 這是一片充滿魔法與神秘的土地。

column1: The other day when I was talking to the Sultan (May-He-Live-Forever!), as Harun Al-Rashid thanked me for my gift of the flying carpet and complimented me upon my courage in fighting against those vile brigands of Spielburg, he mentioned that there might be a need of a hero around here soon.  The Sultan must have talked to the Astrologer.
column2: 前幾天我和蘇丹（願他萬壽無疆！）談話時，哈倫·拉希德感謝我獻上的飛毯，也稱讚我對抗史畢柏格那群卑劣強盜的勇氣，他還提到這一帶可能很快就需要一位英雄。蘇丹想必是找占星師談過了。

column1: Arus was the kindest of men, quick to hug you and call you brother, but I must admit that he was not very brave.
column2: 阿魯斯是再仁厚不過的人,動不動就擁你入懷、喚你一聲兄弟,不過我得承認,他不太有膽量。

column1: You hope to come to WIT's end, ha ha!  I have heard rumors that the Wizard's school is around here, but I, who have lived my life here, have seen nothing of it.
column2: 你希望能找到巫院，哈哈！我聽說巫師學院就在這附近，但我在這裡住了一輩子，卻從沒見過它的蹤影。

column1: As I looked around the Sultan's Palace the other day, I marveled at its wealth and beauty.  Perhaps someday you, too, will be important enough to be invited there.
column2: 前些日子我在蘇丹的宮殿四下打量,對它的富麗堂皇驚嘆不已。也許有朝一日,你也能有夠分量,獲邀到那兒去。

column1: There will be a caravan to Raseir leaving later this month.  I, for one, am happy not to have to be on it.
column2: 這個月稍晚,會有一支前往拉希爾的商隊出發。至少我本人,很慶幸自己不必跟著去。

column1: The desert is full of perils for the unwary, and only brave men or fools dare go there.  I used to travel through the desert often.
column2: 沙漠處處是陷阱,專等粗心大意的人,只有勇者或傻子才敢往那兒闖。我以前可是常常穿越沙漠的。

column1: When it comes to money, I am the person to ask!  You will need to find the Money Changer to trade over your coins before you can buy anything here.
column2: 說到錢的事，找我準沒錯！不過你得先找兌幣商人換錢，才能在這裡買東西。

column1: It has been a long time since I have visited there, but I know this city like the back of my hand.  It is to the east of us, off the Plaza of the Fighters.  Or was that the west?
column2: 我已經很久沒去那裡了，不過這座城市我熟得很。它在我們的東邊，靠近戰士廣場。還是西邊來著？

column1: Thieves' Guild?  What need have you for thieves?  There is no such guild in this fair city!
column2: 盜賊公會？你找盜賊做什麼？這座美麗的城市裡才沒有這種公會！

column1: A place to relax, to talk to friends, to eat and drink, to watch entertainment!  What better place to be?
column2: 一個能放鬆、能跟朋友聊天、能吃喝、能看表演的地方!還有哪兒比這更棒?

column1: In the day, it is hot...at night, it is cold.  It is always the way this time of year.
column2: 白天很熱……夜裡很冷。這個時節向來如此。

column1: The fountain is the center of life to our city.
column2: 噴泉是我們這座城的生命中心。

column1: I have heard that in Raseir they now forbid people to speak to one another.  How can one live in such a way?
column2: 我聽說在拉希爾,如今竟禁止人們彼此交談。這種日子叫人怎麼過?

column1: She is a very beautiful woman, and very attracted to me, I can tell.  You will find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, a street to the...hum...I think to the west of here.
column2: 她是個非常美麗的女人，而且我看得出來她對我很有意思。你可以在第納爾塔里克街的盡頭找到她，那條街在……嗯……我想是在這裡的西邊。

column1: The Astrologer is a very important man here.  He can see the future in the stars!  You should go to him and find out your fortune, for is it not written, "A man should know his fate"?  His house is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 占星師在這裡是位舉足輕重的人物。他能從星象中窺見未來！你該去找他算算你的命運，畢竟古書不是寫著「人應知曉自身的命運」嗎？他的家就在星辰塔里克街的盡頭。

column1: It is said, "The Poet sees the soul and lets it fly."  I, too, was a poet in my youth.
column2: 古語有云：「詩人洞見靈魂，並放它自由翱翔。」我年輕時，也曾是個詩人。

column1: I was always more trader than merchant, for I loved to travel too much.
column2: 我這人一向是行商多於坐賈,只因我太愛四處奔波了。

column1: The Kattas make great neighbors and friends, and Shameen and Shema are the finest of Kattas!
column2: 卡塔族是很棒的鄰居和朋友，而沙明與舍瑪更是卡塔族中的佼佼者！

column1: He lives in the desert by the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.
column2: 他住在沙漠裡的綠洲旁，是位非常有智慧的人。

column1: I am Abdulla Doo.  It is an old family name.
column2: 我是阿布杜拉·杜。這是個古老的家族姓氏。

column1: Ah, the enchanting Shema!  Her dance must be seen to be savored.
column2: 啊,迷人的舍瑪!她的舞,非親眼一見不足以細細品味。

column1: Like a cat, Shameen lands upon his paws where ever he goes.  This Inn is the Oasis of Culture for Shapeir.
column2: 就像貓一樣，沙明不論走到哪裡總能穩穩落地。這間旅店就是夏皮爾的文化綠洲。

column1: He was a generous and friendly man, and Raseir was a place worth living.  Now Arus is lost, his brother sits on his throne, and Raseir stagnates.
column2: 他是個慷慨又和善的人,當年的拉希爾也是個值得安居的地方。如今阿魯斯下落不明,他的弟弟坐上了王位,拉希爾就此一蹶不振。

column1: There is no greater treasure than friendship.
column2: 世上沒有比友誼更珍貴的寶藏了。

column1: I have heard that Raseir has become a grim and unpleasant place to be, now that my good friend Arus is no longer the Emir.
column2: 我聽說自從我的好友阿魯斯不再是埃米爾,拉希爾就變成了個陰森難熬的地方。

column1: If you had not returned our stolen carpet and treasures, I would still be sitting there, wasting away to a mere shadow of myself.
column2: 若不是你找回了我們被偷走的飛毯與財寶,我至今仍會枯坐在那兒,日漸消瘦,只剩一具軀殼罷了。

column1: If I had not fought off so many in the battle in Spielburg Pass, perhaps you would not have made it through the brigand lair, my friend.
column2: 若不是當年我在史畢柏格隘口那一戰殺退了那麼多敵人,朋友,你或許就闖不過那山賊巢穴了。

column1: I have given the carpet to the Sultan, and in return he has made me wealthy beyond even my dreams.
column2: 我把那飛毯獻給了蘇丹,作為回報,他讓我富甲一方,連我做夢都不敢想。

column1: Why, it is the month of the Serpent, a time of ill omen.
column2: 哎呀,如今正值巨蛇之月,是個凶兆當頭的時節。

column1: This is the year of the Djinn.  A most inauspicious time.  Perhaps I should consult my friend the Astrologer.
column2: 今年是精靈之年,最是不吉的年份。也許我該去請教我那位占星師朋友。

column1: To speak of omens, you should talk to the Astrologer.
column2: 若要談起預兆,你該去找占星師談談。

column1: You should get out and see the city.  There are many people to meet and talk with in Shapeir.
column2: 你該出去走走,看看這座城。夏皮爾裡有許多人值得你結識、攀談。

column1: Shema's cooking is fantastic and her tea is heavenly.  Truly the Kattas are friends beyond measure.
column2: 舍瑪的廚藝一流，她泡的茶更是絕妙。卡塔族真的是無可比擬的好朋友。

column1: It seems my friend Arus Al-Din has been missing for almost a year now.
column2: 看來我的朋友阿魯斯·阿丁已經失蹤將近一年了。

column1: Magic is very much a part of life in shapeir.
column2: 魔法早已深深融入夏皮爾人的生活之中。

column1: A dreadful Fire Elemental stalks the plazas, burning all it touches!
column2: 一頭可怕的火元素精怪在廣場間遊蕩,凡它所觸,無不焚毀!

column1: Why, the very plazas of our city are a thriving marketplace.
column2: 哎呀,我們城裡的座座廣場,本身就是一處繁榮興旺的市集。

column1: Upon that question     I would further ponder,  for guidance I suggest     that thee seek yonder.
column2: 關於此問     容我再三思量，  若要指引     不妨向遠方尋訪。

column1: The poet Omar (May His Words Be Ever Heard!) has no answer for you.
column2: 詩人奧馬爾（願他的話語永傳於世！）對此沒有答案。

column1: Upon some things     I can but little say,  that others yet might     further guide your way.
column2: 有些事     我所知甚少，  但願旁人     能為你指路明了。

column1: Perhaps it would be wiser for you to ask someone who knows more about the subject.
column2: 或許,你去問問更懂這方面的人,會更明智些。

column1: Companion, conscience,     ready to defend,  Teller to those that     they may comprehend,  A man of pride,     yet sits he by my side,  A person I am pleased     to call my friend.
column2: 良伴、良知，     隨時挺身相護，  向人訴說道理，     使人得以明悟，  一個驕傲的人，     卻甘願伴我身側，  這樣一個人，     我樂於稱他為友。

column1: The Poet, (May His Poems Be Praised!) is most flattering in his assessment of me.  I am but his follower.
column2: 那位詩人(願他的詩篇受讚頌!)對我的評語實在過獎了。我不過是他的追隨者罷了。

column1: Rasha Rakeesh Sah Tarna,     it has been said,  Is a lion in the soul     as in the head.  Denying glory,     walks where heart has led,  A ruler who now     rules himself instead.
column2: 拉夏·拉基什·薩·塔納，     據說如此，  靈魂與頭腦     皆如雄獅。  拒絕榮耀，     只循本心而行，  如今他所統御的，     唯有自己。

column1: Rakeesh has walked a path that few can follow.  He is more a Man than many men.
column2: 拉基什走的路,鮮少有人能跟得上。比起許多人,他更稱得上是個真正的人。

column1: A Paladin travels     where his conscience leads,  Quietly helping others     with their needs.  Not for glory,     neither then for gain,  Good alone shall be     the judge he heeds.
column2: 聖騎士的足跡，     隨良知而至，  默默助人     解其所需。  不為榮耀，     也不為私利，  唯有良善，     是他遵循的準繩。

column1: A Paladin goes wherever there is need, and obeys only true Justice and Goodness.
column2: 聖騎士哪裡有需要，就往哪裡去，只遵從真正的正義與良善。

column1: By actions rash,     a person might then find,  That others take exceptions     to his crime.  And rudeness returned     rudely thus remind,  That person to return     to things sublime.
column2: 因魯莽行事，     人終將發現，  旁人對他的過錯     心生不滿。  無禮換來無禮的回敬，     藉此提醒，  那人該當     回歸崇高之境。

column1: It would be best if a person would sit down, that the poet Omar might read and illuminate the minds of all who hear.
column2: 若能坐下聆聽最好，詩人奧馬爾的朗誦，能啟迪在場每一位聽眾的心智。

column1: If it would please     the Hero so,  Then there is that     which he should know.  A simple prize     from Sultan wise,  For deeds of Air,     the land does owe.
column2: 若這能討     英雄歡心，  那麼有件事，     他該知悉。  一份薄禮     來自賢明蘇丹，  只因這片土地     虧欠他驅風之功。

column1: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elemental of Air.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德（願他統治萬世！）特賜此錢袋，以酬謝你對抗風元素時所展現的無畏英勇。

column1: My thanks that you returned my purse,  Although it seems that it is much the worse  For a thief has stolen what was there  And gives me back a purse that now is bare.
column2: 感謝你尋回我的錢袋，  只是看來情況更加不堪，  竊賊已偷走裡頭的財物，  還回來的，是個空袋子而已。

column1: The poet is bothered more by the thief that by the loss of money.
column2: 比起損失的錢財，詩人更在意的是那個竊賊。

column1: The Sultan of Shapeir     has now decreed,  That this hero is a Hero     here indeed.  For actions fundamental     in dispelling elementals,  A reward for he who freed     us with all speed.
column2: 夏皮爾的蘇丹     如今已頒下詔令，  宣告此人確實     堪稱真英雄。  因其驅逐元素的     根本之功，  獎賞獻給那位     迅速解救眾人之人。

column1: From the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elementals of Earth and Water.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德（願他統治萬世！）特賜此錢袋，以酬謝你對抗土元素與水元素時所展現的無畏英勇。

column1: That which I lost     is now returned,  I give you the praise     of gratitude earned.
column2: 我所失去的，     如今失而復得，  這份感激的讚譽，     理應歸於你。

column1: The memory of a poet is long for those who aid him.
column2: 詩人對助他之人,記性可長著呢。

column1: You may ask, "What is a Hero?"  Yet the answer's very clear,  He's the one that faces fear  When the Darkness gathers near.
column2: 你或許會問：「何謂英雄？」  答案其實再清楚不過，  他就是那個直面恐懼的人，  當黑暗逼近之時。

column1: A Hero will brave the very Darkness for the needs of others.
column2: 英雄會為了他人的需要，勇敢面對黑暗本身。

column1: The Darkness gathers, and a man then strives     To do evil to another - Darkness thrives  The Darkness smiles, and those it gathers fight  Delighting Darkness as it takes their lives.
column2: 黑暗聚集，一人便開始     對另一人行惡——黑暗因此茁壯，  黑暗微笑，被它聚攏之人相互廝殺，  黑暗樂在其中，奪走他們的性命。

column1: Darkness delights in doing evil.
column2: 黑暗以作惡為樂。

column1: You are most welcome,     Hero who came here,  May you always be so     welcome in Shapeir.
column2: 你備受歡迎，     來到此地的英雄，  願你在夏皮爾     永遠如此受歡迎。

column1: Such a thing was done with pleasure, Hero, and needs no mention.
column2: 舉手之勞,英雄,何足掛齒。

column1: From the mountains whose tips     touch the stars,  To the city streets     filled with bazaars,  To the deserts of     flowing golden sand,  A tapestry of art     across the land.
column2: 從山峰之巔     直觸星辰，  到城中街道     市集紛陳，  再到沙漠裡     流動的金沙，  織就一幅     遍及大地的錦繡畫卷。

column1: The beauty of this land must be experienced to truly appreciate it.
column2: 這片土地的美,非得親身體會,才能真正領略。

column1: The Dark has touched her,     and has turned aside,  All things of light that     once did there abide.  Raseir is left to crumble     in despair,  Thus wickedness and folly     now reside.
column2: 黑暗已觸碰她，     並驅散了，  一切曾經     棲息於此的光明。  拉希爾只能     在絕望中崩解，  邪惡與愚昧，     如今盤據其間。

column1: But in the Doer's     arrogance and pride,  The light grows ever stronger     now outside,  A Hero strives to force     the darkness back,  And into morning shall    be Raseir's guide.
column2: 但在那行惡者的     傲慢與自負之中，  光明卻在外頭     日漸茁壯，  一位英雄奮力     將黑暗逼退，  引領拉希爾，     邁向黎明。

column1: The city of Raseir has been overcome by darkness, yet there shall be a hero to return her to the light.
column2: 拉希爾城已被黑暗吞沒,但終會有一位英雄,讓她重返光明。

column1: Mighty beings are     the magic Djinn  Who set the sands in motion     as the wind.  Who as the fire flames     the earth within  In the desert dance     undisciplined.
column2: 精靈是     強大的魔法造物，  能如風般     掀動沙塵起舞，  能如烈焰般     燃燒大地深處，  在沙漠中     恣意狂舞，不受拘束。

column1: The Djinn are a magical people, vastly powerful and best avoided.
column2: 精靈是一個擁有魔法的族類,法力浩大無邊,最好別去招惹。

column1: The Fire Elemental,     living flame,  Destruction unleashed,     wild and never tame.  Causing all it touches     then to burn,  Leaving only ashes     in return.
column2: 火元素，     是活生生的烈焰，  釋放出的毀滅，     狂野而永不馴服。  它所觸及的一切，     終將付之一炬，  留下的唯有     灰燼一片。

column1: The Fire Elemental is created by a spell cast by a User of Magic.  Once created, it burns and destroys, uncontrolled by any master.
column2: 火元素是由法師施展法術所創造。一旦誕生，它便肆意燃燒、破壞，不受任何主人控制。

column1: A thing of water,     living contradiction,  Despite the waters     flowing benediction,  For in its power     at its very worst  Forces men to suffer,     die of thirst.
column2: 水之造物，     是活生生的矛盾，  儘管流水     本是恩澤的象徵，  然而它的力量     發揮到極致時，  卻能逼人受苦，     渴死其中。

column1: The Water Elemental by its nature absorbs water, thus those around it die of thirst.
column2: 水元素天生就會吸盡水分,因此它周遭的人都會渴死。

column1: The Wind which turns and twists     with speeding force,  It wrecks and ruins all     without remorse.  The dust of devils,     Vortex of despair,  A living Elemental     formed of Air.
column2: 狂風翻騰扭轉，     挾著疾速之力，  它摧毀一切，     毫無悔意。  魔鬼揚起的塵埃，     絕望的漩渦，  一個活生生的元素，     由風凝聚而成。

column1: The Elemental of Air, by the sheer power of wind, is capable of vast destruction.
column2: 風元素單憑狂風之力,就能造成滔天的破壞。

column1: The might of Earth     and Strength of Rock transformed,  And through the land     the creature rose and stormed.  The shape of man     was made by force of spell  Until the fire's fury     shall it quell.
column2: 大地之力     與磐石之強化為一體，  這頭生物     破土而出，橫掃大地。  法術之力     賦予它人形，  直到烈焰之怒     將其鎮壓平息。

column1: The Elemental of Earth is a force of great devastation, but it can be overcome by flame.
column2: 土元素是一股毀天滅地的力量,但烈火能將它制伏。

column1: Thing of magic shaped     by Magic Spell,  Unleashed upon the land     by Wizard fell.  Half-life thing that hates     all things alive,  Chaos loosed, and in     destruction thrive.
column2: 魔法所塑之物，     由法術賦形，  被邪惡的巫師     放逐於世間。  這半生不死之物     憎恨一切生靈，  混沌因它而生，     並在破壞中茁壯。

column1: Elementals are formed by a magic spell and gain in power as they destroy.
column2: 元素是由魔法咒語凝成的,它們每毀滅一分,力量就增長一分。

column1: A Monster is a thing     which all must find,  And that which is most fright'ning,     hides within the mind  All must face this shadow     to give to it a name,  For without the knowing,     the thing will but remain.
column2: 怪物是     人人必須找出之物，  而最令人恐懼的，     其實藏於心中。  人人都須面對這陰影，     為它命名，  否則不明真相，     它便始終存在。

column1: Monster is a word for many things, unless one knows what it truly is, it cannot be overcome.
column2: 「怪物」一詞可以指許多東西，除非弄清楚它究竟是什麼，否則無法戰勝它。

column1: Learn ye now the magic     of this land,  To summon up the djinn     and give command,  To force your very foe     to be a friend  To turn a man to beast     and beast to man.
column2: 且來學習     這片土地的魔法，  召喚精靈，     並對其下令，  逼迫你的敵人     化敵為友，  讓人化為野獸，     野獸化為人形。

column1: Shapeir has a magic all its own.
column2: 夏皮爾有它自成一格的魔法。

column1: There is a Sultan     in the land Shapeir  Who speaks but truth and     counsels all to hear,  To whom all the people     praise and cheer,  The righteous rejoice,     and the foul all fear.
column2: 夏皮爾這片土地上     有位蘇丹，  他只說真話，     教誨眾人聆聽，  百姓對他     讚頌歡呼，  正直者為此欣喜，     邪惡者為此畏懼。

column1: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May His Name Be Known Always!) is a most powerful and perceptive ruler.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願他的名永世流傳!)是一位無比強大又洞察人心的統治者。

column1: A tale is told of     this same time last year,  That Magic caused     the man to disappear.  A brother now is     ruling in Raseir,  And no one knows the     fate of the Emir.
column2: 有個故事     說的正是去年此時，  魔法使得     那人就此消失。  如今是他的兄弟     在拉希爾當政，  無人知曉     埃米爾的下落。

column1: The true Emir of Raseir vanished last year at this same time.
column2: 拉希爾真正的埃米爾,去年此時憑空消失了。

column1: A Wizard once did     open up a door  Unto a place that     none had seen before,  Where spells are taught     and magic is the key  To ponder over     dusty books of lore.
column2: 曾有一位巫師     開啟了一扇門，  通往一處     從未有人見過的地方，  那裡教授法術，     魔法即是鑰匙，  用以鑽研     滿是塵埃的典籍。

column1: The doorway to the Wizard's Institute of Technocery is in this city.
column2: 通往巫師技術學院的門,就在這座城裡。

column1: The Palace gates are open     to but few  Only those the Sultan summons     can go through.
column2: 宮殿的大門     只為少數人敞開，  唯有蘇丹傳召之人，     方能通過。

column1: Entrance to the palace is by invitation only.
column2: 要進王宮,非有召見不可。

column1: To journey far across     this mighty land,  To face the deadly driving     dusts of sand,  The shining sun which sears     the naked hand,  And to survive, it must     be caravanned.
column2: 要遠行橫越     這片遼闊大地，  要面對致命     漫天飛沙，  灼人的烈日     曬傷裸露的雙手，  想要存活，     便得隨商隊同行。

column1: A caravan is the best way to travel across Shapeir.
column2: 要橫越夏皮爾,跟著商隊走是最好的法子。

column1: A place where all must fight     just to survive  Forethought and wit shall help     thee stay alive.
column2: 在這裡人人都得奮戰     只為求生存，  遠見與機智     將助你活下去。

column1: Only the toughest or smartest can survive the desert alone.
column2: 唯有最強悍或最聰明的人,才能獨自在沙漠中活下來。

column1: Some questions are far     better left unsaid  For greater troubles     follow in their stead.
column2: 有些問題     還是不問為妙，  因為更大的麻煩，     將隨之而來。

column1: It is best that you do not ask.
column2: 這事兒你最好別問。

column1: A place where the adventurer     may pause,  And practice for a while,     or choose a cause.  No matter where he travels,     there he knows  He will find the Guild Hall waiting     where he goes.
column2: 一處讓冒險者     得以歇腳之地，  可稍作練習，     或選定志向。  無論他行至何方，     他都知曉，  公會堂總會     在那裡等候。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild Hall is on the west side of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會大廳在格鬥者廣場的西側。

column1: It is said that in Shapeir     there is an Inn  That in all the world     you will not find its twin.  The food is fine when     there you care to dine  Most excellently     run by Katta kin.
column2: 據說夏皮爾     有間旅店，  舉世之間     再無其二。  若你願意在此用餐，     食物美味無比，  由卡塔族人     悉心經營。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn is unexcelled in its food and entertainment.
column2: 卡塔之尾客棧的菜餚與表演,無人能及。

column1: In the desert in the day     it's always hot,  And then again at night     it's always not.
column2: 沙漠裡的白晝     總是酷熱，  到了夜裡，     卻又不然。

column1: The desert can get surprisingly cool after the sunset.
column2: 日落之後,沙漠會涼得出乎你意料。

column1: In the center of the city     stands the fountain flowing free,  Pours the lifeblood of the living:     water washing endlessly.
column2: 城市的中心，     矗立著自由流淌的噴泉，  傾注著生命之血：     不絕不息的清水。

column1: The water of the fountain sustains everything around.
column2: 這座噴泉的水,滋養著周遭的一切。

column1: It is whispered     by the Katta  That a hero     had been sought.  By their kindred     seeking elsewhere  To this city     he was brought.
column2: 卡塔族之間     悄聲流傳，  說有位英雄     曾被尋覓。  族人四處     奔走尋訪，  終將他     帶來此城。

column1: From their homeland     they were banished;  Now they seek there     to return  And their hero     shall return them  To the place     they ever yearn.
column2: 他們曾被     逐出家園；  如今渴望     重返故土。  他們的英雄，     終將帶領他們，  回到那個     日夜思念之地。

column1: The Katta were driven from their home in Raseir last year.  It is said that they now have a hero who shall return their home to them.
column2: 卡塔族去年被逐出拉希爾的家園。據說,如今他們有了一位英雄,將帶他們重返故土。

column1: When a stranger seeks     to purchase in this land,  Some things of finery     or of purpose grand,  From the shops of the bazaar,     he must first have a dinar,  The Money Changer's purse     will fill his hand.
column2: 當異鄉人     想在此地購物，  無論是精緻之物，     或別有用途之物，  想從市集的店鋪購入，     須先握有第納爾，  兌幣商人的錢袋，     將填滿他的手。

column1: The Money Changer exchanges coins from distant lands to our own centimes and dinars.
column2: 兌幣商人會將遠方異地的錢幣，兌換成我們的分幣與第納爾。

column1: Through the door at the end     of the Tarik of Stars, is a man  Who can see in the lights     of the sky in the night nature's plan  For the men on the sands     and the towns of the lands down below  What is writ of the date     and the deeds that their fate will bestow.
column2: 穿過星辰塔里克街盡頭的     那扇門，住著一人，  他能從夜空中的     點點星光，看出天命的安排，  為沙漠中的人們     與地上的城鎮，  解讀日期所載，     以及命運將賜予的作為。

column1: The Astrologer sees the future in the patterns of the stars at night.
column2: 占星師能從夜裡的星辰排列中,窺見未來。

column1: A Wizard Woman weaves     the warp of spells,  By which is watched     what incidents befell,  Aziza sees the present     and the past,  Perceives what is,     and thus, shall be, foretells.
column2: 一位女巫師     編織著法術的經緯，  藉此窺見     曾經發生的種種，  阿姿莎洞悉當下，     也洞悉過去，  她感知現狀，     並由此預言未來。

column1: The Enchantress Aziza uses her magic to see events which occur in this land.
column2: 女術士阿姿莎用她的魔法,看見這片土地上發生的種種事件。

column1: I am the Poet, teller of tales     that all might hear,  Speaker of Speeches, Weaver of words     for all Shapeir,  Giver of gossip, Writer of rhymes,     Narrator of News,  I am called Omar, Teacher of truths     to all now near.
column2: 我是詩人，訴說著     人人可聞的故事，  演說之人，為全夏皮爾     編織字句之人，  傳遞閒言，寫下詩句，     講述時事之人，  人稱奧馬爾，向在場眾人     教誨真理之人。

column1: The poet is a source of information and entertainment for the land.
column2: 這位詩人,是這片土地上消息與娛樂的來源。

column1: For all who journey from     the lands afar,  The center of the city,     the bazaar,  is filled with merchants' magic,     and shall be,  A wonder to behold,     a joy to see.
column2: 對所有遠道而來的     旅人而言，  這座城市的中心，     市集，  滿是商人施展的魔法，     它必將，  成為一處奇景，     一份賞心悅目。

column1: The bazaar of the merchants is a lure for tourists.
column2: 商人雲集的市集,是吸引遊客的一大去處。

column1: The Dervish of the desert     sits upon the sands beside the pool,  Of the Oasis as men pass him     and they laugh in ridicule at such a fool,  But he watches and he listens     and he thinks upon the things which pass him by,  And he smiles upon his mockers     when they question all his answers in reply.
column2: 沙漠中的苦行僧，     坐在綠洲水池旁的沙地上，  過路的人們見了他，     嘲笑他是個傻子，  但他靜靜觀察、靜靜聆聽，     細細思索著身旁流逝的一切，  當嘲笑他的人質疑他所有的答案時，     他只是報以微笑。

column1: Many people go to the Dervish for knowledge, but few understand him.
column2: 許多人去向苦行僧求教問道,可真正聽懂他的人沒幾個。

column1: A poem is but words     used in a different way,  In which the meaning is     far more than words can say.
column2: 詩不過是     以不同方式運用的文字，  其中蘊含的意義，     遠比文字本身能道出的更多。

column1: A poem goes beyond the mere meaning of its words to express the deeper truths within it.
column2: 詩超越了字面的意義，表達出其中更深層的真理。

column1: You applaud loudly and long for your friend Shema's dancing prowess.
column2: 你為朋友舍瑪高超的舞技，鼓掌喝采，久久不停。

column1: You receive a reward of 50 Dinars.
column2: 你獲得50第納爾的獎賞。

column1: You receive a reward of 100 Dinars.
column2: 你獲得100第納爾的獎賞。

column1: There is no need for that.  I am a poet rich in spirit already.
column2: 不必了。我是個精神上已然富足的詩人。

column1: They do not respond.
column2: 它們沒有反應。

column1: Is it not amazing how quickly we have set up this Inn?   The Katta's Tail is already the center of Shapeir's entertainment.  Where else can you watch Shema dance?
column2: 這間旅店這麼快就開起來，是不是很神奇？「卡塔之尾」已經是夏皮爾娛樂的中心了。你還能去哪裡欣賞舍瑪跳舞呢？

column1: You know you will always have a place to stay and fine food to eat in Shapeir.  Shameen, Shema, and I cannot thank you enough for helping us recover our stolen treasure.
column2: 你知道,在夏皮爾,你永遠有個落腳處,有美食可享用。沙明、舍瑪和我,真不知該如何謝你幫我們尋回被偷走的寶藏。

column1: It is good to be back home, even though I have had troubling news.  My old friend Arus Al-Din, the Emir of Raseir, is missing.  He was a good man, but not as wise as I.
column2: 能回到家真好，儘管我聽到了令人不安的消息。我的老朋友阿魯斯·阿丁，拉希爾的埃米爾，如今下落不明。他是個好人，只是不如我聰明。

column1: Ah, but you have not even seen our beautiful Shapeir, and already I speak of problems.  You need to go out, explore and discover why this is indeed a land of enchantment.
column2: 唉,你都還沒好好看過我們美麗的夏皮爾,我就先談起這些煩心事了。你該出去走走,探一探,親自發掘為何這裡真是一片充滿魔法的土地。

column1: Good Day, my fine friend.  I trust you are finding your stay in Shapeir interesting?  There is always so much to see and do here.
column2: 日安，我的好朋友。相信你在夏皮爾這段日子過得挺有意思吧？這裡總有數不完的新鮮事。

column1: The subject is too hot for me.
column2: 這個話題對我來說太燙手了。

column1: Only a true Hero could have defeated the dreadful Fire Elemental.
column2: 唯有真正的英雄，才能擊敗那可怕的火元素。

column1: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Djinn A shadow passed over the Katta's Tail Inn Astrologers foretell that Doom shall come to dwell And Shapeir shall be but sand upon the wind.
column2: 在靈蛇之月，     精靈之年，  一道陰影掠過了     「卡塔之尾」旅店，  占星師預言　　厄運將降臨此地，  夏皮爾終將     化作隨風而逝的黃沙。

column1: Comes a hero from the North,     riding on the very air, And this is sign the first to then beware, For Darkness soon shall fall and shadow cover all, The city and the ones now living there.
column2: 一位英雄從北方到來，     乘風而行，  這正是第一個徵兆，     提醒世人警惕，  因黑暗即將降臨，     陰影將籠罩一切，  籠罩這座城市，     以及城中所有生靈。

column1: The first Doom shall be Fire,     which shall burn the very stone, The next is Air, and rocks are overthrown, Earth shall be the third,     then the final Doom is heard, The Water gone, the city parched like bone.
column2: 第一災厄將是烈火，燒盡頑石本身，次者為疾風，巨岩傾覆崩摧，第三為大地，隨後終末災厄降臨，水源枯竭，全城乾涸如骨。

column1: Unless the one called Hero is a Hero true indeed, Who comes to help the city in its need, Then will face the depths of Doom     in the darkness of the Tomb, From the Elemental's Master, we are freed.
column2: 除非那被稱為英雄之人真是名副其實的英雄，前來在危難之際拯救此城，直面墓穴黑暗深處的災厄，我們才能從元素之主手中獲得解脫。

column1: Came a Hero drawing near     to our land of fair Shapeir, The Katta called and carried that one here. I shall tell an allegory     'bout a Quest for Love and Glory Which I shall relate if you will lend an ear.
column2: 曾有一位英雄，來到我們美麗的夏皮爾之地，卡塔族的呼喚將他帶到此處。我將訴說一則寓言，關於愛與榮耀的探求，若你願意傾聽，我便為你細細道來。

column1: The Hero first faced fire     which was making matters dire, For the flames he fought were flitting ever higher. With water first he fought it     and into a lamp he caught it 'Ere our city of Shapeir became our pyre.
column2: 英雄首先面對的是烈火，情勢危急萬分，他奮戰的火焰竄升得越來越高。他先以水與之搏鬥，將它困進一盞油燈之中，才沒讓我們的夏皮爾之城化為柴堆。

column1: The next he faced was Air,     which I'm sure that you're aware, Was blustering and blowing here and there. Before the Winds became our bane     as a fearsome hurricane, With a bellows then he caught it fair and square.
column2: 接著他面對的是疾風，想必你也知曉，四處呼嘯、狂吹不止。趁狂風尚未化為可怕的颶風、成為我們的禍患之前，他用一具風箱將它穩穩捕獲。
