column1: So let us raise a cheer for the Hero of Shapeir, And remember that our praise is quite sincere, For in the Quest for Glory as related by this story, Is a Hero who has saved us from our fear, Is a Hero who's a Hero without peer!
column2: 讓我們為夏皮爾的英雄歡呼喝采，謹記我們的讚頌出自真心，因為在這則故事所述說的榮耀探求之中，有一位英雄拯救我們脫離恐懼，有一位英雄舉世無雙！

column1: In the Month of the Serpent,     in the Year of the Spear, Darkness passed over the town of Raseir, And the rumor had been said     that the old Emir was dead, For Arus al-Din had seemed to disappear.
column2: 在長矛之年、靈蛇之月，黑暗籠罩了拉希爾城，謠言四起，說老埃米爾已然身亡，因為阿魯斯·阿丁似乎已經消失無蹤。

column1: The Emir had a brother,     who quickly took the throne, but the Shadow lurking under soon was known For the Katta were exiled,     and Raseir was then defiled, By the rules and regulations that were shown.
column2: 埃米爾有位兄弟，迅速登上王座，但潛伏其下的陰影很快便為人所知，卡塔族遭到放逐，拉希爾隨之被玷污，被那些頒布的律法規章所玷污。

column1: The Sultan sent his men to restore Raseir to right, But they did not even have a chance to fight, For as they crossed the land,     a tremendous storm of sand, Forced his forces to retreat from their plight.
column2: 蘇丹派遣部眾前去匡正拉希爾，但他們連交戰的機會都沒有，因為當他們穿越大地之時，一場猛烈的沙暴襲來，迫使他的軍隊從困境中撤退。

column1: So now the rumor's told of the city of Raseir, That the Darkness shadows everything in fear. And He that now does rule has the Emir as his tool, Disobedience brings punishment severe.
column2: 如今拉希爾城的傳聞是這樣說的，黑暗籠罩一切，令人恐懼萬分。而如今掌權者將埃米爾當作工具，違抗者將受到嚴厲懲罰。

column1: The moral of this story as the best of tales must tell Of the horrors of the orders that befell, Is the simple corollary that the Hero must be wary, 'Ere He journeys to that Shadow's Citadel, And we bid him luck and wish him to fare well.
column2: 這則故事的寓意，如同最好的傳說所訴說的，關於降臨的律令是何等恐怖，其簡單的結論便是：英雄在啟程前往那陰影堡壘之前，務必萬分謹慎，我們在此為他祈求好運，願他一路平安。

column1: As you travel to Raseir, %s, know that all of us in Shapeir wish for your safety and the success of your mission.
column2: %s,當你動身前往拉希爾時,請記得,我們夏皮爾的每一個人都祈願你平安,也祝你此行任務圓滿成功。

column1: The alley through which you entered looks even darker than the dark alcove in which you now find yourself.
column2: 你進來時經過的巷子，看起來比你現在所在的這處陰暗壁龕還要更黑。

column1: The alley through which you entered looks ominously dark compared to this well-lit alcove.
column2: 與這處燈火通明的壁龕相比，你進來時經過的巷子顯得陰森黑暗。

column1: This small alcove at the end of the alley has a small window with an iron grate above it.  The two oil sconces used for illumination are doused and there is no one about.
column2: 巷子盡頭這個小凹間,有一扇小窗,窗上方裝著鐵柵。原本用來照明的兩盞油壁燈都熄了,四下無人。

column1: This small alcove at the end of the alley has a small window with an iron grate above it.  Two oil sconces illuminate the guard standing nearby and the woman behind the window.
column2: 巷子盡頭這個小凹間,有一扇小窗,窗上方裝著鐵柵。兩盞油壁燈照亮了站在一旁的衛兵,以及窗後的女子。

column1: You have found the Money Changer.
column2: 你找到了錢幣兌換商。

column1: She does look like a tasty morsel.  "Maybe later," you think.
column2: 她看起來確實像一道美味的佳餚。「或許晚點再說，」你心想。

column1: Men!  All talk and no action.
column2: 男人哪!光出一張嘴,從不來真的。

column1: My, you certainly have a way with words.  You might say you have a golden tongue.
column2: 哎呀,你嘴可真甜。簡直可以說你有一條金舌頭呢。

column1: That's all well and good, but I'd rather be conducting a little business, if there's any to be conducted.
column2: 這都很好,不過要是有生意可做,我倒寧願做點買賣。

column1: Sorry, my dear, but you'll need a bigger tool.
column2: 抱歉,親愛的,你得換把更大的傢伙才行。

column1: Nice.
column2: 不錯。

column1: She coyly evades your kiss.
column2: 她嬌羞地避開了你的吻。

column1: Always happy to be of service, Handsome.
column2: 隨時樂意效勞,帥哥。

column1: Flowers are sweet, but I prefer diamonds to daisies any day.
column2: 花兒雖甜美,但要我選,我寧取鑽石,不要雛菊。

column1: You look at the dangerous-looking guard and think better of it.
column2: 你看了看那一臉兇相的衛兵,還是打消了念頭。

column1: Well, young Jackal, may fortune always favor you.
column2: 好啦,小豺狼,願好運永遠眷顧你。

column1: So you are a Jackal among the goats, then.  I am very fond of those of the profession.  This could be the start of a beautiful relationship.
column2: 所以你是混跡在山羊群裡的豺狼啊。我對這行當的人特別有好感。這或許會是段美好情誼的開始。

column1: Come back tomorrow evening or so if you are interested in doing for me a little favor.  It could be to our mutual benefit.
column2: 你若有意替我幫個小忙,明晚前後再來。這或許對你我都有好處。

column1: And do come fully equipped.  No one of your profession should travel without the proper tool, and a little oil to make the insertion easier.
column2: 記得裝備齊全再來。幹你這行的,出門怎能不帶上趁手的工具,還有一點好讓它更易插入的油。

column1: That's a real cute sign, my little Hero, but these are business hours.
column2: 這手勢還真可愛,我的小英雄,可現在是做生意的時間。

column1: Is that a Thieves' sign, or are you just happy to see me?
column2: 那是盜賊手勢，還是你單純看到我很高興？

column1: Show me something worth exchanging.
column2: 拿些值得兌換的東西給我看看。

column1: Show me something worth bargaining for.
column2: 拿點值得我討價還價的東西出來瞧瞧。

column1: That's as far as I'll go, Hero.
column2: 就只能到這兒了,英雄。

column1: You drive a hard bargain.  I can pay you an additional 20%, but no more.
column2: 你真會殺價。我可以多付你20%，但僅止於此。

column1: You're not very good at this, are you?  But I'll give you an additional 5% more just because I happen to like dealing with you.  You're such a card.
column2: 你不太擅長討價還價，是吧？不過我就因為喜歡跟你打交道，額外多給你5%。你這人真有意思。

column1: That's nice of you to show me your money.
column2: 你把錢秀給我看,真好心。

column1: Show me something more interesting.
column2: 拿些更有意思的東西給我看看。

column1: Dinarzad just smiles at you.
column2: 蒂娜薩德只是對你淺淺一笑。

column1: You say, "Why yes, I have it right here."  Dinarzad smiles and looks at you expectantly.
column2: 你說：「喔，是的，我這裡就有。」迪納莎德微笑著，滿懷期待地看著你。

column1: I'm sorry, but I never got the chance to get anything for you.
column2: 很抱歉，但我一直沒機會替你弄到什麼東西。

column1: Then hand me your money.
column2: 那就把你的錢交給我。

column1: What a pity.  Perhaps another time, then.
column2: 真可惜。那就下次吧。

column1: Alas, my heart is broken.
column2: 唉,我的心都碎了。

column1: I will give you a fair price for anything of value you can bring me.
column2: 只要你帶來有價值的東西，我都會給你公道的價錢。

column1: Franc, the guard, Marks you with his glare.  You suspect he would give no Quarter in a fight.  He would likely Pound you into Ruble.
column2: 衛兵法蘭克狠狠瞪了你一眼。你猜他在打鬥中絕不會手下留情，很可能會把你打得粉身碎骨。

column1: In any case, he has no Dime to speak to you (to Coin a phrase).  To fight him would make no Cents.  Better leave him a Loan for a Change.
column2: 總之，他沒空跟你多說什麼。跟他打架完全不划算，還是給他留點餘地比較好。

column1: The two oil sconces are not lit.
column2: 兩座油燈台都沒有點燃。

column1: The flickering flames in the two oil sconces eerily illuminate this alcove.
column2: 兩座油燈台上搖曳的火焰，將這處壁龕照得詭異幽暗。

column1: Rumor has it that a thief broke into the Metal Worker's house the other night.   There will be extra guards patrolling the streets tonight.  It will be a good night to sleep in.
column2: 謠傳前幾晚有個盜賊闖入了金屬工匠家中。今晚街上會有額外的守衛巡邏，今晚最好乖乖待在屋裡睡覺。

column1: By the way, did you happen to pick up the little item I mentioned before?
column2: 對了,我先前提的那件小東西,你可有順手帶來?

column1: If you are interested, and I think you will be, I do have a job for you.
column2: 要是你有興趣——我想你會有的——我這兒倒有件差事要交給你。

column1: If you find yourself walking the streets at around Noon of this Night, perhaps you should stroll down a certain street I could mention and check out the lock on a certain door.
column2: 如果你在今晚的夜之正午時分在街上閒晃，或許該去我提過的某條街走走，看看某扇門上的鎖。

column1: Most of the family will be staying out very late tonight, and at that house is a silver tea service for which I have a great fondness.
column2: 那家人今晚大多會在外頭待到很晚,而那棟屋子裡,有一套我十分鍾愛的銀茶具。

column1: If you happened to be in the neighborhood, you might pick it up for me.  Are you interested in this little job?
column2: 要是你剛好經過那一帶,不妨替我把它取回來。對這件小差事有興趣嗎?

column1: Ah, the sunshine returns to our corner of the alley.  Would you like me to be of service to you?
column2: 啊,陽光又照進我們這條巷子的角落了。要我為你效勞嗎?

column1: So, we have a stranger to our fair land.  Welcome, Traveler from afar.  Come closer, that you might find all that you seek here.
column2: 看來我們美麗的土地上來了位陌生人。歡迎，遠道而來的旅人。靠近些吧，你在這裡能找到你所尋求的一切。

column1: I am Dinarzad, Changer of money for Shapeir.  Would you like to exchange something with me?
column2: 我是迪納莎德，夏皮爾的錢幣兌換商。你想跟我兌換些什麼嗎？

column1: As you search through your pouch, you discover that you have no more coins from Spielburg to exchange.
column2: 你翻遍錢囊,發現再也沒有史畢柏格的錢幣可換了。

column1: You have %d gold coins from Spielburg.  How many will you exchange?
column2: 你有%d枚來自斯柏堡的金幣。你想兌換多少？

column1: But that would be cheating!  You only have %d gold coins, and can only Peso much.
column2: 但那就是作弊了！你只有%d枚金幣，能兌換的金額有限。

column1: Here you are.  I just love intimate exchanges like this.
column2: 給你。我就愛這種親密的交流。

column1: Oh, is your pouch deflated?  I'm so sorry.  Well, I hope you can come again soon.
column2: 喔,你的錢囊癟了?真遺憾。好吧,希望你很快能再來。

column1: Dinarzad takes the %s and hands you the money.
column2: 迪納莎德收下%s，把錢遞給你。

column1: By the way, you might like to know where a certain someone (who shall be nameless, yet for whom I have little love) conceals his safe.
column2: 對了，你或許會想知道，某位人士（我就不點名了，反正我對他沒什麼好感）把保險箱藏在哪裡。

column1: Why do you offer that which you do not have?
column2: 你為什麼要拿出你根本沒有的東西？

column1: You'd better not.  Franc, the guard, looks like he would Pound you into Ruble if you tried that.
column2: 你最好別這麼做。衛兵法蘭克看起來要是你敢那樣做，會把你揍得體無完膚。

column1: The %s is worth %d Dinars.  I will give you %d Dinars for it.
column2: %s值%d第納爾。我會付你%d第納爾買下它。

column1: The %s is worth %d Dinars.  I would normally give you %d Dinars for it, but I will pay you %d Dinars because I like you.
column2: %s值%d第納爾。我通常會付你%d第納爾買下它，但因為我喜歡你，就付你%d第納爾。

column1: Let us talk about things more important to us.
column2: 我們來聊點對彼此更要緊的事吧。

column1: I am sorry, but I have no information of value to you on that subject.
column2: 抱歉,關於那件事,我沒有什麼對你有用的消息。

column1: You and I could make a very fine couple.
column2: 你跟我,可以湊成很登對的一對呢。

column1: I know of a place where you can, ah, practice your skills.  You have only to bring to me what you find there for both of us to benefit.  See me tomorrow evening for details.
column2: 我知道有個地方能讓你，呃，磨練一下技藝。你只要把在那裡找到的東西帶來給我，我們倆都能得利。明晚來找我談細節。

column1: Early evening is best for talk.  It leaves the night for the fun part.
column2: 傍晚最適合聊天,這樣才能把夜晚留給好玩的部分。

column1: I prefer Jackals.  They're so much better for preying around.
column2: 我比較喜歡豺狼。牠們在四處狩獵這方面實在厲害多了。

column1: Don't be so sheepish.  I love to kid around.
column2: 別這麼畏畏縮縮像隻綿羊嘛。我最愛跟人「逗」著玩了。

column1: You need me, and perhaps some day I'll knead you.
column2: 你需要我,而說不定哪天,我也會好好「揉捏」你一番。

column1: I will gladly trade your foreign money for our Dinars and Centimes.  I always enjoy helping others, and I give the best rate around.
column2: 我很樂意把你的外國貨幣換成我們的第納爾和分幣。我一向樂於助人，而且匯率是這一帶最優惠的。

column1: Bring things of value for me to evaluate.  I'm sure you'll find I make more cents for you that way.
column2: 帶些值錢的東西來讓我估價吧。你會發現,這樣我能替你多賺幾個「分」。

column1: Dinars are the gold coins of Shapeir.
column2: 第納爾是夏皮爾的金幣。

column1: Centimes are the brass coins that are used everywhere around here.
column2: 生丁是這一帶到處通用的黃銅幣。

column1: Fool!  You have only to bring me what you find from where I send you, and I shall pay you what those things are worth.
column2: 蠢蛋!你只管把我派你去的地方找到的東西帶回來給我,我自會按它們的價值付你錢。

column1: Do not trust him in Raseir unless you are a thief.  Even then, watch your backside.
column2: 在拉希爾,除非你自己也是個賊,否則別信他。就算你是賊,也得當心點,別讓人從背後捅你一刀。

column1: You seem to be a very dangerous man.  A girl cannot be too careful.  Perhaps when we know each other better, though...
column2: 你看起來是個很危險的男人。女孩子家可不能不小心。不過嘛,等我們彼此更熟一點,或許……

column1: If you have to ask about it, then you certainly don't deserve it.
column2: 這種事你要是還得開口問,那你肯定就不配擁有它。

column1: If you are the right sort of person for the right sort of work, then when the time comes, arrangements might be made.
column2: 如果你是適合做這種工作的那種人,那麼時機一到,事情或許就能安排妥當。

column1: Work is a little hard to come by in Shapeir.  After what you have already done here, you should lay low, if you'll pardon the expression.
column2: 在夏皮爾找活兒有點難。你既已在這兒幹了那些事,恕我直言,你該低調一陣子。

column1: At the noon of this night, you will take Sharki Darb to Rani Tarik.  Then turn right into the unnamed alley.  The first house on your right will be locked, but unbarred.  You will find things of interest there, but know that there are those who sleep in the next room.  Be wary, and if you are very good, we will both share your profits.
column2: 在今晚的夜之正午時分，你要沿著沙爾基·達爾布走到拉尼·塔里克。接著右轉進入那條無名小巷。你右手邊的第一間房子雖然上鎖，但沒有加裝門閂。你會在裡面找到些有意思的東西，但要知道隔壁房間裡還睡著人。小心行事，若你表現得夠好，我們可以平分你的收穫。

column1: The Guard, Franc, is my own and does not speak.
column2: 那個守衛法蘭克是我的人,他不會說話。

column1: Shapeir is a very safe and orderly place under the watch of our Sultan.  It can be very dangerous for those who break the law -- and get caught.
column2: 在我們蘇丹的看顧下,夏皮爾是個十分安全又井然有序的地方。對那些犯了法——又被逮到的人來說,這裡可凶險得很。

column1: Raseir was once a place where anyone with some brains and skills could make some quick cash.  Now that things have changed under the new Emir, only those who toady to the authorities can get ahead.  Ferrari is an excellent example of that type of person -- only too willing to kiss and tell.
column2: 拉希爾曾經是個只要有點腦子和本事，就能快速撈到錢的地方。如今在新埃米爾的統治下情勢已變，只有向當局阿諛奉承的人才能出人頭地。費拉里就是這種人的絕佳範例——巴不得逢迎拍馬、四處通風報信。

column1: I am far better than any old Djinni in a lamp, provided you rub me the right way.
column2: 只要你會「摸」得對,我可比那種困在燈裡的老神靈強多了。

column1: The Sultan (May He Live Forever!), is very wise and watches over everything.  It is best not to try to break the laws around here unless one is very, very cunning.
column2: 蘇丹（願他萬歲！）非常睿智，洞察一切。除非你非常、非常狡猾，否則最好別在這一帶以身試法。

column1: The old Emir of Raseir was a fool, and fools are easy to deal with.  But from all I hear, whoever controls Raseir now is far from a fool.
column2: 拉希爾的老埃米爾是個蠢蛋,而蠢蛋好對付得很。可就我聽到的種種消息來看,如今掌控拉希爾的那個人,可一點都不蠢。

column1: The Palace is a place I would very much like to visit someday.
column2: 王宮是個我很想哪天去逛逛的地方。

column1: There is a caravan traveling to Raseir nearly every month, I believe.
column2: 我記得幾乎每個月都有一支商隊前往拉希爾。

column1: I deal in money.  I will be only too happy to exchange your old, worn-out foreign money for money which is a bit more practical around here.
column2: 我做的是錢的生意。我非常樂意把你那些又舊又破的外幣,換成在這一帶比較好用的錢。

column1: There is no Thieves' Guild around here.  Certainly, I can satisfy your needs better than any mere guild.  Only in Raseir do you need such union scum.
column2: 這一帶沒有盜賊公會。當然，我能比任何公會更能滿足你的需求。只有在拉希爾才需要那種工會敗類。

column1: The Guild Hall is to the west of here.  You have only to follow the alleyway until it again turns west, and then follow it to the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 公會大廳在這裡的西邊。你只要順著巷子走,直到它再次往西轉,然後一路走到戰士廣場就是了。

column1: The Inn is to the southeast of here, but you will have to take the round-about route to get there.
column2: 旅店在這裡的東南方，但你得繞路才能到達。

column1: The only weather I care about is whether you and I can exchange something besides glances.
column2: 我唯一在意的「天氣」,就是你我之間除了眉來眼去,還能不能交換點別的。

column1: The Fountain Plaza is to the east of here.  Follow the street eastward whenever you can.
column2: 噴泉廣場就在這裡的東邊。能往東走的時候就盡量沿街往東走。

column1: There is a rumor to the effect that I am very fond of strangers.
column2: 有個傳聞是這麼說的:我這人很喜歡外鄉客。

column1: I am always pleased to exchange money for strangers.
column2: 我向來樂意替外鄉人兌換錢幣。

column1: There are many merchants around here.
column2: 這一帶商人多得很。

column1: My name is Dinarzad, which means "Child of Gold Coins."
column2: 我的名字叫迪納莎德，意思是「金幣之子」。

column1: Oil is to loosen locks, relieve squeaky hinges, and make other things slide smoothly.
column2: 油是用來鬆動鎖頭、潤滑吱嘎作響的鉸鏈,還有讓其他東西滑順起來的。

column1: I love a good Thieves' Tool!
column2: 我最愛稱手的盜賊工具了！

column1: There is a Weapon Shop in this city which has a trapdoor beneath its anvil.  If you can but move the anvil and open the trapdoor, you shall be richly rewarded.  I shall also be rewarded, knowing that I have contributed to the fall of the Weaponer's house.
column2: 這城裡有家武器鋪,鐵砧底下藏著一道暗門。只要你能搬開鐵砧、打開暗門,就會獲得豐厚的報酬。而我也算有所回報——畢竟我出了一份力,促成那武器匠一家的垮台。

column1: The anvil is made of iron, and is very heavy.  You will need either a great deal of strength or proper preparation to move it.
column2: 這座鐵砧是鐵製的，非常沉重。你需要極大的力氣，或是妥善的準備才能搬動它。

column1: Happy to see me, or is that just the money in your pocket?
column2: 見到我太高興了,還是只是你口袋裡的錢在作怪?

column1: A pleasure to see you again.  I like a man who just keeps on coming.
column2: 很高興又見到你。我就喜歡這種一來再來的男人。

column1: I've seen many men in my time, and you're certainly one of them.
column2: 這輩子我見過的男人多了去,你嘛,當然也算其中一個。

column1: Good.  I hate to part with it unless there is a very good cause.
column2: 很好。要不是有天大的理由,我可捨不得割愛。

column1: The Apothecary Shop is aflame with reds, yellows, and oranges, and smoky incense fills the air.  The druggist is busy creating pills and powders.
column2: 藥鋪裡紅黃橙三色熊熊燃燒,空氣中瀰漫著裊裊薰香。藥師正忙著調製藥丸與藥粉。

column1: Ah, good day.  I am busy as always, making up a batch of pills.  If you need something, just let me know.
column2: 啊,你好。我一如往常地忙,正在調一批藥丸。有什麼需要,儘管說。

column1: To serve you is an honor.
column2: 能為您效勞是一種榮幸。

column1: I have already given you my very last speck of powder.
column2: 我已經把最後一點粉末都給你了。

column1: I should think that someone with your particular skills would be able to think of another way to defeat the Earth Elemental.
column2: 我想像你這般身懷絕技之人，應該能想出別的辦法擊敗土元素。

column1: My Powder of Burning is much too valuable to be wasted needlessly.
column2: 我的燃燒之粉太過珍貴，不能白白浪費。

column1: Someone with your skills can do without my Powder of Burning.
column2: 以你的本事，應該不需要靠我的燃燒之粉。

column1: With fiery emotion, you explain to Harik that the Earth Elemental is attacking the city.  At first, he seems unconvinced, but your eloquence finally sways him.
column2: 你情緒激昂地向哈里克說明土元素精靈正在攻擊這座城市。起初他似乎半信半疑,但你的口才終究說服了他。

column1: As I said, I really could not give you the little bit that I have.
column2: 我說過了,我手頭這一點點,實在沒法給你。

column1: I have already given you my very last speck of it.
column2: 我最後一點點都已經給你了。

column1: I only have a little left.  I really couldn't part with it for any price.
column2: 我只剩一點點了,無論出多少價,我都實在割捨不下。

column1: Are you really certain you need this?
column2: 你真的確定你需要這個?

column1: Yes, Powder of Burning might be of some use against an Earth Elemental.
column2: 沒錯，燃燒之粉或許能對土元素派上點用場。

column1: Thank you.  I wish I could have seen it, though.  The fire must have been lovely.
column2: 謝謝你。不過,真希望我能親眼瞧瞧。那把火想必美極了。

column1: Humph.  Nobody likes a braggart.  You seem to have forgotten who gave you the Powder of Burning.
column2: 哼。沒人喜歡愛吹噓的人。你似乎忘了是誰給你燃燒之粉的。

column1: You ardently describe the Fire Elemental to Harik.
column2: 你熱切地向哈里克描述火元素精靈。

column1: Yes, I have heard of these things.  Wouldn't it be a wondrous sight!
column2: 是啊,這些東西我聽說過。那該是多麼奇妙的景象啊!

column1: Isn't it exciting?  Of course, some people don't appreciate its beauty.  They actually want to get rid of it!
column2: 夠刺激吧?當然啦,有些人不懂得欣賞它的美,還真想把它弄掉呢!

column1: I must sadly admit that it did get out of hand.   I suppose you did the right thing in vanquishing it.
column2: 我得遺憾地承認,那場面確實失控了。我想,你把牠收拾掉是做對了。

column1: Although you know that Harik would be most generous if offered a real Fire Elemental of his own, you decide that the creature in the lamp might prove invaluable to you at some later date.
column2: 你雖知道,若送哈里克一頭真正屬於他自己的火元素精靈,他必定慷慨相報,但你還是決定:燈裡那頭生物,日後或許對你有無可估量的用處。

column1: You breathily describe the Air Elemental to Harik.
column2: 你氣喘吁吁地向哈里克描述風元素精靈。

column1: Such a thing is not nearly so grand as the Fire Elemental.
column2: 這種東西可遠遠及不上火元素精靈那般了得。

column1: You fluidly describe the Water Elemental to Harik.
column2: 你流暢地向哈里克描述水元素精靈。

column1: Well, I hope you figure out how to deal with it.  This city will soon run out of water.
column2: 好吧,但願你想得出對付它的法子。這座城快沒水了。

column1: Such a thing would not be nearly as grand as a Fire Elemental.
column2: 那種東西,可遠遠比不上火元素那般氣派。

column1: You give Harik a brief description of the caged beast.
column2: 你向哈里克簡短描述了那頭籠中野獸。

column1: That sounds most interesting.  It is probably not a natural creature, but around here, who can tell?  You could try a Dispel Potion on it, though.
column2: 聽起來相當有意思。牠恐怕不是天然生物，不過在這一帶，誰說得準呢？不過你可以試試對牠用解咒藥水。

column1: Most interesting, I'm sure, but I'm a busy man.
column2: 想必非常有趣,不過呢,我是個大忙人。

column1: I have many pills for sale.  Which do you want?
column2: 我這兒有許多藥丸出售。你要哪一種?

column1: There is no way that I could possibly reduce my price.
column2: 我不可能再降價了。

column1: Did you wish to buy some pills?
column2: 你想買些藥丸嗎?

column1: I am trying to stay in business.  The more I give, the more I lose.  It shall be what I asked for, 70 centimes.
column2: 我還想繼續做生意呢。我給得越多，虧得就越多。就照我開的價，70分幣。

column1: My price is firm.
column2: 我這價可是說一不二。

column1: I have already made this potion for you.  I have no more ingredients with which to make another.
column2: 這瓶藥水我已經幫你調好了。我也沒有材料再調一瓶了。

column1: If you will but bring me the ingredients, I should be happy to make the potion for you at no charge.
column2: 只要你把材料帶來給我,我很樂意免費替你調這瓶藥水。

column1: To make a dispel potion, I will need the feather of a Griffin, and let me think,... ah, that may be a problem.  I need the "Fruit of Compassion."
column2: 要製作解咒藥水，我需要一根獅鷲的羽毛，讓我想想……啊，這可能有點麻煩。我還需要「慈悲之果」。

column1: And just what is it you are bargaining for?
column2: 你到底是在跟我討價還價什麼？

column1: You need to add the victim's hair to the potion I made for you just before you give it to the victim.  It must be freshly collected from the victim.
column2: 在你把藥水交給受害者之前的那一刻,得先把受害者的頭髮加進我替你調的藥水裡。而且那頭髮必須是剛從受害者身上採下的。

column1: I suggest you do not touch what you do not know.  The wrong drug could easily be fatal.
column2: 我勸你別碰不熟的東西。下錯藥可是很容易要命的。

column1: If you need something, I'd be pleased to sell it to you.
column2: 你要是需要什麼,我很樂意賣給你。

column1: Most strange.  If you have an upset stomach, perhaps you should buy a Healing Pill.
column2: 真是奇怪。要是你鬧肚子，或許該買顆治療藥丸。

column1: That was in exceptionally poor taste.
column2: 那實在是低級得可以。

column1: Forgive me, but I have no time for such a thing.
column2: 請見諒,我沒空理會這種事。

column1: You take the money and put it away.
column2: 你拿了錢收了起來。

column1: Since you put it that way, I guess I will just have to give you the Powder of Burning.
column2: 既然你這麼說了，我想我也只能把燃燒之粉給你了。

column1: Be very careful with it.  You need to remember to be as far from the target as you can, or you might be damaged by the reaction.
column2: 用它時務必小心。你得記得離目標越遠越好,否則可能被那反應波及。

column1: It will probably take all the powder I can give you to defeat such a thing as the Earth Elemental.  Do not waste it, and please do not miss when you throw it.
column2: 要擊敗土元素精靈這種東西,大概得用上我能給你的全部粉末。別浪費,擲的時候也千萬別失手。

column1: Please tell me what happens when you use it.  I'm sorry I can't be there, but I have much work to do.
column2: 請告訴我你用它時發生了什麼。抱歉我不能在場,實在有太多活兒要忙。

column1: You pick up the powder of burning and put it away.
column2: 你撿起燃燒之粉收了起來。

column1: Ah, I did not notice you come in.
column2: 啊,我沒注意到你進來。

column1: Have you seen the Fire Elemental?  It is magic of the most marvelous kind.  So very beautiful, the dancing flames.  If I but had such a creature at my command, the pills I would make!
column2: 你見過火元素精靈嗎?那是最奇妙的一種魔法。那舞動的火焰,美極了。我若能差遣這麼一頭生物,做得出的藥丸可不得了!

column1: I have heard that it was you that vanquished the Fire Elemental.  How woundrously brave you must be.
column2: 我聽說擊敗火元素的人就是你。你想必是何等的英勇非凡。

column1: I have heard that it was you that vanquished the Elemental of Air as well as of Flame.  Perhaps you now need to know something about the Element of Earth.
column2: 我聽說風與火兩頭元素精靈都是你擊敗的。或許你現在該了解些關於土元素的事了。

column1: I have heard a rumor that you are taking a caravan to Raseir.  It would be best if you purchased some extra pills to take with you.
column2: 我聽聞你要搭商隊去拉希爾。你最好多買些藥丸隨身帶著。

column1: This is the Apothecary, where one can buy many wondrous pills and oils.  I am Harik.  If you need something, just let me know.
column2: 這裡是藥舖,各種神奇的藥丸與藥油都買得到。我是哈里克。有什麼需要,儘管說。

column1: Welcome back.  If you see something which interests you, let me know.
column2: 歡迎回來。要是看到什麼中意的東西，儘管跟我說一聲。

column1: Watch this.
column2: 看好了。

column1: See how the flame reacts to incense?  Fire is attracted to the things it burns because it is very vain.
column2: 瞧瞧火焰是怎麼回應焚香的?火會被它所燃燒的東西吸引,因為它很愛慕虛榮。

column1: See how the earth is consumed by the flames!  Pretty amazing, isn't it?  I'm sure there's a lesson to be learned from this, although I don't quite know what that might be.
column2: 瞧瞧泥土是怎麼被烈焰吞噬的!很驚人吧?我敢說這裡頭一定有什麼道理可學,雖然我也說不上來究竟是什麼。

column1: It would be very possible to lure the Fire Elemental away from the plazas using this incense, and where it is weak from hunger and water, capture it.
column2: 用這薰香把火元素精靈引離廣場,再趁牠因飢渴而虛弱時將牠捕獲,大有可為。

column1: You know, incense would make quite a lure for a Fire Elemental.
column2: 你說,薰香拿來引火元素精靈倒是挺管用。

column1: As you have discovered, incense is quite a lure for Fire Elementals.
column2: 你已經發現:薰香對火元素精靈很有吸引力。

column1: Maybe you could use some magical fire to burn magical earth?  I do have some Powder of Burning that might work.  But it is very rare, and it makes such pretty flames!  I'd hate to have it wasted.
column2: 或許你可以用魔法之火來燒魔法之土？我確實有些燃燒之粉，或許能派上用場。不過它非常稀有，而且燃燒起來的火焰美極了！我實在不忍心讓它白白浪費。

column1: Now I must get back to my work.  If you see something which interests you, let me know.
column2: 我現在得回去工作了。要是看到什麼中意的東西，儘管跟我說一聲。

column1: The pills you request are a mere %d dinars.
column2: 閣下要的這些藥丸,區區 %d 第納爾而已。

column1: Because you are such a fine person, for you I will make a special deal.  I will sell you three doses of pills at regular price, and throw in a fourth dose free of charge.
column2: 因為你是個大好人，我就特別給你一個優惠。三劑藥丸照原價賣你，再免費送你第四劑。

column1: The incense will cost you but the price of my ingredients, a mere 70 centimes.
column2: 這香只收你材料費就好，才70分幣而已。

column1: Very well.  If it will save the city, you may take the incense.
column2: 好吧。要是這能拯救這座城,焚香就拿去吧。

column1: Very well.  You may take the incense.
column2: 好吧。焚香你拿去吧。

column1: The oil will cost you 20 centimes.
column2: 這油會花你20分幣。

column1: You speak as one born to bargain.  It shall be 15 centimes.
column2: 你這口才,簡直是天生來討價還價的。就算 15 生丁吧。

column1: I still need the "Fruit of Compassion."  You need to add the victim's hair at the last moment.
column2: 我還需要「慈悲之果」。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻加進去。

column1: I still need the feather of the Griffin.  You need to add the victim's hair at the last moment.
column2: 我還需要那根獅鷲羽毛。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻加進去。

column1: I am in awe of your bravery that you have defeated so many of theses fearful monsters, but I am presently overstocked with scorpion venom, and I can purchase no more.
column2: 你擊敗了這麼多可怕的怪物，你的英勇讓我敬佩不已，但我目前的蠍毒存貨已經過多，沒辦法再收購了。

column1: My, you are the brave and fortunate one!  I am now almost completely stocked with scorpion venom.  I will purchase one more scorpion tail.
column2: 哎呀,你還真是又勇敢又走運!我的蠍毒如今幾乎備齊了。我再收一條蠍尾。

column1: Ah, wonderful.  I was almost out of Poison Cure Pills.  Now I can make up some more.  Here is your reward of %d dinars.
column2: 啊，太好了。我的解毒藥丸快要用完了，現在可以再多做一些了。這是你的報酬，%d第納爾。

column1: My spine shivers at the thought of so much battle with the fiendish Ghouls, but despite your bravery, I regret that my larder of Ghoul claws is full, and I can purchase no more.
column2: 一想到要跟那些兇殘的食屍鬼這般廝殺,我就脊背發涼。可儘管你英勇過人,很遺憾,我儲爪的庫房已經滿了,沒法再收。

column1: Your valor in the face of the dreaded Ghouls is impressive.  My supply of claws is almost complete, but I will yet purchase a few more.
column2: 你面對可怕的食屍鬼時那份英勇,著實令人佩服。我的爪子快收齊了,但還願意再收幾隻。

column1: Now I can continue with my experiments.  A thousand thanks for these, and may this reward of %d dinars gladden your heart.
column2: 這下我又能繼續做實驗了。為這些東西,千恩萬謝,願這 %d 第納爾賞金能讓你開心。

column1: You take an oil-filled lamp.
column2: 你拿了一盞盛滿油的燈。

column1: I'm sorry, but I can only accept the coins of Shapeir.  You will need to first see the Money Changer.
column2: 很抱歉，我只收夏皮爾的錢幣。你得先去找錢幣兌換商。

column1: I'm afraid you do not have enough money for the purchase.
column2: 恐怕你的錢不夠買這個。

column1: There is a special money-back guarantee with every pill I sell.  If you die because of a pill, I will gladly refund your money.
column2: 我賣的每一顆藥丸都附帶特別的退款保證。要是你因為某顆藥丸而送了命,我很樂意把錢退給你。

column1: A potion to break enchantments?  I know of one in which the victim drinks the potion and the spell ends.
column2: 破除魔咒的藥水?我聽過一種——只要中咒的人喝下去,法術便會解除。

column1: It is a pity that I do not have any of the potion made up.  I have most of the ingredients, though.  If you could find the ingredients I am missing, then I could make some dispel potions for you.
column2: 可惜我手頭沒有調好的藥水。不過材料我大半都有。你若能找來我缺的那些材料,我就能替你調幾瓶驅散藥水。

column1: To make a dispel potion, I will need the feather of a Griffin, and, let me think,... ah, that may be a problem.  I need the "Fruit of Compassion."
column2: 要製作解咒藥水，我需要一根獅鷲的羽毛，讓我想想……啊，這可能有點麻煩。我還需要「慈悲之果」。

column1: I cannot make the potion without all the ingredients.
column2: 材料不齊,我這藥水就調不成。

column1: I will need a Griffin's feather and the "Fruit of Compassion".  You must add the hair of the victim yourself at the last moment.  It must be fresh.
column2: 我需要一根獅鷲的羽毛和「慈悲之果」。你必須在最後一刻親自加入受害者的毛髮，而且必須是新鮮的。

column1: Oh yes.  Have you had a chance to use it yet?  I would be most anxious to hear how it worked.
column2: 噢,對了。你有機會用上它了嗎?我可急著想知道它效果如何呢。

column1: You give the Fruit of Compassion and the Griffin Feather to Harik.
column2: 你把慈悲之果和獅鷲羽毛交給哈里克。

column1: This will only take me a second.
column2: 這只要花我一點時間。

column1: There.  I do for you what I would not for another.  This is a gift for all you have done for the city.
column2: 好了。我為你做的,是別人求都求不來的。這是給你的禮物,謝你為這座城所做的一切。

column1: I have made up three potions from what you gave me.
column2: 我用你給的東西調出了三瓶藥水。

column1: Remember to have the victim drink the potion in order to break the spell.
column2: 記得讓受害者喝下藥水,才能解除咒語。

column1: You will need to remember to add a freshly collected sample of the victim's hair before the victim drinks it.
column2: 你得記住,在受害者喝下之前,加入一撮新採集的受害者頭髮。

column1: You take the three potion bottles and put them away.
column2: 你拿了那三瓶藥水收了起來。

column1: Now, please excuse me.  I really must get back to my work.
column2: 好了,失陪了。我真的得回去幹活兒了。

column1: But I have already given the dispel potion to you.
column2: 可是驅散藥水我已經給過你了。

column1: If you can but get me the Griffin's feather, I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能替我弄來獅鷲的羽毛,我就把藥水給你。

column1: If you can but get me the "Fruit of Compassion", I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能幫我弄到「慈悲之果」，我就會把藥水給你。

column1: If you can but get me the Griffin's feather and the "Fruit of Compassion", I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能幫我弄到獅鷲的羽毛和「慈悲之果」，我就會把藥水給你。

column1: I'm not sure what you wish to capture.
column2: 我不太明白你想捕捉的是什麼。

column1: It's a scale, without which Harik would be unbalanced.
column2: 這是一副秤，沒有它哈里克可就要失衡了。

column1: You can't get a weigh with that. You'd get pounded.
column2: 這你可矇混不過去(秤不過去)。你會被搗成肉泥的。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as %s
column2: 這裡似乎有許多種語言的書籍，你能看懂的沒有幾本，不過你倒是認出了幾本熟悉的老書，例如%s

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects.  You see something that looks like %s.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的物品。你看見一樣東西，看起來像是%s。

column1: My knowledge of such things is scarce.
column2: 我對這類事情所知甚少。

column1: It is not in my power to give you the answer.
column2: 這個答案,可不在我能力所及的範圍內。

column1: This city and land are the very heart of the world.
column2: 這座城、這片土地,正是世界的心臟所在。

column1: When the old Emir was overthrown, the Apothecary of Raseir was taken over by the new Emir.  If you plan to go to Raseir, it might be wise to take some extra pills just in case such things are no longer available there.
column2: 老埃米爾被推翻後,拉希爾的藥鋪就被新埃米爾接管了。要是你打算去拉希爾,多帶些藥丸或許是明智之舉,以防那邊再也買不到這些東西。

column1: Such magical creatures are capable of actions both good and evil, much like mankind.
column2: 這類魔法生物善惡兩端的行徑都做得出來,跟人類差不了多少。

column1: Fire glowing in the very semblance of a Man.  A marvel to behold, and yet a deadly peril, as well.  To understand the Fire Elemental, you must know the Flame.
column2: 熊熊烈焰化作人形之貌。既是奇觀，亦是致命危機。要理解火元素，你必須先了解火焰本身。

column1: I do not deal much with water, so I have little I can tell you.
column2: 我跟水打的交道不多,能告訴你的也有限。

column1: The nature of air eludes me.  I think the keeper of the Magic Shop understands it better than I.
column2: 風的本質我參不透。我想魔法店的店主比我更懂這個。

column1: The Elemental of Earth is a thing of vast destructive capabilities.  In my studies, I have found that to master something, you must understand it.  What is Earth?  That is what you need to know.
column2: 土元素是一種擁有龐大破壞力的存在。以我的研究所得，要駕馭一樣東西，必須先理解它。什麼是大地？這正是你需要弄明白的事。

column1: The Elements of Earth, Air, Water, and Fire are the essence of the universe.  It is said that certain Users of Magic can give the semblance of life to an element, unleashing the destructive side of its nature.
column2: 土、風、水、火四大元素乃是宇宙的本質。據說某些法師能賦予元素生命的模樣，釋放出其本性中具破壞力的一面。

column1: Many parts of monsters are usable.  I use some for pill components.
column2: 怪物身上有許多部位都派得上用場。我會拿一些來當藥丸的成分。

column1: I know only the Magic required to do my work.  You should ask across the plaza for a better answer.
column2: 我只懂做我這行所需的魔法。你該到廣場對面去問，才能得到更好的答案。

column1: Even the Sultan, (May Peace Be Always With Him), has often used my pills.
column2: 就連蘇丹（願他常享太平！）也經常服用我的藥丸。

column1: The new Emir, it has been said, uses drugs of the darkest nature -- the ones which poison the mind and soul.
column2: 據說新埃米爾用的是最陰暗的藥物——那種會毒害心智與靈魂的東西。

column1: You can see the Palace of the Sultan over its gates east of here.
column2: 往這兒以東,你能在城門那頭望見蘇丹的宮殿。

column1: The desert is a wonderful source of components for my pills.
column2: 沙漠是我藥丸成分的絕佳來源。

column1: The prices I charge but reflect the cost of materials.
column2: 我開的價,不過是反映原料的成本罷了。

column1: I have a reward notice posted there.  I pay for pill components.
column2: 我在那兒貼了一張懸賞告示。我會付錢收購藥丸成分。

column1: The Inn, I think, is in a plaza to the south of here.
column2: 我想旅店是在這裡南方的一處廣場上。

column1: I have no idea.  I have been inside all day.
column2: 我哪知道。我一整天都待在店裡。

column1: The Fountain is the source of water for the city.
column2: 噴泉是這座城市的水源。

column1: I have been told that the teeth of a dragon are very useful for components, but I have never been fortunate enough to find any.
column2: 有人跟我說,龍牙拿來當成分非常有用,可惜我從沒那個運氣弄到過。

column1: Her shop is west of us, at the end of Dinar Tarik.
column2: 她的店在我們以西,第納爾街的盡頭。

column1: I consult him occasionally for good days to make pills.  He is near the end of Tarik of Stars.
column2: 我偶爾會找他算算製藥的好日子。他就住在繁星街盡頭附近。

column1: The Enchantress Aziza has a house along the west side of Shmali Tarik.
column2: 女術士阿姿莎在希瑪利街西側有一棟房子。

column1: I sell pills to fix your ills.  Catchy phrase, is it not?
column2: 我賣藥丸,治你百般。這口號朗朗上口,是不是?

column1: The Kattas are very similar to us in physiology.
column2: 卡塔族在生理構造上跟我們非常相似。

column1: I am Harik Attar, which in your language means, "Fire Druggist."  I was well named.
column2: 我是哈里克·阿塔爾,用你們的話來說,意思就是「火藥師」。這名字取得真貼切。

column1: This incense is made from the finest Frankincense.  It should attract the flame as the moth is attracted to it.
column2: 這香是用最上等的乳香製成的。它應該能像飛蛾撲火般吸引火焰。

column1: It is only 70 centimes.
column2: 只要70分幣。

column1: Powder of Burning is a really excellent mixture of Potassium Nitrate from bat guano, powdered charcoal, sulfur, and ambergris.  The ambergris doesn't really do anything, but it makes the powder smell more interesting.
column2: 燃燒之粉是一種絕佳的混合物，成分包括取自蝙蝠糞的硝酸鉀、木炭粉、硫磺，還有龍涎香。龍涎香其實沒什麼實際作用，只是讓這粉末聞起來更有意思。

column1: All you have to do is to throw it at the Earth Elemental.  It makes such a lovely flame when it explodes.
column2: 你只管把它往土元素精靈身上一扔就成。它炸開時,那火花可漂亮得很。

column1: Throw it quickly at your target.  Try not to miss.  It will do a lot of damage when it strikes and ignites.
column2: 快把它擲向目標,別失手。它擊中並炸開時威力可不小。

column1: I do not usually make potions unless they are special orders.  I make pills that do wonderful things.  However, I do have a recipe for a Dispel Potion.
column2: 除非是特別訂製,我平常是不調藥水的。我做的是功效奇妙的藥丸。不過,驅散藥水的配方我倒是有一份。

column1: Mana pills are very useful for those with Magical abilities.  They restore the magical energy used in spells.
column2: 法力藥丸對有魔法能力的人非常有用，能恢復施法所消耗的魔力。

column1: Vigor pills aid iron-poor blood, restore vital minerals used up in strenuous exercise, and act as quick picker-uppers in times of stress.
column2: 活力藥丸有助於改善缺鐵性貧血，補充劇烈運動所消耗的重要礦物質，並在壓力時刻能迅速提振精神。

column1: Healing pills accelerate the normal healing of the body.  A hero such as yourself will undoubtedly need a large supply.
column2: 治療藥丸能加速身體的自然癒合。像你這樣的英雄，想必需要大量的庫存。

column1: The venom of the desert scorpion is deadly unless you take these pills before you are struck by its tail.
column2: 沙漠蠍子的毒液可是致命的——除非你在被牠的尾刺扎到之前,先吃下這些藥丸。

column1: I have the following pills for sale:  Vigor pills for 5 Dinars.  Healing pills for 10 Dinars.  Poison Cure Pills for 12 Dinars.  Mana pills for 15 Dinars.   Take one pill with water and get plenty of rest.
column2: 我這裡有以下藥丸出售：活力藥丸5第納爾。治療藥丸10第納爾。解毒藥丸12第納爾。法力藥丸15第納爾。服用時請配水，並充分休息。

column1: Pills tend to stick in your throat if you don't drink plenty of water.
column2: 藥丸若不配著大量的水吞下,可是很容易卡在喉嚨裡的。

column1: The Fruit of Compassion is extremely rare.  It can only be given, never taken or bought.  I have heard rumors of but one Plant in the entire land where you might find this.
column2: 慈悲之果極為稀有。它只能被贈與，無法被奪取或購買。我聽說整片大地上，僅有一株植物可能找得到它。

column1: There are tales told to me of a woman transformed into a plant through a Djinni's spell.  She stands like a tree somewhere out in the desert, but it is said she bears no fruit.
column2: 有人對我說起一個女子被鎮尼的法術變成植物的故事。她像棵樹般佇立在沙漠某處,但據說她不結果。

column1: You should talk to the Enchantress Aziza, for she would know more about this than I.
column2: 你該去找女術士阿姿莎談談,這事她比我清楚。

column1: I am always out of Scorpion Venom to make Poison Cure Pills and Ghoul claws for my experiments.  I will pay twenty dinars for the tail of a scorpion, and fifteen dinars for the claws of a Ghoul.
column2: 我製作解毒藥丸用的蠍毒，還有做實驗用的食屍鬼爪，總是不夠用。蠍子的尾巴我出二十第納爾收購，食屍鬼的爪子出十五第納爾。

column1: The sting of the scorpion's tail is death, unless you take my Poison Cure Pills.  Unfortunately, even they will not help if you are struck by the tail more than once.
column2: 蠍尾一螫足以致命，除非你服用我的解毒藥丸。可惜的是，要是被螫超過一次，就連藥丸也救不了你。

column1: I do not recommend the use of drugs.  They can be very dangerous.  MY pills, now...they are not drugs.  They are a mixture of all natural ingredients (no artificial colors, flavors, or preservatives), and Magic.
column2: 我不建議使用藥物，那可能非常危險。不過我的藥丸嘛……那些可不是藥物。它們是純天然成分的混合物（不含人工色素、香料或防腐劑），再加上魔法。

column1: The griffin is half bird and half beast.  It has the head and wings of an eagle and the body of a lion.  Since it is neither wholly one nor another, it can be used as a catalyst for change.
column2: 獅鷲是半鳥半獸的生物，擁有鷹的頭與翼，以及獅的軀體。正因為牠既非純粹的這一種，也非純粹的那一種，所以能作為變化的催化劑。

column1: The hair is the last thing added to the Potion just before you give it to the enchanted victim.  As the hair of the enchanted creature dissolves in the potion, so too shall the spell dissolve when the victim drinks the potion.
column2: 毛髮是在把藥水交給受咒受害者之前，最後加入的東西。當受咒生物的毛髮在藥水中溶解，受害者飲下藥水時，咒語也將隨之消解。

column1: The hair must be freshly pulled.  That is important.
column2: 那頭髮必須是剛拔下來的。這點很重要。

column1: I am always in need of certain components for my work.  If you are brave enough to bring some of these components to me, I am more than willing to pay you for them.
column2: 我做事總是少不了某些素材。你要是膽子夠大,願意替我弄些來,我也十分樂意付你錢。

column1: The Ghoul is a creature of the night.  The scrape of its claws chills the soul and draws the very life away from you.  It takes a very agile adventurer to avoid death at its hands.
column2: 食屍鬼是夜行的生物。牠利爪一刮,寒透人的靈魂,還會把你的生命力一點一點抽乾。得是身手極為敏捷的冒險者,才能逃過喪命於牠爪下的命運。

column1: I am trying to develop a pill which will protect against the effect of the claws of the Ghoul.  I have not been able to get many claws, for few Ghouls are willing to give them to me, and few adventurers are willing to get them for me.
column2: 我正試著研發一種抵禦食屍鬼爪子效力的藥丸。可惜爪子我弄不到多少——願意給的食屍鬼沒幾隻,願意替我去取的冒險者也沒幾個。

column1: I think you could capture a Fire Elemental in something designed to hold flames, such as a lamp.
column2: 我想,你可以用某種專門盛裝火焰的東西來捕捉火元素,好比說……一盞燈。

column1: I sell oil for filling lamps and for quieting squeaky hinges.  A small container of oil costs 20 centimes.
column2: 我賣的油可以拿來加滿油燈，也能讓吱嘎作響的鉸鏈安靜下來。一小罐油要價20分幣。

column1: I get my lamps from the Brass Seller at the Bazaar.
column2: 我的油燈是跟市集裡的黃銅商買來的。

column1: Do you need one to capture the Fire Elemental?  I can let you have this one.
column2: 你需要一個來捕捉火元素精靈嗎?這個可以讓給你。

column1: This is the Apothecary, where one can buy many wondrous pills.  I am Harik.  If you need something, just let my know.
column2: 這裡是藥鋪，可以買到許多神奇的藥丸。我是哈里克。有需要的話儘管跟我說。

column1: You can use my marvelous pills to heal your wounds.
column2: 你可以用我這了不起的藥丸來治療傷口。

column1: Mana is magical energy.  My Mana Pills help restore it.
column2: 法力就是魔法能量。我的法力藥丸能幫助你恢復它。

column1: The things you see here are mine and not for sale.  Only my pills are for sale.
column2: 你在這裡看到的東西都是我的，不賣。只有我的藥丸才有在賣。

column1: You close the trap door.
column2: 你關上了活板門。

column1: You open the trap door to reveal a hole in the floor.  You can see Issur's strongbox inside the hole.
column2: 你打開活板門，露出地板上的一個洞。你能看見洞裡有伊瑟的保險箱。

column1: Hey!  You don't have the money for this.  What do you think this is, a charity?  Get some money or get lost!
column2: 喂!你根本付不起。你當這裡是慈善機構嗎?有錢再來,不然滾遠點!

column1: Wait a minute!  What do you think I am, a money changer?  Your money is no good.  You need to get this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buy anything.
column2: 等一下！你把我當成什麼，錢幣兌換商嗎？你的錢在這裡不管用。你得先把它換成第納爾和分幣，才能買東西。

column1: Ha!  Easiest 10 dinars I ever made.  Let me know if you want to try again.
column2: 哈！這是我賺過最輕鬆的10第納爾。要是還想再試一次，儘管跟我說。

column1: Ha!  Easiest way I know of to get 20 dinars.  Let me know if you want to try again.
column2: 哈！這是我知道最輕鬆賺到20第納爾的辦法。要是還想再試一次，儘管跟我說。

column1: Aw right.  Anyone can get lucky.  Here's your dough.
column2: 好吧好吧。誰都有走運的時候。錢拿去。

column1: Thanks, pal.  Easy money.
column2: 謝啦,老兄。輕鬆賺。

column1: There are swords, shields and many other weapons on the wall.  This shop seems well equipped to do business.
column2: 牆上掛著劍、盾牌，還有許多其他武器。這間店看起來備貨齊全，做生意很像一回事。

column1: There's no need to throw things around an empty shop.
column2: 沒必要在一家空店裡亂扔東西。

column1: Wasn't the purpose of breaking in to steal something?
column2: 闖進來不就是為了偷東西嗎？

column1: A pointless thought, since you do not have a sword to trade nor do you have the skill to use one.
column2: 想了也是白想，反正你既沒有劍可以交易，也沒有使劍的本事。

column1: A finer sword cannot be had than the one you've already purchased.
column2: 沒有比你已經買下的這把更好的劍了。

column1: Issur is not willing to change his price on his daggers.
column2: 伊瑟不願意在匕首的價格上讓步。

column1: You have the bellows.  What more do you want?
column2: 你已經有風箱了，還想要什麼？

column1: You must need my bellows badly.
column2: 你一定是很需要我的風箱。

column1: Well, since you put it that way, I guess you do need the bellows.  I'll get it for you.
column2: 好吧，既然你這麼說了，我想你確實需要這風箱。我這就去幫你拿。

column1: OK, OK.  Since you are such a wimp, you only have to pay me 10 dinars if you lose at arm wrestling.
column2: 好吧好吧。看你這麼孬,腕力輸了你只要付我十個第納爾就好。

column1: Take it or leave it.
column2: 要就拿去，不要就算了。

column1: You told me the story already, and besides, you have the bellows.  What else do you want?
column2: 這故事你已經跟我說過了，而且你已經有風箱了，還想要什麼？

column1: You have told me that story already!
column2: 這故事你已經跟我講過啦!

column1: Do tell.  Well, if you want to use my bellows, then you've got to prove your worth.  You'll have to defeat me at Arm Wrestling.  You win, you get the bellows.  You lose, I get 20 dinars.
column2: 說來聽聽。好吧，如果你想用我的風箱，就得證明你的實力。你得在比腕力上打贏我。你贏了，風箱就是你的。你輸了，我拿20第納爾。

column1: That's a nice speech impediment you have there, but I don't know what you're saying.
column2: 你這口齒不清的毛病還真特別,可惜我聽不懂你在講啥。

column1: Big deal.
column2: 了不起喔。

column1: There's no one to wrestle with.  Wasn't the purpose of breaking in to steal something?
column2: 這裡沒人可以跟你比腕力。闖進來不就是為了偷東西嗎？

column1: Go away.  I've got better things to do than waste my time on you.
column2: 走開。我可沒空浪費在你身上。

column1: It will cost you another 20 dinar to wrestle me for the bellows.
column2: 想再跟我比腕力搶風箱，還得再付20第納爾。

column1: It'll cost you more to bet me this time.  You'll have to put down %d Dinars.
column2: 這次跟我打賭要花更多錢。你得下注%d第納爾。

column1: You want to wrestle, eh?  Just to make it worth my while to put you in your place, why don't we make a little wager, say, 1 dinar?  Put down your money and we'll play.
column2: 想比腕力是吧?既然要我費神教訓你,不如下個小注,就一個第納爾如何?把錢押上,咱們就開始。

column1: Yeah, whaddya want?
column2: 幹嘛,你想怎樣?

column1: The strongbox is much too heavy to move.
column2: 這保險箱太重了,搬不動。

column1: You see no strongBox here.
column2: 你這裡沒看到保險箱。

column1: Issur's weapons and armor are very distinctive.  You would not be able to use or sell them without being caught.
column2: 伊蘇爾的武器和盔甲特徵太鮮明。你要是拿去用或拿去賣,鐵定會被逮個正著。

column1: Unfortunately, it appears that someone has already stolen all of its contents.  Now who would have done such a thing?
column2: 可惜,看來裡頭的東西早就被人偷個精光了。到底是誰幹的好事呢?

column1: You find 100 dinars and 500 centimes, which you happily put away.
column2: 你找到了一百第納爾和五百生丁,樂呵呵地收進了口袋。

column1: You close and lock the strongbox.
column2: 你把保險箱關上並鎖好。

column1: You got it!  The chest is now open.
column2: 搞定!箱子打開了。

column1: You'll have to get the anvil out of the way first.
column2: 你得先把鐵砧挪開才行。

column1: There's something heavy preventing the trap door from opening...
column2: 有個很重的東西擋住了活板門，讓它打不開……

column1: Oh, it's you!  Stand somewhere else.
column2: 噢,是你啊!站到別的地方去。

column1: The anvil is so heavy you can't even budge it.  Better work on those muscle-building exercises.
column2: 這鐵砧重得你連動都動不了。看來你得多練練肌肉了。

column1: You've returned the anvil to its original position to hide your illicit activity.
column2: 你把鐵砧搬回原位,好掩蓋你那見不得人的勾當。

column1: You move the anvil aside.  Now you can get to the trapdoor.
column2: 你把鐵砧挪到一旁。現在搆得著活板門了。

column1: All right, you can have the stupid bellows.  See if I care.
column2: 行了行了,那破風箱給你就是了。我才不在乎呢。

column1: He gets the bellows down for you and throws them at you.
column2: 他替你把風箱取了下來,然後朝你扔了過來。

column1: Having traded in your old sword and paid 100 dinars, you now have one of Issur's swords.  For all his bragging, he does make a fine weapon.
column2: 你用舊劍抵價並付了100第納爾，現在你有了一把伊蘇爾的劍。雖然他老是吹噓，但這劍確實是把好武器。

column1: With your skill in fast talking, you manage to bargain him down to 80 dinars.  Having traded in your old sword and paid the money, you now have one of Issur's swords.  For all his bragging, he does make a fine weapon.
column2: 憑著你的三寸不爛之舌，你成功把價錢殺到80第納爾。你用舊劍抵價付了錢，現在你有了一把伊蘇爾的劍。雖然他老是吹噓，但這劍確實是把好武器。

column1: He seemed thoroughly unimpressed by your bargaining skill.  Having traded in your old sword and paid 100 dinars, you now have one of Issur's swords.  For all his bragging, he does make a fine weapon.
column2: 他似乎對你的殺價功夫完全不買帳。你用舊劍抵價並付了100第納爾，現在你有了一把伊蘇爾的劍。雖然他老是吹噓，但這劍確實是把好武器。

column1: Issur seems to make a well-balanced dagger, even if he does make you pay for the quality.
column2: 伊蘇爾打的匕首手感平衡得很,雖說這品質得讓你掏不少錢。

column1: You have shown yourself worthy by defeating me at arm wrestling.  Let me get them for you.
column2: 你在腕力上贏了我,證明了自己的本事。我去拿給你。

column1: He gets the bellows down for you and tosses them to you.
column2: 他把風箱取下來丟給你。

column1: You need to have the money before you go wagering!
column2: 要下注,先得有錢才行!

column1: I don't have any use for foreign coin, go see the money changer.
column2: 我這裡不收外國錢幣，去找兌幣商吧。

column1: Silly you, the door is already open.
column2: 你真傻,這門早就開著了。

column1: Open the door if you want out.
column2: 想出去的話就把門打開。

column1: Leave the door alone!
column2: 別碰那扇門!

column1: It's open already.
column2: 已經是開著的了。

column1: It's closed already.
column2: 已經是關著的了。

column1: Issur's strongbox is in this hole in the floor.
column2: 伊蘇爾的保險箱就在地板上的這個洞裡。

column1: Not much point in that!  The chest is already open.
column2: 這麼做沒啥意義!箱子早就開著了。

column1: You don't have the skill or the tools.
column2: 你既沒有這門技術，也沒有工具。

column1: The lock is too tricky for you.  You'll have to keep practicing.
column2: 這鎖對你來說太刁鑽了,你還得多練練。

column1: Not in the middle of the day!
column2: 大白天的可不行!

column1: The strongbox is already open.
column2: 保險箱已經是開著的了。

column1: You can't open the strongbox.  It's locked.
column2: 你打不開這保險箱,它上鎖了。

column1: The strongbox is already closed.
column2: 保險箱已經是關著的了。

column1: The small wooden trap door is now accessible.
column2: 這扇小木製活板門現在可以碰到了。

column1: You can just see the small wooden trap door underneath the anvil.
column2: 你勉強可以看到鐵砧底下的小木製活板門。

column1: The trap door is securely attached to the floor.
column2: 活板門牢牢固定在地板上。

column1: The trap door is already open.
column2: 活板門已經是開著的了。

column1: The trap door is already closed.
column2: 活板門已經是關著的了。

column1: You don't think Issur would appreciate that.
column2: 你看伊蘇爾可不會領情。

column1: That's funny -- your Open spell doesn't seem to work on an already open door.  Oh, yeah, that's right -- it isn't supposed to.  You resolve to be more diligent about your studies in the future.
column2: 真奇怪──你的「開啟」法術似乎對已經開著的門不起作用。喔對，這才是對的──它本來就不該起作用。你下定決心以後要更用功唸書。

column1: The anvil is oiled.
column2: 鐵砧抹好油了。

column1: An interesting idea, but you don't have any oil.
column2: 這主意不錯，可惜你沒有油。

column1: Try going over to the anvil first.
column2: 先走到鐵砧那邊看看。

column1: You spread some oil around the base of the anvil.
column2: 你在鐵砧底座四周抹了些油。

column1: You surreptitiously spread some oil around the base of the anvil, making sure Issur isn't watching.
column2: 你趁伊蘇爾沒注意,偷偷在鐵砧底座周圍抹上了油。

column1: You can't move the anvil -- the trap door is in your way.
column2: 你搬不動鐵砧──活板門擋住了。

column1: The anvil is in its proper place.
column2: 鐵砧就在它該在的位置。

column1: Issur's heavy iron anvil doubles as a table for arm-wrestling.
column2: 伊蘇爾那座沉重的鐵砧，同時也充當比腕力的桌子。

column1: You think it would take all your strength to even move the anvil.  Taking it is out of the question.
column2: 你覺得光是要搬動這座鐵砧就得用盡全身力氣，拿走它更是想都別想。

column1: The anvil, not being a door, is unaffected by your spell.
column2: 鐵砧又不是門,你的咒語對它起不了作用。

column1: You have the bellows.  What else do you want?
column2: 你已經有風箱了，你還想要什麼？

column1: You have the bellows.
column2: 你已經有風箱了。

column1: The bellows are not in here.
column2: 風箱不在這裡頭。

column1: Why would you need my bellows?  What's the story, bub?
column2: 你要我的風箱幹嘛?到底怎麼回事,老兄?

column1: You'll have to wrestle me for them.
column2: 你得跟我比腕力才能拿到。

column1: You'd better not.  Issur would get upset.
column2: 你最好別這麼做。伊蘇爾會發火的。

column1: Keep your distance!
column2: 離我遠一點!

column1: You'd better ask Issur about the weapons.
column2: 武器的事,你最好問伊蘇爾。

column1: The shop owner glares back at you.  He looks strong and decidedly unfriendly.
column2: 店主回瞪你一眼。他看起來壯實,而且擺明了不友善。

column1: He's too busy flexing to talk.
column2: 他忙著秀肌肉,沒空說話。

column1: That is very interesting.
column2: 這真是有意思。

column1: You talk much, but say little.
column2: 你話說了一堆，卻沒講出什麼名堂。

column1: Come over here and face me when you talk to me, wimp.
column2: 跟我說話就過來當面說,膽小鬼。

column1: The bellows is a sign of my trade.  What about it?
column2: 這風箱是我這行的招牌，你要問什麼？

column1: Hey, what are friends for?  Let me get them down for you.
column2: 嘿，朋友不就是幹這個用的嗎？我幫你拿下來。

column1: He gets the bellows down for you and you put it away.  Good thing your spell lasted long enough.
column2: 他把風箱取下來給你，你把它收好。還好你的法術效力撐得夠久。

column1: If you want my bellows for something, you've got to earn it.  If you beat me at arm wrestling, you can have it.  If I win, you give me 20 dinars.
column2: 你要是想拿我的風箱做什麼，就得靠本事贏來。你要是在比腕力贏了我，風箱就是你的；要是我贏了，你得給我20第納爾。

column1: No more questions.  Buy something or get out!
column2: 別再問了。要嘛買東西,要嘛出去!

column1: Issur continues to smile at you.
column2: 伊蘇爾繼續對你笑著。

column1: Issur seems much more mellow now.
column2: 伊蘇爾現在看起來和氣多了。

column1: The guards in Shapeir are nothing if not efficient.  You took too much time, and one of Shapeir's finest caught you in the act of this decidedly unheroic pursuit.
column2: 夏皮爾的衛兵辦事可一點都不含糊。你磨蹭得太久,被一名夏皮爾的精銳衛士當場逮個正著——幹這種毫不英雄的勾當。

column1: The guards seem to have been waiting for you.  Someone must have robbed this store recently.
column2: 守衛似乎一直在等你。這家店最近八成被人搶過。

column1: That does it!!
column2: 夠了！！

column1: You back again?  I'll let you stay if you want to buy something, but don't push it.
column2: 你又來啦?想買東西就讓你待著,別給我得寸進尺。

column1: What do you want now?
column2: 你又想幹嘛?

column1: Don't be coming over here!
column2: 別給我過來！

column1: The trophy seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這座獎盃似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: The shield seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這面盾牌似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: The shrine seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這座神龕似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The rack seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這個架子似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: The large mace seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這把大狼牙棒似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The axe seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這把斧頭似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The sword seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這把劍似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The scimitar seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這把彎刀似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The mace seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這把狼牙棒似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The Tisk tusk is firmly attached to the wall with Tisk tusk tacks.  Tsk, tsk!
column2: 提斯克獠牙被提斯克獠牙釘牢牢地釘在牆上。嘖,嘖!

column1: The sheath seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這個劍鞘似乎牢牢固定在牆上。

column1: The coat of mail is very heavy and too easily recognizable.  You decide to leave it alone.
column2: 這件鎖子甲又重又太顯眼,你決定別碰它。

column1: It seems to be located in the plaza, not in the shop.
column2: 它似乎是擺在廣場上,不在店裡頭。

column1: Go ask someone else.
column2: 去問別人吧。

column1: You think I got nothing better to do than answer stupid questions?  Go away.
column2: 你當我閒到只能回答蠢問題嗎?走開。

column1: This place has the best Weapon Shop in the world.
column2: 這裡是全世界最棒的武器店。

column1: The thing is magic.  You need a magic user, not a fighter.
column2: 那玩意兒是魔法。你得找法師,不是戰士。

column1: Now that's one tough sucker.
column2: 那傢伙還真是個硬骨頭。

column1: Magic is magic.  Go ask the weird guy at the Magic Shop.
column2: 魔法歸魔法。去問魔法鋪那個怪傢伙吧。

column1: If you want monsters, go out in the desert at night.  That's where real men get weapon practice, not with some wimpy dame at the Guild Hall.
column2: 想找怪物,晚上去沙漠就有。那才是真男人練武的地方,不是跟公會大廳裡那個孬婆娘磨蹭。

column1: The Sultan has some good men working for him.  Some of the guards are my best friends.
column2: 蘇丹手下有不少好漢。有些衛兵是我的死黨。

column1: The desert is where you separate the men from the wimps.
column2: 沙漠就是分辨真男人和孬種的地方。

column1: The stuff I sell is expensive, but you get what you pay for.
column2: 我賣的東西是貴了點，但一分錢一分貨。

column1: I don't like thieves.  The thief who breaks in here is in real trouble.
column2: 我討厭盜賊。膽敢闖進這裡的盜賊,有他好受的。

column1: That place is for sissies.  After all, it's run by a woman.
column2: 那地方是給娘娘腔待的。畢竟是個女人在管嘛。

column1: It's hot.  It's always hot.
column2: 熱。一直都這麼熱。

column1: I don't much like nosy little twerps.
column2: 我可不太喜歡愛管閒事的小屁孩。

column1: Nice dame.
column2: 不錯的妞兒。

column1: I am Issur of the House of Usher.
column2: 我是亞瑟府的伊蘇爾。

column1: I am Issur, and this is my weapon shop.  I make the best swords and daggers.  What do you want?
column2: 我是伊蘇爾，這是我的武器店。我打造的劍和匕首是最好的。你要什麼？

column1: Hero of the Kattas, huh?  Only a Katta would call you a hero.
column2: 卡塔族的英雄，是吧？也只有卡塔族才會叫你英雄。

column1: You again?  Come to taste defeat once more, no doubt.
column2: 又是你?八成是來再嘗一次落敗的滋味的吧。

column1: Ah, easy money,  So you want to make me rich?
column2: 啊，好賺的錢。你是想讓我發財嗎？

column1: Well, wimp, it's about time you dare to pit your puny skill against mine at arm wrestling.
column2: 喲,軟腳蝦,你總算敢拿你那點微末本事,來跟我比比腕力了。

column1: It's the fool and his money again.
column2: 又是這蠢蛋帶著他的錢來了。

column1: Ho, ho.  The beardless wonder has come to be put in his place again, I see.
column2: 呵,呵。看來這位沒長鬍子的小子又自己送上門來討教訓了。

column1: What a stubborn fool.  Just can't admit to yourself that I'm so much better than you at skills of strength.
column2: 真是個倔脾氣的蠢蛋。就是不肯承認論力氣這玩意兒,我比你強上一大截。

column1: Well, Curly Locks, time for your daily defeat.
column2: 喲,捲毛兄,又到了你每天認輸的時候啦。

column1: You know, we could save a lot of time if you just give me your money instead of losing it at Arm Wrestling.
column2: 你知道嗎，你要是直接把錢給我，而不是在比腕力時輸掉，能省下不少時間。

column1: I make the finest weapons in all Shapeir!
column2: 我打的是全夏皮爾最頂尖的武器!

column1: I can sell you the best daggers you've ever seen for only 5 dinars apiece.
column2: 我可以賣你這輩子見過最好的匕首，一把只要5第納爾。

column1: You need my sword.  A guy like you needs all the help you can get.  It will only cost you 100 dinars when you trade in your own sword.
column2: 你需要我的劍。像你這種人，能得到多少幫助都不嫌多。只要拿你自己的劍來抵，只要100第納爾。

column1: It takes real muscle to be good at Arm Wrestling.  I'm the best.  If you want to prove it, let me know and I'll be glad to wrestle you.
column2: 要比腕力厲害，得靠真本事。我是最強的。你要是想證明什麼，儘管跟我說一聲，我很樂意跟你比一場。

column1: You got to work out daily if you want to build muscles like mine.
column2: 想練出我這身肌肉,就得天天操練。

column1: If you Arm Wrestle with me, you got to expect to lose.
column2: 你要是跟我比腕力，就得有輸的心理準備。

column1: What's a wimp like you asking about EOF for?  Only real men join EOF.
column2: 你這種膽小鬼問永恆戰士團做什麼?只有真男人才進得了永恆戰士團。

column1: Paladins can fight good, but they're still wimpy goody-goodies and too high and mighty for EOF.
column2: 聖騎士打起架來還算有兩下子，但終究是軟弱的好好先生，太高高在上，不適合加入EOF。

column1: Those are my trophies from Arm Wrestling tournaments in Petalumeir.  They are not for sale.
column2: 那些是我在佩塔盧梅爾比腕力錦標賽贏來的獎盃，非賣品。

column1: That is sold to someone else.  They'll pick it up tomorrow.
column2: 那是賣給別人的。他們明天會來取。

column1: I work the metals there for my fine weapons.
column2: 我在那兒打鐵,做我那些好武器。

column1: That is sold to one of the Sultan's guards.  He picks it up today.
column2: 那是賣給蘇丹一個衛兵的。他今天要來取。

column1: It is the finest suit of mail in all the realm.  Its value is far beyond you.
column2: 這是全國最上乘的一副鎖子甲,它的價值遠非你所能企及。

column1: It's hot.  Don't touch it.
column2: 那很燙。別碰。

column1: Those bellows are my sign.  What about them?
column2: 那個風箱是我的招牌，你要問什麼？

column1: YOU
column2: 你

column1: ISSUR
column2: 伊蘇爾

column1: HOW TO PLAY  Enter key - holds back opponent SpaceBar - attempts slam LeftArrow - increases pressure applied RightArrow - decreases pressure applied
column2: 玩法說明　Enter鍵－頂住對手　空白鍵－嘗試將對手壓倒　左方向鍵－增加施力　右方向鍵－減少施力

column1: Ready!
column2: 預備！

column1: Set!
column2: 就位！

column1: GO!
column2: 開始！

column1: Your staying power diminished, Issur takes advantage of the opportunity to slam your hand to the anvil.
column2: 你的耐力一弱,伊蘇爾就趁機把你的手壓向鐵砧。

column1: You can feel Issur weaken from the great effort he has taken to overcome your staying power.  You take advantage of the opportunity to slam his hand to the anvil.
column2: 你感覺得到伊蘇爾為了壓制你的耐力已經筋疲力竭。你趁機把他的手壓倒在鐵砧上。

column1: That be my place to stay.  I think you shall not go there.
column2: 那是我住的地方。我看你還是別進去的好。

column1: That will be the place to see just how good a fighter you are.  I get tired of not using spear and shield for so long a while.  Maybe you and I will go check out skills there.
column2: 那裡正好能看出你身手到底有多好。長矛和盾牌擱著沒用這麼久,我都手癢了。或許咱倆可以去那兒切磋切磋。

column1: No magic, though.  I don't do magic.
column2: 不過不准用魔法。我不碰魔法。

column1: This Guild Hall is more colorful and primitive than others that you have seen.  As is the custom, artifacts are displayed on the walls, notices are posted on the Quest Board, and the emblem of the Eternal Order of Fighters is prominent.
column2: 這間公會大廳比你見過的其他公會大廳更加色彩繽紛、更顯原始。一如慣例，牆上展示著各種文物，任務告示板上貼滿了公告，永恆戰士團的徽章格外醒目。

column1: The open doorway leads to an area where we may practice.
column2: 這扇敞開的門通往一處可供我們練習的地方。

column1: Uhura has already given you the note.
column2: 烏胡拉已經把便條給你了。

column1: You have my sword, Hero.
column2: 你已經有我的劍了，英雄。

column1: I will keep my sword, thank you.
column2: 我的劍我自己留著，謝謝。

column1: Step over to the book if you wish to read it.
column2: 想讀這本書的話，走到它旁邊。

column1: The last entry is "%s, Hero of Spielburg."
column2: 最後一條條目寫著「%s,史畢柏格的英雄」。

column1: The log is for the most part written in a language you can't read.
column2: 這本記事簿絕大部分是用一種你看不懂的文字寫成的。

column1: Step over to the book if you wish to sign it.
column2: 想在這本書上簽名的話，走到它旁邊。

column1: You have already signed it.
column2: 你已經簽過名了。

column1: You sign your name in the book.
column2: 你在書上簽下自己的名字。

column1: You would probably anger her if you tried, and you don't want to see her mad.
column2: 你要真那麼做,八成會惹火她,而你可不會想看她發飆的樣子。

column1: One does not just join the Eternal Order of Fighters, nor is this the place if you could.
column2: 永恆戰士團可不是你說加入就能加入的,就算能,這兒也不是入團的地方。

column1: You will need the magic of a flaming sword to defeat the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你需要一把燃燒之劍的魔力,才能擊敗土元素精靈。

column1: Permit me to loan you my sword, "Soulforge."
column2: 讓我把我的劍「魂鑄」借給你。

column1: When you are in battle against the Elemental, the flame of the sword will aid you.
column2: 當你與元素精靈交戰時,劍上的火焰會助你一臂之力。

column1: It will flame for the battle only, for it draws its strength from mine.
column2: 它只在戰鬥時燃燒,因為它的力量取自於我。

column1: Please leave the sword with Uhura in the Guild Hall when you are done with it.
column2: 用完那把劍,請交還給公會大廳的烏胡拉。

column1: Good sword, good fight.  There are many be proud of you.
column2: 好劍，好一場戰鬥，許多人都以你為榮。

column1: That was better.
column2: 這次好多了。

column1: Goo goo ga ga goo.
column2: 咕咕嘎嘎咕。

column1: Poltergeists make up the principle types of spontaneous material manifestations.
column2: 「騷靈是自發性物質顯現的主要類型之一。」

column1: Goo goo, Maa Fhimt.
column2: Goo goo, Maa Fhimt.

column1: Watch it buddy!  I'm not getting hazardous duty pay on this gig, so don't mess with me.  They don't pay me enough to put up with a lowlife like you. Cheeeeeezzzz!!
column2: 小心點，老兄！這活兒又沒給我算什麼危險加給，別惹我。他們付的錢還不夠讓我忍受你這種下三濫。唉唷∼∼！！

column1: The warrior woman cradles baby Simba in her arms.
column2: 那女戰士懷裡搖著小辛巴。

column1: The warrior woman stands tall and proud.
column2: 那女戰士站得挺拔而自豪。

column1: Maybe you find work for Hero here, too.
column2: 也許你在這裡也能找到英雄該做的工作。

column1: These make good salad for dinner.  That be very kind of you.
column2: 這些拿來當晚餐的沙拉正好。你真是太好心了。

column1: I try to be of help.
column2: 我盡量幫忙。

column1: Maybe today you and I get in some more practice at fighting.   It will be good to feel the spear in my hand again.
column2: 也許今天你我可以再多練練戰鬥。能再次感受長矛握在手裡的感覺真好。

column1: I am honored to aid you.
column2: 能助您一臂之力,是我的榮幸。

column1: It is good to see you, young Hero.  A nice day to be adventuring.
column2: 見到你真好,年輕的英雄。今天是個適合冒險的好日子。

column1: You've already returned the reward.
column2: 你已經歸還過獎賞了。

column1: My thanks, but I have no need for gifts.
column2: 謝謝你,但我不需要禮物。

column1: You show great honor.  May your deeds continue to shine!
column2: 你展現了崇高的榮譽，願你的功績繼續閃耀！

column1: Imagine!  Such a warrior's tail used as a playtoy for a baby!
column2: 你想想!這麼一條戰士的尾巴,竟拿來給嬰兒當玩具!

column1: Rakeesh relaxes but his tail stays ever alert.
column2: 拉基什放鬆了下來，但他的尾巴始終保持警戒。

column1: The baby fearlessly plays with the Liontaur's tail.
column2: 那嬰兒毫不畏懼地玩弄著獅人的尾巴。

column1: The kid's hangin' loose.
column2: 這小傢伙倒是逍遙自在。

column1: Don't bother.  He only tries to show off.
column2: 別費事了。他只是想顯擺罷了。

column1: Rakeesh wouldn't appreciate it.
column2: 拉基什不會欣賞這種行為的。

column1: Hero types don't do that sort of thing more than once.
column2: 英雄這種人,同樣的蠢事是不會做第二次的。

column1: Hero types don't do that sort of thing.
column2: 像英雄這種人,可不幹這種事。

column1: Uhura would have your head off before you got 10 feet. Besides, there is no baby market in Shapeir.
column2: 你還沒走出十呎遠，烏胡拉就會砍下你的腦袋。再說，夏皮爾根本沒有嬰兒買賣這回事。

column1: Maybe you do not hear so good.  I be thinking you give me back Rakeesh's sword, or maybe I be getting plenty mad.
column2: 你耳朵不大靈光吧?我看哪,你最好把拉基什的劍還來,不然我可要發大火了。

column1: The standard Guild Hall Quest Board has an assortment of notices posted, but you must move closer in order to read them.
column2: 這座標準的公會任務告示板上貼著各式各樣的公告，但你得靠近一點才能讀。

column1: If you got real guts and don't know the meaning of fear; If you got real muscles and don't take guff from no one; If you got a real weapon and know how to use it; then maybe you are good enough to join the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 如果你有真正的膽識，不知恐懼為何物；如果你有真正的力量，不受任何人擺布；如果你有真正的武器，還懂得如何使用；那麼你或許夠格加入永恆戰士團。

column1: We pick only the best to join and we'll be watching you, so don't just be good, be great!  Be a member of EOF!  Eternal Order of Fighters The Guts, The Glory, The Greatest!
column2: 我們只挑最頂尖的人加入，我們會盯著你，所以別只求優秀，要成為最強！加入EOF！永恆戰士團──膽識、榮耀、至強！

column1: There is some writing on the EOF plaque on the wall, but you are not close enough to read it.
column2: 牆上的EOF匾額寫著些字，但你離得不夠近，看不清楚。

column1: There will be given to the one who restores the Emir Arus Al-Din to his rightful place in the palace at Raseir the sum of 1000 dinars.  By order of the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid.
column2: 凡能讓埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁重返拉希爾宮殿、恢復其應有地位者，將獲賞1000第納爾。蘇丹哈倫·阿拉希德諭令。

column1: He who is willing to brave the desert should seek Harik Attar in his shop at the sign of the mortar and pestle by the fountain.  That one shall find rewards for his courage.
column2: 凡願冒險穿越沙漠者,當往噴泉旁掛著研缽與杵招牌的店鋪,尋訪哈里克·阿塔爾。此人將因其勇氣而獲得酬賞。

column1: There is needed for a Spell of Great Power the whirling part of a Dervish.  Inquire at the Magic Shop.  Keapon Laffin, Prop.
column2: 一項大威力法術需要旋轉苦行僧的旋轉部位，請洽魔法店詢問。奇朋·拉芬　店主謹啟。

column1: Those who do service to the city and land of Shapeir shall know the rewards of goodness.  By order of the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid.
column2: 為夏皮爾城邦效力者，必得善報。蘇丹哈倫·阿拉希德諭令。

column1: The Way of the Paladin: To seek. To learn. To do.
column2: 聖騎士之道：探求、學習、實踐。

column1: Uhura would be angry if you threw that at her Quest Board.
column2: 你要是把那東西丟向她的任務告示板，烏胡拉會生氣的。

column1: You will find better opportunities for social climbing by reading the quests.
column2: 讀讀這些任務，會比較有機會往上爬。

column1: There is no reason to cast a spell at the Quest Board.
column2: 沒理由對任務告示板施法。

column1: I do not know what you mean.
column2: 我不懂你的意思。

column1: Maybe we be not speaking the same voice.  I be not knowing.
column2: 也許咱倆說的不是同一種話。我聽不明白。

column1: Rakeesh be saying that bad things happen here soon.  Bad magic, he come down, somebody has got to stop him.  Maybe you.
column2: 拉基什說這兒很快要出壞事了。邪惡魔法,它要降下來,總得有人去阻止它。也許就是你。

column1: I not be seeing who leave it.  It come while I feed Simba.
column2: 我沒看到是誰留下的。我餵辛巴的時候它就在那兒了。

column1: That be where you sign in to be adventurer in Shapeir.
column2: 那是你在夏皮爾登記成為冒險者的地方。

column1: That be the motto of the EOF.  Read it.
column2: 那是永恆戰士團的格言。讀一讀。

column1: I learned to use that weapon when I was only little bigger than Simba.
column2: 我學會用那武器的時候,個頭只比辛巴大一點點。

column1: Simba means lion in my language.  Rakeesh say he sounds like a lion cub when he cries.
column2: 「辛巴」在我的語言裡是「獅子」的意思。拉基什說他哭起來的聲音像隻小獅子。

column1: My land and people are many months travel from here.
column2: 我的家鄉和族人離這兒要走上好幾個月。

column1: If you be fighting scorpion, you must watch the tail.  If the tail gets you, then you will be plenty sick, maybe dead, unless you get healing.  Block the Tail with shield, dodge the claws.
column2: 你要是跟蠍子打鬥，得盯緊牠的尾巴。尾巴要是螫到你，你會病得很重，搞不好會死，除非你得到治療。用盾牌擋尾巴，閃開牠的爪子。

column1: They be strong fighters, and they will gladly kill you for what you carry, if you let them.
column2: 牠們是強悍的戰士，你要是給牠們機會，牠們會很樂意為了搶你身上的東西殺了你。

column1: Ghouls be bad Magic things out only at night.  They eat the dead, but they will not be picky if they get the chance to eat you.  If they claw you, you get cold.  Colder you get, slower you move.  Too slow and you will be dead.  Then they eat.  You need good defense against them or they will pick your bones.
column2: 食屍鬼是邪惡的魔法之物，只在夜裡出沒。牠們吃死人，但要是有機會吃到你，牠們也不會挑食。牠們抓傷你，你就會發冷，愈冷動作就愈慢，太慢就會沒命，然後牠們就會把你吃掉。你得有好的防禦對付牠們，不然牠們會把你啃到只剩骨頭。

column1: They not be all that tough, but many Jackalmen be wearing down even good fighters.
column2: 牠們不算特別強悍，但一大群豺狼人連厲害的戰士都能耗垮。

column1: Dinosaur thing with many claws and teeth.  It likes to eat people.  Look out when it rears back on tail.  It can tear you up, if you do not dodge.
column2: 一種長滿爪子和牙齒的恐龍怪物，牠喜歡吃人。牠用尾巴撐起身體時要小心，你不閃開就會被牠撕成碎片。

column1: Strong fighter with claws and beak, it be.  Tear you to shreds unless you block and fight better.
column2: 那是有爪子和喙的強悍戰士，你不擋招、打得不夠好，就會被牠撕成碎片。

column1: Simba's father be in the Sultan's guards.  Strong fighter, he be almost better than me.
column2: 辛巴的爸爸在蘇丹的衛隊裡。他是個強悍的戰士,差一點就比我還厲害了。

column1: There be jobs, plenty rewards for good adventurers.  There be many more before I come here, but I be taking care of Simba too much now for jobs.
column2: 這裡有工作,好冒險者能拿到不少賞金。我來這裡之前還更多呢,只是現在我顧辛巴顧得緊,沒空接活了。

column1: Rakeesh be very fine warrior.  He knows to be gentle and kind, too.  Very wise, very strong, but his leg be plenty bad, hurt him all the time.
column2: 拉基什是個了不起的戰士。他懂得溫柔、也懂得仁慈。又有智慧又強壯,可惜那條腿傷得很重,一天到晚折磨著他。

column1: One very special, a Paladin be.  Talk to Rakeesh, he be knowing very much about Paladins.
column2: 聖騎士是很特別的存在，去找拉基什談談，他對聖騎士知道得非常多。

column1: Rakeesh leg be broken badly in fight long time.  It did not heal right.
column2: 拉基什那條腿很久以前在一場打鬥裡傷得很重,後來就沒好過。

column1: Men join to show off muscles and talk big.
column2: 男人們加進來,不過是想秀秀肌肉、吹吹牛罷了。

column1: Someone must be sending the bad magic that comes here.  That wizard most likely be in Raseir, since the bad things happen there first.
column2: 一定有人在把這些邪惡的魔法送過來。那個巫師八成在拉希爾,因為壞事都是先從那兒開始的。

column1: That thing be one of the people of the Djinn.
column2: 那東西是精靈一族的。

column1: In the plaza of the Fountain be the man who makes things to help people.  He always needs things to be making what he does.
column2: 噴泉廣場那邊有個會製作助人物品的人，他做東西總是需要材料。

column1: That be a very strange man, I think.  His shop of magic be east of here in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 我覺得那是個很奇怪的人。他的魔法店在這裡東邊的噴泉廣場。

column1: That man has more muscles than he has brains.  He makes good weapons, though.  His swords be very fine.
column2: 那人肌肉比腦子多。不過他打的武器是真好,劍尤其精良。

column1: The Sultan be very wise and generous.  Sometimes he has Rakeesh give rewards to those who have done things for him.
column2: 蘇丹既英明又大方。有時候他會叫拉基什替他賞賜那些為他出過力的人。

column1: Rakeesh won his sword in mighty battle.  He call it Soulforge.
column2: 拉基什這把劍是在一場惡戰裡贏來的,他管它叫「鑄魂」。

column1: To be real Hero here, you need to become Paladin.
column2: 要成為這裡真正的英雄，你得成為聖騎士。

column1: Never turn your back to Moose.  Especially near Mooseberry bushes.
column2: 千萬別把背轉向駝鹿，尤其是在駝鹿莓叢附近。

column1: Maybe we practice combat skills, you and I.
column2: 也許咱倆可以練練身手,你和我。

column1: This be a good place to stay while Simba be so small.  Soon he be big enough to travel.  Then we be going back to my home.
column2: 辛巴還這麼小,這裡是個落腳的好地方。等他大到能上路了,我們就回我的家鄉去。

column1: Very bad things happened there, maybe they start to happen here.  I be hoping not.  I be strong fighter, but such things come of bad magic.  Hard to fight magic with a spear.
column2: 那邊出過很可怕的事,搞不好這兒也要開始了。但願不會。我是個強悍的戰士,可這種事是邪惡魔法搞出來的。光憑一桿矛,很難打得過魔法。

column1: Djinn know much magic, some good, some bad.  Best you be leaving them alone.
column2: 精靈懂很多魔法,有好的也有壞的。你最好別去招惹他們。

column1: Fire Elemental be thing of bad magic.  Even if it be stopped, more bad magic will come down until bad magic Wizard be stopped.
column2: 火元素是邪惡魔法之物，就算擋下這隻，邪惡魔法還是會不斷降臨，除非那個邪惡巫師被阻止。

column1: Water be very much needed here.  If Water Elemental in the Fountain Plaza not stopped, there be no water.  Soon there be no people.
column2: 這裡很需要水。要是不把噴泉廣場那個水元素擋下來,就沒水喝。再過不久,人也都沒了。

column1: Wind be strong, be blowing the city to pieces unless someone stop it.
column2: 那風好強,要是沒人阻止,整座城都要被它吹散了。

column1: You should be asking Rakeesh.  He knows much about Earth.
column2: 你該去問拉基什，他對「土」知道得很多。

column1: Thing of very powerful magic.  Only magic and skill will stop it.
column2: 那是非常強大的魔法產物。只有魔法加上本事才擋得住它。

column1: Many monsters live in the desert.  You be going there, you need watch for Jackalmen and Ghouls at night.  During day, you find Brigands.  Scorpions and Terrorsauruses always a problem.
column2: 沙漠裡住著很多怪物。你要去那兒,晚上得當心豺狼人和食屍鬼。白天嘛,會碰上強盜。蠍子和恐爪龍呢,什麼時候都是麻煩。

column1: If you need to know magic, ask a magic person.  I am warrior, not wizard.
column2: 你要是想懂魔法,去問懂魔法的人。我是戰士,不是巫師。

column1: Good leader.  When trouble start to happen in Raseir, he be going with his men there.  Magic wind storm blew up and many men died.  My man say the Sultan be plenty brave, but could not stop Magic.
column2: 是個好領袖。拉希爾出事的時候，他帶著他的人一起趕去。魔法風暴颳了起來，許多人因此喪命。我的男人說蘇丹非常勇敢，但擋不住魔法。

column1: Something magic happen to the Emir of Raseir.  No one knows for sure what but the one who did it.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾出了魔法的事。到底怎麼回事,除了下手的那個,誰也說不準。

column1: My man works at the Sultan's palace.  It be to the east of here.
column2: 我男人在蘇丹的王宮做事。王宮就在這兒的東邊。

column1: To go to Raseir you need be going by caravan.  It leaves in a week or so.  Ask the guard at front gate of the city.
column2: 要去拉希爾,你得跟著商隊走。商隊大概一個禮拜後出發。去城門口問守衛吧。

column1: No place for fools.  Too hot in the day, too many monsters at night.  Very easy to get lost and never come back from there.
column2: 那不是傻子待的地方。白天太曬,晚上怪物又多。一不小心就迷了路,再也回不來。

column1: For money, you could do a job.  Read the quest board.
column2: 想賺錢的話,可以去接個活。看看任務板吧。

column1: This place I be working while Simba be small.  I be good fighter, good adventurer.  You need help fighting, I be good teacher, too.
column2: 辛巴還小,我就在這兒幹活。我是個好戰士、好冒險者。你要人教打鬥,我也是個好師傅。

column1: Hear many good things about your friends at the Inn.  They be heroes to the other Katta.  Say that you be hero too.
column2: 聽到不少關於你在旅店那些朋友的好話。他們對其他卡塔族來說是英雄，說你也是英雄。

column1: Go out at noon, you will find out what hot can be.  No water, and you find out what dead can be.
column2: 正午跑出去,你就會曉得什麼叫熱。再沒水喝,你就會曉得什麼叫死。

column1: You will be finding Fountain if you go east of this plaza.
column2: 你往這座廣場的東邊走，就會找到噴泉。

column1: Maybe they be needing new Guildmaster here soon, I be thinking.  When problems be in Shapeir, I be here.  When problems be going, I be going.
column2: 我在想哪,這裡很快就要找新的公會會長了。夏皮爾有麻煩,我就留在這兒;麻煩一走,我也就走了。

column1: Go to the east and then go south when you must.  Follow the road and you be finding her.
column2: 往東走,該往南時就往南。沿著路走,你就會找著她。

column1: Many people here try to sell many things.
column2: 這裡好多人在叫賣各式各樣的東西。

column1: Katta, they be good hunters if you get them away from the city.
column2: 卡塔族要是離開城裡,可都是好獵手。

column1: Funny old man in the desert Oasis.  To find him, you need to be going from the gates to the City Lookout.  Then go south until you come to the rock shaped like a Griffin's head.  Head east until you come to Oasis.  He will be there.
column2: 沙漠綠洲裡有個古怪的老人。要找到他，你得從城門走到瞭望臺，然後往南走，直到看見一塊形狀像獅鷲頭的岩石，再往東走就會到綠洲，他就在那裡。

column1: My name be Uhura.  It means freedom in my people's voice.
column2: 我的名字是烏胡拉，在我族人的語言裡，這個名字代表自由。

column1: So, Hero of Spielburg, you think you want to go practice some?  I could use workout.
column2: 怎麼樣,史畢柏格的英雄,想去練練嗎?我也正好需要活動活動。

column1: My spear arm is getting tired of just holding Simba.  Good thing for you, too, if we practice today.
column2: 我這持矛的手光抱著辛巴都快累了。今天練一練,對你也是好事。

column1: Today is good day for fighting practice.  Every day is good day for practice.
column2: 今天是練武的好日子。其實天天都是練武的好日子。

column1: Looks like you need to get some more practice today.  You be getting plenty good.
column2: 看來你今天還得多練練。你已經很有長進了。

column1: It is good for Simba to watch us practice.  He will need to learn soon enough.
column2: 讓辛巴看著我們練習是好事。他很快也得學了。

column1: Ah, the hero of the Kattas!  You have some reputation already, to hear merchants talk.  If you are as good as they say you are, then you must be plenty good.
column2: 啊,卡塔族的英雄!聽商人們那麼一說,你已經小有名氣了。要是你真有他們講的那麼厲害,那你肯定是相當了得。

column1: I am Uhura.  I am honored you visit here.
column2: 我是烏胡拉，很榮幸你來訪。

column1: Ah, this be the new Hero, Rakeesh.
column2: 啊，這位就是新來的英雄，拉基什。

column1: The Fire Elemental be plenty hard to fight.  Maybe the city burn down unless someone get it stopped.
column2: 火元素難打得很。要是沒人把它擋下來,這城搞不好就要燒個精光。

column1: My spear not do much to Air Elemental near palace.  Maybe you can do something to stop it.
column2: 我的長矛對宮殿附近的風元素起不了什麼作用，也許你能想辦法制伏牠。

column1: Rakeesh, he say that you stop the Air Elemental.  Now you are even bigger Hero in Shapeir.
column2: 拉基什說你制伏了那隻風元素，現在你在夏皮爾更是了不起的英雄了。

column1: Rakeesh not be here today, Hero.  He be speaking with Sultan.  They talk about more Elementals coming down soon.
column2: 拉基什今天不在這兒,英雄。他正跟蘇丹談話呢。他們說很快還會有更多元素降下來。

column1: That Earth Elemental be plenty tough, but you sure be tougher.
column2: 那土元素是夠硬的,可你肯定更硬。

column1: If you not stop that Earth Elemental today, I think maybe it get too tough to stop.
column2: 你今天再不阻止那土元素,我看牠就要硬到誰也擋不住啦。

column1: You are most welcome in Shapeir.  The sultan has been seeking a Hero since the forces of Dark Magic overwhelmed Raseir.
column2: 夏皮爾非常歡迎你。自從黑暗魔法勢力吞沒拉希爾以來，蘇丹便一直在尋找一位英雄。

column1: It's good sometimes to have Simba with Rakeesh.  Hard to feel like strong warrior when feeding baby.  Maybe you and I do some practice with weapons today.
column2: 有時候把辛巴交給拉基什帶帶也不錯。一邊餵娃娃,可很難覺得自己像個強悍的戰士。也許你跟我今天可以練練武器。

column1: You have saved the city with your courage and your resourcefulness.  The land of Shapeir is indebted to you.  By order of the Sultan, Harun Al-Rashid, I have been asked to give you this reward for your actions.
column2: 你憑著勇氣與機智拯救了這座城市，夏皮爾全境都欠你一份人情。奉蘇丹哈倫·阿拉希德之命，我奉命將這份獎賞交給你，以表彰你的功績。

column1: Rakeesh gives you the 50 dinar reward.
column2: 拉基什把五十第納爾的賞金交給了你。

column1: We are all truly fortunate that you came to this land.
column2: 你能來到這片土地,實在是我們所有人的福氣。

column1: This note was left here for you.
column2: 這張字條是留給你的。

column1: This be stuck to the door with knife.  I think maybe you should find better place to pass notes.
column2: 這是用刀插在門上的。我看你該找個更好的地方傳紙條才對。

column1: This time the note be stuck to the door with an old sword.  Tell your friend I will show him where to stick it the next time.
column2: 這回字條是用一把舊劍釘在門上的。轉告你那位朋友,下次我會讓他知道這玩意該插哪兒。

column1: and small torn spot.
column2: ，還有一個小裂口。

column1: and large torn spot.
column2: ，還有一個大裂口。

column1: You read the crumpled paper with the nearly illegible scrawl%s
column2: 你讀著這張皺巴巴的紙，上面字跡潦草幾乎無法辨認%s

column1: If you know what's good for you, you will obey orders.  We will leave them here.  If you don't follow them, you'll be sorry.  We are watching you.
column2: 「你要是識相，就照命令行事。我們會把命令留在這裡。你要是不照做，肯定會後悔。我們在看著你。」

column1: Tomorrow night is your last night.  You'll get your final orders then.  Be seeing you.
column2: 「明晚是你的最後一晚。到時你會收到最終指令。回頭見。」

column1: At nightfall tonight, go to the door at the end of 'Saif Darb,' off 'Askeri Darb.'  Obey, or you'll regret it.
column2: 「今晚入夜後，到『阿斯凱里巷』岔出去的『賽夫巷』盡頭那扇門。照做，不然你會後悔。」

column1: It seems that Shapeir again needs your skills.  No one else in this city can handle such a magical being.
column2: 看來夏皮爾又得仰仗你的本事了。這城裡再沒別人對付得了這種魔法生物。

column1: If your leg had not failed you this morning, Rakeesh, the elemental would be gone.
column2: 拉基什，要不是你今早的腿傷發作，那隻元素早就被解決了。

column1: Hero, you should have seen how Rakeesh fought it!  His sword burned brightly as he fought that monster.  Rakeesh be a great warrior still!
column2: 英雄,你真該看看拉基什是怎麼跟它拚的!他跟那怪物廝殺時,劍身燒得耀眼通紅。拉基什到現在都還是個了不起的戰士!

column1: I'm afraid I have not the power to deal with the Elemental now.  It will require your skills and magic to overcome the thing, oh Defeater of Elementals.
column2: 恐怕我現在沒有力量對付那隻元素了。得靠你的技藝與魔法才能制伏牠，喔，元素剋星。

column1: If you do seek to defeat the Earth Elemental which now walks our streets, you must first understand its Nature.
column2: 你若真想擊敗如今在我們街上遊蕩的土元素，就得先了解它的本質。

column1: I hear you be plenty good and beat the elemental.  You be some Hero, for sure.
column2: 我聽說你很厲害，打敗了那隻元素，你確實稱得上是位英雄。

column1: It was no little honor, being able to use Rakeesh's sword.  I will be giving it back, if you will be giving it to me.
column2: 拉基什的劍，能讓我使用實在是莫大的榮幸。只要你想要回去，我就會物歸原主。

column1: So, Hero, not being content with having merely saved the city, I see that you are about to attempt to save the land of Shapeir as well.
column2: 看來啊,英雄,光是救了這座城你還不滿足,你還打算連整片夏皮爾的土地一併救下來。

column1: The light shines brightly within you.  You have so far managed to walk the narrow line between Honor and Necessity.  May your deeds continue to shine, and not be dulled by the darkness you will soon encounter.
column2: 你心中的光芒明亮動人。你至今始終能走在「榮譽」與「必要」之間的那條窄線上。願你的作為繼續閃耀，不被即將面對的黑暗所掩蓋。

column1: You have proven yourself both brave and resourceful by your actions.
column2: 你的所作所為已證明了你既勇敢又機智。

column1: When first we met, I could feel there was dark within you.  Now I can see that the light is stronger, if you will only let it be so.  It is a narrow path between darkness and light, and you will always walk that tightrope in your soul.
column2: 我們初次相遇時,我便感覺到你心中有黑暗。如今我看見光明更為強大——只要你願意讓它如此。黑暗與光明之間是一條窄路,你的靈魂將永遠走在那條鋼索上。

column1: I have seldom met one with such Power and so much wisdom and courage as you have shown.  Few users of Magic dare face what you have faced to protect this city.
