column2: 你火候還不到啊老兄,這筆帳我就先不跟你算清。

column1: Say Jay, what the hey?  The rope you've got is one and the same.
column2: 嘿，老兄，怎麼回事？你手上那條繩子根本一模一樣嘛。

column1: Well, the tag says 15 dinars, but I guess I can take 14 for 'em...
column2: 嗯，標籤上寫十五第納爾，不過我想十四個也就賣你了……

column1: Naaaaaah, I guessed wrong.  15 dinars it is, then.
column2: 不不不，我猜錯了。那就十五第納爾吧。

column1: Whew!  Smooth talking, Dude.  Ya got the gift for gab, Tab.
column2: 呼！嘴皮子真溜啊，老兄。你還真有那張利嘴呢。

column1: Tell ya what I'm going ta do.  Seein' as you beein' a Heero and all, I'll reduce the price by %d whole simoleons.
column2: 這樣吧，看在你是個英雄的份上，我就給你降%d塊錢。

column1: (Dinars, that is.  Dough, Flo.  Shapeir Buck an Ear.)
column2: （也就是第納爾啦。銀子，錢啦。夏皮爾的錢幣。）

column1: If you want to do some dealing, then pick a better deck.
column2: 想做點買賣,就拿副像樣點的牌來。

column1: I laid my cards on the table, Grable, and if you're able, then ante up.
column2: 我已經把牌攤在桌上了,有本事你就掏錢下注吧。

column1: Pay or belay, Jay.
column2: 付錢,不然就閃邊去。

column1: Ah, I have that here, Dear.
column2: 啊,我這兒有,親愛的。

column1: Possible, Probable, my blue hen, Make it appear as I say when!
column2: 可能，或許，我的藍母雞，我說現身就得現身！

column1: Nothing up my sleeve...
column2: 我袖子裡可什麼都沒藏喔……

column1: Presto, Change-o, Re-arrange-o!
column2: 唰地一聲，變一變，重新排列！

column1: Eins, Zwei, Drei, Vier, Make it snappy und bring it here!
column2: Eins, Zwei, Drei, Vier，快點把它給我弄過來！

column1: Need more money, Honey.  Your moolah's too low, Moe.
column2: 得再多點錢哪,寶貝。你這荷包也太扁了吧,老兄。

column1: The sum doesn't balance.  COD or DOA.  Pay, or go away.
column2: 帳算不平。一手交錢一手交貨，沒得商量。付錢，不然滾。

column1: So solly, Chollie.  This money's funny.
column2: 真不好意思啊老兄,這錢有問題。

column1: You need to go get some dough from the Money Changer, Stranger.
column2: 你得去跟兌幣商弄點錢來才行，陌生人。

column1: Here you go, Joe.  Hope you're happy, Pappy.
column2: 給你囉,老兄。希望你滿意啦,大爺。

column1: The spell looks and feels very familiar.  In fact, you realize that you already knew it!  What a waste of money!
column2: 這道法術看起來、感覺起來都很眼熟。事實上，你發現自己早就會了！真是浪費錢！

column1: Now let me see where I last saw it.  I'll have to look for it in the back room.  I'll be back in a flash with the stash, Nash!
column2: 讓我想想上回放哪去了。我得進後頭那間屋子翻一翻。我一溜煙就把貨拿來,等著喔!

column1: Silly me.  I had it here the whole time.
column2: 瞧我這記性。它一直都在這兒呢。

column1: Abracabadra and didgery-doo, Make the thing appear for you!
column2: 阿布拉卡達布拉，滴哩咕嚕，讓那東西為你現身！

column1: But I see you already have some dirt, Kurt.
column2: 不過我看你身上已經有點土了呀,老兄。

column1: You put the pot of dirt away.
column2: 你把那盆土收了起來。

column1: Buon giorno!  Good and tag!  Buenas Dias, Senor Caws.  I haven't seen you in so long.
column2: Buon giorno！Good and tag！Buenas Dias，考斯先生。我好久沒見到你了。

column1: Hello!  Bone Jar!  Hi, guy!  Set a spell, take your shoes off!  What may I do to you?
column2: 哈囉！你好呀！嗨，夥計！坐下來歇口氣，把鞋子脫了吧！我能為您做什麼呢？

column1: Konnichiwa!  Come and tell me who!  Hola!  How now, brown cow?
column2: 空你機哇!來告訴我是誰!喔啦!最近如何呀,花斑牛?

column1: Top o' the morning to ya!  Mar Haba!  What's New, Pussycat?
column2: 早安哪！你好呀！近來可好啊，小貓咪？

column1: Hail, fellow, well met!  What da ya know, Joe?
column2: 幸會幸會,老兄!最近如何呀,老喬?

column1: Ahoy, Goy!  Howdy, Duty.  What's up, Doc?
column2: 嘿唷,老兄!安安,任務。怎麼啦,博士?

column1: A River Dirty, Gerty!  Adieu to you!  'Bye for now, purple cow.
column2: 再會啦，格蒂！告辭啦！先這樣囉，紫牛小姐。

column1: Ah, be a toe, Joe!  See ya later, alligator.
column2: 保重啦，喬！等會兒見啦，鱷魚先生。

column1: Bone Sewer, Sir!  After a while, Crocodile.
column2: 晚安啦，先生！待會見啦，鱷魚小弟。

column1: Hasta La Pasta!  Brain those nachos!  See ya soon, Raccoon!
column2: 再見啦，義大利麵！顧好那些玉米片！待會見啦，浣熊小子！

column1: Au revoir, Pa!  Way to go, Pinocchio!
column2: 再會啦,老爹!幹得好,小木偶!

column1: TTFN - Ta-ta for now!  Bon Voyage!
column2: 先這樣啦，掰掰！一路順風！

column1: How do you do, adieu.  Good night and don't let the Bed Bugs bite!
column2: 幸會啦，告辭。晚安囉，小心別被臭蟲咬！

column1: Nharak sa'id!  Aloha Oe!  Well come in!  Gesundheit!  Tip a canoe and how do you do?
column2: 祝你有美好的一天！阿囉哈！歡迎光臨！保重身體！划艘獨木舟，你好嗎？

column1: Pleased to meet you and I mean to please you!
column2: 很高興認識你,我也存心要讓你滿意!

column1: Welcome to my little shop of wonders!  Wonder what shop it is?  It's magic, of course!
column2: 歡迎光臨我這間小小的奇妙店鋪!好奇這是什麼店?當然是賣魔法的囉!

column1: I am Keapon Laffin, Proprietor.  You must be Nobile Caws.   Proud to know you, Mister Caws.  Shapeir always hopes for heroes.
column2: 我是奇朋·拉芬，這裡的老闆。你一定是尊貴的考斯先生了。很榮幸認識您，考斯先生。夏皮爾一直期盼著英雄的到來。

column1: If you need to know some knowledge about the magic way, then go and see Aziza who'll enchant you if you stay.
column2: 若你想了解魔法之道的學問，就去找阿姿莎吧，只要你留下，她會讓你著迷。

column1: Watch out, she's testy, though.
column2: 不過可得小心,她脾氣挺火爆的。

column1: So long, fair well, Oaf, we da same!
column2: 再會啦,珍重囉,大笨蛋,咱倆是一夥的!

column1: Good-bye!  See you real soon, Effendi Caws I like you.
column2: 再見啦！很快就會再見面的，考斯閣下，我喜歡你。

column1: Heard the one about the fire, sire?  It's a torrid story.
column2: 聽過那則關於火的故事沒,大爺?那可是個火辣辣的故事。

column1: The fire elemental had a seething passion for a sultry old flame who burned him once.
column2: 那火元素對一位曾經燙傷過他的性感舊情人，懷著熾熱的激情。

column1: Seems his spark was worse than his spite.  There ardor be a law.
column2: 看來他那把火比他的怨氣還旺。這份熱情啊,真該立法管管。

column1: At least there will be a hot time in the old town tonight.
column2: 至少今晚這老城裡,鐵定要熱鬧上一場。

column1: I don't mean to bluster, buster, but if you're in a hurry, Cain, you need to get down to earth before you blow up.
column2: 我不是要嚇唬你啦,老兄,可你要是趕時間哪,得先腳踏實地,才不會把自己給炸了。

column1: Then if you bellow, fellow, the breeze is sure to freeze.
column2: 那麼,只要你鼓動風箱,小老弟,那陣風保準給你凍住。

column1: How's the Chinook, Schnook?
column2: 那陣熱風吹得怎樣啊,傻小子?

column1: I heard the gusty Air Elemental has a tempestuous relationship with his own twister.
column2: 我聽說那陣風大的氣元素,跟他自己那道龍捲風鬧得正轟轟烈烈呢。

column1: They had a whirlwind affair, but it's winding down, if you catch my drift.
column2: 他倆談了場旋風般的戀愛,不過眼下風頭正漸漸消下去,你聽得懂我的弦外之音吧?

column1: The Dervish of the desert sent a bedouin to my boudoir, and I hear he needs a hero for to help him bait a beast.
column2: 沙漠苦行僧派了個貝都因人到我閨房來，聽說他需要一位英雄幫他誘捕一頭野獸。

column1: You have to watch the old codger - he's being cagey, but Dervish is my command.
column2: 你得小心那老頭子，他行事鬼鬼祟祟的，不過苦行僧的話就是我的命令。

column1: They say an earthy elemental is aground, but he just wants to be left a loam.
column2: 聽說有個土元素擱淺在地上，他只是想被留在自己的泥土裡清靜清靜。

column1: What a clod.  He's not very gneiss.
column2: 真是個土包子。對人一點也不「片麻」客氣。

column1: This town's sure going to rock tonight, but I suppose you prefer old Terra Firma (the more firmer, the less terror).
column2: 今晚這城肯定要被搖個天翻地覆,不過我猜你還是偏愛腳踏實地的老大地(地越穩,人越不慌)。

column1: Arrrrrhhh, me bucko, if you be thirstin' to sponge off the maritime maiden, you'll need to air out a few things first, or you'll be seein' the waters pout, arrrrrhhh!
column2: 呀啊啊——小子哎,你要是渴著想揩那「海上姑娘」的油哪,得先把幾樁事透透氣,不然你就等著瞧那水鬧脾氣吧,呀啊啊!

column1: Water it be today, matey?
column2: 今天「水」事如意嗎,夥計?

column1: Did you sea the swell elemental that's made such a splash in the fountain?
column2: 你見過那個在噴泉裡掀起大浪、引起一陣騷動的水元素嗎？

column1: She's awfully soppy and tends to gush, but I tell you, Eddy, and I don't mean to deluge you, there's an undercurrent of still water running steep.
column2: 她濕答答的,還動不動就嘩嘩傾瀉,不過我跟你說啊,我可不是想拿話淹你,這底下可有一股深沉的暗流靜靜湧著呢。

column1: If I were you, I'd rain away.
column2: 要是我啊,我可就溜之大「雨」嘍。

column1: If you haven't seen her already today, the End Chant Dress wishes to chat.
column2: 如果你今天還沒見過她，那位「女巫師」想找你聊聊。

column1: You need to make a final bow before Auntie Azizie's alter-ego.
column2: 你得在阿姿莎姑姑的分身面前，恭敬地行個禮。

column1: Speaking of finals, Lionel, so you're graduating to Raseir.
column2: 說到收尾哪,老兄,看來你要「畢業」前往拉希爾囉。

column1: Be ware, Larry, I hear it's hairy there, so watch your derriere.
column2: 小心點，賴瑞，聽說那裡挺嚇人的，留神你的屁股。

column1: Happy trails to you, until we meet again.
column2: 一路順風囉,咱們後會有期。

column1: Buy those banditos!  Good luck, Buck!
column2: 去收拾那些土匪吧!祝你好運囉,老弟!

column1: Goat got your throat or just lost your voice?  You might check the Flossed and Crowned for missing teeth.
column2: 山羊叼走你的舌頭了，還是單純沒了聲音？你可以去「潔牙加冠旅店」找找掉了的牙齒。

column1: Asleep at the heel and slipping into slumber?  I deplore being a bore, so just don't snore, senor.
column2: 睡到連腳跟都軟了，就要墜入夢鄉了嗎？我最討厭無聊透頂，所以別打呼囉，先生。

column1: Resting on your Laurels, Hardy, or dreaming of days, Hays?
column2: 躺在功勞簿上睡大覺嗎，哈迪？還是夢見過去的好日子，海斯？

column1: Cousin to Santa and Lost, aren't you?
column2: 你是聖誕老人和迷途者的表親吧？

column1: Clip, clop to the Magic shop to see the owner flip his flop.  I sell spells, can't you tell?
column2: 噠噠噠地走到魔法店，看看老闆耍寶翻跟斗。我在賣咒語，你還看不出來嗎？

column1: Wonder of Wonders and will wonders never cease?  You'll wonder where the yellow went, and whether geese have fleece.
column2: 奇蹟中的奇蹟，奇事永無止境嗎？你會納悶黃色跑哪去了，還有鵝到底有沒有羊毛。

column1: Properly and proudly I propound with all propriety.  Certainly and soundly, I speak with all sobriety.  I am the very model of a major modern magic man (well, Gnome, actually).
column2: 我端莊自持、字字得體地闡述一切。我言之鑿鑿、句句清醒地陳述道理。我正是現代魔法大師的典範（呃，其實是地精啦）。

column1: Do I know Yorick?  He's my cousin, Laspo.  Ah, Laspo Yorick, I knew him, hero.
column2: 我認識約里克嗎？他是我表親，拉斯波。啊，拉斯波·約里克，我認得他，英雄。

column1: I'm a little low on spells in stock, but I do have scrolls of Detect Magic for a negligible 10 dinars, Trigger for only 15, Force Bolt for a measly 20, Levitate for a mere 30 dinars, and Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle for a paltry 25 dinars.
column2: 我這咒語存貨有點少，不過還有幾卷卷軸：偵測魔法只要區區10第納爾，觸發只要15，力擊只要少少20，浮空只要短短30第納爾，還有伊拉斯謨的眩目魔法只要小小25第納爾。

column1: If you have a need for nudging or a bent for bouncing, this spell is swell.  It can punch, it can push, it can even beat around the bush.  All this for a mere 20 dinars.
column2: 如果你需要推一把，或喜歡彈來彈去，這咒語妙極了。它能揍人、能推人，甚至能拐彎抹角地打。這一切只要短短20第納爾。

column1: Detect will select what you suspect but can't inspect.  Expect the effect to wreck the protection by creating a defect in its perfect reflection.
column2: 「偵測」能替你挑出那些你疑神疑鬼卻又查不出的東西。瞧好這效果如何破解防護——只消在那完美的倒影上戳出個破綻。

column1: The Trigger spell is like a witch's switch with a hitch, Rich.  It makes the last magic cast in the past happen fast.  Not only that, Brat, but Trigger makes you hoarse.
column2: 「觸發」這法術啊,就像女巫手裡帶機關的開關,老兄。它能讓你方才施過的最後一道法術瞬間再現。不止如此呢,小鬼,「觸發」還會讓你嗓子發啞喔。

column1: It's got its ups and downs, but if you need a lift, this will get your feet up off the ground.
column2: 它有起有落啦,可你要是需要往上飄一飄,這玩意兒能讓你雙腳離地。

column1: A shining example of a scintillating spell.  It brings a tear to the eyes of the other guys who spy it.
column2: 這可是耀眼法術裡的閃亮典範。誰瞄上一眼,都要被閃得熱淚盈眶。

column1: Whose bed am I in?  What kind of personal question is that?  Oh, bedouin!  He's a desert drifter who shifts with sifting sands.
column2: 我睡在誰的床上？這是什麼私人問題啊？喔，「貝都因」！他是個隨著流沙漂移的沙漠遊子。

column1: Come here, Oh stranger, and save us from danger.  It's a dirty job, and I'm glad you're here to do it, Pruitt.  People say you're pretty neat, Pete.
column2: 過來吧，陌生人，來救我們脫離危險。這是件苦差事，很高興有你來做，普魯特。大家都說你挺不賴的，皮特。

column1: Actually, Pete was a friend of mine who tangled with a wizard.  Now pizza elemental.
column2: 其實，皮特是我一個朋友，他跟一個巫師起了衝突。現在他成了披薩元素了。

column1: You do?  Do what?  How do you do the hoodoo that you do so well?
column2: 是喔?做啥?你那一手把戲到底是怎麼變的呀,變得這麼順手?

column1: You have a dearth of earth?  That's worth my mirth.  I do have some Fooler's Earth in stock, though.
column2: 你缺土啊?這倒值得我樂一樂。不過嘛,我店裡確實還有點「障眼土」的存貨。

column1: Noticed my note, mister?  It is der wish for the whirl of a Dervish.  I will pay 1500 centimes (in dinars, of course) for such a thing.  All you have to do is find it.
column2: 瞧見我那張告示了沒,先生?我心心念念想要苦行僧那旋轉的本事。為了這玩意兒,我願意付一千五百生丁(當然,折成第納爾算)。你只要去把它找來就成。

column1: Be it ever so humble, there's no face like Gnome.
column2: 不管多麼簡陋，沒有一張臉比得上地精。

column1: Erasmus is a wizard I respect.  He can out-pun almost anyone and he jests with the best.  Between Erasmus and Fenrus, it's hard to get a joke in edgewise.  Fenrus is an aristo-rat at that, too.  Their humor is no rumor.
column2: 伊拉斯謨是位我敬重的巫師。論玩雙關他幾乎打遍天下無敵手,說笑也是箇中高手。在伊拉斯謨跟芬魯斯兩個之間,你連插句笑話的空檔都搶不到。芬魯斯那傢伙啊,還是隻貴族「鼠」呢。他倆的幽默可不是空穴來風。

column1: Hard to learn a spell unless you have a scroll.  Once it's read; it's in your head, Fred.
column2: 沒有卷軸很難學會咒語。一旦讀過，就記在腦子裡了，弗雷德。

column1: Behold the beast of burden, it travels in the herd, then it frequently is heard when a Saurus is absurd, men.
column2: 瞧那負重的野獸，牠總是成群結隊，索魯斯發起瘋來，動靜可不小，各位。

column1: Sorry, Charlie.  I don't have a scroll for that Wizard Spell.  It tends to disappear fast.
column2: 抱歉了，查理。我沒有那個巫師咒語的卷軸，它總是賣得特別快。

column1: A magic user went to school, so he could learn the magic rule.  And when he learned what he had heard, he had become a great Wizard.  (He went, to WIT, of course.)
column2: 有個法師去上學，為了學會魔法的規則。等他把聽來的都學會了，他便成了一位偉大的巫師。（他去的當然是巫院。）

column1: It's used to make dark stains on light clothes.  If you need it to ground old Blowhard, it's yours for a song.  Dust in the Wind.  All you need is dust in the wind.
column2: 這東西能在淺色衣物上染出深色汙漬。要是你需要它來制住那個「吹牛大王」，便宜賣你。風中之塵，你需要的就是這一撮風中之塵。

column1: If you are inclined to climb, a Magic Rope can give you hope.  To make it rise, close your eyes, raise your arms high, and watch it fly.
column2: 若你有意攀爬，魔法繩能給你希望。要讓它升起，閉上雙眼，高舉雙臂，看它飛起來。

column1: If the time is right, you're in a plight, you can get it across if you give it a toss.  Burma Shave.
column2: 時機若對,你陷困境,輕輕一擲,便能解圍。——Burma Shave

column1: It's a good tool, fool, but remember the rule - the Magic can't last, and on your last cast, it'll go fast - so don't waste it, or you might find yourself hanging in air with nothing there.
column2: 這是個好工具，傻瓜，但記住這規矩——魔法不會持久，你最後一次施放時它會很快耗盡，所以別浪費，不然你可能會發現自己懸在半空中，什麼支撐都沒有。

column1: We have a special on Magic Ropes this year.  It usually costs 12,500 centimes.  For a limited time only, though, it's only 125 dinars.  So do you wanta scope a rope, Miss Anthrope?
column2: 我們今年魔法繩正在特價。它平常要價12,500分。不過限時特惠，現在只要125第納爾。想不想買條繩子瞧瞧啊，厭世的小姐？

column1: Oh, those old things?  I could never get them to work correctly.  But then again, I've never worn them.
column2: 喔,那些老古董啊?我從來就沒能讓它們好好運作過。不過話說回來,我也從沒真的戴過就是了。

column1: Oh Cod, it makes me Eel when people Carp and Whale just for the Halibut, with no Porpoise.  I'm Net that kind of Buoy, Sea?  I'm Shore tired of Gills in School with their Tails of Roe.
column2: 老天「鱈」,人家一沒「鯨」由就在那兒又「鯉」怨又「鯢」嚎,真叫我渾身「鰻」不自在。我可「平」不是那種人,「魚」道嗎?我「鯊」是受夠了那些成天滿口「鱈」話的傢伙了。

column1: (Coral, Bob, and Brine made me say this.  Tanks for rising to the bait and falling for it, hook, line, and sinker!)
column2: (都是珊瑚、鮑伯跟那一缸鹽水逼我說的啦。多「漁」你上鉤咬餌,還連魚鉤、魚線、鉛墜整套吞下肚!)

column1: (We're REALLY fishing here, folks.)
column2: (各位,我們這可是「真的」在釣魚喔。)

column1: Air, air, it's everywhere.  Way down here, and way up there.  In your bed and in my head and going anywhere it's led, Dunderhead.
column2: 空氣啊空氣,無所不在。低低在這兒,高高在那兒。在你的床上,在我的腦袋裡,被牽到哪兒就飄到哪兒,傻瓜。

column1: My knowledge is scanty, Auntie.
column2: 我懂的可不多哪,大姊。

column1: If you need to know the answer, then ask another, Prancer.
column2: 要想知道答案哪,你就去問問別人吧,小蹦跳。

column1: Shapeir is a city, small and quaint and pretty.  Shapeir is a land, large and great and grand.
column2: 夏皮爾是座城,小巧、古樸又秀麗。夏皮爾是片地,遼闊、偉大又壯麗。

column1: Raseir, I fear, is very queer, and if you do not interfere, I think that it will disappear.
column2: 拉希爾啊,我怕得很,那地方詭異透頂,你要是再不出手干預,我看它遲早要憑空消失。

column1: Djinni, Genie, Jeannie, Djinn; the desert's where they all begin.  Djinni, Ifreet, Marid, Djann; Magic's where they all began.
column2: 精靈、神燈精靈、珍妮、鎮尼，沙漠是牠們的起源。精靈、埃夫里特、瑪莉德、賈恩，魔法是牠們的源頭。

column1: The Fire Elemental has a weakness for water.  He lives in the fast lane, so you'll have to get him restrained if you want to make him remain, so that you can get him contained.  To get the flame to smother, don't ask me, go ask another.
column2: 火元素的弱點就是水。他過的是快節奏的日子,所以你想讓他乖乖待著,就得先把他制住,這樣才好把他關起來。想讓那火苗熄滅嘛,可別問我,去問別人吧。

column1: Ahoy, me boy, and shiver me timbers.  Ya got what it takes to make a lady less limber.  Pieces of eight, mate, and blow the maid down, sweep her off her feet and then clear out of town.
column2: 喂,小子,我的老天爺啊。你還真有本事讓一位姑娘渾身發軟。金幣一塊塊,夥計,把那姑娘哄得神魂顛倒,讓她拜倒在你腳下,然後你再溜出城去。

column1: If you're led to behead an airhead, don't let him lead the chase.  Glue his shoes with goo, Blue, and throw mud in his face.  It's fair to snare the air there, then put him in his place.
column2: 要是你被指派去砍掉某個風一般的呆頭,可別讓他帶著你滿場跑。拿黏糊糊的膠把他鞋子黏死,再朝他臉上甩泥巴。在那兒設陷阱困住那陣風,合情合理,然後好好教訓他一頓。

column1: There is no better way to arouse a rock's ire, than to fight with a light and to smite him with fire.
column2: 要激怒一塊岩石，沒有比用火光與烈焰迎戰它更好的辦法了。

column1: Matter Monster, made by man.  Chaos tossed across the land.  Catch them, grab them if you can.  Then they're there at your command.
column2: 物質怪物，由人所造。混亂散落大地各方。抓住牠們，能抓就抓。牠們便任你差遣調度。

column1: Griffin, Jackal, TerrorSaurus, Ghoul;  learn their ways in Adventurer's School.  Brigand, Djinni, Scorpion, all;  ask for info at the Guild Hall.
column2: 獅鷲、胡狼、恐魯斯、食屍鬼，去冒險者學校學習牠們的習性。強盜、精靈、蠍子，全都能到公會大廳打聽消息。

column1: Magic, magic everywhere.  Find it at Aziza's lair.  Magic, magic, wait a bit.  Find it in the soul of WIT.  Magic, magic in the stars.  The astrologer can see that far.
column2: 魔法，魔法無處不在。到阿姿莎的巢穴裡尋找。魔法，魔法，且慢一下。在巫院的靈魂深處尋找。魔法，魔法在星辰之中。占星師能望得那麼遠。

column1: Magic, magic, where'd it begin?  Go and ask the mighty Djinn.  Magic, Magic, where'd it stop?  Look around my magic shop.
column2: 魔法，魔法，源自何方？去問問強大的精靈吧。魔法，魔法，止於何處？看看我這間魔法店吧。

column1: The Sultan sits upon this throne, I ponder if he's ever home?  The Sultan sits inside his palace.  Is he there? Well, go ask Alice. (If you can find her - Last we met she had a chat with a cat and chatter with the Hatter.  It's a wonder, man.)
column2: 「蘇丹端坐在這王座上，我不禁納悶他是否常在家？蘇丹坐鎮在他的宮殿裡。他在嗎？這個嘛，去問問愛麗絲吧。」（前提是你找得到她——上次見面時，她正跟一隻貓聊天，還跟瘋帽匠閒扯。真是件怪事啊，夥計。）

column1: The Emir of Raseir did disappear a year ago.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾,一年前確實憑空消失了。

column1: If you want to be WITty, then search in the city.  The nitty-gritty to this ditty:  you need magic to find it, 'twas designed by committee.
column2: 你要是想沾上巫師技術學院(W.I.T.)的「智」氣,那就到城裡頭去找吧。這支小曲兒的關鍵就在:你得有魔法才找得到它,畢竟那是一群人合議設計出來的。

column1: You have to be a hero if you want to play the palace.  The Sultan is selective about whom he will see.
column2: 你想進那宮殿可得是個英雄才行。蘇丹對見什麼人可是挑得很。

column1: It's sand, man.  Hot, Scott.  Dry, Guy.
column2: 就是沙啊,老兄。又燙,又乾,沒別的。

column1: The din of the bazaar is filled with the dinar.  If you want to pay a cent, Dean, you have to have a centime.
column2: 市集裡那喧鬧聲,滿是第納爾的銅臭味。你要是想付個一分錢,老兄,那你手裡就得有生丁才行。

column1: The Plaza of the Fighters is west of here, Greer.  There you'll find the hall, Paul.
column2: 戰士廣場在這裡的西邊，格里爾。你會在那找到公會大廳，保羅。

column1: The Kattas trail into the Katta's Tail Inn, he says cattily, as he goes from the feline to the ridiculous.
column2: 卡塔族都拖著尾巴溜進「貓尾客棧」啦——他賊頭賊腦地說,從貓樣一路扯到沒個正經。

column1: In the summer, in the desert, it is hot 'til day is done.  Only mad dogs and hero men go out in the noonday sun.
column2: 盛夏的沙漠,熱到日落都不歇。也只有瘋狗跟英雄,敢頂著正午的大太陽出門。

column1: The center of the city, Mitty.  The water for the town, Clown.
column2: 城的正中央哪,小傻瓜。打水的地方哪,小笨蛋。

column1: Don't quote me, but Aziza has a waterbed and fiddles with fish.
column2: 別說是我講的喔——聽說阿姿莎睡水床,還愛擺弄她那些魚。

column1: There was a Money Changer and her name was Dinarzad.  It's understood that she was goood and very, very Baad.  You can find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, my lad.
column2: 曾有位錢幣兌換商，名叫第納爾扎德。眾所皆知，她既善良，卻也非常、非常壞。你能在第納爾大道的盡頭找到她，小夥子。

column1: Do not hurry.  Do not wait.  The astrologer will tell your fate.  He is not near.  He is not far.  He's at the end of Tarik of Stars.
column2: 別急，也別等。占星師會告訴你命運。他不在近處，也不在遠方。他就在星辰大道的盡頭。

column1: Auntie Azizie lives off the east end of Shmali Tarik.  Mind your P's and Q's when you are there, or she might send you you-know-where.
column2: 阿姿莎姑姑住在什馬利大道東端附近。到了那裡要規矩點，不然她可能會把你送到你知道的那個地方去。

column1: He's not a bad poet, but don't let him know it.  It might go from bad to verse.
column2: 他這詩人還不賴啦,可別讓他知道——不然只會從糟糕變成「更詩」糟糕。

column1: I roll scrolls and sell spells.
column2: 我捲卷軸,也賣法術。

column1: Purrfect Purrsons.
column2: 完喵人選。

column1: The Dervish of the Desert oversees his own oasis as he sits upon the sands by the shore of his small spring.  It is somewhere south of Shapeir and in inches a bit east, If you're going to go and gab with him you'd better take a beast.
column2: 沙漠苦行僧坐在自己那片小泉水畔的沙地上，看守著他的綠洲。那地方大概在夏皮爾南方，再往東一點點。如果你要去找他聊聊，最好帶頭坐騎去。

column1: Keapon Laffin is my name.  Ask me again, and I'll tell you the same.
column2: 我叫奇朋·拉芬。再問一次，我還是這個答案。

column1: Ancient history is such a mystery, that's what it is to me.
column2: 古老的歷史對我來說，就是個謎團。

column1: Too hot to handle, the Force Bolt's my candle
column2: 燙手到難以招架，力擊咒就是我的蠟燭。

column1: Fresh out of stock on that one, although I use it myself.
column2: 那個剛好缺貨啦,雖說我自己也常用呢。

column1: My life's an open book and so are my wares, but I can't help you with that one, Son.
column2: 我的人生跟我的貨品一樣，攤開來給人看，但那件事我幫不上忙，孩子。

column1: Zap's for saps.  Force is the Source.  Besides, I don't carry that one.
column2: 電擊咒是給笨蛋用的，力量的根源在於力擊咒。再說，我也沒進那個貨。

column1: Turn about's fair play, that's what I always say.  But the last time I saw a Reversal was...  well, I don't give out my age and neither does anyone else.
column2: 風水輪流轉，我一向這麼說。但我上次見到「逆轉咒」是什麼時候……這個嘛，我不會透露我的年紀，別人也一樣。

column1: Go find a fellow who'll loan you his bellows.  And if you can't play, just take them away, Jay.
column2: 去找個願意借你風箱的傢伙吧。要是借不來,那就直接拿走,小老弟。

column1: SQUEEEEEEK!!
column2: 吱吱吱吱吱！！

column1: Did you here that noise, Kamar?  See what it might be!
column2: 你聽到那聲音了嗎，卡瑪？去看看是什麼！

column1: You can't see anything in the dark wardrobe.
column2: 衣櫃裡一片漆黑，你什麼也看不見。

column1: You are in the front room of a modestly appointed house.  It is sparsely furnished.
column2: 你身處一間陳設簡樸的屋子前廳，裡頭家具寥寥無幾。

column1: The pots on the shelf are pretty, but pretty worthless.
column2: 架上的陶罐挺好看，但也挺不值錢。

column1: There is, however, a silver tea service on the top shelf which looks valuable.
column2: 不過最上層架子上有一套銀製茶具,看起來挺值錢。

column1: You see an open trapdoor.
column2: 你看見一扇敞開的活板門。

column1: You see a trapdoor concealed in the floor.
column2: 你看見地板上藏著一扇活板門。

column1: That is a good idea.  You should do that.
column2: 這主意不錯，你該這麼做。

column1: Definitely.
column2: 當然。

column1: The small carpet is firmly attached to the floor.
column2: 這張小地毯牢牢地固定在地板上。

column1: You have already rolled up the carpet.
column2: 你已經把地毯捲起來了。

column1: You have to roll it up before you can unroll it.
column2: 你得先把它捲起來，才能把它攤開。

column1: You can't seem to find a lock to pick.
column2: 你似乎找不到可以撬的鎖。

column1: You have already opened the locked chest.
column2: 你已經把上鎖的箱子打開了。

column1: You don't see anything to close.
column2: 你沒看到有什麼可以關上的東西。

column1: The chest is already closed.
column2: 箱子已經關上了。

column1: You don't see anything to open.
column2: 你沒看到有什麼可以打開的東西。

column1: The chest is already open.
column2: 箱子已經開了。

column1: The chest is locked.
column2: 箱子上了鎖。

column1: You don't see any chest here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到任何箱子。

column1: The chest is far too heavy to lift out of this hole.
column2: 這箱子太重了，沒辦法從這個洞裡搬出來。

column1: You've already taken all the dinars.
column2: 你已經把所有的第納爾都拿走了。

column1: You don't see any dinars here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到任何第納爾。

column1: Hiding there would be very difficult.
column2: 要躲在那裡會非常困難。

column1: You're standing tall already.
column2: 你已經站得直挺挺的了。

column1: You find nothing of value.
column2: 你沒找到任何有價值的東西。

column1: You see no chest here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到箱子。

column1: You just can't do that inside the wardrobe.
column2: 你在衣櫃裡就是沒辦法這麼做。

column1: You find a trapdoor concealed below this carpet.
column2: 你發現這塊地毯底下藏著一道暗門。

column1: The chest's lock yields to your lock picking skill, the lid swings open, and you see some dinars within.
column2: 箱子的鎖敵不過你的撬鎖技巧，箱蓋應聲彈開，你看見裡頭有些第納爾。

column1: You find sixty dinars which you quickly put away.
column2: 你發現六十枚第納爾，趕緊把它們收了起來。

column1: Searching further, you find a false bottom in the chest.   Beneath it, you find a silver dagger which you take and stash away.
column2: 再進一步搜尋，你發現箱子裡有個夾層。夾層下方，你找到一把銀匕首，隨即拿起藏好。

column1: You hear the sound of someone singing something about a "Whirling dervisssh" and the sound of a key trying to find a lock.
column2: 你聽見有人哼著什麼「旋轉的苦行僧~」之類的小調,還夾雜著一把鑰匙東戳西戳、找不著鎖孔的聲音。

column1: Is that you, Kareem?
column2: 是你嗎,卡林姆?

column1: Yesh, Fathher.
column2: 係∼父親∼

column1: Lucky for you, Kareem was too inebriated to see you.   Better not press your luck next time.  Find a good place to hide.
column2: 算你走運，卡林姆醉得根本沒看到你。下次可別太挑戰運氣了，找個好地方躲起來吧。

column1: You look pretty silly hiding in the corner.  You were just lucky that Kareem was very, very drunk.
column2: 你躲在角落裡的樣子挺傻的。只是運氣好，卡林姆喝得非常非常醉。

column1: You hear a voice outside.
column2: 你聽見外頭傳來一個聲音。

column1: Now, is it this door?
column2: 那麼,是這扇門嗎?

column1: I think I just passed the alleyway.  But was I coming or going?
column2: 我好像剛走過那條巷子。可我這是要回家,還是要出門來著?

column1: What was it father told me?  If the key around my neck opens the lock... Ah!  This must be my house.
column2: 父親跟我說了什麼來著？如果我脖子上的鑰匙能打開這把鎖……啊！這一定是我家。

column1: You hear a key turning in the lock.
column2: 你聽見鑰匙在鎖孔裡轉動的聲音。

column1: Is that you, Abdul?
column2: 是你嗎,阿布杜?

column1: I think so, Father.
column2: 我想是吧,父親。

column1: Father, why is your secret hiding place open?
column2: 父親,你的祕密藏寶處怎麼是開著的?

column1: WHAT??!!!!
I wonder why father would have left the carpet rolled up?  Oh well...
column2: 真納悶父親怎麼會把地毯捲起來放著？算了……

column1: You were lucky that Abdul wasn't smart enough to suspect a buglary.  Next time, you'd best replace the rug.
column2: 算你走運，阿布杜爾沒聰明到會起疑有人闖空門。下次你最好把地毯鋪回原位。

column1: It's a good thing you are lucky, because you sure pick lousy places to hide.
column2: 幸虧你運氣好,因為你挑的藏身地點實在是爛透了。

column1: Your senses tell you that it might be safe to come out now.
column2: 你的直覺告訴你，現在出來應該安全了。

column1: Have we met before?
column2: 咱們以前見過嗎?

column1: I'm Abdul, the youngest son of Abu bin Ma'amar.
column2: 我是阿布杜,阿布·賓·馬瑪爾家最小的兒子。

column1: Why are you fooling with father's secret hiding place which I'm not supposed ever to mention?
column2: 你幹嘛亂動父親的秘密藏匿處？那地方我本來就不該提起的。

column1: What are you doing in our house this time of night?
column2: 三更半夜的，你在我們家做什麼？

column1: Kamar!  See what foolishness your brother is speaking of.
column2: 卡瑪爾!快來瞧瞧你弟弟在胡說八道些什麼。

column1: Grab him!
column2: 抓住他!

column1: Grab whom?  Oh, him!
column2: 抓住誰?喔,他呀!

column1: You hear two men talking outside the door.
column2: 你聽見門外有兩個人在交談。

column1: Wasn't that fun today?  It isn't often we get to torture a thief.  If you ask me, criminals have too many rights these days.
column2: 今天玩得夠爽吧?難得有個小偷可以拷打。要我說啊,這年頭罪犯的權利還真是太多了。

column1: Boy, yeah!  Can't wait 'till tomorrow when we get to cut off his OTHER hand!!  Har har.
column2: 是啊，沒錯！等不及明天了，我們可以把他另一隻手也砍了！哈哈哈。

column1: Yeah, then he'll have no RIGHTS LEFT!  Ho ho ho.
column2: 對啊，這樣他就左右手都沒了，什麼「權利」也不剩了！呵呵呵。

column1: See ya tomorrow, Jabbie, old buddy!
column2: 明天見啦,老兄賈比!

column1: Right! Ho ho ho!!
column2: 沒錯！呵呵呵！！

column1: (back-slap)
column2: （拍背大笑）

column1: Oh, rat-dung!  You made me drop my keys.  Where'd they go?
column2: 哎呀,該死!被你害得鑰匙掉了。跑哪去了?

column1: Here they are.  See you tomorrow, Baddhi.
column2: 在這兒。明天見,巴迪。

column1: Kamar!  Come here!  There is a thief in the house!
column2: 卡馬爾!過來!家裡有小偷!

column1: Yes, Father.  But why is the carpet not over the secret trapdoor?
column2: 是,父親。可是地毯怎麼沒蓋在那道暗門上?

column1: WHAT??!!!
column2: 什麼？！！

column1: I have returned from the late watch, Father.
column2: 我守完夜班回來了,父親。

column1: Father, that was not me.  There is someone else in the house!
column2: 父親,剛剛那不是我。屋裡還有別人!

column1: You hear the voice of an old man.
column2: 你聽見一位老人的聲音。

column1: Is that you, Kamar?
column2: 是你嗎,卡瑪爾?

column1: You hear another voice.
column2: 你聽見了另一個聲音。

column1: No, Father.
column2: 不是,父親。

column1: But Faddher, that wasn' me who jusht said that.
column2: 可是爹啊,剛剛說話的不是我啦。

column1: Oh, I thought I was in here.  I guess I must be in the other room.
column2: 喔,我還以為我人在這間呢。看來我準是在另一個房間。

column1: No, Fathher.
column2: 不是,爹啊。

column1: Kamar!  There is someone in the house!
column2: 卡瑪爾!屋裡有人!

column1: Is that you, Jabbar?
column2: 是你嗎,賈巴爾?

column1: My Sons! Kamar! Kareem! Abdul! Jabbar! The house is being robbed!
column2: 我的兒子們!卡瑪爾!卡林姆!阿布杜!賈巴爾!家裡進了賊啦!

column1: That's a bad idea, unless you LIKE getting caught.
column2: 這主意很糟，除非你「喜歡」被抓包。

column1: The trial was shorter than you expected, and now so are you.   A full head shorter to be exact.
column2: 審判比你預期的還要短，而你現在也「矮」了一截——確切地說，矮了整整一顆頭。

column1: You can't fetch someone that big.
column2: 你沒辦法把那麼龐大的東西招來。

column1: He's hurt a little, but you're also making him more angry.
column2: 他受了點傷，但你也讓他更加惱怒了。

column1: A good thief knows worthless pottery when he sees it, and this is it.  It's not worth your trouble.
column2: 優秀的盜賊一眼就能看出不值錢的陶器，而這就是一例。不值得你費心。

column1: The wooden door's latch seems to be stuck.  It doesn't respond.
column2: 木門的門閂似乎卡住了,毫無反應。

column1: You can't open the rug.
column2: 你沒辦法打開地毯。

column1: The trapdoor swings open.
column2: 活板門應聲打開。

column1: The chest's lock springs open, the lid swings wide, and you see some dinars within.
column2: 箱鎖彈開,蓋子大張,你看見裡頭有些第納爾。

column1: You can't fetch the rug.  It's much too large.
column2: 你沒辦法把地毯招來，它實在太大了。

column1: You can't fetch the trapdoor.
column2: 你沒辦法把活板門招來。

column1: You've already taken the dinars from the chest.
column2: 你已經把第納爾從箱子裡拿走了。

column1: Your spell latches on to the sixty dinars.
column2: 你的咒語鎖定了那六十枚第納爾。

column1: You can't fetch the chest.  It's much too heavy.
column2: 你沒辦法把箱子招來，它實在太重了。

column1: You don't see any trapdoor here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到任何活板門。

column1: The trapdoor is closed.
column2: 暗門關著。

column1: You see a large chest.
column2: 你看見一個大箱子。

column1: squeeeeek!
column2: 吱吱吱！

column1: You see the heavy chest, its lid still open.
column2: 你看見那口沉重的箱子，箱蓋仍然開著。

column1: You see a heavy, locked chest concealed beneath the floorboards.
column2: 你看見地板下藏著一口沉重上鎖的箱子。

column1: You take the tea service and stash it away.
column2: 你拿走了那套茶具,順手藏進懷裡。

column1: The doors are difficult to open.  The hinges make a loud squeaking sound.
column2: 這些門很難打開，鉸鏈發出刺耳的吱嘎聲。

column1: Fortunately, nobody seems to have heard it.
column2: 所幸,似乎沒有人聽見那聲響。

column1: The doors open slowly.  The hinges make a loud squeaking sound.
column2: 櫥門緩緩開啟,鉸鏈發出一陣刺耳的嘎吱聲。

column1: Someone really should oil those hinges.
column2: 真該有人替那些鉸鏈上點油了。

column1: You need to get closer to the wardrobe first.
column2: 你得先靠近衣櫃才行。

column1: You don't have any oil.
column2: 你沒有任何油。

column1: Get closer to the wardrobe.
column2: 靠近衣櫃一點。

column1: The hinges look well oiled.
column2: 這些鉸鏈看起來上油上得很好。

column1: The hinges look very rusty.  Someone should oil them.
column2: 這些鉸鏈看起來鏽得很嚴重，該有人替它們上油了。

column1: You find an emerald-studded silver bowl and stash it away.
column2: 你找到一只鑲滿翡翠的銀碗,順手藏了起來。

column1: The wardrobe's door isn't open.
column2: 衣櫃的門沒有打開。

column1: The wardrobe's doors are already closed.
column2: 衣櫃的門已經關上了。

column1: You're too busy right now.
column2: 你現在正忙著，脫不開身。

column1: As it strikes the wardrobe, the sound alerts the old man.  He calls out for his sons.  They are less than gentle with you.
column2: 它撞上了衣櫥,那聲響驚動了老人。他喚來了兒子們。他們對你可一點都不客氣。

column1: Just what do you want to do with the door?
column2: 你到底想對這扇門做什麼？

column1: It is a locked door.
column2: 這是一扇上鎖的門。

column1: The door is solidly mounted upon its hinges.
column2: 這扇門牢牢地裝在鉸鏈上。

column1: The lamp burns brightly, lighting this otherwise darkened dead-end.
column2: 那盞燈燃得正旺,照亮了這條原本漆黑的死巷。

column1: The simple lamp is not worth your while.
column2: 這盞簡陋的燈不值得你費心。

column1: Your mind is a jumble of half-completed thoughts.
column2: 你腦中盡是一堆殘缺不全的念頭。

column1: The room is in total darkness as you enter. You can hear some movement around you.
column2: 你走進房間時，四周一片漆黑。你能聽見周圍傳來一些動靜。

column1: Stand where you are. You now face the most important decision of your life....
column2: 站在原地。你現在面臨人生中最重要的決定……

column1: Are you a man, or just a craven coward?
column2: 你到底是條漢子,還是個貪生怕死的懦夫?

column1: Stand and obey, or now run away!
column2: 站著服從，還是現在逃跑！

column1: You hear heavy footsteps beside you as something grabs you and starts to take away your equipment
column2: 你聽見身旁傳來沉重的腳步聲，某樣東西抓住了你，開始拿走你的裝備

column1: You find you are being dragged by your arms.
column2: 你發現自己被人拖著手臂拉走。

column1: You find yourself turned around, and something heavy and cold is attached to your arms with a solid "clank"
column2: 你被轉過身，一陣沉重冰冷的東西「鏗」一聲銬上了你的雙臂

column1: You have 10 seconds to retrieve your weapon.
column2: 你有10秒的時間取回你的武器。

column1: There is no one who wants to chit-chat.
column2: 沒有人想閒聊。

column1: It's the only light in the room.
column2: 這是房間裡唯一的光源。

column1: It has been left standing in the far corner.
column2: 它被留在遠處的角落。

column1: It's standing in the far corner with the shield.
column2: 它和盾牌一起立在遠處的角落。

column1: It looks like the only way in or out.
column2: 這看起來是唯一的進出口。

column1: The ceiling is covered in goldleaf.
column2: 天花板貼滿了金箔。

column1: The floor is paved with large tiles.
column2: 地板是用大塊的石磚鋪成的。

column1: I guess this is the EOF hall.
column2: 我猜這裡就是EOF大廳。

column1: Fighter, what fighter?  I don't see no stinking fighter.
column2: 戰士？什麼戰士？我可沒看到什麼臭戰士。

column1: These chains don't look very strong.
column2: 這些鎖鏈看起來不太堅固。

column1: Of course!  You're trying, you're trying!
column2: 當然！你正在努力，你正在努力！

column1: You're hardly in a position to do that now.
column2: 你現在可沒資格做這件事。

column1: You'd better think of some way to get out of those shackles... and make it snappy!
column2: 你最好想個法子掙脫那些鐐銬……動作快點!

column1: One!
column2: 一！

column1: Two!
column2: 二！

column1: Three!
column2: 三！

column1: Four!
column2: 四！

column1: Five!
column2: 五！

column1: Six!
column2: 六！

column1: Seven!
column2: 七！

column1: Eight!
column2: 八！

column1: Nine!
column2: 九！

column1: TEN!
column2: 十！

column1: Some Hero YOU are!
column2: 你這算哪門子英雄！

column1: CHEAT!  CHEAT!!!
column2: 作弊！作弊！！！

column1: You retrieve your equipment and turn to face your adversary.
column2: 你取回裝備，轉身面對你的對手。

column1: The Eternal Order of Fighters has no need for fools who can't obey a simple order.  Go away!  You'll never be a real Hero!
column2: 永恆戰士團不需要連簡單命令都無法遵守的蠢貨。滾開！你永遠成不了真正的英雄！

column1: You were given an order.  Obey!
column2: 已經給你下令了。服從!

column1: You hear a voice from inside...
column2: 你聽見裡頭傳來一個聲音……

column1: You do not need the light.  Put it away.
column2: 你用不著燈火,把它收起來。

column1: Enter the darkness!
column2: 步入黑暗吧！

column1: You extinquish your lamp.
column2: 你熄滅了油燈。

column1: You're in a blind alley.  There's a door at the end.  The street sign reads 'Saif Darb.'
column2: 你身在一條死巷裡,盡頭有一道門。路牌上寫著「賽夫·達布」。

column1: You've been locked out.
column2: 你被鎖在外頭了。

column1: The door is partly open.  It is very, very dark on the other side.
column2: 門半開著。另一側非常、非常黑暗。

column1: The door looks like it's locked and barred.
column2: 這扇門看起來上了鎖並加了門閂。

column1: You hear no sounds coming from inside the building.
column2: 你聽不到建築內傳出任何聲音。

column1: All you hear is a ringing in your own ears.
column2: 你只聽見自己耳中的嗡嗡聲。

column1: That's good.  You obeyed at least one order.  Now walk through the door.
column2: 很好,你至少服從了一道命令。現在,穿過那道門。

column1: Ok, you drop it.
column2: 好吧，你丟掉了它。

column1: You speed to the door, throw it open, and head for the alley.  You trip over the door's threshold and land sprawling on your face.
column2: 你飛奔到門邊,一把推開門,朝巷子衝去。結果被門檻一絆,整個人臉朝下摔了個狗吃屎。

column1: You are grabbed and roughly tossed through the door back into the alley.
column2: 你被人一把抓住,粗魯地從門口扔了出去,摔回巷子裡。

column1: The Eternal Order of Fighters has no need for cheaters who try to pull dirty tricks in our combat test.  You'll never be a real Hero!
column2: 永恆戰士團不需要在戰鬥測試中耍陰招的騙子。你永遠成不了真正的英雄！

column1: Except for a major headache, along with some cuts and bruises, you seem to be all in one piece.  You even have your equipment back.
column2: 除了一陣劇烈頭痛，加上幾處割傷和瘀青之外，你似乎全身完好。你甚至拿回了你的裝備。

column1: There is a message scrawled on the stone alley floor.  It says:  "The Eternal Order of Fighters does not want Wimps and Losers."
column2: 石板巷地上潦草地寫著一行字:「永恆戰士團不收窩囊廢和魯蛇。」

column1: Couldn't even break out of the chains.  Whatta wimp!
column2: 連鎖鏈都掙不開,真是個窩囊廢!

column1: You knock three times.
column2: 你敲了三下。

column1: You can't come back in here.  We just threw you out!
column2: 你不准再進來。我們才剛把你轟出去!

column1: The door is locked tight, and the sound of your knocks echos hollowly in the alley, without response.
column2: 這扇門鎖得死緊，你的敲門聲在巷子裡空洞地回響，沒有任何回應。

column1: Oh, what the heck! you say aloud.
column2: 「管他的！」你大聲說道。

column1: The door is locked tight.
column2: 這扇門鎖得死緊。

column1: It looks like a cross between your average fraternal organization meeting hall and a dungeon.  The shackles give it a special flavor.
column2: 這地方看起來像是兄弟會集會廳跟地牢的綜合體。牆上那些鐐銬更替它添了幾分「特殊風味」。

column1: You sure showed him who's boss!  He's quite dead.  Or is he?  Something felt a little funny when you stabbed him.
column2: 你狠狠讓他見識了誰才是老大！他已經徹底死了。真的嗎？你刺中他時，總覺得有點不對勁。

column1: He's at your mercy.  The voices urge you to show none.
column2: 他已任你處置,那些聲音慫恿你別留半點情面。

column1: Big little guy, isn't he?
column2: 別看他個子小,塊頭可不小,對吧?

column1: You run away.  No one tries to stop you.
column2: 你逃跑了。沒有人試圖阻止你。

column1: You're so tired you can barely stand.
column2: 你累得幾乎站不住。

column1: Now *that* took some doing!  You entered your fake initiation combat in such bad condition that your opponent managed to kill you with a wooden sword.  Nice work.  Take two aspirin, get some rest, and restore your game to a previous (and more alert) condition.
column2: 這下*可*真有你的！你以這麼糟的狀態進入了假入會戰鬥，結果對手竟用一把木劍就殺了你。做得好。吃兩顆阿斯匹靈，好好休息一下，然後讀取先前（狀態比較清醒）的存檔吧。

column1: Now that's a REAL hero.  Show no mercy to losers!
column2: 這才叫真正的英雄,對魯蛇就該毫不留情!

column1: What a turkey!  Even when you win, you don't have the guts to finish the task.  Never show mercy to an enemy.
column2: 真是個沒用的傢伙!都贏了還沒膽量把事情做絕。對敵人,絕不留情。

column1: Isn't that right, Walid?
column2: 對吧,瓦立德?

column1: Dat's right, Boss!
column2: 冇錯,老大!

column1: Your real sword is in the other room.  You have been tested to prove your worthiness to join the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 你的真劍在另一個房間裡。你已通過測試，證明了自己有資格加入永恆戰士團。

column1: You have shown through your obedience, guts, and skill that you should now be one of the highest ranks of EOF.  You are now a Brother Scorpion.  Welcome to the club!
column2: 你以服從、膽識和身手證明了自己,如今你該躋身永恆戰士團的最高階。從現在起,你就是「蠍子兄弟」了。歡迎入夥!

column1: You can fight, and you've got some guts, but you just can't obey a simple order.  We figured we'd let you join EOF despite the fact that you failed.  You are admitted to the beginning rank of our order. You are now a Brother Saurus.  Welcome to EOF.
column2: 你能打,膽子也還算有,可就是聽不懂一道簡單的命令。雖然你沒過關,我們還是決定收你進永恆戰士團。你被編入我們最底層的階級,從現在起,你就是「索魯斯兄弟」了。歡迎加入永恆戰士團。

column1: You are shown the secret handshake, you learn the secret password:  "Might is Right!", and you are given the secret membership token with your name, rank, and serial number engraved upon it.
column2: 你被傳授了祕密握手方式，學會了祕密通關密語：「力量即真理！」，並獲得一枚刻有你姓名、階級與序號的祕密會員令牌。

column1: After you pay five dinars for membership fees, you are taken into the other room to celebrate.
column2: 繳交五第納爾的會員費後，你被帶往另一個房間慶祝。

column1: The Next Morning.....
column2: 隔天早上……

column1: You can't remember what the party afterwards was like and have no idea when or how you got to bed.  You awake with a terrible headache and feel very sick.  Somehow, this just isn't your idea of how a Hero should be.
column2: 你不記得後來的派對是什麼樣子，也不知道自己是何時、如何上床睡覺的。你醒來時頭痛欲裂，感覺非常不舒服。不知怎的，這完全不是你心目中英雄該有的樣子。

column1: The voices call to you, "Well done.  Now finish him off!"
column2: 那些聲音對你喊道：「做得好。現在把他解決掉！」

column1: Kill Him!!
column2: 殺了他！！

column1: The EOF despises those who attack by any means other than sword-to-sword.  You are immediately surrounded and thrown out of the Hall.
column2: EOF鄙視任何不以劍對劍方式攻擊的人。你立刻被團團圍住，並被扔出大廳。

column1: Pesky saurus!
column2: 討厭的索魯斯！

column1: You are not likely to live long if you go that way.  One cannot travel in the desert unless one is better prepared, or joins a caravan.
column2: 照那個方向走下去，你恐怕活不長。除非準備更充分，或是加入商隊，否則沒人能在沙漠中旅行。

column1: You just can't take a hint, can you?  The Saurus you should have bought went to the wrong customer, and the entire free world will soon be enslaved as a result.
column2: 你就是聽不懂暗示,對吧?你本該買下的那頭索魯斯落到了不對的客人手裡,結果整個自由世界很快就要淪為奴隸了。

column1: I will be honored to put your saurus back into the stable for you, Effendi.
column2: 我很榮幸能為您把索魯斯牽回馬廄，閣下。

column1: Outside of the main gate of Shapeir is a corral for the townspeople's sauruses (saurii?...you decide).
column2: 夏皮爾的正門外有一個圍欄，是給城裡居民的索魯斯（索魯斯們？……你決定吧）使用的。

column1: But today, someone seems to have opened up a business here.
column2: 但今天，似乎有人在這裡開起了生意。

column1: You are riding your saurus.
column2: 你正騎著你的索魯斯。

column1: You left your saurus out in the desert.  You would never find him if you went out looking.  Better hope he wanders in like the guard said.
column2: 你把索魯斯留在沙漠裡了。就算出去找也不可能找到牠。最好祈禱牠會像守衛說的那樣自己晃回來。

column1: Those sauruses don't belong to you.
column2: 那些索魯斯不是你的。

column1: Let the guard do that for you.
column2: 讓守衛替你開吧。

column1: You need to dismount to do that.
column2: 你得先下鞍才能這麼做。

column1: Your saurus is already in the stable.
column2: 你的索魯斯已經在馬廄裡了。

column1: You don't have a saurus.
column2: 你沒有索魯斯。

column1: The Dervish, passing a message through one of the local bedouins, has sent for you.  The Dervish says, "Man who looks for trouble comes to right place." He also says, "Good comes from evil when dark is enlightened."
column2: 德爾維希透過一位當地貝都因人捎來口信，召見你。德爾維希說：「找麻煩的人，來對地方了。」他還說：「黑暗被照亮之時，善將自惡中誕生。」

column1: So, you are the stranger the merchants are talking about.  The gate to Shapeir is always open, but never have I seen an entrance to the city like yours.  To fly in on a carpet, it must be grand.
column2: 這麼說,你就是商人們口中那位陌生人。夏皮爾的城門向來敞開,可我從沒見過有人像你這樣進城的。乘著飛毯而來,那場面想必氣派非凡。

column1: If you are exploring the desert, you should visit the Dervish at the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.
column2: 如果你正在探索沙漠，應該去綠洲拜訪德爾維希。他是位非常有智慧的人。

column1: It is good that you have returned safely from the desert.
column2: 你能從沙漠平安歸來,真是太好了。

column1: Thank heavens that thee hast survived the night desert.  It can be truly dangerous when the sun has set.
column2: 感謝上蒼，你竟能在夜晚的沙漠中存活下來。日落之後，那裡確實非常危險。

column1: Good day, Effendi.
column2: 日安,閣下。

column1: May thee be granted a happy evening, Effendi.
column2: 願您有個愉快的夜晚,閣下。

column1: There is a caravan which will pass by us six days from now.  If you wish to journey to Raseir, you will need to join it.
column2: 六天之後,會有一支商隊路過此地。你若想前往拉希爾,就得跟著它走。

column1: Good day, Hero.
column2: 日安,英雄。

column1: May thee walk safely this evening, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願汝今夜行路平安,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: The caravan to Raseir will leave here at the light of dawn in two days time.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊將在兩天後的黎明時分自此出發。

column1: Hero, the Caravan to Raseir leaves here in two days time, granting that there are no elementals in the city.
column2: 英雄，只要城裡沒有元素出沒，前往拉希爾的商隊將於兩天後從此地出發。

column1: The Caravan traveling to Raseir will depart the morning after this day, oh Hero.
column2: 喔，英雄，前往拉希爾的商隊將於明日清晨出發。

column1: The Caravan to Raseir will leave with the dawn of tomorrow's sun, Hero.
column2: 英雄，前往拉希爾的商隊將隨著明日的曙光出發。

column1: May your journey tomorrow be a safe one, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願你明日的旅途平安順遂,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: I see you left your saurus out in the desert.  Most sauruses find their way back here to the stables.
column2: 我看到你把索魯斯留在沙漠裡了。大部分索魯斯都會自己找路回到這裡的馬廄。

column1: All sauruses must be stabled in the corral.  Only if you dismount may you enter the city.
column2: 所有索魯斯都必須寄放在圍欄裡。只有下鞍後，你才能進城。

column1: My name is Uns al-Wujud, which means "Delight in Existing Things".
column2: 我名叫溫斯·阿爾-伍朱德，意思是「萬物存在之喜悅」。

column1: May it please you, I am called "Abdallah bin Tahir".
column2: 若您不介意，人稱我為「阿布達拉·本·塔希爾」。

column1: The desert is a very dangerous place for any man.
column2: 對任何人而言,沙漠都是個極其危險的地方。

column1: In the middle of the day, the sun will burn one.  At night, many foul monsters prowl in search of easy prey.
column2: 正午時分,烈日會把人烤焦。入夜後,許多兇惡的怪物會四處遊蕩,尋找好下手的獵物。

column1: The dunes are all alike, and it is easy to lose one's way.  Only if one is properly prepared can one endure, or when one travels in a caravan.
column2: 沙丘座座相似,人很容易迷失方向。唯有準備周全,或是隨商隊同行,才撐得下來。

column1: It is my duty to protect this city against monsters that approach it.
column2: 保護這座城市、抵禦來犯的怪物,是我的職責。

column1: The brigands of the desert and the swift TerrorSaurus make travel by day in the desert even more dangerous.  The brigands will happily kill you for whatever you carry  . The TerrorSaurus will hunt you for your flesh.
column2: 沙漠中的強盜與迅捷的恐魯斯，讓白天在沙漠中旅行更加危險。強盜會為了你身上的財物毫不猶豫地殺了你。恐魯斯則會為了你的肉獵殺你。

column1: Only the brave and foolish travel the desert alone in the daytime.
column2: 白天還敢獨自橫越沙漠的,不是勇者,就是傻子。

column1: The deadly black scorpion, the Jackals who are as men, the terrible al-ghuls (what you would call ghouls!) -- these creatures roam the desert at night.
column2: 致命的黑蠍、形似人類的豺狼人、可怕的阿爾古勒（也就是你們所稱的食屍鬼！）——這些生物在夜晚的沙漠中遊蕩。

column1: Only a man of great courage or foolhardiness would go off in the desert alone after the sun has set.
column2: 日落之後還敢獨自闖進沙漠的,不是膽識過人,就是有勇無謀。

column1: Ali Fakir said after you left that he had sold his last saurus and must be going.
column2: 阿里·法基爾說，在你離開之後，他賣掉了最後一隻索魯斯，得走了。

column1: The Seller of Sauruses is a strange man.  I have not heard one who can talk faster when trying to sell a saurus.
column2: 那個賣索魯斯的商人真是個怪人。我從沒聽過有誰在推銷索魯斯時能說得那麼快。

column1: A caravan is many travelers journeying together.  It is much less dangerous that way.
column2: 所謂商隊,就是一大群旅人結伴上路——這樣一來,危險可就少多了。

column1: You can see the riding sauruses in the stable over there.
column2: 你可以看到那邊馬廄裡的乘用索魯斯。

column1: They are intelligent and brave creatures, willing to obey the slightest command from their master.  They will take you wherever you wish, and come to your aid against monsters.
column2: 牠們是聰明又勇敢的生物，願意服從主人最細微的指令。牠們會帶你去任何你想去的地方，並在你對抗怪物時前來相助。

column1: Best of all, they can always find their way back to where they are stabled, so you need never get lost.
column2: 最妙的是,牠們總能自己找回棚廄,你再也不必擔心迷路。

column1: The Seller of Sauruses will be here tomorrow.  Perhaps you can purchase one then.
column2: 賣索魯斯的明天會來。或許到時你能買一頭。

column1: You can't open the gate while you're riding your saurus.
column2: 你騎在索魯斯上時無法開門。

column1: You left your saurus out in the desert. You would never find him if you went out looking.  Better hope he wanders in like the guard said.
column2: 你把索魯斯留在沙漠裡了。就算出去找也不可能找到牠。最好祈禱牠會像守衛說的那樣自己晃回來。

column1: The guard tells you the Caravan leaves immediately for Raseir.
column2: 守衛告訴你，商隊即將立刻出發前往拉希爾。

column1: You run and say goodbye to Shema and Shameen.  They give you some food and clothing and wish you luck, knowing you will need it.
column2: 你跑去向舍瑪和沙明道別。他們給了你一些食物和衣物，並祝你好運——他們知道你會需要的。

column1: As you pass back through the gate of Shapeir, you see ahead in the desert sands awaits a mass of Saurii and riders being led by the Caravan Master, a huge Centaur who orders the Caravan into motion.
column2: 當你再次穿過夏皮爾的城門，你看見前方的沙地上，一大群索魯斯與騎士正等候著，由商隊隊長——一頭巨大的半人馬——率領，下令商隊啟程。

column1: As you pass back through the gate of Shapeir, the gate guard presents you with a new saurus, a gift from the Sultan.  Ahead in the desert sands awaits a mass of Saurii and riders being led by the Caravan Master, a huge Centaur who orders the Caravan into motion.
column2: 當你再次穿過夏皮爾的城門，守衛獻上一隻新的索魯斯，是蘇丹賜給你的禮物。前方的沙地上，一大群索魯斯與騎士正等候著，由商隊隊長——一頭巨大的半人馬——率領，下令商隊啟程。

column1: Was that "No, I want to buy a saurus," or "I know I want to buy a saurus?"  You've really got to pronounce things clearly, you know.  Or maybe you don't.
column2: 那句話是「不，我要買一隻索魯斯」，還是「我知道我要買一隻索魯斯」？你講話真的得清楚一點才行。或者也許不用。

column1: At any rate, I'm here to sell you a saurus, and you're ready to buy it, right?
column2: 不管怎樣，我在這裡就是要賣你一隻索魯斯，而你已經準備好要買了，對吧？

column1: Let's get this story straight.
column2: 咱們把話說清楚。

column1: You want to buy a saurus, and I am trying to sell you one.  Does that make sense to you?
column2: 你想買一隻索魯斯，而我正試著賣你一隻。這樣說你聽得懂嗎？

column1: All right, let's try it this way.  I've got the saurus for you, and you're looking for the words to say how much you want him.  That's why you need the saurus, of course.
column2: 好吧，換個方式來說。我這裡有適合你的索魯斯，而你正在想該怎麼說你有多想要牠。這當然就是你需要這隻索魯斯的原因。

column1: So what do you say, shall we shake hands and call it a deal?
column2: 怎麼樣,咱們握個手,這買賣就算成交了?

column1: You know, I'd be willing to buy the saurus for you at this point, as long as it runs away with you on it.
column2: 你知道嗎，事到如今我甚至願意替你買下這隻索魯斯，只要牠會馱著你逃跑就行。

column1: Don't you know an answer when I'm questioning it?  Let's get down to business, so I can do a business on you.  I'm selling you a saurus, and you're trying to buy one, right?
column2: 我在問你問題時，你難道聽不出這是個答案嗎？我們就直接談生意吧，好讓我對你做成一筆生意。我在賣你一隻索魯斯，而你正想買一隻，對吧？

column1: Wait, I think I hear an answer lurking in the curtains.  You can't let those answers lurk, you know.  It's bad for their health.
column2: 等等,我好像聽見有個答案躲在簾子後頭。你可不能讓那些答案老躲著,知道嗎,那對它們健康不好。

column1: Speaking of health, a saurus can save your life.  Let's say you're traveling in the desert, you're lost, and you've run out of food and water.  You are crawling pathetically and hopelessly in the blazing desert sun.
column2: 說到健康，一隻索魯斯可以救你一命。假設你在沙漠中旅行，迷了路，食物和水也都用光了。你在炙熱的沙漠豔陽下狼狽又絕望地爬行。

column1: Then, wait, You see your faithful saurus running up to you.  You are saved! You can eat saurus steaks for a week.  In fact, you can set up a concession stand right there and make a tidy sum off of Saurus steaks.  (Just remember to cut me in for my percentage of the profit.)
column2: 然後，等等，你看見忠心的索魯斯朝你跑來。你得救了！你可以靠索魯斯排骨撐過一整個星期。事實上，你甚至可以就地擺個攤子，靠賣索魯斯排骨賺一筆可觀的錢。（別忘了分我一份利潤就是了。）

column1: Back to the matter at hand.  As a matter of fact, I was just selling you a saurus, wasn't I?
column2: 回到正題。事實上，我剛才不就是在賣你一隻索魯斯嗎？

column1: I've had tough customers before, but most speak better gibberish.  For example, do you understand the phrase, "Do you want to buy a Saurus?"
column2: 我以前也遇過難搞的客人，但大多數人講起胡言亂語都比你好懂。舉例來說，你聽得懂「你想買一隻索魯斯嗎？」這句話嗎？

column1: Ah, there you are!  I've been looking for you.
column2: 啊,你在這兒啊!我正找你呢。

column1: (Although for the life of me, I can't imagine why.)
column2: (雖然我打死也想不通,自己幹嘛要找你。)

column1: You know, you remind me of someone I didn't sell a saurus to.  Nah, it couldn't be.  Surely someone with your brains and looks needs a saurus.
column2: 你知道嗎，你讓我想起一個我沒能賣出索魯斯的傢伙。不會吧，不可能。像你這麼聰明又體面的人，肯定需要一隻索魯斯。

column1: (Either that, or some preschool and a plastic surgeon.)
column2: (不然就是需要去念個幼稚園,外加找個整形醫師。)

column1: So they call you a Hero, eh?  What kind of Hero are you, walking around like a nobody when you could be riding your noble steed across the desert sands.
column2: 所以他們叫你英雄，是吧？你算哪門子英雄，明明可以騎著你高貴的坐騎馳騁沙漠，卻像個小人物一樣走來走去。

column1: I can see it now, you in your flowing white robes, riding with the entire Bedouin nation at your side, your blue eyes blazing as you ride to glory!
column2: 我彷彿都看見了:你一身飄逸白袍,率領整支貝都因大軍並肩而行,湛藍的雙眼熊熊燃燒,策馬奔向榮耀!

column1: No, that was that Lawrence guy, wasn't it?  Well, even YOU would look less reprehensible upon a saurus.
column2: 不對，那是那個叫勞倫斯的傢伙，對吧？不過，就算是你，騎在索魯斯上看起來也會體面一點。

column1: Hi, I'm Ali.  Honest Ali Fakir.  Welcome to Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus lot - where we always know a sucker when we see one.  Pleased to meet you.  Now about that deal we were going to make ...
column2: 嗨，我是阿里。老實人阿里·法基爾。歡迎光臨老實人阿里·法基爾的二手索魯斯場——我們總是一眼就能看出誰是冤大頭。很高興認識你。那麼，關於我們要談的那筆交易……

column1: That's it! I give up.  I've tried to help you save the universe and you won't even buy my lousy saurus.
column2: 夠了！我放棄了。我一直想幫你拯救宇宙，你卻連我這隻爛索魯斯都不肯買。

column1: After all I've gone through just so you could win this stupid game sequel, you wouldn't know a hint if it knocked you in the head.
column2: 我吃了這麼多苦頭,就為了讓你能破這款蠢透了的續作,結果提示就算砸到你頭上,你也認不出來。

column1: In fact, you wouldn't even know your own head!
column2: 說真的,你連自己的頭都認不出來!

column1: If you don't buy my saurus this time, I'm going to pack up my lot and then see if you can win the game.  We'll see who laughs last.  Ha, ha!
column2: 這次你要是還不買我的索魯斯，我就收攤走人，看你還能不能贏得這場遊戲。我們走著瞧，看誰笑到最後。哈，哈！

column1: I'm Ali Fakir.  Honest Ali Fakir.  Welcome to Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus Lot.  "If we don't have the saurus you want, then you don't want one."
column2: 我是阿里·法基爾。老實人阿里·法基爾。歡迎光臨老實人阿里·法基爾的二手索魯斯場。「如果我們沒有你想要的索魯斯，那你根本就不想要索魯斯。」

column1: Of course, even if you don't want one, I'll be happy to sell one to you.
column2: 當然啦,就算你不想要,我也很樂意賣你一頭。

column1: You look like a man looking for a saurus.  I have the finest saddle saurus you can find.  A magnificent saddle saurus!
column2: 你看起來像是在找索魯斯的人。我這裡有你能找到最棒的乘用索魯斯。一隻宏偉出眾的乘用索魯斯！

column1: (If you ride one, that's exactly what you get.)
column2: (你騎上去就曉得,你買到的正是這麼回事。)

column1: You never know when a saurus might come in handy.  Why, just the other day, you were thinking to yourself:  "Self," you thought, "if only I had someone to play pinochle with!"  If you had a saurus, you still couldn't play pinochle.
column2: 你永遠不知道索魯斯什麼時候會派上用場。就說前幾天，你還在心裡想著：「唉，」你心想，「要是有人能陪我玩皮納克爾牌就好了！」不過就算你有了索魯斯，你還是沒辦法玩皮納克爾牌。

column1: (You couldn't even pronounce it.)
column2: （你連這個詞都念不出來。）

column1: It just goes to show you.  And speaking of shows, have I got the saurus for you.  The Saurus.  That's where you look for synonyms.  And speaking of sin, when it comes to sauruses, well, let's just keep your personal preferences a secret, shall we?
column2: 這正說明了一切。說到說明，我這裡正好有適合你的索魯斯。索魯斯詞典，那可是你查同義詞的地方。說到罪過，講到索魯斯這件事，你的個人癖好我們還是別說出去，好嗎？

column1: (They don't call it Saurus Rex for nothing.)
column2: (人家管牠叫「索魯斯·雷克斯」可不是叫假的。)

column1: So you want to buy a saurus, eh?  I'm Fakir, Honest Ali Fakir.  Glad to meet you.  You've come to the right place.  Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus Lot - If you don't buy one here, you don't buy one.
column2: 所以你想買一隻索魯斯，是吧？我是法基爾，老實人阿里·法基爾。很高興認識你。你來對地方了。老實人阿里·法基爾的二手索魯斯場——如果你不在這裡買，那你就是不會買。

column1: I've got a beauty of a saurus for you.  Only had one owner.  A sweet little old lady who only took it out on Sundays to go drag racing.  Or was that dragon racing?
column2: 我這裡有一隻漂亮的索魯斯給你。只有一任主人。一位親切的小老太太，只在星期天騎出去飆車。還是說那是飆龍賽？

column1: At any rate, I'm here to sell you a saurus.  I'll sell you a saurus, and I won't take "No" for an answer.  I don't know the meaning of "No."
column2: 不管怎樣，我在這裡就是要賣你一隻索魯斯。我一定會賣你一隻索魯斯，「不」這個答案我不接受。我根本不懂「不」是什麼意思。

column1: (I couldn't find it in The Saurus.)
column2: （我在索魯斯詞典裡找不到這個詞。）

column1: So you need to buy a saurus to travel in the desert.  You're desperate.  Hello, I'm Honest Ali Fakir.  Pleased to meet you, Desperate.  Have I got a saurus for you.  I don't know, do you?
column2: 所以你需要買一隻索魯斯才能在沙漠中旅行。你走投無路了。你好，我是老實人阿里·法基爾。很高興認識你，走投無路先生。我這裡正好有適合你的索魯斯。我不知道，你覺得呢？

column1: The fact of the matter is that you need a saurus and I've got a saurus, so let's get together and play pinochle.  On the other hand, let's not.  The sauruses would cheat.
column2: 事實就是你需要一隻索魯斯，而我有一隻索魯斯，那我們就湊一塊玩皮納克爾牌吧。話說回來，還是算了。索魯斯們會作弊的。

column1: You DO want to buy a saurus, don't you?
column2: 你真的想買一隻索魯斯，對吧？

column1: Perhaps there is more a brain about you than I suspected.
column2: 看來你腦袋比我想的還要靈光些。

column1: Shall we shake hands on forty dinars for the saurus?
column2: 我們就以四十第納爾成交這隻索魯斯，握手為定，如何？

column1: You certainly know how to drive a hard bargain.  Why don't we just agree at thirty for bridle, saddle, and saurus?
column2: 你還真懂得殺價。不如我們就以三十第納爾談定，連韁繩、馬鞍和索魯斯一起算進去，如何？

column1: Well, we certainly are going nowhere fast.  I'm willing to make you my best offer.  Twenty dinars and you ride away on a new (for you) saurus.  What do you say?
column2: 好吧，看來我們真是毫無進展。我願意給你我最好的報價。二十第納爾，你就能騎著一隻（對你來說）全新的索魯斯離開。你意下如何？

column1: You know, you drive a hard bargain.  I respect that in a man.
column2: 說真的,你這價殺得真狠。我就敬重這樣的男人。

column1: (Whether I'll respect you in the morning is something else.)
column2: (至於明早我還會不會看得起你,那就是另一回事了。)

column1: This is my last and final offer.  Ten dinars.  If I go any lower I'll have to stand on a chair to take your money.
column2: 這是我最後的出價了,十第納爾。再低下去,我都得站到椅子上才收得到你的錢。

column1: Now, do we have a deal, or is this a deal?
column2: 怎麼樣,這筆買賣成不成?

column1: This is ridiculous.  Here I am, a man of unquestionable integrity and dignity, forced to sell his sole remaining saurus for a mere fraction of its dubious value.
column2: 這太荒謬了。像我這樣一個正直有尊嚴、毋庸置疑的人，竟然被逼得要以其可疑價值的區區一小部分，賣掉自己僅剩的索魯斯。

column1: I will take 5 dinars for the saurus, and I'll even throw in Travel Assurance.  You'll travel, I'll assure you.  Now, shall we agree?
column2: 這隻索魯斯我只收五第納爾，我甚至還會附送「旅行保障」。你負責旅行，我負責保障你。那麼，成交嗎？

column1: Well, that was going nowhere the long way.
column2: 唉,這一通繞下來,還是白忙一場。

column1: You know, you remind me of a saurus I once knew.
column2: 你知道嗎，你讓我想起了我曾經認識的一隻索魯斯。

column1: Nevertheless, shall we make a deal at forty dinars?
column2: 不過呢,四十第納爾,這筆買賣成交如何?

column1: You wouldn't happen to be HARD OF HEARING, WOULD YOU?   SHALL WE AGREE UPON thirty dinars?
column2: 你該不會是耳背吧？我們就講定三十第納爾，如何？

column1: I've had better luck trying to buy a Glow-in-the-dark-turban.  I think I'll quit while I'ma behind.
column2: 我去買夜光頭巾運氣都比這好。我看我還是趁還沒輸更多之前見好就收吧。

column1: Seeing as no one in his right mind would actually want a saurus, you won't mind that I have the right saurus for you.
column2: 既然神智正常的人根本不會想要索魯斯，那你應該也不會介意我這裡正好有一隻適合你的索魯斯。

column1: Just to show you what a swell person I am, I'll throw in a free saddle and bridle with the purchase.  In fact, I'll even let you have free room and board at the stable.
column2: 為了讓你見識見識我這人多夠意思,買了我還免費附送馬鞍和馬轡。事實上,連在馬廄的食宿我都讓你白住白吃。

column1: (I've had enough boardom as it is.)
column2: (這日子早就無聊得快發霉了。)

column1: All this for a mere %s dinars.  What do you say?
column2: 這一切只要%s第納爾。你意下如何？

column1: Let's see, my asking price was 50, your calling price 10, I raised you 2 clubs, you doubled, I redoubled, the stock market crashed, I doubted your double, you called me (just what, I'll never repeat), and we came to a gentlemen's agreement of 20 dinars.
column2: 讓我想想,我開價50,你出10,我加注兩組梅花,你加倍,我再加倍,股市大崩盤,我對你的加倍存疑,你回敬了我一句(到底罵什麼,我絕不重複),最後我們紳士協議,20第納爾成交。

column1: (How we got the gentlemen to agree is beyond me.)
column2: (至於我們是怎麼把那兩位君子湊到一塊兒同意的,我也想不明白。)

column1: Let's see, my initial asking price was 50, you parried with 10, I gracefully riposted with 55, you countered with 20, I recountered with 60, you accounted, I encountered, we recounted and came up a couple short, so to make up the difference, we agreed upon whether the party of the first part is to pay the party of the second part part of the sums owing, but we tore up that contract and you are buying the Saurus for 50 dinars, COD.
column2: 讓我想想,我起初開價50,你擋了一招10,我優雅回擊55,你反擊20,我再反擊60,你來算帳,我來遭遇,我們重新點算,結果還差一截,為了補這個差額,我們協議由甲方支付乙方部分應付款項,但後來這份合約被撕毀,你最後用50第納爾買下這隻索魯斯,貨到付款。

column1: (I knew there was something fishy about that last part, but let's not carp now that the deal's been made.)
column2: (我就知道最後那段有點不對勁,不過生意都成了,咱也別再雞蛋裡挑骨頭啦。)

column1: After all we've been through to come to an agreement, and you have the nerve not to have enough money.
column2: 費了這麼大勁才談攏,你倒好,竟厚著臉皮說錢不夠。

column1: How much money do you have?  Maybe we can still work something out.
column2: 你身上有多少錢?也許我們還能談得攏。

column1: Why don't we shake hands, go back to our corners, and come out fighting at the sound of the bell.  Assuming, of course, that you DO want to deal.  Better yet, let ME deal.  That way I can take the cards off the bottom.  Shall we take it from the top?
column2: 何不握個手,各自退回角落,鈴聲一響就出來拚?當然,前提是你真的想成交。不如讓我來發牌,這樣我才能從牌堆底下抽牌。我們從頭來過如何?

column1: Do you think you can cheat Honest Ali Fakir?  You have to get up pretty early in the morning to do that, since I sleep in.  I need my beauty rest.
column2: 你以為騙得過誠實的阿里·法基爾?想騙我可得起個大早才行,不過我向來睡到很晚,美容覺不能少。

column1: (You should see the beauty I rest with.)
column2: (你真該瞧瞧陪我歇腳的那位美人。)

column1: Where was I?  Oh, yes.  You tried to pass me counterfeit coins.  I counter with a fit.  We agreed upon dinars, not whatever you tried to give me.  You need to see the Money Changer before we can deal again.
column2: 我剛剛說到哪?對了。你想拿假幣騙我,我可不是省油的燈。我們說好的是第納爾,不是你想塞給我的那種東西。你得先去找換錢商,我們才能繼續交易。

column1: (Speaking of the Money Changer, I'd like to deal with her myself.)
column2: (說到換錢商,我倒是很想親自跟她「交易」一番。)

column1: Why don't we shake hands, go back to our corners, and come out fighting at the sound of the bell?  Assuming of course, that you DO want to deal.  Better yet, let me deal.  That way I can take the cards off the bottom.  Shall we take it from the top?
column2: 何不握個手,各自退回角落,鈴聲一響就出來拚?當然,前提是你真的想成交。不如讓我來發牌,這樣我才能從牌堆底下抽牌。我們從頭來過如何?

column1: What you need is a loan.  That's where I'll be leaving you until you come up with the cash.
column2: 你需要的是一筆貸款。在你湊出錢來之前,我就跟你聊到這兒。

column1: You pay the man his money.
column2: 你把錢付給了那人。

column1: Just a minute and I'll introduce your saurus to you.
column2: 稍等一下,我這就把你的索魯斯介紹給你。

column1: Ah, true love at first sight.  And what a sight for saurus eyes.
column2: 啊,一見鍾情。這索魯斯的模樣,可真是賞心悅目啊。

column1: Seeing that you two kids are hitting it off from the start, it sounds like a good time to start heading off.
column2: 瞧你倆小傢伙一見如故,看來我也該識相地走人了。

column1: Hello, I must be going.  An exit line if ever I heard one.
column2: 你好,我得走了。要說退場台詞,這可真是一句。

column1: You know, that's a very good question.  You'll have to ask me another sometime.
column2: 你曉得嗎,這問題問得真好。改天你得再問我一個。

column1: I've heard questions in my time, and that's certainly one of them.
column2: 我這輩子聽過不少問題,而這肯定算是其中一個。

column1: Shapeir is God's country, and he can have it.
column2: 夏皮爾是上帝的應許之地——這地方就送祂吧。

column1: I used to dream about a girl with light brown hair and that name.
column2: 我以前常夢見一個淺棕髮的姑娘,就叫那名字。

column1: There's a lot of big game around here.  You know, pinochle, blackjack, soccer ...
column2: 這附近的「大場面」可不少喔,你知道的,梅花拱豬、二十一點、足球……

column1: It's spring, and there is magic in the air!  Well, actually, it's summer, and there is something else in the air considering our proximity to the stable.
column2: 這是春天,空氣裡瀰漫著魔法的氣息!呃,其實現在是夏天,而且我們離馬廄這麼近,空氣裡瀰漫的其實是別的東西。

column1: If you think that's insulting, you haven't heard anything yet.
column2: 你要是覺得這話很傷人,那你還沒見識到後頭呢。

column1: I've played in the palace before the queen.  Fortunately, the king wasn't around.
column2: 我曾在宮殿裡,當著皇后的面出牌。還好國王不在場。

column1: To travel the desert around here, you need a good saurus.  A good saurus will never desert you.  However, the saurus I have for you is your just desserts.
column2: 要在這一帶穿越沙漠,你需要一隻好索魯斯。好索魯斯絕不會在沙漠中拋棄你。不過,我這隻要賣你的索魯斯,倒是你自找的下場。

column1: Ah, money!  The sound of centimes, the din of dinars!  Music to my ears!
column2: 啊,錢哪!生丁叮噹作響,第納爾喧鬧不休!簡直是我耳中的天籟!

column1: Speaking of money, you wouldn't happen to be interested in making an investment in the future?  You could contribute to the "Used Saurus Seller's Retirement Fund".
column2: 說到錢,你該不會有興趣為未來投資一下吧?你可以贊助一下「二手索魯斯商退休基金」。

column1: This is your chance.  All you need to do is contribute, and I'll throw in a used saurus.  Better yet, buy the saurus in the first place, and then I'll retire.
column2: 機會來了。你只要贊助一筆,我就奉送一隻二手索魯斯。或者更乾脆,你直接買下索魯斯,我就退休了。

column1: What do you take me for, a common criminal?  There is nothing common about me.
column2: 你把我當什麼了,一個尋常罪犯?我這人可一點都不尋常。

column1: If you want to talk of thieves, take the money changer.  I'd like to take her myself.  She will certainly take you for everything you've got.
column2: 說到盜賊,那就得提提換錢商了。我倒是很想親自「拿下」她。她肯定會把你身上的一切都「拿走」。

column1: You call this hot?  I've been in places where every suit was a sweat suit.  I've seen it so hot that matches struck themselves.  I've been to Fresno!  Hot?  Ha!
column2: 你管這叫熱?我去過那種地方,人人穿的都是「汗」裝。我見過熱到火柴自己點燃的地方。我可是去過弗雷斯諾!熱?哼!

column1: I've worked the fountain, but I'm no soda jerk.  Anyway, I certainly seem to be a fountain of information for you.
column2: 我在噴泉邊討過生活,可我不是賣汽水的小弟。話說回來,對你而言,我倒真像座取之不盡的「情報泉源」。

column1: Go for Harrass in the third race.  It's a sure thing unless they scratch her.
column2: 第三場賽你壓「哈拉絲」準沒錯。除非她臨時退賽,否則十拿九穩。

column1: Ah, fair Dinarzad!  She'll change your money and steal your heart...or is it the other way around?
column2: 啊,美麗的迪娜莎德!她會幫你換錢,也會偷走你的心……還是說,順序反過來才對?

column1: Steal one kiss from her and you'll probably be missing your eyeteeth.
column2: 你要是偷她一個吻,八成連犬齒都得賠進去。

column1: Actually, one kiss from her, and you won't notice that your eyeteeth are missing.
column2: 說真的,被她一吻,你連犬齒不見了都不會察覺。

column1: Now that's what I call a woman.
column2: 這才叫女人嘛。

column1: Personally, I'm a merchant.  I sell sauruses.  I'm the local saurus source.  Would you be interested in buying one?  I can make you a deal on a late model with all the options thrown in.  Options like saddle, bridle, two feet, ...
column2: 說到我自己,我是個商人。我賣索魯斯,是本地的索魯斯供應商。你有興趣買一隻嗎?新款式我可以給你優惠價,配件全都送,像是馬鞍、韁繩、兩隻腳……

column1: I'm Ali Fakir.  Honest Ali Fakir.  Honest Ali T. Fakir.  I bet you don't know what the T. stands for?  Edgar.  But you were close though.  My name is Honest Ali Fakir.  Pleased to meet you.
column2: 我是阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·T·法基爾。我賭你猜不出那個 T 代表什麼?答案是「艾德加」。不過你也猜得挺接近了。我叫老實人阿里·法基爾,幸會幸會。

column1: You know, the stuff you need when you go to the diner to buy dinner around here.  Or maybe you don't know.  In that case, when you get some dinars, I'll be glad to trade some dandy centimes for them, at say, one for ten.
column2: 你曉得的,就是在這一帶上小館子買晚飯時用得著的玩意兒。也許你不曉得。那這樣吧,等你弄到幾枚第納爾,我很樂意拿幾枚漂亮的生丁跟你換,比方說,一換十。

column1: I've seen an awful lot of sauruses and I've seen a lot of awful sauruses.
column2: 我見過非常多索魯斯,也見過非常糟糕的索魯斯。

column1: The saurus is the ship of the desert, and you can't ship it far enough to suit me.  All a saurus takes is a little grass (say, a bale), some water (around 20 gallons), and a little love, and it will follow you anywhere, which can get mighty embarrassing at times, believe me.
column2: 索魯斯是沙漠之舟,不過要我說,這艘「船」開多遠都嫌不夠遠。索魯斯只需要一點草(一捆左右)、一些水(大約20加侖),再加上一點愛,牠就會跟你走到天涯海角——相信我,這有時候會很尷尬。

column1: Picture yourself, mounting your very own saurus for the first time.  You get up, you fall off, you mount up again and sit proud in the saddle, knowing you can ride with the best of them.
column2: 想像一下,你第一次騎上自己的索魯斯。你爬上去,你摔下來,你再爬上去,驕傲地坐在馬鞍上,知道自己絕不輸給任何一位騎士。

column1: It makes cents to carry centimes here.
column2: 在這兒帶著生丁,可是「分分(cents)」精打細算的明智之舉。

column1: Ah, I wondered when we would get around to a reasonable question.  Do you have any idea how much a Saurus is worth?  What price glory?  Never mind that now.  I'll be happy to sell you a saurus for 100 dinars, but I'll settle for 50.
column2: 啊,我還在想你什麼時候才會問個像樣的問題。你知道一隻索魯斯值多少錢嗎?榮耀值多少錢?算了,先別管那個。我很樂意用100第納爾賣你一隻索魯斯,不過50也行。

column1: It grieves me to the last degree that my knowledge of that subject could never be of any use to you.
column2: 我懂的那點東西對你毫無用處,這讓我難過到了極點。

column1: I regret it very much, but I can not help you with that.
column2: 我深感抱歉,但這件事我幫不了你。

column1: The duty of a guard in Shapeir is to make certain all people are safe from violence or ill-doing.
column2: 夏皮爾衛兵的職責,是確保所有人免遭暴力與惡行。

column1: It is said that the duty of the guards of Raseir is to make certain all people obey the many laws.
column2: 據說拉希爾衛兵的職責,就是確保所有人都遵守那一條條律法。

column1: There is little I can do about such a thing except to help the merchants get away and try to keep the fire from spreading.
column2: 這種事我能做的不多,頂多幫商人們脫身,再設法不讓火勢蔓延。

column1: The Water Elemental destroys all who go near her.
column2: 水元素精靈會毀滅一切靠近她的人。

column1: An Air Elemental grows in power the more it blows.
column2: 風元素精靈颳得越狂,力量就越驚人。

column1: The Earth Elemental crushes everything in its path.
column2: 土元素精靈會輾碎一切擋路之物。

column1: I know only that it is something to be greatly feared.
column2: 我只知道,那是個極其可怕的東西。

column1: The Magic Shop owner might help you with such questions.
column2: 魔法店的老闆或許能解答你這類問題。

column1: The Sultan (May He Reign Forever) is truly wise and powerful.
column2: 蘇丹(願他統治萬世)真是既睿智又強大。

column1: The Sultan's army went out to discover what happened to the Emir of Raseir.  A great magic stopped them, and few returned.
column2: 蘇丹派軍隊去查拉希爾埃米爾的下落。一股強大的魔法擋住了他們,生還者寥寥無幾。

column1: The palace is a marvelous place to work.
column2: 宮殿是個很棒的工作場所。

column1: Traveling through the desert is much safer in a caravan.
column2: 跟著商隊穿越沙漠安全多了。

column1: The coins of Shapeir are stamped with an image of the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Rule Forever).
column2: 夏皮爾的錢幣上,鑄著蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願他統治萬世)的肖像。

column1: It is foolish to ask that of me.
column2: 拿這種事問我,未免太傻了。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild is on the west end of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在戰士廣場的西端。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn is in the Gate Plaza.
column2: 「卡塔之尾客棧」在城門廣場。

column1: The summer heat is dangerous for those who are not used to it.
column2: 對不習慣的人來說,這夏日酷熱十分危險。

column1: The Fountain is the heart of the city.
column2: 噴泉是這座城市的心臟。

column1: It is said that in Raseir, it is death to break the smallest of laws.
column2: 據說在拉希爾,哪怕違犯最小的律法,也是死罪。

column1: Her shop is at the south end of Dinar Tarik.
column2: 她的店在 Dinar Tarik 的南端。

column1: His shop is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 他的店在「群星之街」(Tarik of Stars)的盡頭。

column1: I have been by her house on Kudra Darb.  It has an eye painted upon the door.
column2: 我去過她在庫德拉達布的家。門上畫著一隻眼睛。

column1: His poetry is very good.  I listen to him when he reads in the Plaza.
column2: 他的詩寫得很好。他在廣場朗誦時,我都會去聽。

column1: There are merchants for almost anything in this town.
column2: 這鎮上幾乎什麼東西都有商人在賣。

column1: Only the Katta can stand to work the Bazaars in the summer.  The rest of the year, you cannot walk through the Plazas, there are so many merchants.
column2: 夏天只有卡塔族受得了在市集裡工作。其他季節,廣場上商販多到你根本走不過去。

column1: Foo.
column2: 呸。

column1: One must never travel in the desert without a source of water.  You will need to buy a waterskin from one of the merchants.
column2: 進沙漠絕不可沒有水源。你得向某位商人買個水袋才行。

column1: A compass is useful to keep from getting lost.  Food supplies to keep away hunger are a good idea.
column2: 羅盤能幫你免於迷路,很管用。備些口糧以防飢餓,也是個好主意。

column1: The one thing that will aid you most to stay alive in the desert is a good riding saurus.
column2: 在沙漠裡求生,最有幫助的莫過於一隻好的坐騎索魯斯。

column1: It costs but 50 centimes to stable a saurus there.  It shall be watered and fed regularly.
column2: 把索魯斯寄放在那裡只要50分,牠會定期得到餵水餵食。

column1: It is against our laws to bring a saurus into the city, since it can be fierce in defense of an owner.
column2: 把索魯斯帶進城裡是違法的,因為牠護主心切,可能會變得凶猛。

column1: The Dervish lives in the Oasis to the South.  He knows everything that goes on in the desert.
column2: 苦行僧住在南方的綠洲。沙漠裡發生的一切,他都瞭若指掌。

column1: A skareen is the distance from here to over there.
column2: 一個「斯卡林」,就是從這裡到那邊的距離。

column1: Bedouins are nomads whose home is the entire desert.
column2: 貝都因人是遊牧民族,整片沙漠都是他們的家。

column1: The Oasis is a place in the desert where there is life-giving water.  It is five skareen lengths to the South and three skareen lengths to the east from the town overlook.
column2: 綠洲是沙漠中擁有救命之水的地方。從城鎮瞭望台算起,往南五斯卡林、往東三斯卡林。

column1: Dunes are the waves of the desert.
column2: 沙丘,就是沙漠的波浪。

column1: I have seen a Griffin flying out over the desert.  It must have a nest somewhere near here.
column2: 我見過一頭獅鷲飛越沙漠。牠的巢一定就在這附近某處。

column1: Griffins tend to nest near the mountains.
column2: 獅鷲多半在山邊築巢。

column1: The bazaar is wherever the merchants are.  In the summer only the kattas can stand the heat.  Other times of year, you cannot move in the plazas for so many stands and shops.
column2: 市集就在商人聚集的地方。夏天只有卡塔族受得了酷熱。其他時候,廣場上攤位商店多到你寸步難行。

column1: It has been said that you are going to journey to Raseir.  The Katta all hope for your safety and wish for your success, Friend of the Katta.
column2: 聽說您即將動身前往拉希爾。卡塔族都祈願您一路平安、馬到成功,卡塔之友。

column1: The thing of Earth was most fearsome.  The very city trembled from it.   We are most fortunate that Shema and Shameen brought you here.
column2: 土之異物實在可怕,整座城市都因牠而震動。幸好舍瑪和沙明把你帶來這裡。

column1: It was most brave and wise of you to defeat the thing of air, Hero of Shapeir.  Again, the city is safe through your actions.
column2: 您擊敗那團風怪,既英勇又睿智,夏皮爾的英雄。城市再一次因您的作為而平安無事。

column1: Hero of the North, a thousand thanks for defeating the thing of fire.   We were forced to flee for our lives when it set the stand of Alichica aflame.
column2: 北方的英雄,萬分感謝您擊敗了那團火怪。它把阿利奇卡的攤子點著時,我們只能拚命逃命。

column1: Whatsa matta you?  You no like a big deal when you see it?
column2: 你是咋了?好東西擺眼前你還不識貨?

column1: You can't see inside the waterskin.
column2: 你看不到水袋裡面的東西。

column1: waterskin?  What waterskin?
column2: 水袋?什麼水袋?

column1: My name, he is Alichica
column2: 我的名字,他是阿里奇卡。

column1: Ehhh...you wood knot want it.
column2: 呃……你才不會想要,這木頭滿是結呢。

column1: Sorry boss, all I got is a buncha burnt wood.
column2: 抱歉老闆,我這兒只剩下一堆燒焦的木頭。

column1: Hey!  What you think this is, a charity?  You ain't gotta the money.
column2: 喂!你當這是在做慈善哪?你錢都不夠。

column1: Wait a minute!  What you think I am, a money changer?   You money, itsa no good.  You need to getta this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buya something.
column2: 等一下!你當我是換錢的啊?你這錢,不是這裡用的。你得先拿去換成第納爾跟分,才能買東西。

column1: Hey you!  Come over here if you wanta buy something!
column2: 喂,你!想買東西就過來這兒!

column1: It's hot, it's red, it's something you cook with, it burned my stand down.
column2: 它很燙,它是紅色的,是拿來煮東西的玩意兒,它把我的攤子燒個精光。

column1: You look like sucha fine person, have I gotta deal for you!
column2: 你瞧著就是個體面人,我這兒可有筆好買賣等著你!

column1: Special today only, I sell you a genuine Made-in-Japan, Glow-in-Dark Turban which says "My father went to Shapeir and all he brought me was..."
column2: 今日特價,賣你一頂正港日本製、會在黑暗中發光的頭巾,上面寫著「我爸去了夏皮爾,結果只帶回這個給我……」

column1: But maybe you gotta no kids?  Then what you want is what I gotta right here--a "Shapeir in a Bottle".  You shake it and you see Shapeir in a sandstorm.  I sell it justa for you for only twice what it worth.
column2: 不過也許你沒有小孩?那你要的就是我這裡的這個──「瓶中夏皮爾」。搖一搖,你就能看到沙塵暴中的夏皮爾。我特別以雙倍價錢賣給你。

column1: The stand seems to have been badly burned.
column2: 這個攤子看起來被燒得很嚴重。

column1: The odd character at this stand seems vaguely familiar.
column2: 這攤子上那個古怪傢伙,看著有點面熟。

column1: I am sorry, but I really cannot afford to give you any more free flowers.  Please visit the Money Changer.
column2: 很抱歉,我真的沒辦法再免費送你花了。請去找換錢商吧。

column1: You will need to go to the Changer of Money to trade your coins for those of Shapeir.  But since you are a Hero to my people, you may have some of my flowers anyway.
column2: 你得去找換錢商,把你的錢幣換成夏皮爾的貨幣才行。不過既然你是我族人心中的英雄,我還是送你一些花吧。

column1: Since you are a friend of my people, then you may have some flowers even though you have not the money.
column2: 既然你是我族人的朋友,就算你沒有錢,也可以拿走一些花。

column1: I hope these flowers will bring you happiness.
column2: 願這些花能為您帶來歡喜。

column1: You put the flowers away.
column2: 你把花收了起來。

column1: Please approach my flower stand if you wish to buy my flowers, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,若您想買花,請靠近我的花攤。

column1: My name is Lisha, which means "Quiet One" in the language of the Katta.
column2: 我叫麗莎,在卡塔語裡是「安靜的人」的意思。

column1: A strange sort of person, even for a human.
column2: 真是個怪人——即便以人類的標準來看也一樣。

column1: The strange man decided to leave town with his friend of the golden hair and strange social behavior.  He said something about going to "Fredonia."
column2: 那個怪人決定和他那位金髮、舉止怪異的朋友一起離開城鎮。他好像說要去什麼「弗里多尼亞」。

column1: Flowers of beauty, flowers of fragrances most wonderful.   Flowers to bring cheer to your day.
column2: 美麗的花,芬芳到不可思議的花。能讓你一整天心花怒放的花。

column1: The musician seems too busy to answer you.
column2: 那樂師似乎忙得無暇答理你。

column1: You're not close enough to the musician.
column2: 你離那位樂師還不夠近。

column1: You don't dare, with such a vicious snake so near.
column2: 那條凶猛的蛇離得這麼近,你可不敢。

column1: You see a Katta musician playing some sort of musical instrument and charming a dangerous-looking snake.  There are some brass coins thrown onto his carpet.
column2: 你看到一位卡塔族樂師正吹奏著某種樂器,馴著一條看起來很危險的蛇。他的地毯上散落著幾枚銅幣。

column1: The snake took a wrong turn one day and wound up listening to a Katta who likes to think he's Benny Goodman.
column2: 那條蛇某天拐錯了彎,結果聽起一個自以為是班尼·古德曼的卡塔族人吹奏。

column1: You need a snake like you need a five-pound desert mosquito of your very own.
column2: 你需要一條蛇,就跟你需要一隻自己專屬的五磅重沙漠蚊子一樣(根本不需要)。

column1: Charmed, I'm sure.
column2: 幸會幸會,真是榮幸。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn sign has been burned beyond recognition
column2: 「卡塔之尾旅店」的招牌被燒得面目全非

column1: The sign says that the door leads to the Katta's Tail Inn.
column2: 招牌上寫著,這扇門通往「卡塔之尾」客棧。

column1: Fire sale today!   I sella you real wood burned by real Fire Elemental.  Be the first on your block to owna this.
column2: 今日大拍賣!賣你貨真價實、被真正火元素燒過的木頭。當街坊第一個擁有它的人吧。

column1: You!  Stranger!  You justa in luck.  I gotta everything you need right here.
column2: 喂!外地人!你運氣好得很哪。你要的東西,我這兒樣樣都有。

column1: You needa map?  I gotta map!
column2: 你要地圖?我這兒有地圖!

column1: You needa compass?  I gotta compass!
column2: 你要羅盤?我這兒有羅盤!

column1: You needa souvenirs of Shapeir for friends and relatives?  I gotta best souvenirs in town.
column2: 想買夏皮爾的紀念品送親朋好友?全城最棒的紀念品都在我這兒。

column1: The musician nods and smiles while he plays.
column2: 那樂師一邊演奏,一邊點頭微笑。

column1: The musician has no need of your Spielburgian money.
column2: 那樂師不需要你的史畢柏格錢幣。

column1: You haven't any to give.
column2: 你一點也拿不出來給。

column1: Here you go, boss.  One compass for %s.
column2: 拿去吧,老闆。一個羅盤,%s。

column1: %d centimes
column2: %d分

column1: You pay for the compass and put it away.
column2: 你付了羅盤的錢,把它收了起來。

column1: You no gonna regret this, boss.  Guaranteed the best in town or you no getta you money back.
column2: 老闆,你絕不會後悔的。保證全城最棒,不然錢退給你。

column1: Sorry, boss--I justa ran outa them.  Maybe you come back tomorrow and I still notta have it.
column2: 抱歉,老闆──我剛好賣完了。也許你明天再來,我可能還是沒有。

column1: Lemme see if I got one.
column2: 讓我瞧瞧還有沒有貨。

column1: Here you go, boss.  One map for %s.
column2: 拿去吧,老闆。一張地圖,%s。

column1: You pay for the map and put it away.
column2: 你付了地圖的錢,把它收了起來。

column1: Gronk!!
column2: 咕隆!!

column1: Will you please take this Saurus back to the stables?   I have been unable to move it back there.   It seems to have been waiting for you.
column2: 能否請您把這頭索魯斯帶回馬廄?我怎麼也牽不動牠,牠似乎一直在等您。

column1: The Saurus is perfectly willing to follow you back to the stables.
column2: 這頭索魯斯非常樂意跟著你回馬廄。

column1: You're not close enough to the beggar.
column2: 你離那個乞丐還不夠近。

column1: He doesn't need it.
column2: 他不需要它。

column1: Centime for the poor, Effendi?  Centime for the poor?
column2: 行行好,賞個小錢給窮人吧,閣下?賞個小錢給窮人吧?

column1: Alas, Effendi, money from the land of Spielburg is of no use to such as I.  You need to exchange it for the coin of our realm.
column2: 唉,閣下,史畢柏格之地的錢幣對我這種人毫無用處。您得先把它換成我們這裡的貨幣才行。

column1: You have no food for even yourself.
column2: 你自己都沒有食物了。

column1: What a wonder!  Is it a dream?  Oh, Effendi, there is no end to your goodness.
column2: 真是奇蹟!莫非是夢?啊,閣下,您的善心沒有止境。

column1: Thank you, thank you, Effendi.  You shame me with your kindness.
column2: 謝謝您,謝謝您,閣下。您的善意讓我受之有愧。

column1: Forgive me, Effendi.  I am not worthy of your kindness.
column2: 請原諒我,閣下。我當不起您的善意。

column1: You have lured the Fire Elemental away from the Plaza.  Now you must try to weaken and capture it.
column2: 你已經把火元素引離了廣場。現在你得設法削弱牠,並將牠捕捉起來。

column1: You're already laying it out as fast as you can!
column2: 你已經用最快的速度鋪開了!

column1: There's no need to drop more incense -- the Fire Elemental is well inside the alley now.
column2: 不必再丟薰香了——火元素精靈現在已經好好待在巷子裡了。

column1: Unless you feel like scraping some off the alley floor, you'd better get used to the fact that you are incenseless.
column2: 除非你想去巷子的地上刮一點起來用,不然你最好習慣自己沒有薰香可用這件事。

column1: You should lead the Elemental farther into the alley.
column2: 你應該把元素精靈再往巷子深處引。

column1: Ok, "You worthless, second-hand, unpolishable, thriftstore-hatched piece of unwanted brass!  Your kind makes me sick!  (and various grumblings)."  How's that?
column2: 好吧,「你這沒用的二手貨,擦不亮的破銅爛鐵,跳蚤市場孵出來的廢物!你們這種東西真讓我噁心!(以及各種碎念)。」這樣如何?

column1: You drop the lamp.  It is very hurt and angry.   "I have a new flame now," you say to the lamp, "and she's the light of my life.  You couldn't hold a candle to her."  But inside your heart burns with the loss of your bright young friend, the lamp.
column2: 你放下油燈。它又傷心又憤怒。「我現在有了新的火焰,」你對油燈說,「她是我生命中的光。你拍馬也比不上她。」但你心裡其實正為失去這位明亮的小夥伴——油燈——而隱隱作痛。

column1: You have no water.
column2: 你沒有水。

column1: You should let the elemental get farther into the alley first.
column2: 你應該先讓那元素再深入巷子一點。

column1: The Elemental is too far away to douse.
column2: 那元素離得太遠,潑不到水。

column1: You barely manage to escape the Fire Elemental's searing heat.
column2: 你勉強逃過了火元素精靈灼人的高熱。

column1: Being fired really burns you up, doesn't it? Welcome to our exclusive, embers-only club.
column2: 被火烤可真讓你「火」大,對吧?歡迎加入我們這個只收灰燼成員的尊榮俱樂部。

column1: As you pick up the lamp and rub it, a small flame appears.  When you rub it again, the flame goes away.  You now have a magic lamp.  You put the lamp safely away in your backpack.
