column2: 你撿起油燈擦了擦,一小簇火焰冒了出來。再擦一次,火焰便消失了。你得到了一盞神燈。你把油燈妥善地收進背包裡。

column1: You put the lamp away.
column2: 你把燈收了起來。

column1: You don't see a lamp here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到燈。

column1: You can see the Fire Elemental burning brightly inside the brass lamp.
column2: 你可以看到火元素在銅燈裡熊熊燃燒。

column1: The lamp is made of brass.
column2: 這盞油燈是黃銅製的。

column1: You don't see one here.
column2: 你在這裡沒看到這個東西。

column1: Without any means of containment, the Fire Elemental makes a hasty retreat back into the plaza.
column2: 由於沒有任何能收容它的器具,火元素精靈急忙退回了廣場。

column1: You gonna ask me questions, or you gonna buy what I sell?  I no make-a living off of answers.
column2: 你是要問我問題,還是要買我賣的東西?我可不是靠回答問題過活的。

column1: You needa answer to that, you aska someone else.
column2: 那問題你想要答案,去問別人吧。

column1: This is a nice-a town, a nice-a land.
column2: 這是個好地方,好城好土哪。

column1: I understand the sightseeing business is notta too good there.
column2: 我聽說那兒的觀光生意不太好做哪。

column1: The Fire Elemental, he burna down this place.  I guess maybe he no like-a souvenirs.
column2: 那火元素啊,把這地方燒個精光。我猜大概是他不愛紀念品吧。

column1: Thatsa what goes in the Elementary Canal.
column2: 那是流進「元素運河」裡的東西啦。

column1: Thatsa what inherits the wind when-a Elemental makes a will.
column2: 元素立遺囑時,繼承到的就是這陣風囉。

column1: Thatsa dirty word to me.
column2: 那詞兒對我來說可是髒話。

column1: You take-a something and you make it alive.  Then you make-a the something dig a ditch.  Thatsa Elementary Canal.
column2: 你拿個東西讓它活過來,然後叫這東西去挖溝渠。這就是「元素運河」啦。

column1: Actually, itsa like-a the alphabet.  You know, "H-I-J-K-Elemental-P.
column2: 其實啊,就像是字母表一樣。你知道的,「H-I-J-K-元素-P」。

column1: I gotta great coconut ashtray with his face on it.  You putta your cigarette in his mouth and smoke rings come outa his ears.
column2: 我這兒有個超棒的椰殼煙灰缸,上頭刻著他的臉。你把香菸塞進他嘴裡,菸圈就會從他耳朵裡冒出來。

column1: You wanna know where the Palace is, justa look on the map.  You finda the palace right by the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 你想知道宮殿在哪?看地圖就知道啦。宮殿就在「宮殿廣場」旁邊。

column1: Sorry, I don't carry vans.
column2: 抱歉,我這兒不賣廂型車。

column1: Isn't that where you take-a ice cream and putta whipped cream and fudge onna top?
column2: 那不就是拿冰淇淋,上頭再加鮮奶油跟巧克力醬的那玩意兒嗎?

column1: I can no sell you something unless you gotta some dinars or centimes.
column2: 你得有點第納爾或生丁,我才能賣東西給你。

column1: I tella you where to find the Guild Hall.  You go outa the Plaza here and take-a the street until you can turna left.  You go left until you can no go more.  Then you go right.
column2: 我告訴你公會大廳怎麼走。你從這個廣場出去,沿著街走,直到能左轉為止。一直往左走到不能再走,然後右轉。

column1: You needa my map to find it.
column2: 你得靠我的地圖才找得到它。

column1: I gotta carved statue of Shapeir that tells you what the weather is like.  You leave it outa doors.  Then you picka it up.  If itsa wet, then the day is rainy.  If itsa warm, then the day is hot.
column2: 我這兒有尊夏皮爾雕像,能告訴你天氣如何。你把它擺在門外,然後撿起來摸摸看。要是濕的,那今天就是下雨;要是熱的,那今天就是大熱天。

column1: The way to the fountain?  Take-a the road Junub Tarik.  Keep going until you get wet.  Then you finda the fountain.
column2: 去噴泉的路?走「朱努布塔里克」那條路,一直走到你被淋濕為止,那就找到噴泉啦。

column1: I heard that there was a stranger in town who asks a lotta questions.
column2: 我聽說城裡來了個外地人,問題多得不得了。

column1: You want to find the Money Changer's, right?  No problem, boss.
column2: 你想找換錢商的地方,對吧?沒問題,老闆。

column1: Well, you go north until you see a way to go left, and you take-a the way.  Now you go until you see a way to go right, and you take-a thatta way.
column2: 這樣啊,你往北走,看到能往左的路就走那條。接著一直走,看到能往右的路,就走那條。

column1: You go until you can see a way to go right and you go to the right.  Then you go until you can go to the right again.  Thatsa right way.  Go thatta way until you can turn right.  Go right until you come to a place where you can go right again.  Turn left.  Follow the road and you no can miss it.
column2: 你一直走,直到看到能右轉的路,就右轉。然後繼續走,直到又能右轉為止。那就是對的路。照那個方向走,直到能右轉。右轉之後,再走到能再右轉的地方。左轉。沿著路走,你絕對不會錯過。

column1: To find the Astrologer, you go uppa the street until you can turn right.  Then you go downa the street until you can turn right.  When you see a door at the end of the street, you finda your place.
column2: 要找占星師,你沿街往上走到能右轉為止,再沿街往下走到能右轉為止。看見街尾那扇門時,你就找著地方了。

column1: You think maybe you go visit the Enchantress, huh?  Well, go north from the Plaza of the Fountain.  When you come to a place where you can go south, go thatta way.  Keep going until you go north for two times.  The next time you go north, her house is the first door on the west.
column2: 你在想要不要去拜訪女巫師吧?這樣,從噴泉廣場往北走。走到能往南走的地方時,就往那邊走。一直走,直到你往北走了兩次。第三次往北走時,她家就是西邊的第一扇門。

column1: I notta know where he lives.
column2: 我不曉得他住哪兒。

column1: I sella you what you need.  What more can you want?
column2: 你要啥我賣啥,你還能要求啥?

column1: You gotta watch them.  When you make-a deal with a Katta, it always seem fair.  They musta be up to something.
column2: 你可得提防著他們。跟卡塔人做生意,表面上總是公道得很。準是在打什麼鬼主意。

column1: My name, he is Alichica.
column2: 我的名字嘛,叫阿利奇卡。

column1: Hey you!  I gotta justa thing!  A keychain with real Shapeir brass centime on the end.  Only costa you one dinar.
column2: 喂喂!我這兒有個好東西!鑰匙圈尾端掛著貨真價實的夏皮爾黃銅生丁幣,只要一第納爾。

column1: This you gotta see!  The ashtray of Shapeir made from imported coconut shell, hand-carved by Katta natives into face of the Sultan!  You notta finda this anywhere else!
column2: 這你非看不可!夏皮爾煙灰缸,進口椰殼製,卡塔原住民親手雕成蘇丹的臉!別處你絕對找不到!

column1: Special today only -- "Guide to Sightseeing in Beautiful Raseir".  Only costa you one dinar since it only gotta one page.
column2: 今日限定特價──「拉希爾美景導覽」。只要一第納爾,因為它只有一頁。

column1: Since you my besta customer, I make you a deal.  I sella you a duck.
column2: 看在你是我最好的顧客份上,我給你個優惠。我賣你一隻鴨。

column1: I have-a here what you need 'cause you so smart!  Bookends!  It gotta donkey head on one side anda ass on the other.  Makesa great gift.
column2: 我這兒有你需要的東西,因為你聰明嘛!書檔!一邊是驢頭,另一邊是驢屁股。送禮一流。

column1: Genuine saurus foot trashcan for Mother's Day!  Show your mother what you thinka her.
column2: 母親節限定,正港索魯斯腳做的垃圾桶!讓你媽知道你心裡怎麼想她。

column1: Why a duck?  I tella you why a duck.
column2: 為啥是鴨子?我來告訴你為啥是鴨子。

column1: Say you on top of a mountain.  You on top of this mountain and you wanna get down.  Well, you no get down off a mountain, you get down off a duck.  There you have it.
column2: 假設你在山頂上。你站在這山頂上想下來。可你下不了山,你只能從鴨子身上「下」絨毛呀。這不就對了。

column1: And this a very special duck.  It will save your life.  Say you getta lost in the desert.  You getta lost and you no can find your way.
column2: 而且這可是隻很特別的鴨子,它能救你一命。假設你在沙漠裡迷路了,迷了路又找不到方向。

column1: You hungry and thirsty and you donna know what to do.  Then you take out your duck and it will save you.  You make-a duck soup.
column2: 你又餓又渴,不知如何是好。這時你掏出鴨子,它就能救你。你拿牠煮鴨湯嘛。

column1: You needa souvenirs, I gotta them.  How about a pair of Glow-in-the-Dark Socks of Shapeir?  This-a way, you no lose your feet at night.
column2: 你需要紀念品,我這裡都有。來雙夏皮爾夜光襪如何?這樣一來,晚上你就不會找不到自己的腳了。

column1: Seeing you a stranger, you gotta strange money.  You need to get the coins of Shapeir, the centime and dinar.
column2: 瞧你是個外地人,帶的也是外地的怪錢。你得去換夏皮爾的錢幣,也就是生丁跟第納爾。

column1: This is finest souvenir stand in town.  I personally guarantee it.  This is also only souvenir stand in town.
column2: 這是城裡最棒的紀念品攤,我親自掛保證。這也是城裡唯一的紀念品攤。

column1: Hey, the Fire Elemental burn down my shop.  I thinka maybe I gotta go into fire insurance next.  If I insure you, you get fired.
column2: 嘿,火元素精靈把我店給燒了。我想啊,下回也許該改賣火災保險。我要是承保你,你就會被「開除」(著火)。

column1: How you think you going to finda yourself with no map?  I sella you one cheap, only one dinar.  Special edition today, says-a "You are here" witha big-a "X."
column2: 沒有地圖你要怎麼找到自己在哪?我便宜賣你一張,只要一第納爾。今天特別版,上面寫著「你在這裡」,還有個大大的「X」。

column1: With a compass, you always know whicha way you going.  How many people you meet know that?  I give you a compass.  It only costs a dinar.
column2: 有了羅盤,你永遠知道自己往哪走。你認識的人裡有幾個懂這個?我送你一個羅盤,只要一第納爾。

column1: All right, you drive a hard bargain.  I give-a you this and you give-a me 50 centimes.
column2: 好吧,你真會殺價。這個給你,你給我50分。

column1: Okay, okay, we make a deal.  I sella you this for only 150 centimes.
column2: 好啦好啦,成交。這個只賣你150分。

column1: It grieves me to the last degree, but I have no answer for you.
column2: 小的萬分慚愧,實在答不出您要問的。

column1: A thousand pardons, Effendi, but I know not what you ask.
column2: 萬分抱歉,閣下,小的不明白您要問的是什麼。

column1: This is my home, Effendi.
column2: 這兒就是小的的家,閣下。

column1: I have heard that everyone is poor in Raseir.
column2: 小的聽說,拉希爾的人個個都窮得很。

column1: The Sultan (May He Live Forever) is a very great man and a father to us all.  He knows all that goes on in our land.
column2: 蘇丹(願他萬壽無疆)是位偉大的人物,是我們所有人的父親。我們土地上發生的一切,他都瞭若指掌。

column1: The Sultan (Around Whom The World Turns) was most displeased when the Emir Arus was deposed.  I have heard that there is a reward for restoring the Emir to his throne.
column2: 蘇丹(世界繞其運轉之人)對埃米爾阿魯斯被廢黜一事極為不悅。我聽說,凡是能助埃米爾重登王位者,將有賞賜。

column1: The Palace is the home of our Sultan, Harun al-Rashid, (May His Sons Be Many).
column2: 宮殿是我們蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願他兒孫滿堂)的居所。

column1: Money is very important to those who have little, Effendi.  Could you who are wealthy beyond belief share some with one who is poor?  The heavens will praise your name for it.
column2: 對手頭緊的人來說,錢可是頂要緊的,閣下。您富甲一方,能不能分一點給窮人?上天會因此讚頌您的名。

column1: Ah, truly you belong there, for it is a place of heros!  It is to the west of the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 啊,你確實該去那裡,那是英雄聚集之地!就在噴泉廣場的西邊。

column1: Those of the Inn are generous beyond belief, for they share what food they have left from the day with me.
column2: 旅店裡的人慷慨得令人難以置信,他們會把當天剩下的食物分給我。

column1: The weather is as it always is, Effendi.
column2: 天氣嘛,一向如此,閣下。

column1: The fountain is the center of our universe, Effendi.  It is shared by all, the rich and poor.  Without it, we are nothing.
column2: 那座噴泉是咱們世界的中心,閣下。不分貧富,人人共用。沒了它,咱們什麼都不是。

column1: It has been said that the Sultan (Praise his name!) is even now searching for a Paladin, Effendi.
column2: 據說蘇丹(讚美其名!)此刻正在尋找一位聖騎士,閣下。

column1: The Money Changer is at the end of the street Dinar Tarik, Effendi.
column2: 換錢商就在第納爾塔里克街的盡頭,閣下。

column1: The house of the Astrologer is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 占星師的宅子在星辰塔里克街的盡頭。

column1: The poet shares his wisdom and art with all, and may heaven reward him for his kindness.  Sometimes words are worth more than food.
column2: 詩人把他的智慧與才華分享給眾人,願上天回報他的善心。有時候,言語比食物更有價值。

column1: The Katta make good neighbors, Effendi.
column2: 卡塔族是好鄰居,閣下。

column1: My name, Effendi, is Shihhad, which means beggar.
column2: 閣下,我的名字是希哈德,意思是乞丐。

column1: It is a title of respect for a learned man such as you.
column2: 那是對您這般飽學之士的尊稱。

column1: The Paladin is a mighty hero who only appears where he is needed, so the wise men say.
column2: 聖騎士是位強大的英雄,智者說,他只會出現在需要他的地方。

column1: This is my only job, Effendi.  It is the job of my father and his father before him.
column2: 這是小的唯一的營生,閣下。小的的父親,還有父親的父親,做的也是這一行。

column1: I live on the generosity of all men, and remind them what it is to be poor.
column2: 我靠眾人的慷慨過活,也提醒他們貧窮是什麼滋味。

column1: The elemental dodges easily in the open plaza.
column2: 在開闊的廣場上,那元素輕易就能閃避。

column1: There is a Fire Elemental loose in the plaza!
column2: 廣場上有一隻火元素在肆虐!

column1: Hey, now there's an idea!  Maybe you should see about getting some.
column2: 嘿,這倒是個好主意!也許你該去弄一些來。

column1: You are currently leaving a trail of incense.  I wouldn't stand in one spot if I were you.
column2: 你這會兒正一路撒著線香呢。換作是我,可不會傻站在原地不動。

column1: You don't have any water.
column2: 你沒有水。

column1: Your waterskin won't squirt that far.
column2: 你的水袋噴不了那麼遠。

column1: There is a strong sense of magic upon the Fire Elemental.
column2: 火元素精靈身上有股強烈的魔法氣息。

column1: The Fire Elemental seems to like that a lot.
column2: 火元素精靈似乎很受用。

column1: That doesn't seem to affect the living flames of the Elemental.
column2: 這對元素精靈那活生生的火焰,似乎起不了作用。

column1: You can't seem to fetch the flames.
column2: 你似乎沒辦法取得那些火焰。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn sign is aflame!
column2: 卡塔之尾客棧的招牌燒起來了!

column1: It's a Fire Elemental dropping.
column2: 那是火元素精靈掉落的東西。

column1: You pick out a basket large enough to contain a good deal of earth.
column2: 你挑了一個夠大的籃子,能裝下不少泥土。

column1: I am Saba, and my husband Tasman and my three daughters help weave the baskets.
column2: 我叫薩巴,我丈夫塔斯曼和三個女兒一起幫忙編籃子。

column1: For that you must talk to Lasham, across the Plaza.
column2: 這件事你得去找廣場對面的拉沙姆。

column1: He is across the plaza.
column2: 他在廣場對面。

column1: For that you must talk to Tashtari, right here next to me.
column2: 那件事您得去找塔什塔里,就在我旁邊這兒。

column1: He is next to me.
column2: 他就在我旁邊。

column1: The Magic Shop is right here in this Plaza.  As a matter of fact, it is looking at you right now.
column2: 魔法店就在這個廣場上。事實上,它現在正看著你呢。

column1: As you look the baskets over, you realize that you have little need for a basket right now.
column2: 你看了看這些籃子,發現自己目前不太需要籃子。

column1: If I may aid you by giving you some earth, I shall be more than happy to do so.  Here, take this.
column2: 若能贈你些泥土聊表心意,我求之不得。來,拿去吧。

column1: You put away a small pot of earth.
column2: 你收起了一小罐泥土。

column1: Lasham I am called; sharing the living beauty of plants is my joy.
column2: 小女子名叫拉珊;能與人分享花草那鮮活的美,是我的樂趣。

column1: May fortune reward you.
column2: 願好運眷顧你。

column1: You don't have it to give.
column2: 你沒有東西可以給。

column1: For that you must talk to Saba, across the Plaza.
column2: 這件事你得去找廣場對面的莎巴。

column1: For that you must talk to Tashtari, across the Plaza.
column2: 這件事你得去找廣場對面的塔什塔里。

column1: The Plaza of the Fountain bustles with the activities of the local merchants.
column2: 噴泉廣場上熙來攘往,滿是當地商人的活動。

column1: As you look the plants over, you realize that you don't have the time or place to keep a potted plant.
column2: 你把這些花草打量一番,意識到自己既沒時間、也沒地方養盆栽。

column1: May this lamp light your darkness.
column2: 願這盞燈照亮您的黑暗。

column1: I am called Tashtari, which means "Sunset Glow."
column2: 我叫塔什塔里,意思是「落日餘暉」。

column1: For that you must talk to Saba, right here next to me.
column2: 那件事您得去找薩巴,就在我旁邊這兒。

column1: This lamp of brass should serve you against the thing of Fire.  It is now your own.
column2: 這盞銅燈應該能助你對付那火之異物。現在它是你的了。

column1: You're not hungry right now.
column2: 你現在不餓。

column1: You put the rations away in your pack.
column2: 你把口糧收進了背包。

column1: You try it and find that you like it.  It is soon eaten.
column2: 你嚐了一口,發現自己很喜歡。很快就吃完了。

column1: For that you must talk to Toshur, across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找托舒爾談。

column1: The apothecary shop is right here in this Plaza.
column2: 藥鋪就在這個廣場裡。

column1: May it please you, my name is Toshur.
column2: 若您不嫌棄,小的名叫托舒爾。

column1: Here is a small but lovely pot.
column2: 這是個小巧卻討喜的陶罐。

column1: You are blessed with a tongue of fortune.  You need pay me but 200 centimes.
column2: 你天生一副能說善道的好口才。你只需付我200分錢。

column1: Three dinars are the worth of the pot.
column2: 這個罐子值三第納爾。

column1: For that you must talk to Sloree and Scoree, across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找斯洛里和斯科里談。

column1: Sloree and Scoree are across the plaza.
column2: 斯洛里和斯科里在廣場的另一頭。

column1: That is most terrifying.  I do not know how I might be able to help you.
column2: 這太可怕了。我不知道自己能幫上你什麼忙。

column1: But, wait, perhaps Lasham, the plant merchant, might be able to assist you.
column2: 不過,等等——或許賣花草的商人拉珊幫得上你。

column1: And they ask, "What is a Hero?",    though the answer's very clear, He's the one who faces danger    when the darkness hovers near.
column2: 人們問道:「什麼是英雄?」，    答案其實很清楚，他是那位挺身面對危險的人    當黑暗逼近之時。

column1: He will face the fiercest foe    when another needs his aid, He will dare to defy Death    even though he is afraid, He works not just for glory    and he does it not for gain, But because he knows that others    will be spared a greater pain.
column2: 他將面對最兇猛的敵人    當旁人需要相助之時，他甘冒忤逆死神之險    即便心懷恐懼，他所做的不只是為了榮耀    也不是為了私利，只因他明白    這樣旁人才能免於更大的苦難。

column1: He won't always follow orders,    for he dares to answer, "Why?", And unless he likes the reason,    he refuses to comply, He will brave the battle boldly    even though he may not win, He will face his fate unflinching,    for he is a Paladin.
column2: 他不會事事都聽命行事，    因為他敢於反問「為什麼?」，若理由無法說服他，    他便拒絕從命，他將勇敢奮戰    即便未必能夠獲勝，他將無畏面對自己的命運，    因為他是一名聖騎士。

column1: And they ask "What is a Hero?",    though the answer's evident, He's the one who faces death    knowing that his life's well spent.
column2: 人們又問「什麼是英雄?」，    答案其實顯而易見，他是那位面對死亡的人    因為他知道此生無憾。

column1: You pick up the purse and open it.  It has 6 dinars and 25 centimes.  It also has a small scrap of paper with the words, "Quelling Chaos from Shapeir, Driving Darkness from Raseir".
column2: 你撿起錢袋並打開。裡面有6第納爾又25分。還有一張小紙片，上面寫著:「平息夏皮爾的混亂，驅逐拉希爾的黑暗」。

column1: At the west end of the Plaza of the Fighters is the entrance to the local Guild Hall.
column2: 戰士廣場西側就是當地公會大廳的入口。

column1: That was a waste.
column2: 真是浪費。

column1: The door is barred from the inside.
column2: 這扇門從內側被閂住了。

column1: Move to the door first.
column2: 先走到門邊。

column1: The doors are locked; a sign on the door says "Back at 8 a.m."
column2: 門鎖著;門上的牌子寫著「早上八點回來」。

column1: The beast has been running through the city searching for you!  There was no stopping him.  You must take this thing back to the stables where it belongs.
column2: 這頭畜生滿城亂竄找你呢!根本攔不住牠。你得把這玩意兒送回牠該待的馬廄去。

column1: You get the feeling Uhura was really serious when she asked you to return Rakeesh's flaming sword.
column2: 你感覺得出來,烏胡拉要你歸還拉基什那把烈焰之劍時,是認真的。

column1: Left Arrow . . try to step forward Right Arrow . hold your position Up Arrow . . . correct by leaning right Down Arrow . . correct by leaning left
column2: 左方向鍵：嘗試向前邁步　右方向鍵：保持平衡　上方向鍵：向右傾修正　下方向鍵：向左傾修正

column1: Is there no one of courage among you?  Will no one take my challenge?
column2: 你們裡頭就沒一個有膽量的嗎?難道沒人敢接我的戰帖?

column1: A tightrope walker and his equipment take center stage in the Plaza today.
column2: 今天廣場中央有位走鋼索藝人和他的道具。

column1: No more, Effendi.  I have lost too much money on you.
column2: 不行了，閣下。我在你身上已經輸太多錢了。

column1: First, Effendi, you must wager with me!
column2: 首先，閣下，你得先跟我打賭!

column1: Effendi, first your dinar.
column2: 閣下,先付你的第納爾。

column1: Rest, recover before you seek to cross the rope again.   You might do yourself an injury, and I cannot permit a brave man to harm himself for my gain.
column2: 先休息恢復一下，再嘗試走鋼索。你可能會傷到自己，我不能讓一位勇者為了我的利益而受傷。

column1: I have lost all the money I can lose.
column2: 我能輸的錢都輸光了。

column1: Yes, yes.  Climb up and win the ten dinars
column2: 好，好。爬上去贏走那十第納爾吧

column1: Yes, yes.  Climb up and win the five dinars
column2: 好，好。爬上去贏走那五第納爾吧

column1: Well then, Effendi, where is your dinar?
column2: 那麼，閣下，你的第納爾呢?

column1: You don't have one to give.
column2: 你沒有可以給的。

column1: I only accept the coin of Shapeir, my friend.
column2: 我的朋友,我只收夏皮爾的錢幣。

column1: We'll wager no more.  I have lost all the money I can lose.
column2: 我們不再打賭了。我能輸的錢都輸光了。

column1: But Effendi, I have your dinar.  But climb up and cross the rope, and %s dinars are yours.
column2: 可是閣下，我已經收下你的第納爾了。只要爬上去走過鋼索，%s第納爾就是你的。

column1: So, Effendi, let us see if you can walk the rope again.
column2: 那麼，閣下，讓我們看看你能否再走一次鋼索。

column1: Did you wish to wager, Effendi?
column2: 你想打賭嗎，閣下?

column1: Effendi, you must not turn away now that we have wagered.  Climb the rope!
column2: 閣下,賭都賭下了,您可不能這會兒掉頭就走。爬上繩子去!

column1: I know not.  Of what do you speak, Effendi?
column2: 我不明白。你在說什麼，閣下?

column1: It is such a simple thing, to walk this rope.  Surely you have the skill.
column2: 走這繩索可是樁再簡單不過的事。你準有這本事。

column1: You will gain five dinars because of it.  Will you wager with me?
column2: 走過去,你就能賺到五枚第納爾。可願跟我賭一把?

column1: You have but to give me a dinar, and you will gain five, if you walk the rope.
column2: 您只消給我一枚第納爾,要是走得過這繩子,就能贏得五枚,閣下。

column1: And since there is a wind today, I will give you an additional five dinars when you cross to the other side.
column2: 而且今天有風，如果你能走到對面，我會再多給你五第納爾。

column1: You are most brave.  Now let us see if you are skilled as well.
column2: 你非常勇敢。現在讓我們看看你是否也有本事。

column1: It is so simple, even you could do it!  You have but to climb the rope.
column2: 這太簡單了，連你也做得到!你只需要爬上鋼索。

column1: You collect your 10 Dinars.
column2: 你收下了你的10第納爾。

column1: You collect your 5 Dinars.
column2: 你收下了你的5第納爾。

column1: We seem to have an expert among us.
column2: 看來我們之中出了一位高手。

column1: It appears you are not yet skilled enough to make me pay, but with practice you will surely be as agile as I.
column2: 看來你的本事還不足以讓我掏錢，但只要多加練習，你一定能和我一樣靈巧。

column1: Gee, you didn't even reach the end of your rope.
column2: 唉呀,你連繩子的另一頭都還沒走到呢。

column1: At the west end of the Plaza of the Fighters is the entrance to the local Guild Hall.  Sitting near the entrance is a rather large Liontaur.
column2: 戰士廣場西側就是當地公會大廳的入口。入口附近坐著一頭相當高大的獅人。

column1: May you have a pleasant day as well.
column2: 也祝你有愉快的一天。

column1: You have truly earned the title of Hero.
column2: 你著實掙得了「英雄」這個稱號。

column1: The Elemental of Earth was a difficult adversary.  You showed yourself to be a great Hero.
column2: 土元素是個難纏的對手。你證明了自己是位偉大的英雄。

column1: The Elemental of Earth is a fearsome adversary.
column2: 土元素是個令人生畏的對手。

column1: The Elemental of Earth would be a fearsome adversary to face.
column2: 土元素會是個令人生畏的對手。

column1: Soulforge was given to you to deal with the Earth Elemental.
column2: 魂鑄劍是為了讓你對付土元素而交給你的。

column1: I am sorry, but I do not practice much anymore.
column2: 抱歉,我已經很少練了。

column1: It is not wise to try to bargain with Rakeesh.
column2: 想跟拉基什討價還價可不是明智之舉。

column1: You do not have Rakeesh' sword.
column2: 你並沒有拉基什的劍。

column1: Go and defeat the Earth Elemental before returning my sword.
column2: 先去打敗土元素，再把我的劍還來。

column1: The Elemental of Earth will soon destroy the city.  It seems that you will need to be a hero again.
column2: 土元素很快就會摧毀這座城市。看來你又得再當一次英雄了。

column1: To deal with the Elemental, you must understand its Nature and you will need to use a magic sword against it.
column2: 要對付元素，你必須了解它的本質，還得用一把魔法劍來對抗它。

column1: Please leave the sword with Uhura in the Guild Hall when you are done with it.
column2: 用完那把劍,請交還給公會大廳的烏胡拉。

column1: You take the Flaming Sword.
column2: 你拿起了烈焰之劍。

column1: Good Day.  A most pleasant one for those such as myself who like the heat, although most humans might find it hot.  Simba here, is more like a lion. He is having a wonderful time.
column2: 日安。對像我這樣喜歡炎熱的人來說，今天真是舒服極了，雖然大多數人類可能會覺得很熱。這隻辛巴倒挺像頭獅子，牠玩得可開心了。

column1: You hand the Flaming Sword back to Rakeesh.
column2: 你把烈焰之劍還給了拉基什。

column1: Thank You, It was a fine battle.
column2: 謝謝你，這是一場精彩的戰鬥。

column1: Upon that, I can say but little, for I only know a little about it.
column2: 這事我只能說上一點點,因為我懂得也就那麼一點點。

column1: That subject has little to do with the challenge.
column2: 那話題跟這場挑戰,可沒什麼干係。

column1: This city is a perfect place to improve your skills at climbing and balancing.
column2: 這座城是磨練攀爬與平衡身手的絕佳去處。

column1: I have not been back there since the Emir Arus was overthrown. Entertainers and wagering have been forbidden there.
column2: 自從埃米爾阿魯斯被推翻,我就再也沒回去過那裡。賣藝跟賭博在那兒都被禁了。

column1: I know only that it makes the challenge all the greater, for it is hard to cross with such wind.
column2: 我只知道這會讓挑戰難上加難,畢竟刮著這麼大的風,要走過去可不容易。

column1: The Sultan (May He Rule Forever) must be a wise man, for he allows those like myself to make a living without interference.
column2: 蘇丹陛下(願他統治萬世)一定是位睿智的人，因為他允許像我這樣的人不受干擾地謀生。

column1: The Emir Arus used to watch as I performed in Raseir, and throw me coins. He was always laughing.  It is a shame that he is gone now.
column2: 從前埃米爾阿魯斯總愛看我在拉希爾表演,還賞我錢幣。他老是笑呵呵的。如今他不在了,真叫人惋惜。

column1: I hope someday for a command performance there.
column2: 我盼著有朝一日能在那兒來場御前獻藝。

column1: The caravan to Raseir takes a week to cross the desert, but it is never boring.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊得花上一週橫越沙漠,不過這趟路可一點都不無聊。

column1: That is a place best avoided.
column2: 那地方,最好還是別去。

column1: I make a living by challenging others.
column2: 我靠著向旁人下戰帖討生活。

column1: I do not know about such things, but I have heard rumors that the Money Changer has some connections.
column2: 這種事我不清楚，不過聽說兌幣商跟這方面有些關係。

column1: The Guild Hall is a good place for me to be working, for many pass by.
column2: 公會大廳是個讓我擺攤的好地方,因為來往的人多。

column1: The weather is seldom windy this time of year.
column2: 一年裡這個時節,難得颳風。

column1: There are too many people and things in that plaza for me to set myself up there.
column2: 那座廣場上人多物雜,我可沒法在那兒擺攤。

column1: There have been rumors about ill omens around the city
column2: 城裡一直有不祥徵兆的傳聞

column1: I have heard that she is not above working with thieves.
column2: 我聽說,她可不介意跟盜賊打交道。

column1: The many merchants around town help attract my customers.
column2: 城裡這麼多商人,倒幫我招攬了不少客人。

column1: I never challenge Kattas.  They are too good at this.
column2: 卡塔族我可從來不下戰帖。這玩意兒他們太在行了。

column1: I am called Agi the Agile.
column2: 人家都叫我「靈巧阿吉」。

column1: I challenge you all to a wager of skill versus skill.
column2: 我向各位下戰帖,來場本事對本事的賭局!

column1: Who will dare to follow where I lead across this narrow path of rope?
column2: 誰敢跟著我,走過這條窄窄的繩索?

column1: Who will risk a dinar and his very life that he may gain five dinars in return?
column2: 誰肯押上一枚第納爾與身家性命,換回五枚第納爾?

column1: Is there no one of courage and daring among you?  Will any take my challenge?
column2: 難道你們當中就沒一個有膽有種的?可有人要接我的挑戰?

column1: Ah, the Brave One returns!  Will you take my challenge?
column2: 啊，勇者回來了!你要接受我的挑戰嗎?

column1: Golden Hair, are you willing to risk a dinar and take the challenge?
column2: 金髮的朋友,可願押上一枚第納爾,接下這場挑戰?

column1: Shall we challenge one another, Hero of the Kattas?
column2: 我們來一較高下如何，卡塔族的英雄?

column1: Will no one dare to walk the rope?  Will no one take my challenge?
column2: 難道沒人敢走這條鋼索嗎?難道沒人願意接受我的挑戰?

column1: In all this city, is there not one of you who is skilled as I?  Will no one wager with me?
column2: 這整座城裡，難道沒有一個人的本事能與我匹敵?難道沒人願意跟我打賭?

column1: I will challenge any who dare to walk this rope!  Risk but a dinar, and you may gain five!
column2: 我向任何敢走這條鋼索的人挑戰!只要賭上一第納爾，你就有機會贏得五第納爾!

column1: You, stranger from a distant land!  Are you brave and skilled enough to cross this rope?
column2: 喂,你這位遠方來的客人!可有膽量、有本事走過這條繩索?

column1: Will you wager with me?
column2: 可願跟我賭一把?

column1: This day of all days is a special day for those of us who dare walk the rope.  It is a day when the wind adds spice to the challenge!
column2: 對我們這些敢走鋼索的人來說，今天可是個特別的日子。今天有風助陣，挑戰更加刺激!

column1: Shall anyone take my wager?  Then I will give ten dinars to the one who can brave both wind and height to walk the rope!
column2: 可有人要接我的賭局?誰能頂著風、踩著高,把這繩索走完,我就賞他十枚第納爾!

column1: My noble friend, will you challenge the very element of air as well as the rope by trying to cross now?  You will gain ten dinars if you can do it.
column2: 我高貴的朋友,可願趁此刻一闖,既鬥這繩索、也鬥這風之元素?走得過去,十枚第納爾就是你的。

column1: The wind is high and so is the rope.  Hero, will you dare to wager?
column2: 風很大，鋼索也架得很高。英雄，你敢打這個賭嗎?

column1: Practice is the only way to success.  Defeater of the Elementals, will you try the challenge?
column2: 唯有勤練方能成事。擊退元素精靈的勇者啊,可願試試這場挑戰?

column1: Hero, will you pit your courage and skill against mine?
column2: 英雄,可願拿你的膽識與身手,跟我一較高下?

column1: The day is bright, and the land is free of Elementals because of your courage, Hero.  Will you not improve your skills by taking my challenge?
column2: 今天天氣晴朗，多虧了你的勇氣，這片土地已不再受元素威脅。英雄，何不接受我的挑戰，磨練一下自己的技藝?

column1: You have more than proved your skills in saving our city.  Yet you do not have the skill at rope that I have, and you never know what skills may come in handy during one's travels.  Take the challenge, and you cannot but improve.
column2: 你救了我們的城,本事早已不在話下。可走繩這門功夫你還比不上我,而出門在外,誰知道哪一手本領哪天就派上用場了。接下挑戰吧,你只會更上一層樓。

column1: Ah, you of great skill, do you think you can rest on your laurels?  I challenge you again to face the rope and see if it was skill or mere luck that you crossed it before!
column2: 啊，身懷絕技的你，難道以為可以就此高枕無憂?我再次向你挑戰，看看你上次走過鋼索究竟是靠本事還是純粹運氣!

column1: You can not get off so easily!  Let us test the rope again, that I may regain my lost dinars.
column2: 想這麼輕易脫身可不行!咱們再走一回繩,讓我把輸掉的第納爾贏回來。

column1: You have the luck of the Katta, but you cannot win on luck alone.  Come, we shall again try our skills!
column2: 你有卡塔族的好運氣,可光靠運氣是贏不到底的。來,咱們再比一場本事!

column1: Hero, you are good, but you are not as good as I!  Let us test your skills and dinars against mine!
column2: 英雄,你是不賴,可還沒我厲害!來,拿你的本事和第納爾,跟我的賭一賭!

column1: I hope you are not injured.  You need to practice at landing better.
column2: 但願你沒摔傷。落地這門功夫,你還得多練練。

column1: It is said that the best thing to do after failing is to try again.
column2: 人家說,失敗過後最該做的事,就是再來一次。

column1: Do not be discouraged.  Such things take practice.
column2: 別灰心，這種事需要多加練習。

column1: That was much better.  You are starting to learn balance.
column2: 這次好多了，你開始抓到平衡的訣竅了。

column1: Ah, you are learning.  You will soon be making a profit off me.
column2: 啊，你在進步了，用不了多久就能從我這裡賺一筆了。

column1: You are getting too good for me.  Surely I will be forced to pay you next time.
column2: 你的本事對我來說太厲害了，下次我肯定得付你錢了。

column1: So close!  I told you it only took practice.
column2: 差一點點!我就說嘛,多練練就成了。

column1: You almost made it.  It was mere bad luck that you did not cross this time.  The dinars were nearly yours.
column2: 你差一點就成功了，這次沒走過去純粹是運氣不好，那些第納爾差點就是你的了。

column1: If you can but walk from one end to the other, I will give you five dinars.
column2: 你只要能從這頭走到那頭,我就給你五第納爾。

column1: The challenge is greater today, for the wind must be countered with skill.
column2: 今天的挑戰更難，得靠本事才能對抗這股風。

column1: You have only to give me a dinar, and if you manage to cross the rope from one end to the other, then you will gain five dinars more.
column2: 你只要給我一第納爾,要是能從繩子這頭走到那頭,就能再贏得五第納爾。

column1: Since the wind is high, I am willing to offer ten dinars to the one who can cross this rope.
column2: 既然風這麼大，誰能走過這條鋼索，我願意奉上十第納爾。

column1: It takes a mere dinar to take my challenge.
column2: 只需一第納爾就能接受我的挑戰。

column1: The wind is blowing such that it is hard to balance against it.  Even I have troubles on such a day.
column2: 這風吹得讓人難以保持平衡，就連我在這種日子也會有點吃力。

column1: As the door closes, you hear the bar fall into place behind you.  The door is now barred from the inside.
column2: 門一關上,你聽見門閂在身後落定。這扇門現在從裡頭閂死了。

column1: You realize that they don't carry Hero sizes at this stand.
column2: 你發現這個攤子沒有適合英雄穿的尺寸。

column1: You really don't see anything you want.
column2: 你在這裡真的看不到想要的東西。

column1: For that you must talk to Mirak, who is across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找米拉克談。

column1: Mirak is across the plaza.
column2: 米拉克在廣場對面。

column1: The Weapon Shop of Issur is right here in this Plaza.
column2: 伊蘇爾的兵器鋪就在這個廣場裡。

column1: If this bag of cloth will but aid you, it shall be yours!
column2: 這只布袋若幫得上你,就歸你了!

column1: You should become a storyteller like my sister Sava.
column2: 你該去當個說書人,就像我妹妹薩瓦那樣。

column1: It is my pleasure.
column2: 這是我的榮幸。

column1: I am Kiram.
column2: 我是基拉姆。

column1: My nephew Shameen the Innkeeper said you were smooth of tongue!   Very well, you may have the bag for only 20 centimes.
column2: 我姪子沙明，那位開旅店的，說你口才伶俐!好吧，這個袋子算你20分就好。

column1: With a family as large as mine, I must make what little profit I can.  The bag sells for fifty centimes.
column2: 我家裡人口眾多，能賺一點是一點。這個袋子賣五十分。

column1: I am Mirak.
column2: 我是米拉克。

column1: Since what you say is said so well, it must be that I will sell it for only fifty centimes.
column2: 既然你說得這麼動聽，那就只賣你五十分吧。

column1: Alas, I can sell you a waterskin for no less than 1 dinar.
column2: 唉，這個水袋我最少也得賣你1第納爾。

column1: For that you must talk to Kiram, who is across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找基拉姆談。

column1: Kiram is across the plaza.
column2: 基拉姆在廣場對面。

column1: Since your need is great, this waterskin shall be a gift.
column2: 既然你急需,這個水袋就送你了。

column1: We live in interesting times.
column2: 我們可活在多事之秋啊。

column1: I am honored to serve you.
column2: 能為你服務是我的榮幸。

column1: The Plaza of the Fighters bustles with the activities of the local merchants.
column2: 戰士廣場裡熙來攘往，滿是當地商人的忙碌身影。

column1: You're not in a good position to do that.
column2: 你的位置不適合那麼做。

column1: There are no bellows here to get.
column2: 這裡沒有風箱可以拿。

column1: You're not quite close enough to get the bellows.
column2: 你離風箱還不夠近，拿不到。

column1: Very nicely done, Hero.  You've just destroyed the only bellows in the city.  Too bad there's no bellows doctor here, because these need major healing.
column2: 幹得真漂亮啊,英雄。你剛把全城唯一的風箱給毀了。可惜這兒沒有風箱大夫,不然這玩意兒可得好好搶救一番。

column1: We are again in your debt.
column2: 我們又欠了您一份情。

column1: Your name will be told in the tales of the Katta as long as there are Katta to tell them.
column2: 只要還有卡塔人能傳頌,您的名字就會永遠留在卡塔族的故事裡。

column1: The fear of fire is great to a seller of rugs.  A thousand thanks for saving our city, brave one.
column2: 對賣地毯的人來說,火最是可怕。萬分感謝您拯救了我們的城市,勇者。

column1: Shameen and Shema have spoken truly of your courage.  May fortune favor you, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 沙明與舍瑪所言不假,您的確勇氣過人。願好運眷顧您,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: My carpets nearly went flying by the force of the wind.  But for your bravery, there would be little left here that was not blown away.  Our people are in your debt.
column2: 那陣狂風差點把我的地毯全捲走。若不是您的英勇,這裡只怕沒幾樣東西能留下來。我族都欠您一份情。

column1: You have the gratitude of the Katta.
column2: 卡塔族對您感激不盡。

column1: It is good to know that such Heroes as yourself are around when misfortune strikes.
column2: 知道有你這樣的英雄在，遇上災禍時真是讓人安心。

column1: The Elemental of Earth would have crushed the city, but for you.
column2: 要不是有您,土元素早就把這座城市夷為平地了。

column1: We Katta tried to think of some way to say how much we were grateful.  Please accept this gift of a sapphire pin.   May it do you good.
column2: 我們卡塔族絞盡腦汁,想表達心中的感激。請收下這枚藍寶石別針,願它能助您一臂之力。

column1: You take the sapphire pin and place it through your lapel.
column2: 你接過藍寶石別針,將它別在衣領上。

column1: The shape of the pin stirs an old memory.  You recall how you used to practice with such simple pins when first learning how to pick locks.  You decide not to mention this to the honest merchant.
column2: 別針的形狀勾起一段舊回憶。你想起當年初學開鎖時,就是拿這種簡單的別針練習的。你決定還是別跟這位老實的商人提這件事。

column1: For that you must talk to Tiram, across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找提拉姆談。

column1: Tiram is across the plaza.
column2: 提拉姆在廣場的另一頭。

column1: After trying to make a deal for some twenty minutes, you realize that you really don't need the pin anyway, and certainly can't afford it.
column2: 跟他殺價殺了約莫二十分鐘,你才醒悟自己根本用不著那枚別針,更何況也買不起。

column1: Such a shame that we could not make a deal.  The pin was truly perfect for you.
column2: 這筆買賣沒成,真是可惜。那根別針真是再適合你不過了。

column1: I have no other suitable pins, Effendi.
column2: 我沒有其他合適的別針了，閣下。

column1: It has a value of 500 dinars.  Would you care to bargain?
column2: 這個價值500第納爾。你想討價還價嗎?

column1: No other pin is as fine as the one you already obtained from Sashanan.
column2: 沒有別的別針比你已經從莎莎南那裡得到的那枚更精緻了。

column1: There is a beautiful sapphire pin in Sashanan's display case.
column2: 莎莎南的展示櫃裡有一枚漂亮的藍寶石別針。

column1: The Plaza of the Palace bustles with the activities of the local merchants.
column2: 宮殿廣場裡熙來攘往，滿是當地商人的忙碌身影。

column1: Jewelry of the finest gems inlaid in rings of gold and silver, necklaces of beautiful stones, things of beauty which will last forever--all of which I sell.
column2: 鑲著頂級寶石的金銀戒指，綴滿美麗寶石的項鍊，這些能夠恆久流傳的美麗事物，我這裡通通都有賣。

column1: I am called Sashanan, the "Diamond of the Desert."
column2: 我叫莎莎南，人稱「沙漠之鑽」。

column1: For that you must talk to Sashanan, across the Plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去廣場另一頭找莎莎南談。

column1: Sashanan is across the plaza.
column2: 薩珊南在廣場的另一頭。

column1: Forgive me, but Shema would be most displeased if I sold you something which you could not easily carry and for which you have no real use.
column2: 請見諒,我若賣你一件你帶起來不便、又派不上用場的東西,雪瑪會很不高興。

column1: The finest of carpets in all of the land I have for your perusal, Hero of the North.
column2: 全境最上等的地毯都在此,任您細賞,北方的英雄。

column1: I am Tiram, and all my family makes the carpets.
column2: 我是提拉姆,我們一家子都做地毯。

column1: It is but a token of our gratitude.
column2: 這只是我們的一點心意。

column1: It is a symbol by which all Katta who look upon it will know you are a true hero.
column2: 這是個象徵，所有卡塔族人一看到它，就會知道你是真正的英雄。

column1: Only a friend can see it upon you.
column2: 只有朋友才能看見你身上的它。

column1: The city is destroyed! Great job, Hero!
column2: 這座城市毀了!幹得好啊，英雄!

column1: It is most regrettable that I am so ignorant of such a thing that I could tell you nothing which would add to your own knowledge.
column2: 很遺憾,小的對這件事一無所知,實在說不出半點能增進您見識的話。

column1: A thousand pardons, Effendi.  I could add little to your knowledge of that subject.
column2: 萬分抱歉,閣下,那個話題小的實在沒什麼能補充給您的。

column1: This is a most friendly and comfortable place to reside.
column2: 這是個再友善舒適不過的居處了。

column1: There were once many Katta in Raseir.  When the new Emir took over, he drove our people away.
column2: 拉希爾從前住著許多卡塔族。新埃米爾上台後,把我族人都趕走了。

column1: Such a destructive thing in the Plazas means a terrible loss of business and lives.  When water is thrown at it, the thing of fire merely leaps away!
column2: 這種東西在廣場裡搞破壞，商家和人命都損失慘重。潑水根本沒用，那個火焰怪物一下就跳開了!

column1: There is little the Katta can do with such a thing except suffer.
column2: 面對這種東西,卡塔族除了受罪之外幾乎無能為力。

column1: Wind of such force can easily blow our stands away.
column2: 這麼大的風輕輕鬆鬆就能把我們的攤子吹跑。

column1: It is a thing of such force that little can stand in its way.
column2: 它力量大到幾乎沒什麼能擋得住它。

column1: We know little of such things but that we will suffer when Elementals are around.
column2: 這類東西我們所知不多,只曉得元素精靈一出現,我們就要遭殃。

column1: The Katta know but little magics, practical magics.  Perhaps you might ask at the shop of Magic.
column2: 卡塔族只懂一點魔法，都是實用型的。或許你可以去魔法店問問看。

column1: The Katta respect the Sultan very much.
column2: 卡塔族非常敬重蘇丹。

column1: The Emir Arus of Raseir was a friendly man who chatted daily with the Katta merchants in the plazas.  We mourn the loss of such a one.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾阿魯斯是位和善的人,天天在廣場上和卡塔商人閒話家常。失去這樣一位人物,我們深感哀痛。

column1: The palace is where the Sultan Harun al-Rashid resides.
column2: 宮殿是蘇丹哈倫·拉希德居住的地方。

column1: Our people have always lived in the deserts around here.
column2: 我族人世世代代都住在這一帶的沙漠裡。

column1: The only coins we can accept are the dinars and centimes of Shapeir.
column2: 我們只收夏皮爾的第納爾和生丁這兩種錢幣。

column1: The Guild of Adventurers is on the west side of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在戰士廣場的西邊。

column1: It is good to have Shameen and Shema back to run the inn.
column2: 沙明和舍瑪回來打理客棧,真是太好了。

column1: The weather is always wonderful this time of year.
column2: 每年這個時節,天氣總是好得很。

column1: The fountain is very beautiful, but it is also very wet.
column2: 那座噴泉很美,不過也很濕就是了。

column1: It is told that a Hero from the north will come to restore the Katta to their home in Raseir.  So did Shema and Shameen travel to find such a one.
column2: 傳說會有一位來自北方的英雄，帶領卡塔族重返他們在拉希爾的家園。因此舍瑪與沙明便啟程尋找這樣一位英雄。

column1: The Money Changer will give you the dinars and centimes with which you need to purchase things in this land.  She is on Dinar Tarik.
column2: 兌幣商會給你在這片土地上購物所需的第納爾和分錢。她就在第納爾街上。

column1: She is most wise and powerful and one to be respected.
column2: 她睿智又有法力,是位值得敬重的人物。

column1: The poetry of the poet is most illuminating.
column2: 這位詩人的詩作極具啟發。

column1: We Katta enjoy the chance to sit in the sun and chat with our neighbors as a way of living.
column2: 我們卡塔族就愛這樣過日子——曬曬太陽,和左鄰右舍閒話家常。

column1: We are a quiet people who endure like the sands of the desert.
column2: 我們是個安靜的民族,像沙漠裡的黃沙一樣堅忍。

column1: He is a very brave and daring Katta.
column2: 他是位非常勇敢、敢作敢為的卡塔族。

column1: Shema is most wise and clever.
column2: 舍瑪最是睿智聰慧。

column1: It is feared that he has been imprisoned in Raseir.
column2: 只怕他已經被囚禁在拉希爾了。

column1: We merchants set up the bazaar in all the plazas.
column2: 我們這些商人在每一座廣場都擺起了市集。

column1: I would be honored to sell you some flowers, Hero of the North.  They are a mere 10 centimes.
column2: 能賣你一些花是我的榮幸，北方的英雄。只要10分錢。

column1: His name is Sitar.  He is shy, but a very good musician.  He performs at the Inn sometimes in the evening.
column2: 他叫西塔爾，個性害羞，但是位優秀的樂師。他有時晚上會在旅店表演。

column1: The name of the snake is Hissa.  He is Sitar's companion and music critic.
column2: 那條蛇叫嘶撒,牠是西塔爾的夥伴兼樂評家。

column1: He makes his living by performing in the bazaar.
column2: 他靠在市集裡表演維生。

column1: The Bazaar of Shapeir is in every plaza of this city.
column2: 夏皮爾市集遍布這座城市的每一座廣場。

column1: You are most skilled at bargaining.  They shall be but half the price!
column2: 你討價還價的本事真是高明，就算你半價吧!

column1: It is most unfortunate that I cannot reduce my price.
column2: 很遺憾，我無法再降價了。

column1: I sell the finest of rugs, hand-woven by my family.
column2: 我賣的是全城最上等的地毯,全由我家人親手織就。

column1: A thousand pardons, but I do not know how to create such things.
column2: 萬分抱歉，但我不知道該怎麼做出這種東西。

column1: I have many types of necklaces and bracelets, but my specialty is to sell the jewelry best suited to the customer.
column2: 我這兒項鍊、手鐲樣樣都有,不過我的拿手好戲,是替客人挑出最相襯的那一件。

column1: I believe, O Hero, that for you I have a pin.
column2: 英雄啊,我想我這兒有根別針正合你用。

column1: Here is a pin with a sapphire of exceeding clarity and color like that of your eyes, and a simple but elegant gold setting with the color like that of your hair.  It would be a gift worthy of a Sultan.
column2: 這枚別針鑲著一顆無比清澈的藍寶石,色澤如你的雙眸;簡樸而雅致的金鑲座,則似你的髮色。這是配得上蘇丹的一份禮物。

column1: The pin is a token of the Katta's trust in you, O Hero.
column2: 英雄啊,這枚別針是卡塔族對你信任的信物。

column1: The pin has a value of 500 dinars.
column2: 這枚別針價值500第納爾。

column1: May it someday be of value to you.
column2: 願它有朝一日能對你派上用場。

column1: My baskets are made of reeds brought from the east, hand-dyed and woven by myself and my family.  They cost but one dinar each.
column2: 我的籃子是用從東方運來的蘆葦編的，由我和家人親手染色編織，每個只要一第納爾。

column1: You bargain as a merchant born.  It is well that you are a traveling adventurer, or we would all be soon out of business with your skill at haggling.  It shall be but 75 centimes.
column2: 你討價還價的本事簡直是天生的商人。還好你是個四處闖蕩的冒險者，不然憑你這身本事，我們大家都要關門大吉了。這樣吧，就算你75分錢。

column1: Alas, if I listened to you, my children would starve.  You are very good at bargaining, but I am very poor and cannot take any less than one small dinar.
column2: 唉，要是聽你的，我的孩子們就要挨餓了。你很會討價還價，但我實在窮困，最少也得收你一第納爾。

column1: I have baskets of all colors, sizes, and shapes to contain whatever you wish.  I will be most honored if the Hero of the North were to purchase one of my baskets.
column2: 我這兒有各色、各種尺寸與形狀的籃子,您想裝什麼都成。北方來的英雄若肯買下我一只籃子,小的將深感榮幸。

column1: O Hero, would you but honor this humble katta by spending a trifle of your great riches for one of the baskets I have spent many months of making just for you.
column2: 喔，英雄啊，能否賞臉，用你萬貫家財中的一點小錢，買下這個我花了好幾個月專為你編織的籃子。

column1: The merchants in the Plaza of the Palace could not set their stands there because of a strange wind.
column2: 王宮廣場的商人因為一陣怪風,沒法子在那兒擺攤了。

column1: There is a strange thing upon the streets.  It is formed of the stone of the city.
column2: 街上出現了個古怪的東西,是由這座城的石頭凝聚而成的。

column1: Only a true Hero such as yourself can deal with such a wicked thing of water magic!  There is no doubt that your kindness and bravery is great.
column2: 只有像你這樣真正的英雄，才能對付那種邪惡的水系魔法怪物!你的仁慈與勇氣毋庸置疑。

column1: There is no doubt that you are the Hero all the Katta have waited for! You are truly wonderful to travel to Raseir to restore our home.
column2: 毫無疑問，你就是所有卡塔族人期盼已久的英雄!你願意遠赴拉希爾，重建我們的家園，真是太了不起了。

column1: It is a mixture of copper and zinc, minerals most plentiful in this region, and is beautiful and long lasting.  It makes very fine lamps, braziers and tea servers.
column2: 這是銅和鋅的合金，在這一帶蘊藏豐富，既美觀又耐用，很適合拿來做精美的油燈、火盆和茶具。

column1: Braziers are mostly used for cooking or for apothecary work.  You most likely have no need for them.
column2: 火盆多半是用來煮東西、或是給藥鋪煉藥用的。您八成用不上。

column1: My tea servers are exquisitely wrought, an object of elegance whenever you serve tea.
column2: 我的茶具做工精緻，每次泡茶都是一件優雅的藝術品。

column1: My oil lamps will light the way through the darkest night.  Surely an adventurer such as yourself would have need for such a thing.  For you, Effendi, I will charge but 15 dinars.
column2: 我的油燈能照亮最黑暗的夜路。像你這樣的冒險者一定用得上這種東西。看在你的份上，閣下，我只收你15第納爾。

column1: Alas, I just sold my last bottle last week to a stranger named Hadji.  He said something about a dream of Djinni.
column2: 唉呀,上禮拜我才把最後一只瓶子賣給一個叫哈吉的外鄉人。他嘴裡還唸著什麼夢見精靈的事。

column1: Though it is far below the price I must pay for such an object, nevertheless I am out-bargained and you shall have it for a mere 10 dinars.
column2: 這價格遠低於我進貨的成本，不過既然講不過你，就算你10第納爾吧。

column1: It grieves me very much, but what can I do?  It is impossible for me to charge any less than 15 dinars for such a beautiful and valuable object.
column2: 這讓我十分心痛，但我又能怎麼辦?這麼精美貴重的東西，我實在不能收低於15第納爾。

column1: Brass is essence of all that I sell:  lanterns and lamps, braziers and bottles, tea servers and cups.  I have the goods of the finest workers of metal in the city.
column2: 黃銅是我所賣一切東西的精髓:燈籠與油燈、火盆與瓶罐、茶具與杯盞。我這兒擺的全是城裡頂尖金工師傅的手藝。

column1: Surely a great adventurer as yourself will need a lamp on your travels.  Allow me to sell to you the finest oil lamp in the land.
column2: 像您這樣的大冒險家,旅途上想必少不了一盞燈。容我把全國最上等的油燈賣給您。

column1: It is said that an Elemental of Air blows in the city.
column2: 聽說城裡颳起了一個風元素。

column1: As I left where I live this morning, I heard something very large behind me.
column2: 今早我出門的時候,聽見身後有個好大的東西。

column1: All the fine brasswork that I sell is worth nothing to a city without water.  Surely you can do something, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 一座沒有水的城市，我賣的這些精美銅器都毫無價值。夏皮爾的英雄，你一定能做點什麼。

column1: It has been spoken on the streets that you journey to Raseir.  Surely our homeland will be returned.
column2: 街頭巷尾都在說您要前往拉希爾了。我們的家園想必能夠收復。

column1: The apothecary sells oil, if you wish to buy some.
column2: 藥鋪有賣油，如果你想買一些的話。

column1: I have flowering plants and green plants, planted in specially prepared earth and placed in pots from the potter across the way.  All will brighten a room and freshen the air.
column2: 我這兒有開花的盆栽,也有觀葉的綠植,種在特製的土裡,擺在對面陶匠做的罐子中。每一盆都能讓房間生色、空氣清新。

column1: The earth the plant is in was specially suited to the type of plant.  If you wish, I will gladly give you some.
column2: 這株植物所在的土壤，是特別適合這種植物的。如果你想要，我很樂意送你一些。

column1: Living plants to bring the outdoors inside.  Flowers alive to grow and thrive.
column2: 把戶外的綠意帶進屋裡。活生生的花朵,自會生長茁壯。

column1: Enjoy the beauty of the living plant.  Its very breath lightens the air and fills a room with gladness.
column2: 好好欣賞這活生生植物的美吧。它的吐息讓空氣清新,為滿室帶來歡愉。

column1: It is feared that the wind that now blows is an ill one.
column2: 人人都怕,如今颳起的這陣風是不祥之兆。

column1: My plants are trembling as is the very city.  You can feel it shake beneath your feet.
column2: 我的花草都在發抖,整座城也跟著抖。您腳下都能感覺到它在晃。

column1: My plants wither already because I cannot water them.  The grant of this single favor is worth to me a thousand others. Please rid this land of the Elemental of Water.
column2: 我的花草已經因為澆不了水而枯萎了。您若肯成全這一件事,勝過為我做一千件別的。求求您把這片土地上的水元素除掉吧。

column1: May good fortune watch over you always.
column2: 願好運永遠眷顧您。

column1: Pots for sale, earthenware pots in brightly colored glazes.  I have the pot you have long sought!
column2: 賣陶罐囉,上了鮮豔釉彩的陶罐。您找了好久的那只罐子,我這兒就有!

column1: I will be happy to sell you the pot which best suits your needs.  I have pots in every size and many colors.
column2: 我很樂意賣您一只最合用的罐子。各種尺寸、各種顏色,應有盡有。

column1: It will take a most brave hero to deal with the thing of wind.
column2: 要對付那個風造出來的東西,得要一位最英勇的英雄才行。

column1: My pots are shaking with every step the Earth Elemental takes.
column2: 那土元素每踏一步,我的陶罐就跟著抖一下。

column1: What good is a pot which cannot be filled?  We need your aid!
column2: 裝不了東西的罐子有什麼用?我們需要您的幫助啊!

column1: There are no thanks great enough for all you have done and will do.
column2: 您所做的、以及將要做的一切,再多的感謝都不足以回報。

column1: My pots are all made from clay which is shaped and painted by my hand.  I would be most pleased if you were to buy one.  There is a very pretty one here for only 3 dinars.
column2: 我的陶罐都是用黏土親手塑形彩繪的。要是你能買一個，我會非常高興。這裡有個很漂亮的，只要3第納爾。

column1: Clay is a special type of earth which is used to make pots.
column2: 黏土是一種特別的泥土,專門用來捏陶罐。

column1: We have delicious things for you to eat.
column2: 我們有好吃的東西供您享用。

column1: Mouth-tempting falafels of fresh roasted lamb.
column2: 令人垂涎的新鮮烤羊肉法拉費。

column1: Dates stuffed with honeyed almonds to tease your tongue.
column2: 椰棗裡塞滿蜜漬杏仁,挑逗您的味蕾。

column1: It is a certainty that you will enjoy what we sell.
column2: 我們賣的東西,您準會喜歡,這是一定的。

column1: Would you care today to try some fried bread and honey?
column2: 今天要不要嚐嚐炸餅淋蜂蜜?

column1: Perhaps you would like hot-spiced Saurus on a Stick?
column2: 要不要來一份香辣索魯斯肉串?

column1: What about some nice lizard jerky?
column2: 不然來點上好的蜥蜴肉乾?

column1: Mmmmm--mmm!  I could go for some lizard jerky, myself!
column2: 嗯——嗯!我自己都想來點蜥蜴肉乾了!

column1: The Plaza of the Palace has been abandoned by merchants today.
column2: 今天王宮廣場的商人全跑光了。

column1: There is an Elemental of Air which blew them from the plaza.
column2: 有個風元素把他們從廣場上吹跑了。

column1: The Katta are afraid to walk the streets.
column2: 卡塔族都不敢上街了。

column1: An Elemental of Earth is moving there.
column2: 有個土元素正在那邊到處遊蕩。

column1: Friend of the Katta, the Water Elemental will have us all dying of thirst!
column2: 卡塔族的朋友,那個水元素會讓我們全都渴死啊!

column1: There must be something you can do to help us, Hero of Shapeir!
column2: 您一定有辦法幫幫我們的,夏皮爾的英雄!

column1: Rumor has said that you are journeying to Raseir.
column2: 外頭傳說您要動身前往拉希爾了。

column1: It has been said that you seek to restore the old Emir!
column2: 大家都說您打算讓老埃米爾復位!

column1: You could not forget your friends the Katta.
column2: 您不會忘記卡塔族這些老朋友的。

column1: The homeland of our people shall be restored through your actions!
column2: 靠著您的行動,我們族人的家園終將重建!

column1: Since you speak so convincingly, then it will cost you a centime less.
column2: 既然你說得這麼有說服力，那就便宜你一分錢吧。

column1: No, it is not right that such a speech be rewarded so little.  It should be twenty centimes less.
column2: 不，這樣的說詞不該只換來這麼一點回報，應該要再便宜二十生丁才對。

column1: Twenty centimes??  Do you wish to simply give away our food?  Five centimes!
column2: 二十生丁？？你是想把我們的食物白送人嗎？五生丁！

column1: Ten centimes for such a hero.
column2: 像您這樣的英雄，十生丁就好。

column1: Very well.  For you we will make a special price.  It will cost you ten centimes less than our normal price.
column2: 好吧，為您破例算個特價。比我們平常的價錢便宜十生丁。

column1: The prices we charge are but a pittance of what our food is worth.
column2: 我們開的價,跟這些食物的真正價值比起來不過是九牛一毛。

column1: The finest food in all the bazaar is also the cheapest.
column2: 整個市集最棒的吃食,同時也是最便宜的。

column1: A Falafel is pita bread stuffed with spicy roasted lamb.  A hearty meal.
column2: 法拉費是塞滿香辣烤羊肉的口袋餅，是頓豐盛的餐點。

column1: You will find it most filling.
column2: 你會覺得它很管飽。

column1: The price is but 70 centimes for such a delicacy.
column2: 這樣的美味，只要七十生丁。

column1: It is cheap at any price.
column2: 無論開價多少,它都算便宜。

column1: The bread is first fried in olive oil.
column2: 麵包要先用橄欖油煎過。

column1: Then honey is poured upon it.
column2: 接著淋上蜂蜜。

column1: It is only 30 centimes.
column2: 只要三十生丁。

column1: The taste will sell itself.
column2: 這味道自會替自己招攬生意。

column1: Our fresh dates are stuffed with the finest of almonds, roasted over the brazier carefully and then glazed in honey.  We personally stuff the dates ourselves.
column2: 我們新鮮的椰棗裡塞滿上等杏仁，在炭爐上仔細烘烤後再裹上蜂蜜。椰棗都是我們親手塞的。

column1: We are very fastidious when working, and you will find no Katta hairs in the dates.
column2: 我們做事一絲不苟,你在椰棗裡絕找不到半根卡塔族的毛。

column1: For two handsful, you need pay only thirty centimes.
column2: 兩把只要三十生丁。

column1: You cannot find a better treat in all the bazaar.
column2: 走遍整個市集,你也找不到比這更好的美味。

column1: Our honey is made by bees that have supped upon the nectar of spring wildflowers.
column2: 我們的蜂蜜,是採食春日野花花蜜的蜜蜂釀成的。

column1: It has been but freshly gathered.
column2: 還是剛採收下來的喔。

column1: It makes a delicious glaze for almonds.
column2: 拿來淋杏仁,香甜得不得了。

column1: It is wonderful poured over hot bread.
column2: 澆在熱騰騰的麵包上更是一絕。

column1: The saurus meat is first dipped in spicy paprika.
column2: 索魯斯肉會先沾裹辛辣的紅椒粉。

column1: Then it is quickly cooked over the brazier there.
column2: 然後在那邊的炭爐上迅速烤熟。

column1: It is an experience found nowhere but in Shapeir.
column2: 這是唯有在夏皮爾才能有的體驗。

column1: It is something you will never forget.
column2: 這是你一輩子都忘不了的經歷。

column1: This will cost but 70 centimes.
column2: 這個只要七十生丁。

column1: Surely but a small amount for such a treat.
column2: 這等美味,這點錢算什麼。

column1: Our lizard jerky rations are very cheap.
column2: 我們的蜥蜴肉乾乾糧很便宜。

column1: But delicious!
column2: 可是好吃得很!

column1: They travel very well.
column2: 牠們很耐遠行。

column1: Surely an adventurer gets hungry at times.
column2: 冒險者總有餓肚子的時候吧。

column1: You can get five meals for but a dinar.
column2: 一第納爾就能買到五份餐點。

column1: You never need be hungry again!
column2: 你再也不必挨餓了!

column1: The jerky is prepared with only the finest, most succulent, rock-flattened lizards.
column2: 這肉乾只選用最上等、最多汁、用石頭壓扁的蜥蜴製成。

column1: Our special blend of preservatives lets the rich, full-bodied lizard flavor come through.
column2: 我們特調的防腐配方,能把那濃郁醇厚的蜥蜴風味完整保留下來。

column1: I am Scoree.
column2: 小的叫斯科里。

column1: I am Sloree.
column2: 小的叫斯洛里。

column1: We are lifemates.
column2: 我倆是終生伴侶。

column1: What you would call husband and wife.
column2: 也就是您說的夫妻啦。

column1: May you be much the better for it.
column2: 願它讓您過得更好。

column1: May it please you well.
column2: 願它叫您稱心如意。

column1: That is amazing.
column2: 太了不起了。

column1: You are beyond doubt a hero!
column2: 您毫無疑問是位英雄!

column1: The finest in leatherwork will I sell to you, Hero from a distant land.  My leather is noted the world over for its beauty and durability.
column2: 來自遠方的英雄,我要賣給你的是最上乘的皮件。我的皮革以美觀耐用聞名天下。

column1: Every Hero has a need for belts and boots and waterskins.  May it be that I sell you something, and I shall be most pleased to serve you.
column2: 每位英雄都需要腰帶、靴子和水袋。若能賣您點東西，那將是我的榮幸。

column1: The talk of the bazaar is that something strange stalks the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 市集上盛傳,有個怪東西在皇宮廣場上潛行作祟。

column1: It is said that the alleyways are not safe to travel today, for the very earth is starting to move.
column2: 聽說今天那些巷弄走不得,因為連大地本身都開始動起來了。

column1: My waterskins can not be filled today, for the very fountain is beset with an elemental of water, and no one can get even a drink.
column2: 我的水袋今天沒法裝滿，因為噴泉被一隻水元素纏上了，誰都喝不到水。

column1: All Katta rejoice that you now go to Raseir, for we know you will restore our homeland.
column2: 卡塔族人都為你即將前往拉希爾而歡欣,因為我們知道,你會讓我們的家園重獲新生。

column1: The belts and boots I sell are made from dyed Saurus skin. The very ones you wear came from my stand.  I sell many other items made from leather, but the one thing that I sell which every adventurer needs most in this land is a waterskin.
column2: 我賣的腰帶和靴子都是染過的索魯斯皮做的。您身上穿的那雙就是出自我這攤子。我還賣許多其他皮件,不過在這片土地上,每個冒險者最少不了的,就是一只水袋。

column1: The belts and boots are cut and shaped for the customer and take about a week to create.
column2: 腰帶和靴子都是按客人量身裁製的，大約要花一週才能做好。

column1: The waterskin will hold enough water for a day's journey in the desert.  It can be refilled in the fountain.  It is often the only thing between a traveler and certain death when one explores around the city.  It will cost you but one dinar.
column2: 這個水袋能裝下沙漠中一天旅程所需的水量，可以在噴泉重新裝滿。在城外探索時，它往往是旅人與死亡之間唯一的屏障。只要一第納爾。

column1: Cloth of the finest of weaves and the loveliest of colors, clothes to entice the touch, veils to reveal and conceal, all this have I and more!  You can attest to how comfortable and conforming my wares are, for you wear them well!
column2: 最細密的織法、最動人的色彩,摸了讓人愛不釋手的衣裳,半遮半掩的面紗,這些我這兒通通有,還不止呢!我這貨色有多舒適、多合身,您自己最清楚,穿在您身上多好看!

column1: See how fine my clothes look upon you!  Make sure all who ask about what you wear know that I, Kiram the Cloth Merchant, made them for you!
column2: 瞧瞧我做的衣服穿在您身上多合適！要是有人問起您這身行頭，一定要讓他們知道，是布商基蘭替您做的！

column1: My second cousin on my mother's side, Tiram, was unable to set up his carpet stand today, for the wind was blowing far too fiercely!
column2: 我母親那邊的遠房表親提拉姆,今天沒法擺他的地毯攤,因為風刮得實在太猛了!

column1: My brother by marriage, Tashtari the brass merchant, had something huge chase him in the streets as he was going to his work this morning!
column2: 我那當連襟的塔什塔里,就是賣銅器的那個,今早去開工時,在街上被一個巨大的東西追著跑!

column1: My aunt, Toshar the potter, was nearly caught by the Elemental which now inhabits the fountain!
column2: 我阿姨，陶匠托夏爾，差點被盤據在噴泉裡的那個元素抓住！

column1: My cousin, Sashanan the Jeweler, has something from the Katta for you!  Seek his stand in the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 我表哥，珠寶匠薩沙南，有件卡塔族的東西要給您！去宮殿廣場找他的攤位吧。

column1: I have clothes of all sizes and styles, cloth of the finest dyes!  If it is made of cloth, then I carry it in my stand.  Perhaps I could sell you a cloth bag to contain your extra equipment?
column2: 我這兒有各種尺寸、各種款式的衣裳,還有染得最漂亮的布料!只要是布做的,我這攤子上通通有。要不要我賣您一個布袋,裝您多出來的裝備?

column1: The bags I have are woven so fine they can carry the sand and plaster used to repair the city walls, yet strong enough that they will not break under so much weight.  They cost but fifty centimes.
column2: 我這些袋子織得極為細密，就連修補城牆用的沙子和灰泥都裝得下，卻依然堅固不會被壓破。只要五十生丁。

column1: It can be said that all Katta are related to one another.  We are all family.
column2: 可以說,所有卡塔族彼此都是親戚。我們全是一家人。

column1: Hey!  You want a free drink, go find a free world!
column2: 嘿!想喝免費的酒,找個免費的世界去吧!

column1: We only accept Raseirian coin here, Foreigner.
column2: 我們這裡只收拉希爾的錢幣，外地人。

column1: You get a small glass of tepid, stale-tasting water.
column2: 你拿到一小杯溫吞、走味的水。

column1: The coffee has been boiled for hours, it would appear, and it best resembles brown ooze.  You can understand why people remember Raseirish Coffee.
column2: 這咖啡看樣子已經煮了好幾個鐘頭,最像一灘褐色的黏液。你這下明白,人們為何對「拉希爾咖啡」念念不忘了。

column1: This drink sizzles in the glass.
column2: 這飲料在杯子裡嘶嘶作響。

column1: (It's the only way to get the glasses here clean!)
column2: (這可是把這兒杯子洗乾淨的唯一辦法!)

column1: There is one prerequisite.  You need a drink.
column2: 有個先決條件，您得先有杯飲料。

column1: You only have water.
column2: 您只有水。

column1: You have only a cup of coffee.
column2: 您只有一杯咖啡。

column1: You have a Djinn Sling, remember?
column2: 您有一杯精靈斯林，記得嗎？

column1: It is very dull, but it does quench the thirst.
column2: 味道平淡得很,不過倒真能解渴。

column1: It tastes very bitter, yet the flavor is oddly likable.
column2: 喝起來苦得很,可那股味道又莫名地討人喜歡。

column1: Wow!  You now know where the drink got its name.  You feel as if you've just been slung by a Djinn.
column2: 哇!你這下總算明白這酒名是怎麼來的了。你只覺得自己彷彿被一個精靈狠狠甩了一把。

column1: Welcome to the Blue Parrot.  I am Ferrari, Signor Ferrari.  Sit down, my dear sir, relax.  You are among friends here.
column2: 歡迎光臨藍鸚鵡。我是費拉里,費拉里先生。坐下吧,我親愛的先生,放輕鬆。在這兒你身邊都是朋友。

column1: There is a large, closed door blocking your way.  Apparently, the sleeping rooms are only accessible at night.
column2: 一扇又大又緊閉的門擋住了你的去路。看樣子,客房只有到了夜裡才開放。

column1: Through the haze of smoke, you see a place that reminds you a bit of a dream which turned into a nightmare.
column2: 透過瀰漫的煙霧，您看見一個地方，讓您想起某個變成惡夢的夢境。

column1: You think back to Ferrari's warning, and decide that practicing on this lock is a little too dangerous.  Better wait until evening when the door will be unlocked.
column2: 你想起費拉里的警告,覺得拿這道鎖來練手有點太冒險了。還是等到傍晚門解鎖再說吧。

column1: It is locked.  Apparently, the sleeping rooms are only accessible at night.
column2: 鎖著呢。看來臥房得等到晚上才進得去。

column1: The person glares at you, then glances nervously over at Signor Ferrari.
column2: 那人狠狠瞪了您一眼，接著緊張地瞥向費拉里閣下。

column1: You're already seated, remember?
column2: 你不是已經坐下了嗎?

column1: There's no need to sit at this time.
column2: 這會兒沒必要坐下。

column1: You'll have to sit down before you get up.
column2: 您得先坐下才能站起來。

column1: That is all very interesting.
column2: 這可真是有意思。

column1: No one pays any attention to you.
column2: 沒人理會你。

column1: Great idea!  You'll need a drink, though.
column2: 好主意！不過您得先有杯飲料。

column1: You don't see one you could get without being noticed.
column2: 你看不到哪一盞能神不知鬼不覺地弄到手。

column1: If you wish to buy a drink you must go talk to Wilmer.
column2: 如果您想買飲料，得去找威爾默談談。

column1: If you want a drink, come up to the front of the bar.
column2: 想喝酒,就到吧台前頭來。

column1: You've already got a drink.
column2: 你已經有酒喝了。

column1: What do you want?
column2: 你想幹嘛?

column1: I only serve the good stuff, and that ain't it.
column2: 我只供應好貨,那玩意兒可不算。

column1: As you reach the front door, you suddenly realize the strain of the day's events.  You are in no condition to go anywhere other than bed.
column2: 走到大門口,你才猛然驚覺這一天折騰下來有多累。除了上床睡覺,你哪兒也去不了了。

column1: In case you were not aware of the matter, my dear sir, it is against the law in Raseir to be abroad at night.
column2: 親愛的先生,若您還不知道的話:在拉希爾,夜裡在外遊蕩可是違法的。

column1: I should like to speak with you tomorrow about the use of your skills.  As head of all illegal activity in Raseir, I am an influential and respected man, and I have no intention of letting an amateur jeopardize my position, if you understand my meaning.  Good night, for now.
column2: 我想明天跟您談談您那身本事的用途。身為拉希爾所有非法活動的頭子，我是個有勢力、受人敬重的人，可不打算讓個外行人壞了我的地位，您明白我的意思吧。那麼，晚安了。

column1: Please do not disturb me.  I need to think.
column2: 請別來打擾我。我得好好想想。

column1: Ferrari ignores you.
column2: 費拉里夫人對你不理不睬。

column1: You see several guards burst into the inn.
column2: 您看見幾名衛兵闖進旅店。

column1: You are under arrest for breaking the law of Raseir on this day, the 29th of the Serpent, Year of the Djinn.  Come quietly, or you will be taken by force.
column2: 你因觸犯拉希爾律法被捕——本日為精靈之年蛇月二十九日。乖乖就範,否則就動粗硬帶。

column1: You follow the guard through the dark streets, past the gates of the palace, and into the dungeon of Raseir.
column2: 您跟著衛兵穿過陰暗的街道，經過宮殿大門，進入拉希爾的地牢。

column1: Ah, when I first saw you, I said to myself, there is a man who knows how to play the game.  I appreciate that in a man.
column2: 啊,第一眼見到你,我就對自己說:這是個懂得門道的人。我就欣賞這樣的男人。

column1: Know this, though.  I am the Thieves' Guild here.  Do not even think of using your skills around here without consulting me first.
column2: 不過你給我聽好:這兒的盜賊公會就是我。沒先跟我打過招呼,你休想在這一帶動你那身本事。

column1: Ha ha, heh!  You seem very sure of yourself, don't you?
column2: 哈哈,呵!你對自己倒是挺有把握,是吧?

column1: The man at the corner table uses his whip as a fly-swatter.
column2: 角落桌邊那名男子,拿他的鞭子當蒼蠅拍使。

column1: I asked you to SIT DOWN!  Please.
column2: 我叫你坐下!拜託。

column1: Ferrari just gestures you away with his fly-swatter.  You find the bartender glaring at you.
column2: 費拉里只用他的蒼蠅拍揮手把你趕開。你發現酒保正狠狠瞪著你。

column1: I do not know what Ugarte has to say, and I am most intrigued.  Perhaps you should relieve our curiosity and pay the man.
column2: 我也不曉得烏加特要說些什麼,實在好奇得很。要不你就行行好,把錢付給他,也好了卻咱們的好奇心。

column1: You should hurry.  There is little time.
column2: 您該快點了，時間不多了。

column1: Ferrari just stares at the black bird.
column2: 費拉里只是直勾勾盯著那隻黑鳥。

column1: Ferrari avoids your eye and your question as he studiously swats flies.
column2: 費拉里一本正經地拍著蒼蠅,既不看你的眼睛,也不答你的話。

column1: Do as Signor Ferrari orders.
column2: 照費拉里先生的吩咐去做。

column1: You stealthily nab the oil lamp.
column2: 你悄悄地順走了那盞油燈。

column1: If you're going to steal a lamp, you'd better choose one in a less conspicuous corner of the room.
column2: 要偷油燈,你最好挑房裡某個比較不顯眼的角落那盞。

column1: You hear cursing and grumbling nearby.
column2: 你聽見附近傳來咒罵和牢騷聲。

column1: I do what I can to help those poor unfortunates who live around here.  Water is my speciality.
column2: 我盡力幫助住在這附近的可憐人，水是我的專長。

column1: Since the fountain has dried up, the only place to get water is from the Emir's men.  The only place other than myself, that is. It is surprising how much water is worth to one who is thirsty.
column2: 自從噴泉乾涸,要弄水就只能找埃米爾的手下了。當然,除了我這兒以外。對一個渴壞了的人來說,水能值多少錢,實在叫人吃驚。

column1: That is all that I have for you now, but I will be keeping my ears open, now that I know you are such a valuable customer.
column2: 眼下我能告訴你的就這些了。不過既然我曉得你是這麼貴重的一位顧客,我會豎起耳朵替你留意的。

column1: I am always fond of those who are willing to pay for my services.
column2: 凡是肯為我的服務掏錢的人,我向來都挺喜歡的。

column1: Khaveen is a very dangerous man to trifle with, yet there are ways to get around him.
column2: 哈維因是個招惹不得的危險人物,不過要繞過他,還是有法子的。

column1: This 'Someone' told Khaveen to watch for you.  It is interesting to see Khaveen play the lackey around here.
column2: 這位「某人」吩咐哈維因留意你。看哈維因在這兒當條走狗,還挺有意思的。

column1: Khaveen likes to be the one to give the orders around here.  He is not happy when he is forced to follow them.
column2: 哈維因喜歡在這兒當那個發號施令的人。一旦被迫聽人差遣,他可就不痛快了。

column1: Khaveen is not the person in charge in Raseir, although he would like to believe otherwise.
column2: 哈維因並不是拉希爾真正當家作主的人,儘管他自個兒挺想這麼以為的。

column1: About that one, I can say no more.  it is said that the whisper of his name will draw his attention.  I have no wish for him to notice me.
column2: 關於那個人，我不能再多說了。據說只要低聲提起他的名字，就會引起他的注意。我可不希望被他盯上。

column1: How else is an honest businessman to make a living around here?
column2: 不然,一個老實本分的生意人在這一帶還能怎麼討生活呢?

column1: It is said that, ah, someone, is waiting for someone to open a door to something.  I have had no desire to learn more about it.  Things dealing with magic are best left alone.
column2: 據說，啊，有個人正等著另一個人去打開某扇門。我沒興趣多了解。跟魔法扯上關係的事，還是別管的好。

column1: It is said that the only way to leave Raseir's dungeon is to die.
column2: 據說離開拉希爾地牢的唯一方法，就是死。

column1: We are old friends, are we not, Ferrari?
column2: 我們是老朋友了,不是嗎,費拉里?

column1: No! Nothing! I know nothing about him.  It is best such a name be forgotten.
column2: 不!沒什麼!關於他我什麼都不知道。那種名字,最好還是忘了乾淨。

column1: I'm afraid there is little I can tell you that would be of any use.
column2: 恐怕我能告訴你的,沒幾句派得上用場。

column1: I am very sorry, but such matters should not be your concern.
column2: 非常抱歉,可這種事情,不該由您來操心。

column1: it would be hard for someone such as myself to make a living there, so I have heard.  There are so few laws to work around.
column2: 聽說像我這樣的人，在那裡很難討生活，因為沒什麼法律漏洞可鑽。

column1: There is much profit to be made here if a man keeps his eyes open for opportunity.
column2: 只要眼觀四方留意機會，這裡有不少財可以發。

column1: I think at times there is too much magic around here.
column2: 我有時候覺得,這一帶的魔法實在太多了。

column1: The new emir is as big a fool as his brother was.  I have heard that he hides in the palace and will not leave his room.
column2: 新任埃米爾跟他哥哥一樣是個大傻瓜。聽說他躲在宮殿裡，連房間都不肯出。

column1: The palace of Raseir is very lovely and dangerous.  All the wealth of Raseir remains there, and all the dungeons as well.
column2: 拉希爾的宮殿既華美又危險，拉希爾所有的財富都藏在那裡，所有的地牢也是。

column1: It is said that money is the root of all evil.  It just shows you how spiteful men can be who do not have any money.
column2: 人家說,金錢是萬惡之源。這話正好說明了,沒錢的人能有多刻薄。

column1: It is best that you speak to Signor Ferrari about such matters.
column2: 這類事情,你最好去找費拉里先生談。

column1: Since the fountain does not give water, it stands to reason that someone has to.  And you would not believe how much the Emir's men charge for water.  It is a crime.  Fortunately, my prices are much more affordable.
column2: 既然噴泉不出水,照理就得有人來供水。你絕對想不到埃米爾的手下賣水要價多高,簡直是搶劫。所幸,我的價錢親民多了。

column1: I make my living off of rumors.
column2: 我這個人,是靠流言過活的。

column1: There was one katta that remained behind when the others left.  I have heard that he went into hiding, but no one has seen him since.
column2: 其他卡塔族人離開時，有一隻卡塔族留了下來。聽說他躲了起來，之後就沒人見過他。

column1: You may call me Ugarte.
column2: 你可以叫我烏加特。

column1: We sell water for 50 centimes, Raseirish Coffee for 75 centimes, and Djinn Slings for 1 dinar.
column2: 我們這兒水五十生丁,拉希爾咖啡七十五生丁,精靈一甩一第納爾。

column1: Where else can you get water in this city?  The Waterman only comes once a week.
column2: 這城裡還能上哪兒弄到水?送水人一週才來一趟。

column1: The Emir's man sells a week's worth of water for 10 dinars.
column2: 埃米爾的手下,賣一週份的水要十第納爾。

column1: This city is known for its coffee.
column2: 這座城就以咖啡聞名。

column1: You will not forget one, once you have tried one.
column2: 喝過一杯,你這輩子都忘不了。

column1: Signor Ferrari is to be respected and obeyed.
column2: 費拉里先生這人,得敬著、順著。

column1: That is Signor Ferrari to you, punk.
column2: 給我叫費拉里先生,小子。

column1: If you wish to order a drink, order one.  Otherwise, go away.
column2: 想點酒就點,不然就給我走開。

column1: You think that is so important?  That is nothing to me.
column2: 你以為那很要緊?對我來說啥也不是。

column1: If you have no further need of me, sir, there are thirsty customers in need of my wares.
column2: 先生,您若沒別的吩咐,還有些口渴的客人等著買我的貨呢。

column1: My water will cost you 20 dinars, Sir.
column2: 我的水要二十第納爾，先生。

column1: Normally my water costs 20 dinars, Sir, but for you I will only require 15.
column2: 我的水平常要二十第納爾，先生，但您的話，十五就好。

column1: Surely, a man such as yourself can easily afford 20 dinars?
column2: 像您這樣的人，二十第納爾應該不成問題吧？

column1: I am sorry, Sir, but I do not accept foreign coin.
column2: 很抱歉，先生，我不收外地錢幣。

column1: Surely, Sir, you did not intend to buy water with your good looks?
column2: 先生，您該不會是想靠一張臉買水吧？

column1: Ugarte takes your money and pours some water from his supply into your waterskins.
column2: 烏加特收下你的錢,從他的存貨裡舀了些水倒進你的水袋。

column1: Please, sir, my dinars?
column2: 先生,勞駕,我的第納爾呢?

column1: It seems before I give you what you want, you should give me what I want.  It is only fair, no?  My dinars, please.
column2: 看來在我給你想要的東西之前,你得先給我我想要的。這才公平,不是嗎?把第納爾交出來吧。

column1: You will know what I speak of when you see it.
column2: 等你親眼見著了,自然就明白我說的是什麼了。

column1: Wilmer is like a son to me.
column2: 威爾默對我來說,就像親兒子一樣。

column1: You go past the bar through the door over there.  It will be ready for you at nighttime.  You need not worry about how much it will cost.  We will just put it on your tab.
column2: 你穿過酒吧,走那邊那道門。到了夜裡,房間就會替你備好。費用您不必操心,我們記在您的帳上便是。

column1: Do not let such matters bother you.
column2: 這種事就別放在心上了。

column1: When it gets dark outside, the guards patrol the city in search of fools to feed the dungeon.
column2: 天一黑，衛兵就會在城裡巡邏，搜捕蠢蛋來填滿地牢。

column1: Ugarte and I are old friends.
column2: 烏加特和我是老交情了。

column1: Life is a game, my friend.  It is all a matter of what cards you are dealt and how you play them.
column2: 朋友,人生就是一場牌局。全看你拿到什麼牌,又怎麼打。

column1: Come now, we both know what you are good at.  The question is, just how good are you?
column2: 行了,你擅長什麼咱倆心知肚明。問題只在於——你到底有多行?

column1: I shall find myself missing Ugarte.
column2: 我會想念烏加特的。

column1: I will warn you, that name is best not mentioned around here.
column2: 我警告你,那個名字最好別在這一帶提起。

column1: Ugarte would still be here if he had not tried to find more information out for you.  They made an example of him for those that are too curious.
column2: 要不是烏加特替你多打聽了些消息,他這會兒還好端端在這呢。他們拿他開刀,給那些太愛多管閒事的人殺雞儆猴。

column1: The new regime as ostensibly led by Khaveen certainly altered Raseir.
column2: 表面上由哈維因掌權的新政權,的確把拉希爾整個變了個樣。

column1: You should ask Ugarte what services he provides.
column2: 你該去問問烏加特,他能幫人辦什麼事。

column1: Information is an important commodity in Raseir.
column2: 在拉希爾,情報是一種重要的商品。

column1: You have to respect a man like Khaveen.  He does his job so very well.
column2: 像哈維因這樣的人,你不得不佩服。他把份內的事做得太好了。

column1: I do not believe in such superstition as Ugarte.  Still, the man who caused the overthrow of the Emir is not a person whose name to use in vain.
column2: 我可不像烏加特那樣迷信。不過,那個一手扳倒埃米爾的人,他的名字確實不該隨意掛在嘴邊。

column1: It seems you do not drink.  Perhaps you do not trust my drinks?  Ha ha, heh.  You are indeed a dangerous man.  I like that.  You will do well in Raseir.
column2: 看來你滴酒不沾。莫非是信不過我的酒?哈哈,呵。你果然是個危險人物。我喜歡。你在拉希爾會混得很好。

column1: We will reserve a room here for you tonight.  You will need to return here before dark, for it is forbidden to be out at night.  Join me this evening and we will talk some more.  I have some information which may prove useful to you.  I will see you later.  I have some thinking to do now.
column2: 我們今晚會替您留個房間。天黑前您得回來，因為晚上禁止外出。今晚來找我，我們再多聊聊。我有些消息可能對您有用。晚點見，我現在得想點事情。

column1: But now, my friend, it is time we discussed some things together, you and I.
column2: 不過現在嘛,朋友,咱倆是時候談談一些事了。

column1: I have little interest in such matters.
column2: 這種事我可沒什麼興趣。

column1: That is not relevant to matters at hand.
column2: 那跟眼前的事無關。

column1: There is nothing about Shapeir which interests me, except for what you have told me.
column2: 夏皮爾沒有什麼能引起我興趣的,除了你方才告訴我的那些。

column1: I came to Raseir a little over a year and a half ago now.  A man of my stature has no problem making a profit out of any situation.  It is not profit that brought me here, though.  I came seeking... ah, but I see that I am boring you.  Enough of that subject.
column2: 我來拉希爾,如今算來已一年半多了。像我這般身分地位的人,從任何境況裡撈油水都不成問題。不過,把我引來這兒的並不是利潤。我來,是為了尋找……啊,可我看得出我把你給說悶了。這話題就到此為止吧。

column1: The situation in Raseir has at its center a good deal of magic.  I, being a prudent man, do not let such things concern me.
column2: 拉希爾如今的局面,核心就纏著一大堆魔法。我這人謹慎,這種事我才不去沾。

column1: The current Emir is but a stepping stone for the one behind the throne.
column2: 現任埃米爾,不過是寶座背後那位的一塊墊腳石罷了。

column1: It is said that all the wealth of Raseir remains in the palace.  That is not entirely true.
column2: 人家說,拉希爾所有的財富都藏在王宮裡。那話可不全對。

column1: Money is not truly what matters in this world.  It is what one gains from having money that is important.
column2: 金錢本身並不是這世上真正要緊的東西。要緊的,是有了錢之後能換來什麼。

column1: This is the Thieves' Guild, my dear sir, and I am the man who controls it.
column2: 這裡是盜賊公會,親愛的先生,而我正是掌控它的人。

column1: The Blue Parrot is the very center for life in Raseir.  Everybody goes to the Blue Parrot.
column2: 藍鸚鵡正是拉希爾生活的中心。人人都往藍鸚鵡跑。

column1: The fountain of Raseir dried up last year around the time that the Emir first was missed.
column2: 拉希爾的噴泉是去年乾涸的,差不多就在埃米爾頭一回失蹤的那陣子。

column1: There are no secrets in Raseir.  At least, none I am willing to spread.
column2: 拉希爾沒有什麼秘密。至少,沒有什麼是我願意往外傳的。

column1: I am Ferrari.  You may call me Signor Ferrari.
column2: 我就是費拉里。你可以稱我費拉里閣下。

column1: You have nothing to drink.
column2: 您沒有東西可喝。

column1: The drink you ordered resembles nothing you've dared to drink in the past.  Therefore, it must be a Djinn Sling.
column2: 您點的這杯，跟您以往敢喝的東西完全不像，那想必是精靈斯林了。

column1: The cup of black liquid before you more resembles coffee than a Djinn Sling.  Come to think of it, you did order coffee.
column2: 您面前這杯黑色液體，比起精靈斯林更像咖啡。想想也是，您點的本來就是咖啡。

column1: You don't have one of those, but you pick up the glass in front of you.
column2: 您沒有那種東西，不過您拿起了面前的杯子。

column1: Have patience!  The bartender doesn't seem too interested in moving quickly.
column2: 耐心點！酒保看起來沒什麼興趣動作快。

column1: It looks more like a Djinn Sling.
column2: 看起來比較像精靈斯林。

column1: It looks fizzy and dangerous.
column2: 看起來冒著泡,挺危險的。

column1: It looks more like coffee.
column2: 看起來比較像咖啡。

column1: It looks like brown ooze.
column2: 看起來像一灘褐色的黏液。

column1: The drink is sweet, with a very bitter aftertaste.
column2: 這酒甜甜的,後勁卻苦得很。

column1: You drink the bitter, thick liquid.
column2: 你喝下那濃稠苦澀的液體。

column1: It has an odd, almost chemical, taste.
column2: 喝起來有股怪味,幾乎像化學藥劑似的。

column1: You feel strange for a few seconds, but the feeling passes.  Ferrari starts to ask some very probing questions, but you evade most of them.
column2: 你恍惚了幾秒,那感覺隨即過去。費拉里開始旁敲側擊地盤問,但大半問題都被你含糊帶了過去。

column1: You are a very closed-mouth individual.  You handle your drink very well.  I trust you will handle yourself in Raseir as well.
column2: 你這人嘴可真嚴。酒量也好得很。我信你在拉希爾,也能把自己照看得一樣好。

column1: You feel a bit odd.  Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to question you.  He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly.  You are having a difficult time trying not to say too much.
column2: 你覺得有點不對勁。費拉里先生衝你一笑,開始盤問起來。他對夏皮爾尤其感興趣,問得鉅細靡遺。你費了好大勁,才忍住沒說漏嘴。

column1: Humm, there is much to what you say, and much more to what you don't say, I suspect.
column2: 唔,你說的話裡頭大有文章,而你沒說的,只怕更耐人尋味。

column1: You start to feel a bit dizzy.  Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to ask questions about yourself.  You find yourself answering, telling your friend all about your adventures.  He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly.  After a while, though, you start to get a headache.  Ferrari frowns.
column2: 您開始感到有點頭暈。費拉里閣下對您微笑，開始問起您的事。您發現自己不由自主地回答，把自己的冒險經歷一五一十告訴這位朋友。他對夏皮爾特別感興趣，仔細追問。過了一會兒，您開始頭痛起來。費拉里皺起眉頭。

column1: You have led a most interesting and charmed life, it seems.  But it would take a miracle to restore Raseir, and Khaveen has outlawed miracles.  You are very dangerous to me, for your actions will have serious repercussions.  Nevertheless, you may have your uses.
column2: 看來您這一路過得相當精彩，也算走運。但要復興拉希爾得靠奇蹟，而哈維因已經明令禁止奇蹟。您對我來說非常危險，您的所作所為會帶來嚴重後果。不過，您或許還有點用處。

column1: Now, now.  Pleasure before business.  Here come our drinks even as we speak.
column2: 行了行了。先享樂,再談正事。瞧,咱們的酒這不就上來了。

column1: Let us drink to our fortunate meeting!
column2: 來,為咱們這場有緣的相逢乾一杯!

column1: I am very pleased to make your acquaintance.  I have heard much about you already.
column2: 能認識你我非常高興。關於你的事,我早就聽說了不少。

column1: Ugarte, here, provides an invaluable service to this community.  He obtains for others things which are otherwise unattainable.
column2: 這位烏加特，為這個社群提供了無可取代的服務。他能替人弄到別處弄不到的東西。

column1: For a nominal fee, of course.
column2: 當然嘍,得收一筆象徵性的費用。

column1: It seems he has some information for you.
column2: 看樣子,他手上有些消息要給你。

column1: A rumor, perhaps, that may influence your actions.  I will be happy to share such information for, say, five dinars?
column2: 也許是個能左右你下一步的傳言。這消息我很樂意分享——要不就五第納爾?

column1: Well, seeing as you are a hero and all, perhaps we could settle for say, 3 dinars?
column2: 好吧，看在您是位英雄的份上，我們就談個，三第納爾如何？

column1: I am sorry, but I am just a poor businessman.  Surely a hero such as yourself can spare a few measly dinars for information which might save his life?
column2: 抱歉啊,我不過是個窮做買賣的。像您這樣的英雄,總擠得出區區幾枚第納爾,換一條也許能保命的消息吧?

column1: Forgive me, %s, but I do not accept foreign coin.
column2: 請見諒,%s,小的不收外地的錢幣。

column1: You find you do not have the money.  Ugarte quickly loses interest in you.
column2: 你發現自己根本沒這筆錢。烏加特立刻對你失了興致。

column1: Well, what kind of hero are you?  You come into town acting like you are a wealthy man, and make poor honest businessmen such as myself work for nothing.
column2: 哼，您算哪門子英雄？進城來擺出一副富人的架子，害我這種老實的窮商人白做工。

column1: Khaveen has sent the word out to his men that you are to be watched at all times.  It seems that, ah, Someone is very interested in your actions.  You should be aware that Khaveen does not usually greet strangers at the gate.  You were expected.
column2: 哈維因已經放話給手下,要他們時時刻刻盯著你。看來啊,「某位人物」對你的一舉一動很感興趣。你該明白,哈維因平常可不會親自到城門口迎接陌生人。你的到來,是早在意料之中的。

column1: Why would He take an interest?
column2: 祂為什麼會感興趣?

column1: There is the little matter of a prophecy to be fulfilled and this one may be the one.
column2: 還有個小問題，有個預言要應驗，說不定就是這位。

column1: I suspected before that you were a very dangerous man to have around, %s.  It seems I am correct.
column2: 我先前就懷疑,留你在身邊是個很危險的人物,%s。看來我沒猜錯。

column1: Still, it will be very interesting to see how this game plays out.  You may stay tonight in the first room past the door on the left.
column2: 不過呢,瞧瞧這場好戲怎麼收場,倒也挺有意思。今晚你可以住在左邊那道門進去的第一間房。

column1: You may live to regret arriving here with no money.  Do you think to live on charity?  Wilmer, show our poor hero here to quarters more fitting his purse.
column2: 身上沒帶錢就跑來這兒,你早晚會後悔的。你以為能靠施捨過活?威爾默,給咱們這位窮英雄安排個更配得上他錢包的住處。

column1: Just tell me if you have the bird!
column2: 你倒是說,那隻鳥到底在不在你手上!

column1: Of all the djinn joints in all the world, you had to walk into this one.
column2: 世上這麼多間精靈酒館，您偏偏走進了這間。

column1: A hush comes over the bar as people notice you and eye you sullenly.
column2: 酒館裡的人注意到您，紛紛陰沉地盯著您看，全場靜了下來。

column1: Come join me here, my dear sir.  You are still a novelty in Raseir, and I take a vested interest in all things new.  It is time we discussed some things together, you and I.
column2: 過來陪我坐坐,我親愛的先生。在拉希爾你還是個新鮮人物,而我對一切新事物都頗有興趣。咱倆,是時候談談一些事了。

column1: It is a pity about our friend Ugarte, is it not?  I can see you are as broken up by the affair as I am.
column2: 咱們那位朋友烏加特的事,真是可惜啊,是不是?我看得出,你跟我一樣為這事傷透了心。

column1: However, it seems he asked someone the wrong questions about you.  That was just a performance put on for your benefit this morning.
column2: 不過嘛,看樣子他為了打聽你的事,問錯了人。今早那一齣,不過是專演給你看的一場戲罷了。

column1: Khaveen has known about Ugarte's water smuggling for a long time.  A most dangerous man, Khaveen.  Almost as dangerous as you are.
column2: 哈維因早就知道烏加特走私水的勾當了。哈維因這人,危險得很。差不多跟你一樣危險。

column1: Do you have it?  Do you have the bird?
column2: 你拿到了嗎?那隻鳥在你手上嗎?

column1: Will you look what crawled in from the desert?  He has been out in the sun so long, his hair has gone yellow!
column2: 瞧瞧這是從沙漠裡爬進來的什麼玩意兒?在太陽底下曬太久,頭髮都曬黃啦!

column1: If you wish to discuss something to our mutual advantage, come sit down.
column2: 想談點對咱倆都有好處的事,就過來坐下。

column1: Now, now, Wilmer, he is a stranger to our fair city.  You wouldn't want to give the wrong opinion about the hospitality of Raseir.
column2: 行了行了,威爾默,他可是初來咱們這座好城的外鄉人。你總不想讓人對拉希爾的待客之道留下壞印象吧。

column1: Come here, stranger.  I see you have already made the acquaintance of Khaveen, our illustrious Captain of the Raseirian Guard, so perhaps you already have an idea of Raseirian hospitality.  Come here, sit down, and let us have a little chat, you and I.
