column1: Stop him, Master!  Don't let him cast that spell!
column2: 攔住他,主人!別讓他施那道法術!

column1: The spell is about to go off!  It'll get you for sure!
column2: 法術就要發動了!這下你肯定逃不掉!

column1: You!  My spell is broken!  You will die!
column2: 你!我的法術被破了!你死定了!

column1: We did it!  Hurray!
column2: 我們成功啦!萬歲!

column1: Don't be such a Chatty Cathy.  Get moving.
column2: 別這麼囉哩囉嗦的。動起來。

column1: Good idea!  He does look top-heavy with all those arms, doesn't he?  Unfortunately, his mass is far too great for that spell to have any effect.
column2: 好主意!他長那麼多隻手臂,看起來頭重腳輕,對吧?可惜他份量實在太重,那道法術根本撼動不了他。

column1: You've got to be kidding!  That statue must weigh a thousand pounds!
column2: 你開什麼玩笑!那座雕像少說也有上千磅重!

column1: You've already broken the spell, Master.  Stop that human!
column2: 你已經破解法術了,主人。攔住那個人類!

column1: You must hit an unlit candle to break the spell, Master.   Please stop that human!
column2: 你得打中一根未點燃的蠟燭才能破解法術，主人。快阻止那個人類！

column1: You're too late, Master.  We've failed
column2: 你太遲了，主人。我們失敗了

column1: You're too late, Master.  We've failed!
column2: 你太遲了，主人。我們失敗了！

column1: Just run up to one and do it.
column2: 直接跑到其中一根前面動手就行了。

column1: The brazier is already lit.  Your spell has no effect.
column2: 火盆已經點著了,你的法術不起作用。

column1: Aim a little higher, Master.  Maybe you can tip it over onto him.
column2: 瞄高一點,主人。也許你能把它砸翻、壓到他身上。

column1: The brazier is too heavy to fetch.
column2: 火盆太重了，搬不動。

column1: No, Master!  You need to keep your feet on the ground.
column2: 不,主人!你得腳踏實地。

column1: What a bust.  That chiseler gave you no relief.  Next time, remember not to stand your ground so long with a stone statue.
column2: 真是慘敗。那塊鑿出來的東西可半點沒手下留情。下次記住,別跟一座石像死守原地耗那麼久。

column1: What a beating.  You might've gone another round if you'd been healthier when you came in.
column2: 這頓打真慘。要是你進來時體力更好一點，或許還能再撐一回合。

column1: You sure got a charge from that lightning.  Perhaps you should have been galvanized into action instead.
column2: 那道閃電可真讓你「來電」了。也許你該被刺激得趕快動起來才對。

column1: Some wizard you are.  How do you Spell relief?  M-A-G-I-C!
column2: 你算哪門子巫師啊。要怎麼「拼」出解脫？魔—法！

column1: Your transmogrification by incantation presages your expiration, but your instigation prevented devastation by Iblis's domination.  That is, you saved the world, but all you got for it was saurus breath.  Next time, take a moment to reflect before you enter this situation.
column2: 你被咒語變形，本該預示著你的終結，但你的挺身而出，阻止了伊布利斯稱霸帶來的浩劫。也就是說，你拯救了世界，換來的卻只有一口索魯斯的口臭。下次行動前，不妨先仔細想清楚再踏入這種處境。

column1: Unable to penetrate your reversal spell, Ad Avis caused the floor around you to begin to burn.  Next time, take a tip from Ad Avis himself.  If your spell rebounds from its target, then find another target.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯的法術無法突破你的反射法術，於是他讓你周圍的地板開始燃燒。下次不妨學學阿德·阿維斯自己的招數：如果法術會從目標身上反彈，那就換個目標。

column1: Ad Avis seems to have turned the tables (and your own spells) on you.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯似乎反將了你一軍(連你自己的法術都用上了)。

column1: The last candle!  Iblis is free!!
column2: 最後一根蠟燭！伊布利斯自由了！！

column1: Your tiny flame spell can't ignite these floors.  You need fire from another source to stop Ad Avis.  (Of course, if you had stayed at WIT, they would have taught you a floor burning spell.)
column2: 你那小小的火焰法術點不燃這些地板。你需要另一個火源才能阻止阿德·阿維斯。（當然，要是你當初留在巫院，他們早就教過你燒地板的法術了。）

column1: Flame darts don't bounce.  But force bolts do.
column2: 火焰飛鏢不會反彈。但力場箭會。

column1: Your force bolt lost its energy by the time it struck the wall, Master.  Perhaps you'd better get closer.
column2: 你的力場箭撞到牆時已經沒了力道，主人。或許你該靠近一點。

column1: Master, I am sorry, but I cannot allow you to leave without Iblis!
column2: 主人,很抱歉,但我不能讓你丟下伊布利斯就走!

column1: You must get an unlit candle to break the spell, Master.   Please stop that man!
column2: 你得拿到一根還沒點著的蠟燭才能破解法術,主人。快攔住那傢伙!

column1: Thank you, Master.  Now I can take Iblis back to his tomb where he will be safe for another thousand years and a year.
column2: 謝謝你，主人。現在我可以把伊布利斯帶回他的墓穴，讓他在那裡再安全沉睡一千年又一年。

column1: I knew I would remember it.  The real prophecy is:
column2: 我就知道我會想起來的。真正的預言是：

column1: One thousand years and a Year,    so shall Iblis be caught, In the tombstones of the City,    where he and I once fought. Until moonlight has been captured,    twixt the Scorpion and Drake, Then comes a hero from the North,    and ancient powers wake.
column2: 「一千年又一年，    伊布利斯將被囚禁，在此城的墓碑之下，    在那裡他與我曾一戰。直到月光被捕捉，    夾在天蠍與飛龍之間，便有英雄自北方而來，    古老的力量甦醒。」

column1: Come at last unto betrayal,    and to 'He who waits behind', Seek ye then to capture Iblis,    'ere he rises unconfined. Else shall come the night eternal,    Darkness overshadows Light, Unless a hero seeks the darkness,    and restores the wrongs to right.
column2: 「終將迎向背叛，    以及那『在後方等候者』，務必設法囚禁伊布利斯，    趁他尚未脫困而起。否則永夜將至，    黑暗將掩蓋光明，除非有英雄尋入黑暗，    撥亂反正，導回正途。」

column1: Bye, bye, Master.  It's been fun!
column2: 再見了，主人。這段時光很開心！

column1: You are wasting your energies, Master.  The Statue of Iblis was created by Suleiman to absorb and contain magic more powerful than yours.
column2: 你在白費力氣,主人。伊布利斯雕像是蘇萊曼所造,能吸收並封鎖比你強大得多的魔法。

column1: The statue of Iblis is protected from harm by the Power of Suleiman.
column2: 伊布利斯的雕像受到蘇萊曼之力的保護，不受傷害。

column1: You are nearly overwhelmed by the amount of magic in the area.
column2: 這一帶的魔法之濃,幾乎讓你招架不住。

column1: Not yet, Master!  You will be seen!
column2: 還不行，主人！你會被看見的！

column1: No, Master!  Not that way!  You will be seen!
column2: 不,大人!別走那邊!你會被人看見的!

column1: Please wait until it's safe, Master!  You must not be caught!
column2: 大人,請等安全了再動!你千萬不能被逮著!

column1: No, Master!  Choose your direction carefully, or you will be caught.
column2: 不，主人！小心選擇方向，不然你會被抓到。

column1: Go quickly.  Hide in the room and I will distract Hajib at the table.
column2: 快走。躲進房裡,我去把哈吉布引到桌邊。

column1: Hajib, would you please come here a minute?  I wish to share some words with you.
column2: 哈吉布,能請你過來一下嗎?我有幾句話想跟你說。

column1: What is it, young and restless one?
column2: 什麼事啊,你這毛躁的小姑娘?

column1: Brothers!  A stranger!  Help!
column2: 弟兄們!有外人!快來人啊!

column1: You quickly discover that despite the fact they are overweight, the Eunuch Guards know how to fight.  You are quickly overwhelmed and captured.
column2: 你很快發現，儘管這些太監守衛體型臃腫，卻很懂得打鬥。你很快就被制伏並俘虜了。

column1: As they start to drag you away, you here the Djinni scream "He's loose!  Iblis is free!"  As the walls collapse around you, you think to yourself that maybe you should have been sneakier
column2: 當他們開始把你拖走時，你聽見精靈尖叫著：「他掙脫了！伊布利斯自由了！」在四周牆壁崩塌之際，你心想，或許自己該再小心一點才對

column1: Now, hurry, Master.  The guard may return any moment!
column2: 快點,大人,衛兵隨時都可能回來!

column1: You had better find a different hiding place, Master, a man is walking back this way.
column2: 你最好另找個藏身處，主人，有個人正朝這裡走回來。

column1: Very clever!  Oh, but that you possessed one.
column2: 真聰明！唉，可惜你根本沒有。

column1: You wouldn't think of harming such a friend.
column2: 你不會想去傷害這樣一位朋友的。

column1: Against the granite cliffs the remains of an ancient city are slowly being entombed by the sands.
column2: 在花崗岩峭壁之間，一座古城的遺跡正緩緩被黃沙掩埋。

column1: The sand and rocks at your feet were once part of the cliffs above you.
column2: 你腳邊的沙石,曾是頭頂那片峭壁的一部分。

column1: Above you in the clear desert sky you see the luminous full moon, framed by uncountable stars and constellations.
column2: 在你頭頂澄澈的沙漠夜空中，你看見皎潔的滿月，被無數星辰與星座環繞著。

column1: Don't bother me with silly quest... Mirror?  Do you have a mirror?  USE it, you fool!
column2: 別拿這種蠢問題煩我……鏡子?你有鏡子嗎?「用」它啊,你這蠢貨!

column1: Silence, fool!  I am pondering the significance of that at this very instant.
column2: 閉嘴,蠢貨!此刻我正在細想那玄機所在。

column1: What about it?  The door is clearly there, emblazoned with stars.  Wait, they are constellations! There is the scorpion, below is the dragon.  Perhaps you are not totally worthless after all.  We need but wait for the moon to shine upon the door.
column2: 那又怎樣？門明明就在那裡，上頭鑲滿了星星。等等，那些是星座！那是天蠍座，下面是飛龍座。或許你也不是完全沒用。我們只需要等月光照上那扇門。

column1: Questions!  Do you have nothing better to do than waste my time?  Let me think, and I'd advise you to do the same!
column2: 問題問題!你就沒別的事做,非得浪費我時間?讓我想想,也勸你自己多想想!

column1: Enough.  You do not want to make me angry!
column2: 夠了。你可不會想惹我發火。

column1: I grow impatient with your nonsense.  Either find a way to make the moon obey you, or you will face my wrath!
column2: 我對你這些胡鬧已經不耐煩了。要嘛想辦法讓月亮聽你的,要嘛承受我的雷霆之怒!

column1: You would try the patience of a dervish!  Do something!
column2: 你連苦行僧的耐性都要磨光了!倒是做點什麼啊!

column1: After One Thousand Years and a Year, the door to the ancient city is unsealed.
column2: 經過一千年又一年，古城之門終於解封了。

column1: The door to the Forbidden City is sealed, as it has been for centuries.
column2: 禁城之門被封印著，數百年來始終如此。

column1: The door is sealed by magic stronger than any you may ever command.
column2: 這道門被一股遠勝你所能駕馭的魔法封住了。

column1: There is a strong aura of magic emanating from the door ahead of you.  Ad Avis seems to be surrounded by magic, and for some reason, so do you.
column2: 前方那道門散發出一股強烈的魔法氣息。阿德‧阿維斯似乎被魔法環繞,而不知為何,你也是。

column1: You there, try walking through the door now!
column2: 喂,你!現在試著穿過那道門!

column1: No, no, fool.  Pass through it! Oh, get out of the way.
column2: 不，不，蠢貨。穿過去啊！唉，讓開。

column1: You have no idea how or why you got here, but it is nice to be able to help such a nice man.
column2: 你完全搞不清楚自己怎麼來的、又為什麼會在這裡,不過能幫上這麼好的人,感覺真不賴。

column1: I cannot be mistaken.  My translation of the prophecy must be correct.  It must be you.  Of all the stupid Heroes in the world, I had to be cursed with you!
column2: 我不可能弄錯。我對這則預言的解讀一定是對的。一定就是你。世上這麼多蠢英雄，偏偏讓我遇上你這個！

column1: What is wrong?  How does it go?
column2: 出了什麼岔子?那段是怎麼說的?

column1: For one thousand years and a year, Iblis will be bound... I know that it has been one thousand and one years to this very day, so that MUST be correct.
column2: 「一千年又一年，伊布利斯將被囚禁……」我知道到今天正好滿一千零一年，所以這一定沒錯。

column1: Beneath the tombstones of the city that he once did found. This is the Forbidden City, there is no doubt of that.
column2: 「在他曾建立的城市墓碑之下。」這裡就是禁城，這點毫無疑問。

column1: Then comes a Hero from the Northland, led unto despair... You are the only Hero that I have found recently, it must be you!  And I have no doubt you will despair before I am through with you.
column2: 「便有英雄自北境而來，被引向絕望……」你是我最近找到的唯一一位英雄，一定就是你！而我毫不懷疑，在我對付完你之前，你一定會陷入絕望。

column1: Passing through the door unopened, that he will find there. The door!  Accursed door.  Why can you not pass through it?
column2: 「穿過那扇他將在那裡尋得的未開之門。」那扇門！該死的門。你怎麼就是穿不過去？

column1: When the moonlight shines between the Dragon's jaws... And is caught and held there by the Scorpion's claws.
column2: 「當月光灑落在飛龍的顎間……並被天蠍的螯爪捕捉、留住之時。」

column1: The Moon!  It is not due to rise between the two constellations until tomorrow!  How can I be wrong!?
column2: 月亮！它要到明天才會升到那兩個星座之間！我怎麼可能會錯！？

column1: I WILL NOT BE DENIED!!!
column2: 我絕不容許被阻撓！！！

column1: I cannot be wrong.  It did not say a thousand and one years and a day.  No!
column2: 我不可能錯。它又沒說是一千零一年又一天。不！

column1: But how can the moon be where it cannot be? You!  You're the Hero.  Answer me how the moon can be between the Dragon and the Scorpion?
column2: 但月亮怎麼可能出現在它不該出現的地方？你！你是英雄。告訴我，月亮怎麼會出現在飛龍與天蠍之間？

column1: So be it! "By the Powers of all Darkness. By the Power of My Name Shall your soul be mirrored outward, and your body be the same!"
column2: 那就這樣吧！「以一切黑暗之力。以我之名之力，你的靈魂將向外映照，你的身軀亦將如是！」

column1: Oooo... I hate it when this happens.
column2: 喔喔……我最討厭發生這種事了。

column1: You whip the mirror out of your pocket, and carefully direct the reflected moonlight between the constellations.
column2: 你迅速從口袋掏出鏡子，小心翼翼地將反射的月光導向那兩個星座之間。

column1: At last!  Now how does the rest of the prophecy go? "And at last the Hero 'He who Waits Behind' shall see, While Iblis rises by the Dark One and the light shall flee. By the name of Suleiman, so shall this be!"
column2: 終於！接下來的預言是怎麼說的？「終於，英雄將見到『在後方等候者』，當伊布利斯藉黑暗者升起，光明將隨之消逝。以蘇萊曼之名，此事必將成就！」

column1: Now you know all you need to know.  Go in there and bring me the statue of Iblis!
column2: 現在你已經知道所有該知道的事了。進去把伊布利斯的雕像帶來給我！

column1: Bah!  What use are you?  Be silent!
column2: 哼！你有什麼用？給我安靜！

column1: You find that you have no desire to speak.
column2: 你發覺自己完全提不起說話的念頭。

column1: Passing through the door... what is it about that door?  Door with stars, constellations!  The scorpion and the dragon are on the door!  It must be when the moon shines upon the door that you may pass though it.
column2: 穿過那扇門……那扇門到底有什麼玄機？門上有星星，還有星座！天蠍與飛龍就在門上！一定是要等月光照上那扇門，你才能穿過去。

column1: Let me see, where is the moon now?  The moon is to the southeast.  But the door is facing north!  The moonlight will not pass over us enough to cast light upon the door.  Why is this happening to me?  After all these years of planning, how can so many things go wrong?
column2: 讓我想想，月亮現在在哪？月亮在東南方。但門朝北！月光不會照到門上。為什麼會發生這種事？籌畫了這麼多年，怎麼會出這麼多差錯？

column1: Well, Hero from the Northland, are you so powerful a magician that you can move the moon from its orbit and force it to shine on the door?  Or are you so strong that you can bend light?  What can you say or do that will let you pass the door?
column2: 哼，來自北方的英雄，你是個能把月亮移出軌道、強迫它照在門上的強大法師嗎？還是你力大無窮，能扭曲光線？你能說什麼、做什麼才能通過這道門？

column1: You find you can speak and move your arms again, but you can't seem to move your legs.
column2: 你發覺自己又能開口、能動手臂了,可雙腿卻怎麼也動不了。

column1: Bah!  You have no way of getting through that door.  And if your usefulness has ended....
column2: 哼！你根本沒辦法通過那道門。要是你已經沒有利用價值了……

column1: What is it about you that will let you pass the door?  There must be something.  The prophecy cannot be wrong.
column2: 你身上到底有什麼能讓你通過這道門？一定有什麼。預言不會錯的。

column1: Too hot?  Wear your thermal underwear next time.
column2: 太熱了?下次記得穿保暖內衣。

column1: The heat of this room is painful.  You feel the sweat pour down from you and hear it sizzle on the ground below.  There is the distinct odor of brimstone around you.
column2: 這房間的高溫讓人痛苦。你感覺汗水如注，滴落地面時發出滋滋聲。你周圍瀰漫著明顯的硫磺味。

column1: You rekindle your lamp.
column2: 你重新點亮了燈。

column1: Most of the water you poured over yourself has evaporated.  You are starting to get very hot.
column2: 你淋在身上的水大半已經蒸發,開始覺得熱得受不了。

column1: This room is filled with molten lava and small geysers.
column2: 這房間裡滿是熔岩與小型間歇泉。

column1: You pour some water over your clothes and feel a little relief from the heat.
column2: 你往衣服上澆了些水,暑氣稍稍緩解了些。

column1: You've just poured some water on yourself.  You're still wet.
column2: 你才剛往自己身上澆了水。你還濕著呢。

column1: You don't have any water, although you wish you did right now.
column2: 你一滴水也沒有,儘管此刻你巴不得有。

column1: OK, but you'd better keep it handy.  There may be other dark caves to explore.
column2: 好吧,不過你最好把它擺在順手的地方。說不定還有別的黑暗洞窟等著你去探。

column1: OK, but it seems silly to walk around holding a flame in a place like this.
column2: 好吧，但在這種地方拿著火把走來走去似乎有點蠢。

column1: You stepped on something that wasn't quite solid rock and felt the heat through your boot.
column2: 你踩到一塊不太像實心岩石的東西,熱氣透過靴子傳了上來。

column1: There is magic throughout this room.
column2: 這整個房間都充滿了魔法。

column1: The glow in this cavern comes from phosphorescent fungi.  The stream flows swiftly to a waterfall which disappears into a great cavern far, far below.
column2: 洞穴裡的光芒來自發光真菌。溪流湍急地流向一道瀑布，消失在遙遠的下方大洞穴中。

column1: You have no need of the magic rope here, hero.
column2: 英雄，你在這裡用不到魔法繩。

column1: You have no rope, hero.
column2: 英雄，你沒有繩子。

column1: Not now, you're busy.
column2: 現在不行，你正忙著。

column1: The rocks in this room are much too slippery to climb.
column2: 這房裡的岩石太滑了,根本爬不上去。

column1: OK, but there's light enough from the phosphorescent fungi on the walls.
column2: 好吧,不過牆上那些會發光的菌類照得也夠亮了。

column1: Many's the time you've regretted your lack of a formal swimming education.
column2: 好多次你都後悔自己沒正經學過游泳。

column1: You have already stopped the wind.
column2: 你已經止住了風。

column1: Go back down the stairs first.
column2: 先下樓梯回去。

column1: You have to cross the river first.
column2: 你得先渡過河才行。

column1: That's a great idea...
column2: 這主意真不錯……

column1: ...but how?
column2: ……可是要怎麼做?

column1: The speed of the rushing water would rip the waterskin out of your hand. and you might slip in on the slimy bank.
column2: 湍急的水流會把水袋從你手中沖走，你也可能在濕滑的岸邊滑倒。

column1: You don't have a waterskin to fill.
column2: 你沒有水袋可以裝水。

column1: Step back a little bit, Hero
column2: 英雄，退後一點

column1: Wouldn't it be nicer to have a diving board?
column2: 要是有塊跳水板不就好多了?

column1: You'd better get closer to dry land first.
column2: 你最好先靠近乾燥的陸地。

column1: Too late!
column2: 太遲了！

column1: 'Bye...
column2: 再見……

column1: Try jumping from the mossy bank by the falls.
column2: 試試從瀑布旁那片長滿青苔的岸邊跳過去。

column1: You think that the log is too far away and you wait for another one.
column2: 你覺得那根木頭太遠了，決定等下一根。

column1: Your foot slips due to the force of the air rushing into that small opening.  There is tremendous pressure from all the wind trying to surge into the hole, and you struggle to overcome it.
column2: 氣流灌進那個小開口的力道讓你腳下一滑。所有的風都想擠進那個洞,形成驚人的壓力,你拼了命才頂住。

column1: Whew!  That was close.
column2: 呼!好險。

column1: You just aren't strong enough to dislodge the pile of rocks.
column2: 你的力氣就是不夠大,撼動不了那堆石頭。

column1: The wind no longer blows as fiercely as before.
column2: 風不再像先前颳得那麼猛了。

column1: You are too weak to break down the rocks.
column2: 你的力氣不夠，無法擊碎這些岩石。

column1: The log is too large and has too much momentum for your Fetch spell to have much effect.
column2: 那根木頭又大又衝勁十足,你的取物術起不了多大作用。

column1: Careful examination of the airhole and the rocks above reveals that there are some loose stones that hold the structure together.  There are small fissures running between its base and the rocks above.
column2: 仔細檢查通氣孔與上方的岩石，你發現有幾塊鬆動的石頭撐住整個結構。底部與上方岩石之間有些細小的裂縫。

column1: There appears to be an opening in the rocks, but it's hard to see from this side of the river.
column2: 岩石間似乎有個開口,但從河這一側很難看清楚。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this room.
column2: 整間房間瀰漫著一股魔法的氣息。

column1: Your spell opens up some of the smaller fissures in the stones.
column2: 你的法術撐開了石頭上幾道較小的裂縫。

column1: The rocks will not burn.
column2: 這些石頭燒不起來。

column1: The rocks shake, but they are still well-supported at the base.
column2: 那些岩石搖晃著,基座卻依然穩固。

column1: Uh-oh, you don't seem to have a lamp.
column2: 糟糕,你身上似乎沒有燈。

column1: On the other side of the stalactites and stalagmites, you see the darkness of a very deep chasm.  Good thing you have this lamp to light your way.
column2: 在鐘乳石與石筍的另一頭,你看見一道極深裂谷的漆黑。幸好你有這盞燈替你照路。

column1: You're already using the lamp.
column2: 你已經在用燈了。

column1: If you did that, you would be lost in the dark.
column2: 你要那麼做,就會迷失在黑暗裡。

column1: You can't see to do that.  The lamp isn't lit.
column2: 你看不見,沒法那麼做。燈還沒點著。

column1: You can't see a thing.  It's too dark.
column2: 你什麼也看不見,太暗了。

column1: It's best if you just walk carefully.
column2: 你還是小心走路為妙。

column1: You can hear the sound of rushing wind.
column2: 你聽見狂風呼嘯的聲音。

column1: The sound of the wind has diminished.
column2: 風聲已經減弱。

column1: The rock glistens with moisture.
column2: 岩石上閃著濕潤的水光。

column1: The wind in here is far too strong for you even to attempt to use your rope.
column2: 這裡的風太猛,你連用繩子的念頭都別想。

column1: There doesn't appear to be a good place to use it here.
column2: 這裡似乎沒有適合用它的地方。

column1: The wind in here is far too strong for you even to attempt the levitation spell.
column2: 這裡的風太強了，你根本無法嘗試施展浮空術。

column1: There doesn't appear to be a good place to levitate here.
column2: 這裡似乎沒有適合施展浮空術的地方。

column1: The air rushing through the passage is so strong that it threatens to blow you off your feet.  You find it impossible to concentrate on anything else.
column2: 穿過通道的氣流強得幾乎要把你掀翻在地。你根本沒法專心去想別的事。

column1: Your eyes are blinded by the flash in the dark, and you still can't see.
column2: 黑暗中那道閃光晃花了你的眼,你還是什麼都看不見。

column1: For a few moments, your mind is overwhelmed by the squalling, whirling wind.
column2: 有那麼一瞬間,呼嘯打轉的狂風淹沒了你的心神。

column1: Then the euphoric feeling starts to fade and you stand alone in the darkness.
column2: 接著那股飄飄然的快感漸漸消退,你獨自一人站在黑暗之中。

column1: The past few hours are hazy in your memory.  You remember only one thing clearly:  you are here to find the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 過去這幾個鐘頭在你記憶裡一片模糊。你只清楚記得一件事:你來這裡,是為了尋找伊布利斯的雕像。

column1: You're busy falling right now.
column2: 你現在正忙著墜落。

column1: The wind is at its worst as it comes from the passage above.  There must be something you can do to stop its fury.
column2: 風從上方的通道灌下來時最是猛烈。一定有什麼辦法能止住它的狂暴。

column1: The wind blew you off like a leaf from a tree.  It doesn't seem as if you can get near enough to cut the wind off at its source.
column2: 風把你像片落葉似的吹得老遠。看來你沒法靠得夠近,從源頭把風截斷。

column1: The wind again caused you to fall like a rock.
column2: 風再一次把你像塊石頭般吹倒在地。

column1: You can't take much more of this.  You must stop the wind from blowing before it stops you.
column2: 你撐不了多久了。你得在風撂倒你之前,先讓它停下來。

column1: This is getting you nowhere.  Maybe you are looking at the problem from the wrong end.
column2: 這樣下去毫無進展。也許你打從一開始就看錯了問題的方向。

column1: Even with the lamp, the darkness can be treacherous.
column2: 就算有燈,這黑暗也可能暗藏殺機。

column1: The door resists all attempts to open it.
column2: 這扇門擋住了一切想打開它的嘗試。

column1: There is a mild feeling of residual magic in the room.
column2: 房間裡殘留著一絲淡淡的魔法氣息。

column1: Such a weak spell has no effect upon the fury of the wind.
column2: 這麼弱的法術,對這狂暴的風絲毫不起作用。

column1: It is difficult to judge where you are going in this darkness.  It is also very dangerous.
column2: 在這片黑暗裡很難判斷自己往哪兒走。而且這很危險。

column1: You feel what might be a wall of this cave.  It feels cold and damp, despite the desert outside.  The sound of the wind is mostly above you.
column2: 你摸到一面像是洞穴牆壁的東西。儘管外頭是沙漠,它摸起來卻又冷又濕。風聲多半從你頭頂上方傳來。

column1: You bump your head rather hard into a stalagmite.  Or is it a stalactite?
column2: 你的頭重重撞上一根石筍。還是說那是鐘乳石?

column1: As you step forward, you find nothing to step onto.  Your hands grab for a stalagmite (or is it a stalactite?), but slip off the slick surface.  You find yourself falling ...
column2: 你往前踏出一步，卻發現腳下空無一物。你的手抓向一根石筍（還是鐘乳石？），卻在光滑的表面上滑開。你發現自己正在墜落……

column1: The rocks around the passageway resist your flame dart.
column2: 通道周圍的岩石抵擋住了你的火焰鏢。

column1: The rock around the passageway is too strong to be affected by your force bolt.
column2: 通道周圍的岩石太堅硬，你的力場箭對它無效。

column1: You're not in a good position to make a jump.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合起跳。

column1: Jumping here won't help.
column2: 在這裡跳躍沒有用。

column1: Looking up at the sheerness of the sides and the slickness of the rock, you know that you wouldn't get very far climbing.
column2: 往上望著陡峭的崖壁和滑溜的岩石,你曉得自己爬不了多遠。

column1: Looking down at the sheerness of the sides and the slickness of the rock, you know you would merely wind up falling.
column2: 往下望著陡峭的崖壁和滑溜的岩石,你曉得自己只會落得摔下去的下場。

column1: You're not in a good position to climb.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合攀爬。

column1: All you can see is the pocket of cavern in which you stand, solid rock on all sides, and a massive doorway.
column2: 你只看得到自己所在的這個洞穴凹處、四面的實心岩壁,還有一道巨大的門。

column1: You can see that you are on a ledge overlooking darkness.  You can see something that appears deliberately fashioned across the brink, but you cannot tell what it is from where you are standing.
column2: 你看得出自己站在一道俯瞰黑暗的岩架上。崖邊橫著一個像是刻意打造的東西,但從你站的位置看不出那是什麼。

column1: You're not in a good position to use the rope.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合用繩子。

column1: You're not in a good position to levitate off of the cliff.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合從懸崖上施展浮空術。

column1: Considering your IMPRESSIVE skill at climbing, that wasn't exactly a brilliant maneuver.  You are beaten, battered, and bruised... but you ARE down.
column2: 以你「驚人」的攀爬技巧來說，那動作可稱不上高明。你被摔得鼻青臉腫……但總算是下來了。

column1: You really aren't up to this kind of physical abuse.
column2: 你還真承受不了這種折騰。

column1: Well, that was ONE way down.  But it hurt...a lot!
column2: 好吧，那也算是一種下去的方法。只是……痛得要命！

column1: You find yourself unable to cast any spells.  The voice's demand still rings within your ears.
column2: 你發覺自己施不出任何法術。那聲音的命令仍在你耳中迴響。

column1: You find yourself unable to move.  The voice's demand still rings within your ears.
column2: 你發現自己動彈不得。那道聲音的命令仍在你耳邊迴盪。

column1: Nothing but the voice's demand seems important right now.
column2: 此刻除了那道聲音的命令,別的都顯得無關緊要。

column1: Yes, by all means.  Give an answer.
column2: 好，儘管說吧。回答我。

column1: Stony silence is your answer.
column2: 回應你的是一片死寂。

column1: You hear a deep, echoing voice:
column2: 你聽見一道低沉、迴盪的聲音:

column1: None shall enter None shall pass but he who speaks the name of Power.
column2: 無人可入，無人可過，除非道出力量之名者。

column1: SPEAK NOW THE NAME OF POWER!
column2: 此刻,道出力量之名!

column1: You hear a sound of thunder.
column2: 你聽見一陣雷聲。

column1: You hear a roar of rocks collapsing in the distance.
column2: 你聽見遠處傳來岩石崩塌的轟鳴。

column1: The crash of boulder upon boulder echoes through the cavern.
column2: 巨石撞擊巨石的轟響在洞穴中迴盪。

column1: Your ears are deafened by rocks crashing and shattering against one another.
column2: 岩石互相撞擊碎裂的巨響,震得你耳朵發聾。

column1: The great, ancient door easily resists your tiny spell.
column2: 你那點微弱的法術,被這扇巨大的古老門扉輕鬆擋下。

column1: You haven't the power to move such a large object.
column2: 你沒有力氣搬動這麼大的東西。

column1: There is a subtle residue of magic in this room.  Once great and powerful, it is now dormant.
column2: 這房裡殘留著一絲微弱的魔法。它曾經強大無比,如今卻已沉睡。

column1: The great, ancient door is not damaged by your puny effort.
column2: 你那點微不足道的力氣,傷不了這扇巨大的古老門扉。

column1: It's too dark to see anything.
column2: 太暗了，什麼都看不見。

column1: You die wandering in the darkness
column2: 你在黑暗中徘徊而死

column1: You are in a roughly-hewn cave or old mine.
column2: 你身處一個粗鑿而成的洞穴或舊礦坑中。

column1: The lamp barely illuminates the dull rock above.
column2: 燈光勉強照亮了上方那片黯淡的岩石。

column1: The floor of this chamber is smooth stone.
column2: 這間石室的地面是光滑的石板。

column1: You are better off walking.
column2: 你還是用走的比較好。

column1: All that glitters is not gold.
column2: 閃閃發光的未必都是金子。

column1: Something is piled up in the dark.
column2: 黑暗中堆著什麼東西。

column1: It looks like treasure of jewels and gold.
column2: 看起來像是珠寶和黃金堆成的寶藏。

column1: It looks like armour of gold.
column2: 看起來像是黃金打造的盔甲。

column1: A strong aura of magic is around the treasure in this room.  An even stronger sense of magic radiates from the room beyond the far door.
column2: 這房裡的寶藏周圍縈繞著一股強烈的魔法氣息。而遠處那扇門後的房間,散發出更為強烈的魔法。

column1: You really fell for it.  As the ground closes up around you, your last thoughts are: "This is really the pits!"
column2: 你還真栽進去了。地面在你四周合攏時,你最後的念頭是:「這下真是掉進坑裡了!」

column1: Your animal magnetism obviously sparked this flash of brilliance.  Next time you proceed, pay heed to the creed:  "To succeed you need to be freed from greed."
column2: 顯然是你那股野獸般的魅力激出了這道靈光。下次行動前,記住這句箴言:「想成功,先戒貪。」

column1: The stalactite (or is it a stalagmite?) has shattered your dreams of becoming a hero.  You feel crushed.
column2: 那根鐘乳石(還是石筍?)粉碎了你當英雄的美夢。你被砸得稀爛。

column1: It is far too dark to be without your lamp. Maybe you should light the lamp again?
column2: 沒有燈實在太暗了。也許你該把燈重新點亮？

column1: Light the lamp!!!!
column2: 把燈點亮！！！！

column1: The structure in the center of the room is now dark, and a huge pile of boulders block the only door to the cavern.
column2: 房間中央的那座結構如今一片黑暗,一大堆巨石把通往洞穴的唯一一扇門堵死了。

column1: Was that supposed to be a wish, Master?  The correct way to make a wish is to say: "Wish for ...".
column2: 主人，那算是許願嗎？正確的許願方式是說：「許願……」。

column1: The statue of Ibis is no longer here.  It has been taken by Ad Avis.
column2: 伊比斯的雕像已經不在這裡了，被阿德·阿維斯拿走了。

column1: A glow illuminates this chamber.  It flows from an object high atop the strange structure before you.
column2: 一道光芒照亮了這間石室。它源自你面前那座奇異結構頂端的某件東西。

column1: At the sight of the glowing object you feel irresistibly drawn forward.
column2: 一看見那件發光的東西,你便感到一股無法抗拒的力量牽引你向前。

column1: Your feet seem to move with a will of their own.
column2: 你的雙腳像是有了自己的意志,自顧自地走著。

column1: As you draw near, you see that the object is a statue made of crystal.  A dark, shapeless form stirs within it.
column2: 你走近一看,才發現那東西是一尊水晶雕像。一團漆黑、沒有形狀的影子在裡頭蠕動。

column1: The crystal's cool glow is numbing.  You feel compelled to pick it up, but as you reach out...
column2: 水晶散發的清冷光芒令人麻木。你忍不住想把它拿起來,可就在你伸出手的瞬間……

column1: You are suddenly unable to move.
column2: 你突然動彈不得。

column1: Fool!  Some hero you are!  A pawn of the very man you sought to destroy.
column2: 蠢貨!虧你還是個英雄!你不過是你一心想剷除之人手中的一枚棋子罷了。

column1: Thanks to your help, I now have that through which I can summon the greatest destructive power ever known - Iblis himself!
column2: 多虧你的幫忙，我現在擁有了召喚有史以來最強大破壞力量的媒介——伊布利斯本尊！

column1: Do you know how long I have awaited this moment?  Seventy years!  Seventy years I have served the Dark Master while watching for the stars to align.  Seventy years I have spent learning the magic that controls men's minds and waiting for a fool to fulfill the prophecy for me.
column2: 你知道我等待這一刻等了多久嗎？七十年！七十年來我侍奉黑暗之主，靜候星辰排列成形。七十年來我鑽研操控人心的魔法，等待一個傻瓜替我實現預言。

column1: Tonight, as the moon arises between the Scorpion's Claws and the Dragon's Teeth, I shall begin the ritual that shall make me the Master!  I will control Iblis, and thus control the world.
column2: 今晚，當月亮升起於天蠍之螯與巨龍之齒之間，我將展開讓我成為主宰的儀式！我將掌控伊布利斯，進而掌控整個世界。

column1: While you, pawn of Prophecy, shall remain behind in this tomb.   You shall be, "He Who Waits Behind."
column2: 而你，這預言的棋子，將永遠留在這座墓穴之中。你將成為「留守者」。

column1: Farewell, Hero!
column2: 再會了，英雄！

column1: You pick up the ring and read the inscription, "He Who Waits Behind."  As you put it on your finger, you twist it a bit to see its design.
column2: 你拾起戒指，讀到上面刻的字：「留守者」。你把戒指戴上手指，轉了轉想看清它的花紋。

column1: I may grant you three wishes, Oh Master of the Ring.  What is your first wish?
column2: 噢,戒指的主人哪,我可以實現你三個願望。你的第一個願望是什麼?

column1: By my will, may you be well in all ways!
column2: 憑我的意志,願你諸事康健!

column1: You feel a warm glow spreading throughout your body.
column2: 你感到一股暖流在全身上下蔓延開來。

column1: I did it!  My spell worked!
column2: 我成功啦!我的咒語靈了!

column1: What is your next wish, Oh Master of the Ring?
column2: 您的下一個願望是什麼呢,噢,魔戒之主?

column1: You have but one wish left.  Choose carefully, Master.
column2: 您只剩最後一個願望了。慎重選擇,主人。

column1: You've wished away your three wishes, Oh Master of the Ring.  Enjoy your stay at Hotel Iblis.  It will be a long one, no doubt.
column2: 噢,戒指的主人哪,你三個願望都許完了。好好享受你在「伊布利斯大飯店」的時光吧。毫無疑問,這會是一段很長很長的住宿。

column1: By my Power, may you have the skill of ten men!
column2: 以我的力量，願你擁有十人之力！

column1: You suddenly feel much more skilled.
column2: 你頓時覺得身手俐落了許多。

column1: Gee, it's so nice when my magic actually works.
column2: 哎呀,我的魔法真的靈了,感覺真好。

column1: Well, I'll try, Master of the Ring.  Here goes.  By my Magic, may it be done!
column2: 好吧，我試試看，戒指之主。開始了。以我的魔法，願此事成真！

column1: Very well.  By my Word, so be it!
column2: 好的。以我之言，如你所願！

column1: I think I know that one.  By the Ring, Do your Thing!
column2: 我想我知道這招。以戒指之力，使命必達！

column1: By the Way, Make my Day!.
column2: 順道一提，讓我心想事成！

column1: There is a quiet pause while nothing happens.
column2: 一陣靜默過去,什麼也沒發生。

column1: I guess I can't grant you that wish.  Sorry.
column2: 我看那個願望我實現不了。抱歉。

column1: There is another moment of silence.
column2: 又是一陣沉默。

column1: I'm afraid I don't know that wish either, Master.  What else would you like?
column2: 那個願望我恐怕也不懂,主人。您還想要什麼呢?

column1: The only sound is your own groan.
column2: 唯一的聲響,是你自己的一聲呻吟。

column1: Well, Master, you can always try again.  I'll get it right sometime.
column2: 嗯,主人,您隨時可以再試一次。我總有一天會弄對的。

column1: This time, the Djinni sighs.
column2: 這一回,精靈嘆了口氣。

column1: Master, I'm sorry.  I guess I can only grant you Health, Prowess, and then Teleport you to Iblis.
column2: 主人，抱歉。我想我只能給你「健康」、「武力」，然後把你傳送到伊布利斯那裡。

column1: There will be no safety for anyone until Iblis is returned, Master.  We must hurry and bring him back!
column2: 在伊布利斯被送回去之前,誰都不會安全,主人。我們得趕緊把他帶回來!

column1: In the name of Suleiman bin Daoud, the Binder, and by my Power as He Who Waits Behind:  Take us to Iblis!
column2: 以束縛者蘇萊曼·賓·達伍德之名，並以我身為「留守者」的力量：帶我們去見伊布利斯！

column1: Something flashes in the darkness.
column2: 黑暗中有什麼東西閃了一下。

column1: You see a small, golden ring around the tip of a stalagmite (or is it a stalactite?).
column2: 你看見一枚小巧的金戒指,套在一根石筍(還是鐘乳石?)的尖端上。

column1: There is still a source of magic in this room.
column2: 這房裡還有一個魔法來源。

column1: Oh Ho Ho, Master!  That tickles.
column2: 哦呵呵，主人！好癢喔。

column1: Master, I am a djinni.  If you want to play fetch, maybe you should get a dog.
column2: 主人，我是個精靈。你要是想玩你丟我撿，不如去養條狗吧。

column1: The djinn is composed entirely of magic, but all other sources of magic in this room are now gone.
column2: 這精靈完全由魔法構成，但這房間裡其他的魔法來源如今都已消失。

column1: No thank you, Master.  I've been sleeping for one thousand and one years already.
column2: 不用了，謝謝，主人。我已經睡了一千零一年了。

column1: wish for %s
column2: 許願 %s

column1: You need to choose a skill or ability that is within the means of your character.
column2: 你需要選擇一項你的角色能力所及的技能或屬性。

column1: But, Master, I have already granted you that wish.
column2: 可是主人,那個願望我已經幫你實現過了。

column1: I am sorry, Master, but honor is the one thing I cannot grant you.  You must earn that yourself.
column2: 抱歉，主人，榮譽是我唯一無法賜予你的東西。你必須自己去贏得它。

column1: Which skill or attribute do you want to increase?
column2: 你想提升哪項技能或屬性？

column1: Well, I can kind of grant you that wish, but it has two problems.  First, I can only teleport you to where Iblis is now, and second, its the last wish I can grant.  You'll have to make all of your other wishes first.
column2: 嗯，這個願望我算是能實現，但有兩個問題。第一，我只能把你傳送到伊布利斯現在所在之處；第二，這是我能實現的最後一個願望。你得先許完其他願望才行。

column1: So, if you still want me to do it, repeat your wish and it shall be granted (I hope!)
column2: 所以，如果你還是要我這麼做，再說一次你的願望，我就會實現它（希望如此！）

column1: Oh, I'm sorry, Master, but I can't do that.  My magic has no effect on Iblis.
column2: 哦，抱歉，主人，我辦不到。我的魔法對伊布利斯不起作用。

column1: Your spell doesn't even begin to move the rocks blocking the doorway.  They are too large.
column2: 你的法術連堵在門口的石頭都撼不動分毫。它們實在太大了。

column1: Um.. Master?  I know I'm pretty useless and all that, but maybe I could help you escape somehow.
column2: 呃……主人？我知道我沒什麼用處，但也許我能想辦法幫你逃出去。

column1: Even with your great strength, it would be impossible to move all of those rocks.  You had better find another way to escape.
column2: 就算你力大無窮,也不可能把那些石頭全搬開。你最好另尋出路逃出去。

column1: It would take years to clear away all of those rocks from the door.
column2: 要把門前那些石頭全清掉,得花上好幾年。

column1: I just don't know too much about that, Master.
column2: 那件事我實在不太懂,主人。

column1: I've been in the ring so long, I can't remember.
column2: 我在戒指裡待太久了,記不得了。

column1: Let's see, that was a small town somewhere around here, isn't it?
column2: 我想想,那是這附近的一座小鎮,對吧?

column1: That's where all the Katta live, I think.
column2: 我記得卡塔族都住在那兒。

column1: We Djinn are very great and powerful.  Well, some of us are, anyway.
column2: 我們精靈可是偉大又強大。嗯,反正有些是啦。

column1: That's some sort of magical fire, maybe.
column2: 那大概是某種魔法之火吧。

column1: It's an elemental made out of water.
column2: 那是個由水構成的元素。

column1: I think that looks something like a lot of wind.
column2: 我覺得那看起來有點像一大團風。

column1: It's big, and it looks like it is made from rocks.
column2: 它好大,看起來像是岩石做的。

column1: Some Djinn can make powerful elementals, but not me.  I did make a pepperoni pizza once, though.
column2: 有些精靈能召喚強大的元素，但我不行。不過我倒是做過一次義式辣腸披薩。

column1: All Djinn know a lot about magic.  I'm just not very good at it.
column2: 所有精靈都很懂魔法。只是我這方面不太行而已。

column1: I think Iblis has been taken to some sort of Palace.
column2: 我想伊布利斯被帶到了某座宮殿之類的地方。

column1: The Ifreet like to live in the desert.  An Ifreet is a kind of Djinn.
column2: 伊夫利特喜歡住在沙漠裡。伊夫利特是精靈的一種。

column1: There's plenty of money next door, but I think it is trapped.
column2: 隔壁有的是錢,不過我想那是有陷阱的。

column1: It was rumored that the Sultan of Isfahan was in love with a cow, but I guess that was a long time ago.
column2: 傳聞伊斯法罕的蘇丹愛上了一頭母牛,不過我想那是很久以前的事了。

column1: The Katta are some cat people that live in the desert.
column2: 卡塔族是住在沙漠裡的一種貓人。

column1: I've been in the ring so long, I think I forgot.  You can call me Djinni, though.
column2: 我在戒指裡待太久了，我想我忘了。不過你可以叫我精靈就好。

column1: I am supposed to make sure Iblis is returned to the Forbidden City.  I do have enough magic to take you with me to where Iblis has been taken, I think.
column2: 我的職責是確保伊布利斯被送回禁城。我想,我的魔法應該夠帶你一起去伊布利斯被帶去的地方。

column1: Iblis was the most powerful of all Djinn.  A thousand years and a year ago he created a huge city here and tried to make all men bow before him.  Suleiman summoned all the Djinn to his side, and with their power turned the city into Iblis's tomb.  Then Iblis himself was caught and bound, and became the statue you saw here.
column2: 伊布利斯曾是所有精靈中最強大的一位。一千零一年前，他在此地建了一座巨城，妄圖讓所有人向他俯首稱臣。蘇萊曼召集了所有精靈到他身邊，以他們的力量把這座城變成了伊布利斯的墓穴。接著伊布利斯本尊被擒拿束縛，化為你在此看到的雕像。

column1: I can give you the ability of ten men at any skill you want to improve upon.  You have but to wish for Strength, or Magic, or Intelligence, or Sneaking, or, well, you get the idea.
column2: 不論你想精進哪項本事,我都能賜你十人之力。你只消許願要力量、魔法、智力、潛行,或者,嗯,你懂我意思的。

column1: I can heal your wounds and injuries and make you all better.
column2: 我能治好你的傷口和病痛,讓你恢復得好好的。

column1: Suleiman ben Daoud was the Wisest of the wise, Strongest of the strong, Most Magicallest of the Magicians.  He was also very kind and made me "He who waits Behind" and put me in the Ring.
column2: 蘇萊曼·賓·達伍德是智者中的智者、強者中的強者、法師中最會法術的法師。他也十分仁慈，封我為「留守者」，把我放進了這枚戒指裡。

column1: I'm the Djinni of the Ring.  Suleiman asked for someone to stay here and guard to make sure Iblis didn't escape.  I volunteered.  All the other Djinn laughed at me, 'cause I'm not very good at magic.  But not Suleiman.  He said I'd do fine and let me be "He who Waits Behind." I guess he was wrong, huh?  Iblis is gone, and you and I are kinda stuck here, aren't we, Master?
column2: 我是戒指精靈。蘇萊曼當時要找人留下來看守，確保伊布利斯不會逃脫。我自願接下這任務。其他精靈都笑我，因為我魔法不太行。但蘇萊曼沒笑我，他說我一定能勝任，讓我當「留守者」。我猜他看走眼了吧？伊布利斯不見了，你和我大概都被困在這裡了，是吧，主人？

column1: The prophecy may have become a bit garbled over the years, but the original went like this when Suleiman taught it to me:
column2: 這預言經年累月或許有些走樣,但當年蘇萊曼教我時,原話是這樣的:

column1: One Thousand Years and a Year, so shall Iblis be caught,  In the Tombstones of the City, Where he and I once fought.  Until moonlight has been captured twixt the Scorpion and Drake;  Then comes a hero from the Northland, and the ancient powers wake.   Led and followed by a Dark one; guide to Deceit and despair.  Passing through the Trial by Fire, Trials of Water, Earth and Air.  Passing through the Door unopened, barrier that yields to none,  By my name of Suleiman, So shall this be done!   Come at last unto betrayal, and to 'He who Waits Behind' (That's me)  And, and, (now, how did that go?)  and Iblis shall rise, and,  something about a "final knight."  I'll remember it sometime.
column2: 「一千零一年，伊布利斯將被擒拿，在此城的墓塚之中，就是他我曾交戰之地。直至月光被擒於天蠍與巨龍之間；來自北方的英雄降臨，古老的力量甦醒。受一名黑暗者引領並尾隨，引向欺瞞與絕望。穿越烈火試煉、水之試煉、土之試煉與風之試煉。穿越那扇緊閉之門，那道無人能破的屏障，以我蘇萊曼之名，此事必成！終將迎來背叛，來到『留守者』（就是我）跟前。然後，然後，（呃，接下來是什麼來著？）然後伊布利斯將會甦醒」，還有什麼跟「最後騎士」有關的。我改天會想起來的。

column1: I can grant you three wishes by the Power of the Ring.  I can get you anything you want, as long as it is health, prowess, or teleporting you to Iblis.
column2: 我能以戒指之力賜你三個願望。只要是「健康」、「武力」，或是把你傳送到伊布利斯那裡，我都能幫你辦到。

column1: You are the Lord of the Ring, since you put it on and twisted it.  Your wish is my command, as long as it helps return Iblis here.
column2: 既然你戴上了戒指並轉動了它，你就是戒指之主。只要有助於讓伊布利斯回到此地，你的願望便是我的命令。

column1: This used to be the city that Iblis created, but after Suleiman and I and all the other Djinn beat Iblis, this became kinda his tomb, I suppose.
column2: 這兒原本是伊布利斯造的城,可自從蘇萊曼、我,還有其他精靈打敗他之後,我想這兒就算成了他的墳墓吧。

column1: Well, I was supposed to make sure Iblis doesn't get loose, so now I guess I got to help you bring him back as the Prophecy says.
column2: 嗯，我本來的任務是確保伊布利斯不會逃脫，所以現在我想我得幫你照預言所說，把他帶回來。

column1: To the west of Shapeir and north of here stands a puzzle.  Five W's.
column2: 夏皮爾以西、此地以北,佇立著一道謎題。五個 W。

column1: Hero business like eating peanuts.
column2: 當英雄這檔事啊,就像嗑花生,一顆接一顆停不下來。

column1: You don't have a rope.
column2: 你沒有繩子。

column1: You don't need to use the rope here.
column2: 你在這兒不需要用繩子。

column1: You really don't want to do that here.
column2: 你真的不會想在這裡這麼做。

column1: The oasis looks blue and cool.  You're not so sure about the character attached to the palm tree.  Cool, maybe.
column2: 綠洲看起來湛藍清涼。至於棕櫚樹旁那個傢伙，你就沒那麼確定了。也許算是「酷」吧。

column1: You can't do that on a saurus.
column2: 你沒辦法騎在索魯斯上做這件事。

column1: Yes, this does seem like a nice place for a short nap.
column2: 沒錯，這裡看起來確實適合小睡片刻。

column1: This does seem like a good place.  You curl up near the pool, and soon fall asleep.
column2: 這裡看起來確實是個好地方。你蜷縮在水池邊，很快便睡著了。

column1: You awaken at dawn, much refreshed.
column2: 你在黎明時醒來，精神煥發。

column1: You awaken just after sunset, much refreshed.
column2: 你在日落後不久醒來，精神煥發。

column1: Quest for Glory is like same old story.
column2: 追尋榮耀,說來說去總是老調重彈。

column1: Man who keep one hand on weapon have difficulty walking in public.
column2: 手不離兵刃者,難以坦然行於人前。

column1: Man who wrongfully attack peaceful dervish will soon find himself up to his earlobes in wrongful attacks.
column2: 無故攻擊和平苦行僧者,不久便會發覺自己深陷無故的攻擊裡,直沒到耳根。

column1: It appears that not only will he not fight with you, your attacks do him no harm.
column2: 看來他不僅不願跟你動手,你的攻擊對他也毫髮無傷。

column1: Your attacks seem unable to hurt the dervish.
column2: 你的攻擊似乎傷不了這位苦行僧。

column1: If you want right answer, ask right person.
column2: 想要正確的答案,就得問對人。

column1: He who attacks without cause soon attracts without pause.
column2: 無故出手攻擊的人,很快就會招來沒完沒了的麻煩。

column1: You take another deep drink of the warm, but refreshing water.
column2: 你又深深喝了一口溫熱卻沁涼提神的水。

column1: You take a deep drink of the warm, but refreshing water.
column2: 你深深喝了一口溫熱卻沁涼提神的水。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskins are full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現所有水袋都已裝滿。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskin is full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現水袋已經滿了。

column1: You then check and refill all your waterskins.
column2: 接著你檢查並把所有水袋都重新裝滿。

column1: You then check and refill your waterskin.
column2: 接著你檢查並把水袋重新裝滿。

column1: It's a shame you don't have a waterskin to fill.
column2: 可惜你沒帶水袋可以裝水。

column1: Perhaps you should not attempt such a thing while riding a saurus.
column2: 或許你不該在騎著索魯斯的時候嘗試這種事。

column1: Move over to the dervish first.
column2: 先移動到苦行僧那裡。

column1: You have some of his beard already.
column2: 你已經取到了一些他的鬍鬚。

column1: Beard must stay or dervish drift away.
column2: 鬍鬚需留，否則苦行僧將飄然而去。

column1: You take a small piece of the Dervishes' beard and put it carefully away.
column2: 你取下一小撮苦行僧的鬍鬚，小心地收了起來。

column1: Man who waits too late gets no response from man who waits even later.
column2: 等得太晚的人,別指望從等得更晚的人那兒得到回應。

column1: Land of Shapeir like rainbow; very colorful and always something new.
column2: 夏皮爾之地恰似彩虹,五光十色,總有新鮮事。

column1: When laws made without justice, men live without freedom.
column2: 律法若無公義,百姓便無自由。

column1: Djinn are like weather; always changeable, never predictable.
column2: 精靈如天氣；恆常善變，難以預測。

column1: Fire Elemental like mule; cannot be driven, only be encouraged to follow.
column2: 火元素如騾；不可驅使，只能勸誘跟隨。

column1: The Water Elemental is to water as the lodestone is to metal.
column2: 水元素之於水,猶如磁石之於鐵。

column1: Wind blows fastest without sand.
column2: 風無沙阻，吹得最疾。

column1: Perseverance can wear away rock.
column2: 滴水穿石,貴在恆心。

column1: Elemental reflects its own nature.
column2: 元素生物,映照的正是自身的本性。

column1: Monster is as monster does.
column2: 怪物的本性,看牠所作所為便知。

column1: Magic is like water - without it, Shapeir is like sand.
column2: 魔法如水——沒有它，夏皮爾便如沙漠。

column1: A ruler who knows his people as his own hand.
column2: 好的統治者熟知子民,如熟知自己的手掌。

column1: A fool does not rule for long.
column2: 蠢人坐不久龍椅。

column1: Wizards sit and study problem while a Hero solves it.
column2: 巫師坐而究問題，英雄起而解之。

column1: Beggar in street happier than prisoner in palace.
column2: 街頭乞丐,也比宮中囚徒來得快活。

column1: Many sauruses who do not wish to go ridden by men who do not wish to stay.
column2: 許多索魯斯不願被不願久留之人騎乘。

column1: A wise man can survive where a fool will perish.
column2: 同一處絕境,智者能活,蠢人必亡。

column1: The mountains stand to the north and south, as they have for as long as I've been here.
column2: 群山屹立南北兩方,自我來到此地起便是如此。

column1: All that glitters is not gold; not all that is gold is for the taking.
column2: 閃閃發光的未必是金;就算是金,也未必拿得走。

column1: Man who steals is like nightfall - sneaks everywhere and takes things from sight.
column2: 竊賊如夜幕降臨——悄然而至，取走眼前所見之物。

column1: Adventurer is one who has no home, but is never homeless; has no help, but is never helpless.
column2: 冒險者沒有家,卻從不無家可歸;沒有援手,卻從不束手無策。

column1: Man who ask about weather has stormy disposition.
column2: 老問天氣的人,脾氣多半像場風暴。

column1: Where all men share, no man thirsts.
column2: 人人懂得分享之處,沒人會口渴。

column1: Three men can keep secret if two are dead.
column2: 三人想守住祕密,除非其中兩人已死。

column1: A trader knows the value of a coin.
column2: 生意人最懂一枚錢幣的份量。

column1: Man who seeks future in stars finds business looking up.
column2: 往星空裡尋找未來的人,生意自然蒸蒸日上。

column1: One who watches world whirl by sees many things.
column2: 靜看世界轉動的人,見得到許多事物。

column1: Man who speaks rhyme for reason.
column2: 出口成韻的人,自有他的一番道理。

column1: Katta like Dervish - content to sit in sun and think.
column2: 卡塔族如苦行僧——安於坐陽思索。

column1: Wise man learn much by saying little.
column2: 智者話說得少,學到的卻多。

column1: Man with no name like question with no answer.
column2: 無名之人,如同無解之問。

column1: Water is the liquid of life, and a mirror to the soul.
column2: 水是生命之液,也是靈魂的明鏡。

column1: It does not give much shade, but I do get a date once in a while.
column2: 它遮不了多少蔭,不過偶爾倒能讓我摘顆椰棗嚐嚐。

column1: Once you start, it's very hard to stop.
column2: 一旦開了頭,就很難收得了手。

column1: Freedom like temper - only valued when lost.
column2: 自由如脾氣——失去方知珍貴。

column1: Your saurus is like reflection in pool; both but seem to be real.
column2: 你的索魯斯如池中倒影；兩者皆似真而已。

column1: Oasis is like wise saying because both seem shallow but are very deep.
column2: 綠洲如智語，皆看似淺薄，實則深邃。

column1: Difference between beard and world is one whirls around tree, the other twirls around me.
column2: 鬍子與世界有何不同?一個繞著樹打轉,一個繞著我打轉。

column1: A Dervish sits from sun to sun, but a Hero's work is never done.
column2: 苦行僧從日出坐到日落，但英雄之事永無止盡。

column1: When he who seeks what is where finds out why.
column2: 當尋找「何物何處」的人,終於參透了「為何」。

column1: Water is like wisdom; without it man is just dust.
column2: 水如智慧；沒有它，人不過是塵土。

column1: Griffin who nest on mountain ledge always edgy.
column2: 在山崖築巢的獅鷲,神經總是繃得緊緊的。

column1: Man who jokes with scorpion gets it in the end.
column2: 與蠍子開玩笑之人，終將自食惡果。

column1: Man who steals purse gets trashed.
column2: 偷錢袋的人,終究會被痛扁一頓。

column1: TerrorSaurus is like man in desert; very fond of lesser Sauruses.
column2: 恐魯斯如沙漠中人；極愛小索魯斯。

column1: Jackalman is like sardines; always goes in packs.
column2: 豺狼人像沙丁魚,出沒總是成群結隊。

column1: Ghouls give fighter the cold shoulder.
column2: 食屍鬼總愛給戰士一個冷颼颼的下馬威。

column1: Early to bed, and early to rise, makes a man... Well, one out of three ain't bad.
column2: 早睡早起使人……嗯，三項中中一項也不錯了。

column1: It is said that man is like a caged beast.  And a beast is like a rumor -- it is best to dispel it early.  Harik knows the way, and I know the where.
column2: 人們說,人就像籠中之獸。而獸又像謠言,最好趁早化解。哈里克知道「如何」,而我知道「何處」。

column1: Creature in need need Hero indeed.
column2: 生物有難，實需英雄。

column1: The where is five skareen lengths to the west of Shapeir, and but two skareen lengths north of here.
column2: 「何處」在夏皮爾以西五個斯卡林長，而在此地以北僅兩個斯卡林長。

column1: That is the heart of the Puzzle.  Harik may help you free the frozen heart from the body of the beast.
column2: 那正是謎團的核心。哈里克或許能幫你從那頭野獸體內取出冰封的心臟。

column1: If not soon, then never.
column2: 若不趁早,便再無機會。

column1: When you discover the Why, you will know more about the Puzzle around the Puzzle.
column2: 當你發現「為何」，你便會更了解謎團之外的謎團。

column1: That is why you must solve the Puzzle.  Knowing Who will help you find out Why.
column2: 這正是你必須解開謎團的原因。得知「何人」將有助於你查出「為何」。

column1: How's are for Heroes.  When you find the What, perhaps you will know How.
column2: 「如何」是英雄的事。當你找到「何物」，或許你便會知道「如何」。

column1: Time flies, desert dries, creature dies.
column2: 時光飛逝,沙漠乾涸,生靈隨之命喪。

column1: Why, a skareen length is about as far across as this area around my Oasis.
column2: 嗯，一個斯卡林長大約就是我這片綠洲周圍的寬度。

column1: You hear a strangled cry of protest from the beast.  When you look back at it, it seems to say, "Water, I must have water".
column2: 你聽見那野獸發出一聲哽咽的抗議叫聲。你回頭看它時，牠似乎在說：「水，我一定要喝水」。

column1: You see the carcass of the beast in its iron prison.
column2: 你看見那頭怪物的屍體,躺在牠的鐵牢之中。

column1: You see a strange creature trapped in a cage in the middle of the desert.  The head resembles a cobra, the body looks like that of a panther, and its tail is like a scorpion.  You find it difficult to believe that such a combination could exist in nature.   It looks half-starved and its eyes have a touch of madness in them.
column2: 你看見一隻奇異的生物被困在沙漠中央的籠子裡。牠的頭像眼鏡蛇，身軀像黑豹，尾巴則像蠍子。你很難相信自然界竟會存在這樣的組合生物。牠看起來餓得半死，眼中還帶著一絲瘋狂。

column1: It stares at you with a cold, dead, gaze, much like your last blind date.
column2: 牠用一雙冰冷、毫無生氣的眼睛盯著你,活像你上回那場相親的對象。

column1: As you look into the eyes of the creature, you hear in your mind, "Come closer, much closer.  Come closer and free me, release me.  Open the cage and free me.  Open the cage and trust me.
column2: 當你注視這生物的雙眼時，你在腦海中聽見：「靠近一點，再靠近一點。靠近並釋放我，解放我。打開籠子放我出來。打開籠子，相信我。

column1: Your saurus isn't thrilled at the prospect of getting any closer to that cage.
column2: 你的索魯斯一點也不想再靠近那個籠子。

column1: It's dead enough already, thank you.
column2: 牠已經夠死的了,謝謝。

column1: You can't fight a caged beast.  Your sword is hampered by the bars of the cage, and if you get too near, the beast could easily strike you with its tail.
column2: 你沒辦法跟籠中的怪物對打。鐵欄擋著你的劍施展不開,而且一旦靠得太近,牠的尾巴一甩就能輕鬆掃中你。

column1: Well, OK, but it probably won't help at this point.
column2: 唔,好吧,不過事到如今這大概也幫不上忙了。

column1: You should move closer to the front of the cage.
column2: 你應該再靠近籠子的正前方一點。

column1: You have nothing to feed the beast.
column2: 你沒有東西可以餵這頭野獸。

column1: You can water the beast until you're blue in the face, but it'll never grow again.
column2: 你就算給這頭怪獸餵水餵到臉發青,牠也再不會長大了。

column1: You have no water to give the beast.
column2: 你沒有水可以給那頭怪物。

column1: Whaddya want a bunch of dead fur fer?
column2: 你要一堆死毛幹啥?

column1: You don't dare get any hair as long as the beast is staring at you.
column2: 只要那頭怪獸還盯著你,你就不敢去取牠的毛。

column1: Whaddya want a bunch of fur fer?
column2: 你要一堆毛幹啥?

column1: By the dead-pan expression on the beast's face, you get the impression he doesn't want any, thank you all the same.
column2: 從那頭怪獸面無表情的模樣,你感覺得出牠並不想要,謝謝你的好意。

column1: You get some hair from the beast and mix it into the dispel potion.
column2: 你從那頭怪獸身上取了一些毛,摻進驅散藥水裡。

column1: The potion doesn't seem to work, but the beast seems overjoyed with the liquid anyway.
column2: 那藥水看來沒起作用,不過那頭怪獸倒是對這液體欣喜若狂。

column1: I know not who you are, or why you saved me, but you are a fool if you expect to be rewarded for it.  I am Al Scurva, an apprentice Sorcerer.
column2: 我不知道你是誰，也不知道你為何救我，但你若指望因此得到回報，那你就是個傻子。我是阿爾·斯庫爾瓦，一名見習巫師。

column1: My former master, Ad Avis, transformed me into that monster and left me to die.  He will be most displeased that I am still alive.
column2: 我的前任主人阿德·阿維斯把我變成了那頭怪物，任我自生自滅。他要是知道我還活著，一定會非常不悅。

column1: Do not think he will be unaware of your interference.  He will be watching you from now on.
column2: 別以為他會察覺不到你的插手。從今往後,他都會盯著你。

column1: I will give you this warning:  Do not go near Ad Avis unless you have some spell of protection or are very quick.
column2: 我給你這個警告:除非你身上備有某種防護咒法,或是身手夠快,否則別靠近阿德·阿維斯。

column1: If he manages to throw a spell at you, you will either be his willing servant or else be transformed into a creature most resembling your soul.
column2: 他一旦對你擲出咒法,你不是淪為他俯首聽命的奴僕,就是被變成一頭最肖似你靈魂的生物。

column1: There is some major ritual magic he has planned for when the moon arises between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth.  If he succeeds in this it would be best not to be in this land.  I certainly will not be.
column2: 他計畫在月亮升起於天蠍之螯與巨龍之齒之間時，施展某種重大的儀式魔法。要是他成功了，最好別留在這片土地上。我肯定不會留下來。

column1: Just remember, Ad Avis awaits you in Raseir.
column2: 只要記住一點:阿德·阿維斯正在拉希爾等著你。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the food and water.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的食物跟水。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the food.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的食物。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the water.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的水。

column1: The beast quickly swallows the dried meat and fruit%s
column2: 野獸迅速吞下了乾肉與果乾%s

column1: The beast greedily laps up the water%s
column2: 野獸貪婪地舔飲著水%s

column1: The beast laps greedily at the potion.
column2: 那頭怪獸貪婪地舔食著藥水。

column1: The beast looks a little weaker now, but still watches your every move with its cold stare.
column2: 這頭野獸現在看起來虛弱了一些，但仍以冷冽的目光緊盯你的一舉一動。

column1: The cage door is stuck fast.
column2: 籠門卡得死死的。

column1: Trusting soul, weren't you?  A starving monster dying of thirst in the desert is not the most honorable of gentlemen.  Things aren't always as they seem, and there definitely seemed to be more to this thing.
column2: 你還真是輕信於人，是吧？沙漠中一頭飢渴瀕死的怪物，可稱不上什麼正人君子。事情往往不是表面看起來那樣，而這東西顯然還有內情。

column1: Freeing a creature like this does not seem to have been a wise decision.  Perhaps the fact that the beast is obviously starving and dying of thirst had something to do with its ill-temper.
column2: 釋放這樣的生物似乎不是個明智的決定。或許這頭野獸明顯飢渴瀕死的狀態，正是牠脾氣如此暴躁的原因。

column1: The beast feels pain, no doubt, but its enchantment seems to prevent it from being killed by anything you are able to do.
column2: 那頭怪物確實會痛,沒錯,但牠身上的魔法似乎讓你用盡任何手段都殺不死牠。

column1: The beast reacts in pain and fury.
column2: 那頭怪物又痛又怒,劇烈掙扎起來。

column1: There is a strong enchantment on the beast and upon the cage.
column2: 那野獸與牢籠上都施著強大的魔咒。

column1: The beast seems at peace for a moment, but not for long.
column2: 那野獸暫時平靜了一會兒,卻維持不久。

column1: The beast only reacts with a few quick blinks.
column2: 那野獸只是飛快地眨了幾下眼。

column1: The beast is much too large to fetch, and you can't control the spell well enough to get only some fur.
column2: 這野獸太大,「取物」術搬不動,你也沒法精準到只取一撮毛。

column1: A closer look at the monster shows that it is fierce indeed, although obviously weakened by lack of food and water.  It will not survive much longer.
column2: 湊近一看,這怪物確實兇猛,只是顯然因缺水缺糧而虛弱不堪。牠撐不了多久了。

column1: Its eyes catch yours, and you can't seem to look away.  Huge eyes, burning eyes that see your very soul.
column2: 牠的目光與你交會，你似乎無法移開視線。那是一雙巨大、灼熱的眼睛，彷彿能看穿你的靈魂。

column1: You seem to hear in your mind the words, "Come closer, much closer.  Come closer and release me, free me.  Come closer and trust me.  Look into my eyes and trust me."
column2: 你似乎在腦海中聽見這些話：「靠近一點，再靠近一點。靠近並解放我，釋放我。靠近並相信我。看著我的眼睛，相信我。」

column1: There are no signs of footprints around the cage.  The door to the cage is fastened shut, but there is no lock.
column2: 籠子四周沒有腳印的痕跡。籠門緊緊閂著，但沒有鎖。

column1: The magic on the cage door seems to be impervious to your spell from this angle.  You might try casting the spell from in front of the cage (if you dare!)
column2: 從這個角度看，籠門上的魔法似乎不受你的法術影響。你或許可以試著站在籠子正前方施法（如果你敢的話！）

column1: Its death has freed it.
column2: 牠的死,反倒讓牠解脫了。

column1: The cage door is unlocked.
column2: 籠門沒有上鎖。

column1: The cage is made of wrought iron.  When the sun is on it, that metal must be scorching.  There is no sign of food or water in the cage and the creature must have been in the cage for at least a couple of days.
column2: 這座籠子是鍛鐵打造的。太陽照射時，這金屬想必燙得驚人。籠內看不到食物或水的痕跡，這隻生物想必已經被關在籠裡至少兩天了。

column1: He's wolfing that down pretty good, for a snake!
column2: 就一條蛇來說,牠這狼吞虎嚥的勁兒可真不賴!

column1: You find yourself tongue-tied as you try to tell your story.  Perhaps you should try again.
column2: 你想講述自己的故事時卻結結巴巴。或許你該再試一次。

column1: You discover how poor a speaker you are as you listen to yourself.  You couldn't convince anyone of anything.
column2: 你聽著自己說話，才發現口才有多差。你什麼都說服不了任何人。

column1: You stutter as you start to speak and can't think of anything to say.  You just haven't talked enough to gain the skill to convince a tree of anything.
column2: 你才開口就結巴，腦中一片空白。你的口才還不足以說服一棵樹相信任何事。

column1: As you begin to speak, you realize you are just saying whatever comes into your head.  You need to stop and think for a moment about what you are going to say and how to say it.  Then you might start again.
column2: 你才開口，就發現自己只是想到什麼說什麼。你得先停下來想清楚要說什麼、該怎麼說，然後再重新開始。

column1: Forget it!  Come back when you've overcome your speech impediment
column2: 算了吧！等你克服口吃的毛病再回來

column1: Get off your saurus first.
column2: 先從你的索魯斯上下來。

column1: Trees and plants are notoriously uncommunicative.
column2: 樹木花草向來以難以溝通聞名。

column1: No matter how hard you try, you seem unable to communicate with the tree about your good deeds.
column2: 無論你怎麼努力，似乎都無法向這棵樹傳達你做過的善行。

column1: Not again!  We've heard it before.
column2: 又來了！這故事我們聽過了。

column1: No matter how hard you try, you seem unable to communicate with the tree about your heroics.
column2: 無論你怎麼努力,似乎都沒辦法跟這棵樹聊你的英雄事蹟。

column1: Please!  Not that story again!!!
column2: 拜託！別再講那個故事了！！！

column1: The tree shows no sign of recognition.
column2: 那棵樹沒有任何認出你的跡象。

column1: Julanar's leaves rustle lightly at the sound of her name.
column2: 聽見自己的名字,茱拉娜爾的葉子輕輕沙沙作響。

column1: Your saurus needs more room before you can dismount.
column2: 你的索魯斯需要更多空間，你才能下來。

column1: Take nothing from the tree except what may be offered.
column2: 除了主動給予的東西，別從這棵樹上拿走任何東西。

column1: The tree has provided the fruit you need.
column2: 這棵樹已經給了你所需的果實。

column1: There is no fruit on the tree.
column2: 樹上沒有果實。

column1: You have come to a sandy alcove in the mountains.  A strange, human-shaped plant seems to have grown out of the sand here.
column2: 你來到山間一處鋪滿細沙的凹地。這裡的沙中,似乎長出了一株奇異的、形似人形的植物。

column1: The plant resembles a woman very much.  It has no leaves or other signs of life.
column2: 這株植物看起來很像一位女子。它沒有葉子，也沒有其他生命跡象。

column1: The plant resembles a woman very much.  It has a beautiful canopy of green leaves.
column2: 這株植物看起來很像一位女子。它有著一片美麗的翠綠葉冠。

column1: The plant vaguely resembles a woman.  It has no leaves or other signs of life.
column2: 這株植物隱約有點像一位女子。它沒有葉子，也沒有其他生命跡象。

column1: The tree's branches are bare.
column2: 這棵樹的枝椏光禿禿的。

column1: You curl up under Julanar for a brief nap.
column2: 你蜷縮在茱拉娜樹下小睡片刻。

column1: The blazing desert sun makes it impossible to rest here.  The withered tree provides no shade at all.
column2: 炙熱的沙漠驕陽讓你無法在此歇息,那棵枯樹遮不了半點蔭。

column1: You awaken at dawn.
column2: 你在破曉時醒來。

column1: You awaken just after sunset.
column2: 你在日落後不久醒來。

column1: You awaken at dawn, after a fitful sleep full of disturbing dreams.
column2: 你在黎明時醒來，經歷了一夜多夢不安的睡眠。

column1: You remember Aziza's story, and you know that the gifts you have given to Julanar are what needed to be done for her.  Her shape is by her own choice, and cannot be dispelled.
column2: 你想起阿姿莎說過的故事,明白你送給茱拉娜爾的那些禮物,正是她所需要的。她的形貌是她自己的選擇,無法被驅散。

column1: You sense that your kind words and deeds are worth more than magic to the tree.  Its shape cannot be dispelled.
column2: 你感覺得到,對這棵樹而言,你的善言善行比魔法更有價值。她的形貌無法被驅散。

column1: You have no water to give.
column2: 你沒有水可給。

column1: You kick a few clods of dirt in the direction of the tree's roots.  The tree remains immutable.
column2: 你朝樹根方向踢了幾塊土塊。這棵樹依然一動也不動。

column1: The tree shivers slightly as you talk, but there is no other response.
column2: 你說話時,樹微微顫了一下,但沒有別的回應。

column1: You don't want to push your luck.
column2: 你不想得寸進尺。

column1: The tree has no idea why you are thanking her, but she nods as in a gentle breeze.
column2: 那棵樹不曉得你為什麼要謝她,但仍像被微風拂過似地點了點頭。

column1: The plant nods as if in a gentle breeze.
column2: 那株植物點了點頭,彷彿微風拂過。

column1: You have thanked her already.  Don't get carried away.
column2: 你已經謝過她了。別得寸進尺。

column1: I talk to the trees... but they don't listen to me...
column2: 我對著樹木說話……但它們不聽我的……

column1: The earth of the Elemental shimmers at the feet of Julanar.
column2: 元素之土在茱拉娜爾的足邊微微閃爍。

column1: The earth rests on the ground at the base of the tree.
column2: 這堆泥土靜靜地躺在樹根旁的地面上。

column1: Indian Giver!
column2: 送了又想拿回去！

column1: You have already opened her heart.
column2: 你已經打開了她的心扉。

column1: Nice try, but you'll have to find a more traditional method of opening her heart.
column2: 想得美,你得用更老派的法子才能打開她的心扉。

column1: How unecological.  Not to mention rude!
column2: 真不環保。更別提有多沒禮貌了!

column1: You think she likes you.
column2: 你覺得她喜歡你。

column1: The plant surely was once a woman.
column2: 這株植物想必曾經是個女子。

column1: You admire the way she rotates her crops.
column2: 你欣賞著她輪作的姿態。

column1: The plant almost looks like a woman.
column2: 這株植物幾乎像個女子。

column1: How rude!
column2: 多無禮！

column1: What lovely foliage she has.
column2: 她的枝葉真是美麗。

column1: You dare not defoliate this lovely plant!
column2: 你不敢摘去這株美麗植物的枝葉！

column1: It is the Fruit of Compassion, a gift from Julanar.
column2: 這是慈悲果，茱拉娜的贈禮。

column1: Julanar has given you the fruit you need.  You need no more.
column2: 茱拉娜已經給了你所需的果實，你不需要更多了。

column1: It smells like pomegranates.
column2: 聞起來像石榴。

column1: The water, as you pour it out on the ground, quickly sinks into the soil and vanishes.
column2: 你把水倒在地上,水很快滲進土裡,消失不見。

column1: The Elemental Earth seems to sparkle a bit in the light as it settles down into the soil around the plant.
column2: 這捧元素土壤沉入植物周圍的泥土時，在光線下似乎微微閃爍。

column1: You sprinkle the dirt around the base of the tree.
column2: 你把泥土灑在樹根周圍。

column1: You start to tell the plant about yourself and your adventures.  You mention how much you wish to be a hero, and how hard it has been to become one.  You then tell her about your experiences in Spielburg with the Dryad and how you aided her by getting the seed of the Spore Spitting Spirea.
column2: 你開始向這株植物訴說自己與冒險經歷。你提到自己有多渴望成為英雄，而這條路走來有多艱辛。接著你告訴她，你在斯皮堡與樹精的經歷，還有你如何幫她取得孢子噴射繡線菊的種子。

column1: You tell about how and why you came to Shapeir, about all the friends you have made in this land of Shapeir and all the fun you have had here.  Finally, you mention that you are giving her water as a simple act of kindness, that it might ease her thirst.
column2: 你說起自己為何來到夏皮爾，說起在這片土地上結交的朋友，以及在這裡度過的快樂時光。最後你提到，你送水給她只是出於單純的善意，希望能解她的乾渴。

column1: You tell her how you took the Famous Adventurer's Correspondence Course and journeyed to Spielburg to become a hero.  You mention how you came to Shapeir and the things you have done here so far.
column2: 你告訴她，自己修習了《知名冒險家函授課程》，並前往斯皮堡成為英雄。你也提到自己是如何來到夏皮爾，以及至今在這裡做過的事。

column1: You begin speaking about the problems facing Shapeir and about the menace of the Earth Elemental.  You tell how frightened the people of the city were, and how scared you were to face it.
column2: 你開始談起夏皮爾面臨的種種難題，以及土元素帶來的威脅。你說起城中百姓有多麼恐懼，自己面對它時又是多麼害怕。

column1: Even though you were afraid, you knew that you had to protect and save your friends.  So you overcame your fear and defeated the monster.  Now you are giving the magical earth to her to help remind her that there is still magic in the world and that others still care about her.
column2: 儘管你很害怕，但你知道自己必須保護並拯救朋友。於是你克服恐懼，擊敗了那頭怪物。如今你把這捧魔法泥土送給她，希望提醒她這世上仍有魔法存在，也仍有人關心著她。

column1: You tell about how you fought the Earth Elemental and how hard it was to defeat.  You tell how your friends needed you.  The Hero business is a dirty business, but someone's got to do it.
column2: 你說起自己如何與土元素交戰，那有多麼難以擊敗。你說朋友們有多麼需要你。英雄這行又髒又累，但總得有人去做。

column1: The act of hugging the tree is fruitless.
column2: 抱樹這個舉動毫無結果。

column1: A purple fruit has been created on one branch of the tree.
column2: 樹上的一根枝椏長出了一顆紫色果實。

column1: The "Fruit of Compassion" drops into your hands, Julanar's gift to you for restoring her spirit.
column2: 「慈悲果」落入你手中，這是茱拉娜為感謝你喚回她心靈所贈的禮物。

column1: You carefully put the fruit away.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把果實收了起來。

column1: Off you go to the computer store for a new monitor. By the way, real Heroes don't throw things at ladies (even rather stiff ones).
column2: 你該去電腦店買台新螢幕了。順帶一提，真正的英雄不會對女士（就算是有點僵硬的女士）動手動腳。

column1: Hmm.  Let's see now.  "chirpy" is the first person indicative...
column2: 嗯。讓我想想看。「chirpy」是第一人稱直述語氣……

column1: No, wait.  "Caw" is the noun form...
column2: 不對,等等。「呱」是名詞形態……

column1: %s "%s", but you're not sure.  %s.
column2: %s「%s」，但你不太確定。%s。

column1: The rock was heavy, but after the sand was moved, you manage to get the rock off the feather.
column2: 石頭挺沉,不過把沙撥開後,你總算把石頭從羽毛上挪了開來。

column1: The rock was heavy, but you manage to move it off the feather.
column2: 石頭挺沉,不過你總算把它從羽毛上挪了開來。

column1: You see sheer cliff walls where the mountains begin, stark against the brilliant blue of the sky.  On a ledge jutting out from the cliff, a Griffin seems to have made a nest.
column2: 你看見群山起始之處豎著陡直的峭壁,在湛藍天空的襯托下格外鮮明。一隻獅鷲似乎在峭壁向外凸出的一處岩台上築了巢。

column1: Some debris has fallen from the nest.
column2: 一些殘屑從巢裡掉了下來。

column1: He's gone.
column2: 他走了。

column1: A Cliff Note:  How high a cliff would make a miffed Griffin stiff, if the gifted Griffin could lift cliffs?
column2: 懸崖筆記：如果這隻有天賦的獅鷲能舉起懸崖，那要多高的懸崖才能讓一隻惱怒的獅鷲僵住呢？

column1: You see various rocks lying on the ground around here.
column2: 你看見這附近的地上散落著大大小小的石塊。

column1: The desert sky is ablaze with stars.
column2: 沙漠的夜空被滿天繁星點得熾亮。

column1: The sun burns down.
column2: 烈日炙烤而下。

column1: It's a Griffin;  it's a plane;  it's....gone!
column2: 是獅鷲！是飛機！是……不見了！

column1: It may have wings and feathers, but the Griffin is not what you'd normally call a bird!
column2: 牠雖有翅有羽,但獅鷲可不是你一般所說的那種「鳥」!

column1: The feather looks just like a Griffin feather.
column2: 這根羽毛看起來活脫脫就是獅鷲的羽毛。

column1: You don't see one just yet.
column2: 你目前還沒看到。

column1: You already have a feather, so don't be greedy.  The Griffin probably needs them more than you.
column2: 你已經有一根羽毛了，別太貪心。獅鷲恐怕比你更需要它們。

column1: The Griffin has many feathers.  Perhaps you can grab one of them.
column2: 獅鷲有許多羽毛。或許你能抓走其中一根。

column1: It's up there.  You're down here.
column2: 它在上頭,你在下頭。

column1: Good idea!  Now if you could just find one.
column2: 好主意！現在只差找到一根了。

column1: You will need to move the rock first.
column2: 你得先移開那塊石頭。

column1: With your skill, you can tell that since it juts outward, it would be too dangerous to climb the cliff.
column2: 憑你的本事,你看得出這峭壁向外凸出,攀爬起來實在太危險。

column1: You can see that the nest is made up mostly of rocks, hair, pin-feathers, and the down from the Griffin's breast.
column2: 你看得出,那巢主要是由石塊、毛髮、初生的針羽,以及獅鷲胸前的絨毛堆成的。

column1: If you'd really wanted to climb, you wouldn't be doing what you're doing right now.
column2: 你要是真想攀爬,現在就不會在做手邊這檔事了。

column1: It is hard to climb when the rock juts out toward you.  You think that maybe climbing these cliffs is just too difficult.
column2: 岩石朝你這方向凸出來,實在難爬。你心想,要爬上這些峭壁恐怕難如登天。

column1: You pick up a large feather and put it away.
column2: 你撿起一根大羽毛,把它收了起來。

column1: You find a large feather in the nest and put it away.
column2: 你在巢裡找到一根大羽毛,把它收了起來。

column1: That sounded an awful lot like "I think there's a feather under that rock there", but of course we all know that Griffins can't talk!
column2: 這聽起來非常像是「我想那塊石頭底下有根羽毛」，不過我們都知道獅鷲是不會說話的！

column1: Perhaps it's your proximity, or perhaps your choice of words, but in any event, you seem to have startled the Griffin.
column2: 也許是你靠得太近,也許是你的措辭,總之你似乎驚動了那頭獅鷲。

column1: From among the Griffin's feathers, you choose a nice-looking one and pluck it.
column2: 你從獅鷲的羽毛裡挑了一根好看的,把它拔了下來。

column1: SQUAAAAWK!
column2: 嘎啊啊啊——！

column1: The Griffin, formerly peaceful and content, now unhappy and upset, leaves the scene.
column2: 那隻獅鷲原本安詳自得,如今卻悶悶不樂,氣呼呼地離開了現場。

column1: Even after moving the sand, you are still too weak to move the rock.  Perhaps you should do some more exercise to build up your strength.
column2: 就算撥開了沙,你還是力氣不夠,搬不動這塊石頭。或許你該多鍛鍊鍛鍊,把力量練上去。

column1: The rock is firmly embedded in the sand.
column2: 這塊石頭牢牢嵌在沙裡。

column1: What fun!  Just like your sandbox back home.
column2: 真好玩!就跟你老家的沙坑一個樣。

column1: You can't quite free the feather, but you do manage to dig some sand from around the rock.
column2: 你還沒法把羽毛弄出來,不過總算把石頭周圍的沙撥開了一些。

column1: The rock is as you left it when you turned it over.
column2: 這塊石頭就維持在你翻過來後的模樣。

column1: There is a feather lying near the rock.
column2: 石頭旁邊躺著一根羽毛。

column1: You can see part of a feather sticking out from under a rock near the cliff base.
column2: 你看見峭壁腳下一塊石頭底下,露出了一截羽毛。

column1: You've moved the rock about as far as it will budge.
column2: 這塊石頭能挪的,你都已經挪到底了。

column1: You're a big Hero around here.  You don't have to be tidy.  You don't have to put all the nice rocks back where you found them.
column2: 你在這裡可是了不起的英雄，用不著這麼愛乾淨。你不必把每塊好看的石頭都放回原位。

column1: Huh?
column2: 嗯？

column1: From this angle, the nest seems to be made up mostly of rocks.
column2: 從這個角度看,那巢似乎大半是用石頭堆成的。

column1: You search for your Saurus.  Sorry, your Saurus is nowhere to be seen.  It seems to have split.
column2: 你四處尋找你的索魯斯。抱歉，你的索魯斯已不見蹤影，看來牠溜走了。

column1: Roget, the Saurus, is at a loss for words.
column2: 這隻名叫羅傑的索魯斯，一時語塞。

column1: The waves of desert dunes roll towards the mountains.
column2: 沙漠中層層沙丘朝著群山起伏綿延。

column1: It was most fortunate that Ali Fakir provided you with some amenities to go with your saurus.
column2: 幸好阿里·法基爾替你的索魯斯準備了一些配備。

column1: The saurus-riding equipment is on the saurus, wherever he is.
column2: 騎乘索魯斯用的裝備都在索魯斯身上，不管牠現在在哪裡。

column1: The blazing stars take on new meanings in the deserts of Shapeir.
column2: 在夏皮爾的沙漠裡,那些燦爛的繁星彷彿有了全新的意義。

column1: Only a Fresno Raisin could love the blazing orange sun of Shapeir.
column2: 也只有弗雷斯諾的葡萄乾,才會愛夏皮爾那輪炙熱的橘色太陽。

column1: The sand and rock maintain their heat, even during the chilly evening hours.
column2: 即使在寒冷的夜晚，沙地與岩石依然保有餘溫。

column1: The searing heat from the bright, burning sands is too much for your strained eyes.
column2: 炙熱滾燙的沙地反射出刺眼強光,你那疲憊的雙眼實在吃不消。

column1: Your saurus seems to want a drink.
column2: 你的索魯斯似乎想喝水。

column1: You scoop some sand into the cloth bag.
column2: 你舀了些沙子進布袋裡。

column1: You have bagged all the sand you can bag.
column2: 你已經把能裝的沙子都裝進袋子了。

column1: You pick up a handful of sand and watch mournfully as it trickles through your fingers and disappears.
column2: 你抓起一把沙,黯然望著它從指縫間流瀉而下,消失無蹤。

column1: Don't these things ever sweat?
column2: 這些傢伙難道都不會流汗嗎？

column1: In Spielburg, these things weren't so friendly.
column2: 在史畢柏格,這些東西可沒這麼友善。

column1: You got it.
column2: 你拿到了。

column1: Just walk on over and get it.
column2: 走過去拿就行了。

column1: Where's that clothespin!
column2: 那個曬衣夾跑哪去了！

column1: If it tastes anything like it smells...!
column2: 如果嚐起來跟聞起來一樣……！

column1: That's not a good idea.
column2: 這主意不妙。

column1: Your saurus is not here.
column2: 你的索魯斯不在這裡。

column1: You have to be ON a saurus to get OFF a saurus.
column2: 你得先騎在索魯斯上，才能從索魯斯上下來。

column1: Q.  How do you get down off a saurus?
column2: 問：要怎麼從索魯斯上下來？

column1: A.  You don't.  You get down off a duck.
column2: 答:你不「下」大象——鴨絨才是從鴨子身上拔的。

column1: Whee!  Nothing like a Saurus ride in the desert!
column2: 咻！在沙漠裡騎索魯斯真是暢快！

column1: Your saurus' natural homing instinct quickly sets you on the right path.
column2: 你的索魯斯天生的歸巢本能，很快就帶你走上正確的路。

column1: You can't do that while riding a Saurus.  You'd fall off.
column2: 騎在索魯斯上沒辦法這麼做，你會摔下來的。

column1: You'll have to catch him first!
column2: 你得先抓到牠！

column1: It is in such bad shape that it would be of no use to you.
column2: 這東西破損得太嚴重,對你毫無用處。

column1: It does not have a tail.
column2: 牠沒有尾巴。

column1: The Scorpion's stinger drips its deadly venom.
column2: 蠍子的尾刺滴著致命的毒液。

column1: It does not have claws.
column2: 牠沒有爪子。

column1: The Ghoul's claws are a deadly weapon.
column2: 食屍鬼的爪子是致命的武器。

column1: You have already taken its tail.
column2: 你已經取下牠的尾巴了。

column1: You get the scorpion's tail, carefully avoiding its venom.
column2: 你小心避開毒液，取下了蠍子的尾巴。

column1: You have no use for its tail.
column2: 牠的尾巴對你沒有用處。

column1: It does not have any claws.
column2: 牠沒有任何爪子。

column1: You have already relieved the poor Ghoul of all of its claws.
column2: 你已經把這隻可憐食屍鬼的爪子全部取走了。

column1: You get the ghoul's claws.
column2: 你取下了食屍鬼的爪子。

column1: You have no use for its claws.
column2: 牠的爪子對你沒有用處。

column1: Bad desert room: room %d, direction %d
column2: 沙漠房間錯誤：房間 %d，方向 %d

column1: You collapse under the %s, and perish from dehydration.  The desert is no place to be without water.
column2: 你在%s下倒下，因脫水而死。沒有水，沙漠絕非能久留之地。

column1: There is no need for that here.
column2: 這裡用不著這麼做。

column1: and %d Centimes.
column2: 和 %d 生丁。

column1: You search the carnage and find %d Dinars%s
column2: 你搜尋屍骸，找到了%d第納爾%s

column1: You remove %d of your daggers from the body.
column2: 你從屍體上取回了%d把匕首。

column1: You wisely demonstrate your inborn self-preservation skills.
column2: 你明智地展現了與生俱來的保命本領。

column1: It might have been a glorious battle... but ones in which the Hero dies rarely make the front page.
column2: 這或許是一場光榮的戰鬥……但英雄戰死的故事很少能登上頭版。

column1: You have dealt the Scorpion your own deadly sting.
column2: 你以致命的一擊回敬了這隻蠍子。

column1: You have triumphed gloriously over the Brigand.
column2: 你光榮地戰勝了這名強盜。

column1: You have killed the mighty Terrorsaurus.
column2: 你擊殺了強大的恐魯斯。

column1: You have bested the Jackalmen.
column2: 你制伏了這群豺狼人。

column1: The Ghoul's body clatters to the sand.
column2: 食屍鬼的屍體嘩啦啦地散落在沙地上。

column1: The Ghoul's poisonous claws have made you too weak to continue.  Drained of energy, you fall, and the Ghoul quickly finishes killing you.
column2: 食屍鬼的毒爪讓你虛弱得撐不下去。氣力耗盡,你倒了下去,食屍鬼迅速結束了你的性命。

column1: The antidote has lost its strength.
column2: 解毒劑已經失效。

column1: You just became scorpion shish-ka-bob.
column2: 你就這樣成了蠍子串燒。

column1: You aren't close to a door.
column2: 你離門還有一段距離。

column1: Everyone here keeps their doors tightly locked and bolted.
column2: 這兒家家戶戶都把門鎖得緊緊、閂得死死的。

column2: Error: new alley 不是物件。

column2: error: %s 在 %d,%d 沒有位置

column2: 從這裡看不到任何街道標誌。

column2: Error:  sideAlley 在 %d,%d 不是物件

column2: %s 號誌%s，朝向%s%s，上面寫著：「%s」。

column2: 這裡有一條沒有標示的巷弄%s通往%s。

column1: The sign on your street reads '%s'.
column2: 你這條街上的標誌寫著：「%s」。

column1: You got some useful lockpicking practice, but the door has been bolted from the inside.  No amount of skill will allow you to open it.
column2: 你藉此練習了開鎖技巧，頗有收穫，但這扇門從裡面被閂死了。再高的技巧也無法把它打開。

column1: Error: no alley index entry for room %d, heading %d
column2: Error: 房間 %d、方向 %d 沒有巷弄索引項目

column1: You hear the sounds of people standing in fear of their drab and wretched lives.
column2: 你聽見人們的聲音，充滿了對自己單調悲慘生活的恐懼。

column1: These guards appear to be the strong, silent type.
column2: 這些衛兵看起來是那種沉默寡言的硬漢。

column1: These guards could whip you like cream.
column2: 這些衛兵三兩下就能把你打得落花流水。

column1: This street is closed by order of Khaveen.   No one is permitted to pass.
column2: 這條街已奉哈維因之命封閉。任何人不得通行。

column1: Halt.  You are under arrest for speaking with a known subversive.  You will come with us.
column2: 站住。你因與已知的叛亂分子交談而被捕，跟我們走一趟。

column1: Two more guards walk up behind you and "escort" you to the dungeon.  After some brutal questioning, you are just locked away for a long time.
column2: 又有兩名衛兵從你身後走來，「護送」你前往地牢。經過一番殘酷的審問後，你就這樣被關了很長一段時間。

column1: You hear the sounds of people coming and going and living their lives.
column2: 你聽見人們來來往往、過著日常生活的聲音。

column1: The mass of rock radiates strong magic.
column2: 這塊巨大的岩石散發著強烈的魔力。

column1: Your attack has no effect on the stone of the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你的攻擊對土元素的岩石軀體毫無效果。

column1: You have enough elemental dirt.
column2: 你已經有足夠的元素泥土了。

column1: You can't be serious.  It is too big and dangerous right now.
column2: 你該不是認真的吧。牠現在太巨大也太危險了。

column1: You carefully put the elemental away in the cloth bag.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把元素收進布袋裡。

column1: The elemental earth keeps slipping through your hands, and you have nothing suitable to put it in.
column2: 這捧元素泥土不斷從你手中滑落，而你手邊沒有合適的容器可以裝它。

column1: You hear a rumbling at the end of the alley...
column2: 你聽見巷弄盡頭傳來一陣轟隆聲……

column1: That wouldn't help you now.
column2: 那現在幫不了你。

column1: You quickly move into position in preparation for combat.
column2: 你迅速就定位，準備戰鬥。

column1: The strange noise seems to be coming closer...
column2: 那陣怪聲似乎越來越近……

column1: The software you are now using was produced through the efforts of many people: designers, artists, programmers, musicians, and lots of other hard-working professionals.  If you make copies of this software for any reason other than to make a personal backup, you are not only breaking the law, but raising the cost of software for all legitimate users.  PLEASE DO NOT MAKE ILLEGAL COPIES OF THIS GAME !
column2: 你現在使用的軟體，是由許多人共同努力完成的：設計師、美術人員、程式設計師、音樂家，以及許多辛勤工作的專業人士。如果你複製這套軟體，且目的並非僅供個人備份，你不僅觸犯法律，也會提高所有合法使用者的軟體成本。請勿非法複製本遊戲！

column1: Oh, by the way . . .  You will need the information contained in the printed documentation to successfully complete this game.  In other words, it's not just the law . . . It's a good idea.
column2: 對了…你會需要印刷手冊裡的資訊,才能順利完成本遊戲。換句話說,這不只是法律問題…也是個好主意。

column1: The city of Shapeir shines in the moonlight.
column2: 夏皮爾城在月光下閃耀著光芒。

column1: The sun reflects off Shapeir's many towers.
column2: 陽光映照在夏皮爾的無數尖塔上。

column1: Now you look like a hero, Shapeir style.
column2: 這下你看起來才像個英雄,夏皮爾風格的!

column1: No, no.  That costume just will not do.
column2: 唔得唔得,呢套行頭實在唔行。

column1: How's about a nice... Wait?!... What is-a that up there?
column2: 來喔，要不要來點...等等？！...那上面是什麼東西？

column1: You did your best, but it just wasn't good enough.
column2: 你已經盡了全力,可惜還是不夠看。

column1: Could not find Hero file -- %s.
column2: 找不到英雄檔案──%s。

column1: I'm terribly sorry.  That doesn't seem to be a proper Quest for Glory I character save file.
column2: 非常抱歉，這似乎不是有效的《英雄傳奇 I》角色存檔。

column1: a:glory1.sav
column2: a:glory1.sav

column1: Please insert the disk on which you saved your winning Hero from "Quest for Glory I: So You Want To Be A Hero" into the disk drive so that your Hero can make the journey to Shapeir.
column2: 請將您《英雄傳奇 I：你想成為英雄》(So You Want To Be A Hero) 中存下的過關英雄磁碟片，插入磁碟機，讓英雄能啟程前往夏皮爾。

column2: 角色匯入成功。

column2: 要再試一次匯入角色嗎？

column2: 哇！你真的成為英雄了！[分數 %d / 500]

column2: 要選擇新的角色類型嗎？

column2: 請為您的角色選擇較長的名稱。
