column2: 你還有點數要分配。

column1: With the help of the Slave Woman, Mayzun, you found the Emir Ali hiding in his bedroom.  With your urging (and a few sharp remarks from Mayzun), the Emir swore he'd restore the city.  By the first light of dawn, the Emir Ali al-Din Hasan ordered all fighting to cease and proclaimed the evil rule of Ad Avis and Khaveen to be over.  As the Emir began an incredibly dull speech, you slipped away to complete your quest.
column2: 在女奴梅尊的協助下，你在臥室裡找到了藏身的埃米爾阿里。在你的勸說（以及梅尊幾句尖銳的提醒）下，埃米爾發誓要恢復這座城市。天色微亮之際，埃米爾阿里·阿丁·哈桑下令停止一切戰鬥，並宣告阿德·阿維斯與哈維因的邪惡統治就此終結。就在埃米爾開始發表冗長乏味的演說時，你悄悄溜走，繼續完成你的任務。

column1: You stand beside the cracked and dry fountain in Raseir's Plaza of the Fountain.  You hold in your hands the waterskin containing the Water Elemental that you captured so long ago in Shapeir.
column2: 你站在拉希爾噴泉廣場那座龜裂乾涸的噴泉旁。手裡握著那只水袋,裡頭裝著你很久以前在夏皮爾捕獲的水元素。

column1: The Elemental, under your command, is released into the fountain.
column2: 那元素聽從你的號令,被釋放進噴泉裡。

column1: You stare in wonder at the powerful transformation of the Plaza by the magic of the Water Elemental.
column2: 你驚訝地凝視著水元素的魔法為廣場帶來的驚人蛻變。

column1: The Sultan personally sent me to return you to his Palace, Hero of all Shapeir.
column2: 蘇丹親自派我來請您回宮,全夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: Will anyone speak for this Stranger from the North?
column2: 有誰願意為這位來自北方的異鄉人發言？

column1: Who will speak for this Hero of Spielburg?
column2: 誰願為這位史畢柏格的英雄作保發言?

column1: We are Shameen and Shema, keepers of the Katta's Tail Inn.  When the Katta were driven from Raseir, we journeyed to the Northland in search of a hero.
column2: 我們是沙明與舍瑪，卡塔之尾旅店的主人。當卡塔族被逐出拉希爾時，我們啟程前往北方尋找英雄。

column1: Through his courage and skills, he restored the Baron's son and daughter and banished the wicked Baba Yaga.
column2: 他憑著勇氣與本事,救回了男爵的兒女,驅逐了邪惡的芭芭雅嘎。

column1: He became Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 他成了史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: He journeyed back with us to Shapeir.  Here he defeated the Elementals of Fire, Air, Earth, and Water to save our city.
column2: 他與我們一同回到夏皮爾。在此，他擊敗了火、風、土、水四大元素，拯救了我們的城市。

column1: He is a true hero.
column2: 他是個真正的英雄。

column1: I am Walid, a guard of the Palace and a member of the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 我是瓦利德,宮廷衛兵,也是永恆戰士團的一員。

column1: This man vanquished me in a fair fight and demonstrated great skills in combat.
column2: 這位先生在堂堂正正的較量中擊敗了我,展現了高超的戰技。

column1: When I lay helpless upon the ground, he was given orders by the members of EOF to kill me.
column2: 當我倒地無力還手時,戰士團的人下令要他殺了我。

column1: Instead, he showed me mercy.  This man is a true hero.
column2: 可他卻饒了我一命。這位先生是個真正的英雄。

column1: Who will speak for this man of mercy?
column2: 誰願為這位慈悲之人作保發言?

column1: I am the Enchantress Aziza.
column2: 我是女術士阿姿莎。

column1: This man of his own free will went into the desert and sought the plant-woman Julanar.
column2: 這位先生出於自願,深入沙漠,去尋找化為樹的女子茱拉娜爾。

column1: Through his kindness and compassion, he allowed Julanar to forget the pain and suffering of her past and to again face life with joy.
column2: 他以仁慈與悲憫,讓茱拉娜爾放下過往的痛苦與磨難,重新帶著喜悅面對人生。

column1: This man also found a caged beast in the desert.
column2: 這位先生還在沙漠裡發現了一頭被囚的野獸。

column1: We shall hope it may learn from the kindness of the hero.
column2: 但願牠能從英雄的仁慈中有所領悟。

column1: This man is a true hero.
column2: 這位先生是個真正的英雄。

column1: Who will speak for this man of compassion?
column2: 有誰願意為這位仁慈之人發言？

column1: Who will speak for this man of courage?
column2: 誰願為這位勇敢之人作保發言?

column1: Who will speak for this man of Honor?
column2: 有誰願意為這位榮譽之人發言？

column1: I am Zayishah, daughter of the Emir Ali Al-Din Hasan.
column2: 我是扎伊莎，埃米爾阿里·阿丁·哈桑之女。

column1: In order to escape the repression of my homeland, Raseir, I asked this stranger for aid.
column2: 為了逃離故鄉拉希爾的暴政,我向這位異鄉人求助。

column1: At the risk to his own life, he helped me escape from the city.
column2: 他冒著性命危險,助我逃出了城。

column1: Gronk.
column2: 咕嚕。

column1: Then let him have a voice that he may speak.
column2: 那就讓他開口,讓他為自己發聲。

column1: You offer a dispel potion which was prepared by Harik for this occasion.
column2: 你拿出哈里克專為此刻調製的驅散藥水。

column1: When Ad Avis transformed me into a Saurus, I lost all hope of returning to my own form.
column2: 當阿德·阿維斯把我變成一頭索魯斯時,我已不抱任何變回原形的指望。

column1: I am Harun al-Rashid, Sultan of the land of Shapeir.
column2: 我是哈倫·拉希德,夏皮爾國度的蘇丹。

column1: I am the Poet Omar, Teller of tales for all to hear.
column2: 我是詩人歐瑪爾，向眾人講述故事的說書人。

column1: By the words which have been spoken,
column2: 憑著方才道出的這番話,

column1: By the deeds that have been done,
column2: 憑著已然成就的這些事,

column1: I proclaim this man a Hero,
column2: 我在此宣告，此人為英雄，

column1: I now call this man my son.
column2: 我自今日起,稱他為吾子。

column1: Is there anyone now who will speak for this son of the Sultan?
column2: 此刻可有人願為這位蘇丹之子作保發言?

column1: This man has shown courage in the face of great danger,
column2: 這位先生臨大難而不懼,

column1: Kindness to those in need, generosity to the poor,
column2: 對困頓者仁慈,對窮苦人慷慨,

column1: Honesty in dealing with others, and Mercy to his enemies.
column2: 待人誠實，對敵仁慈。

column1: He is a man of great Honor.
column2: 他是一位極具榮譽感的人。

column1: I, Rasha Rakeesh SahTarna,
column2: 我，拉沙·拉基什·薩塔納，

column1: You are now a Paladin.
column2: 你現在是一位聖騎士了。

column1: CONGRATULATIONS!! You have successfully completed "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire" with the maximum possible score, %d of %d!! We welcome you to the ranks of the few, the proud, the True Heroes!
column2: 恭喜！！你已成功以滿分通關《英雄傳奇 II：烈火試煉》，%d 分（滿分 %d）！！歡迎加入真英雄──這群為數不多、令人驕傲的行列！

column1: Congratulations!  You have successfully completed "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire." Your final score was %d of %d possible Puzzle Points.
column2: 恭喜！你已成功通關《英雄傳奇 II：烈火試煉》。你的最終得分為 %d 分（滿分 %d 解謎點數）。

column1: If you have not already done so, we encourage you to play "Quest for Glory II" again with the other two character types; many of the puzzles are different, or have alternate solutions.
column2: 如果你還沒試過，建議你用另外兩種角色類型再玩一次《英雄傳奇 II》；許多謎題會有所不同，或有其他解法。

column1: In the meantime, you are already a winner! Please insert a writable disk in your floppy drive to save your winning Hero for use in "Quest for Glory III:  Shadows of Darkness."
column2: 不論如何，你已經是勝利者！請在軟碟機中插入可寫入的磁碟片，儲存你的過關英雄，以便在《英雄傳奇 III：暗影之詛》中使用。

column2: 用來儲存英雄的磁碟檔案。

column2: 角色存檔已建立完成。請保留此磁碟片，以便用於 Sierra On-Line 的《英雄傳奇 III：暗影之詛》。

column1: Do you wish to try saving your character again?
column2: 要再試一次儲存角色嗎？

column1: Thank you for playing "Quest for Glory II:  Trial by Fire," and congratulations again on winning.  We'll see you again soon in "Quest for Glory III: Shadows of Darkness."
column2: 感謝您遊玩《英雄傳奇 II：烈火試煉》，再次恭喜您獲勝。我們將在《英雄傳奇 III：暗影之詛》中再會。

column1: Congratulations, Paladin!  [score %d of %d]
column2: 恭喜你，聖騎士！  [得分 %d，滿分 %d]

column1: Hero of Two Lands!  [score %d of %d]
column2: 兩地英雄！  [得分 %d，滿分 %d]

column1: Ha Ha Ha Haaaaaaah Ha Haah Ha Ha Haaaah Ha Haaaa!
column2: 哈哈哈哈哈哈哈！哈哈哈！哈哈哈哈哈！哈哈哈哈！

column1: And so, as the moon moves slowly through the starry sky above the mountains and the desert of Shapeir, our Hero bids a fond "Adieu" to his latest set of adventures and miserable puns.  Where will the road lead next...?
column2: 就這樣，當月亮緩緩劃過夏皮爾山巒與沙漠上空的星空，我們的英雄向他這趟充滿冒險與蹩腳雙關語的旅程道一聲珍重的「再見」。前方的道路，又將通往何方呢……？

column1: But wait!  There's more to come in the next thrilling saga of "Quest for Glory" by Lori and Corey.
column2: 且慢！柯瑞與蘿莉·柯爾打造的《英雄傳奇》系列，還有更多精彩故事等著你！

column1: Our next game...
column2: 我們的下一部作品……

column1: Twinkle, twinkle.
column2: 閃爍，閃爍。

column1: Centaurs are best at finding their way across the desert.
column2: 半人馬最擅長在沙漠中辨明方向。

column1: The best way to get goods across the desert is by pack saurus.
column2: 要橫越沙漠運送貨物，最好的方法就是使用馱獸索魯斯。

column1: The Bedouins of the desert are good people, for the most part.
column2: 沙漠中的貝都因人大多是善良的人。

column1: Before the sun has risen over the mountaintops, you are awakened by Shameen, who tells you that even now, the caravan to Raseir is preparing to depart.
column2: 天還未亮，太陽尚未升上山頭，沙明便將你喚醒，告訴你前往拉希爾的商隊此刻正準備出發。

column1: Shema greets you as you leave the room with some breakfast and supplies to take with you.  The inn is filled with katta merchants who wish you luck upon your departure.
column2: 你離開房間時，舍瑪迎上前來，遞給你早餐與隨行補給。旅店裡擠滿了卡塔族商人，紛紛祝你一路順風。

column1: As you pass through the gate of Shapeir, the gate guard presents you with a new saurus, a gift from the Sultan.  Ahead in the desert sands awaits a mass of Saurii and riders being led by the Caravan Master, a huge Centaur who orders the Caravan into motion before the sun can be seen.
column2: 當你穿過夏皮爾城門時，守衛送上蘇丹贈予的一頭新索魯斯。前方沙地上，一大群索魯斯與騎手正等候著，由商隊隊長率領──那是一位魁梧的半人馬，他在日出前便下令商隊啟程。

column1: You ride out to join in this new adventure, pausing only to watch the sunrise.
column2: 你騎馬出發,迎向這場嶄新的冒險,只在途中稍稍駐足,看一眼日出。

column1: The only way of getting goods across the desert is by pack saurus.
column2: 橫越沙漠運送貨物的唯一方法，就是使用馱獸索魯斯。

column1: The caravan comes under siege from hundreds of nomadic brigands.  Your small group is vastly outnumbered.  The situation looks grim.
column2: 商隊遭到數百名遊牧盜匪的圍攻。你們人數懸殊、寡不敵眾，情勢看來十分嚴峻。

column1: Enter/Return to continue
column2: 按 Enter/Return 鍵繼續

column1: I love the smell of victory in the morning.      ......  It smells like.....sweat.. no, no      ...oregano ....nooo...like Victory!  Yea      Victory!...Yea! Yea!  That's the ticket.
column2: 我愛清晨勝利的氣味。      ......  聞起來像是.....汗水..不對，不對      ...牛至草....不對不對...像是勝利！耶      勝利！...耶！耶！就是這樣！

column1: After a week of travelling, you leave the Caravan when it comes within sight of the city of Raseir.
column2: 經過一週的跋涉，當商隊終於望見拉希爾城時，你便與商隊分道揚鑣。

column1: The Caravan Master warns you that Raseir is a dangerous place to be and bids you be wary of all you meet there.
column2: 商隊首領警告你,拉希爾是個危險的地方,囑咐你提防在那兒遇見的每一個人。

column1: He rears up and salutes you, and you watch the Caravan move onward into the desert.
column2: 牠人立起身向你致意，你目送商隊繼續深入沙漠。

column1: You ride alone to the entrance to Raseir, and pass through the barred gates which barely open for you.
column2: 你獨自騎行到拉希爾的入口,穿過那道勉強為你開啟的鐵閘城門。

column1: The saurus is an amazing beast.  This steed must have remained at the gate, awaiting your future need, while you carried on your business in Raseir.
column2: 索魯斯真是令人驚嘆的坐騎。想必在你於拉希爾辦事期間，牠便一直留在城門邊，等候你未來的需要。

column1: Defining polygons (or paths):  Move cursor to point and click button to define point. Ctrl click marks path end.  Other useful keys:  F4 or <alt-C>: Show control screen. F2 or <alt-V>: Show visual. E: toggle ego showing or hidden. P: toggle position info on/off. <alt-M>: Modify path. <ctrl-S>: Quit and save all path data ESC: Quit without saving.
column2: 定義多邊形（或路徑）：  將游標移至該點並按下按鍵以定義該點。Ctrl 點擊標記路徑終點。  其他實用按鍵：  F4 或 <alt-C>：顯示控制畫面。F2 或 <alt-V>：顯示視覺畫面。E：切換主角顯示／隱藏。P：切換位置資訊顯示／隱藏。<alt-M>：修改路徑。<ctrl-S>：離開並儲存所有路徑資料 ESC：離開但不儲存。

column1: Modifying defined polygons:  <space>: Move to next defined point. <enter>: Select and edit point. C: Cut point. I: Insert point after current point. ESC: Return to creating new points. F4 or <alt-C>: Show control screen. F2 or <alt-V>: Show visual. E: toggle ego showing or hidden. P: toggle position info on/off.  Editing point:  Move cursor to new point and click mouse button.

column1: You are good, very good.  Soon you will be a master with your sword.  However, the real battle is not always won by a swing or a thrust.  To fight with Honor is not always easy.  Sometimes the Way of Honor will seem foolish to those that have none.  Nevertheless, without Honor, there is no victory.  With Honor, there is no real defeat.
column2: 你很不錯，非常不錯。不久你就會成為劍術大師。然而，真正的戰鬥並非總是靠揮砍或刺擊取勝。秉持榮譽而戰並不容易。有時榮譽之道，在無榮譽者眼中顯得愚蠢。然而，沒有榮譽就沒有真正的勝利；有了榮譽，就沒有真正的失敗。

column1: You need to defend better.  You hit pretty often, but you get hit too often.

column1: Don't get into combat with Scorpion until you dodge better.
column2: 閃避練得更好之前,別去跟蠍子硬碰硬。

column1: You getting much better, but you still be not defending enough.
column2: 你進步很多,可防守還是不夠。

column1: Do not let me touch you with my spear the next time.  I will be plenty mad if some monster tears you to shreds because you do not dodge enough.
column2: 下次別再讓我的矛碰到你。要是哪天有怪物因為你閃得不夠把你撕成碎片,我可要氣壞了。

column1: Enough for now.  You look pretty winded.  We go back to Hall.
column2: 今天到此為止。你看起來喘得很厲害，我們回大廳去吧。

column1: You got a good swing.  My shield arm is sore.
column2: 你這一劍揮得不錯，我持盾的手臂都痠了。

column1: That time, we both got workout.  Pretty soon, I be needing rest.
column2: 這回咱倆都練得夠嗆。再過不久,我可得歇口氣了。

column1: You be getting past my shield many times now.  You be getting good.
column2: 你現在好幾次都突破我的盾了。你越來越行啦。

column1: Brigands in the desert better watch out for you.  They be in big trouble.
column2: 沙漠裡的強盜可得提防你了。他們麻煩大囉。

column1: The TerrorSaurus who tries to eat you will be dead meat first.
column2: 想吃掉你的恐魯斯，會先變成一堆死肉。

column1: You can take on a pack of Jackalmen now.
column2: 你現在能對付一整群豺狼人啦。

column1: The Ghoulyman who tries to get you now will be sorry.
column2: 現在敢來找你麻煩的食屍鬼,可有得後悔了。

column1: That be enough for now.  Soon you be giving me lessons.
column2: 今天就到這兒。要不了多久,就換你來教我啦。

column1: Either I be out of shape from Simba or you be getting too good for me plenty soon.
column2: 要嘛是我被辛巴搞得身手生疏了,要嘛就是你很快就要強到我招架不住啦。

column1: You very fast learner.  I think maybe you be plenty tough enough now.
column2: 你學得真快。我想你現在大概夠硬了。

column1: That be enough for today.  Soon you be giving me lessons.
column2: 今天就練到這兒。要不了多久,就換你來教我啦。

column1: Block my spear or get out of the way.  If it be scorpion's tail, you be dead.
column2: 擋下我的長矛，不然就閃開。若這是蠍尾，你早就死了。

column1: Dodge!  Don't take damage, get out of the way.
column2: 閃開！別硬扛傷害，快閃開。

column1: Move out of the way more.  You got feet, use them.
column2: 多挪動一點身子，你有腳，就該用上。

column1: Don't rely on your weapon to save you. Use your body to avoid damage.
column2: 別指望武器救你。用你的身子來閃傷害。

column1: You let me get you with spear way too often.  If I be ghoul, you be plenty cold right now.
column2: 你被我的長矛刺中太多次了。若我是食屍鬼，你現在早就涼透了。

column1: Never stand still.  Moving target harder to hit.
column2: 千萬別站著不動。會動的目標才難打中。

column1: TerrorSaurus is stupid but strong.  Takes a while before it knows it is dead.
column2: 恐魯斯又笨又壯，要過好一會兒才知道自己死了。

column1: Watch my actions.  Move before I do.
column2: 看我的動作。趕在我之前先動。

column1: Swing high, swing low.  Keep enemy guessing where your weapon will be.
column2: 忽高忽低地揮擊，讓敵人猜不透你的武器會落在哪裡。

column1: Don't watch my weapon, watch my eyes.  Let them show you where I will hit and when.
column2: 別盯著我的武器，看我的眼睛。它們會告訴你我會打哪裡、何時出手。

column1: Put your body into your thrusts.
column2: 刺擊時把整個身子的勁都使上。

column1: Brigands don't defend well.  Keep up your attacks.
column2: 強盜的防禦不佳，持續進攻就對了。

column1: If I be Griffin, swing high.  If I be Ghoul, swing low.  Use best attack for monster.
column2: 若我是獅鷲，就往高處揮；若我是食屍鬼，就往低處揮。針對怪物用最適合的攻擊。

column1: Scorpion always wiggles tail before it strikes.  Be ready to dodge.
column2: 蠍子出擊前總會先擺動尾巴，隨時準備閃避。

column1: Jackalmen may be cowards, but they do fight well.  Keep sword on one at a time.  Take it out as fast as possible.
column2: 豺狼人雖膽小，打起來卻不含糊。一次專心對付一個，盡快解決。

column1: Keep your shield held higher.  Easier to block spears and swords with it there.
column2: 盾舉高點。擺那兒擋矛擋劍都更輕鬆。

column1: When fighting against a sword, do not let a swing catch you off guard.  You can be disarmed that way.
column2: 對上持劍的對手時,別讓一記揮砍打你個措手不及。那樣會被繳了械。

column1: Do not let yourself be distracted by thoughts.  Let yourself be one with your weapon.
column2: 別讓雜念分了你的心。讓自己與武器合而為一。

column1: Let your motion flow smoothly as you dodge.  Always remain in balance.
column2: 閃避時,讓動作流暢自然。永遠保持平衡。
